# Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 July 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/DibjVO_zbvU

## Global backdrop
- Global cues mixed/slightly negative but **GIFT Nifty positive**, indicating a **90-100 point gap-up** open.
- US overnight: **Nasdaq -0.66%, S&P 500 -0.22%, Dow flat**. Kospi down 3-4%, other Asian markets soft, MSCI EM index closed lower — while India closed up, signalling **India outperformance**.
- **Meta +9%** on entering the cloud-infrastructure business (competing with Azure/AWS). Traditional software companies (e.g., Palantir peers) rallied on a growing "anti-AI-tokenization" narrative — criticism that AI firms (Anthropic/Claude, Grok, etc.) are monetizing wrongly and should be open-source; sparking talk of a possible **AI-trade reversal** and cost-benefit/ROI doubts on AI capex.
- US **Q2 earnings season** starting; market expects **18-22% earnings growth**.
- **Crude oil** decisively below **$70/barrel** (down from a war-driven $120 peak) — a major positive for India; West Asia crisis largely defused.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty closed +140 pts above 24,000; Sensex +450 pts** yesterday — first signs of the anticipated **H2-CY26 India outperformance**.
- **Sector rotation**: previously unloved/oversold **FMCG** (HUL, Asian Paints, ITC, Nestle, Dabur), **Realty** (Lodha, DLF, Godrej Properties) and platform names (Paytm, Lenskart, Eternal) rallied hard; market breadth positive. FMCG buying linked to strong **GST data** (implies economic/employment strength) and hopes of monsoon-led rural recovery.
- **IT/Pharma lagged** as domestic-facing sectors led; **KPIT -15%** yesterday. Anchor reiterated a standing "avoid IT" call — business-model risk, likely profit warnings ahead, no bottom-fishing on cheapness alone.
- **FIIs**: light net sellers (~Rs 1,140 cr) but sitting on **~90% short positions** in F&O entering July — a potential **short-covering trigger** if the "feel-good factor" strengthens. **DIIs bought Rs 3,159 cr.**
- **Nifty levels**: resistance **24,250-24,400** (per Kranthi) / **24,260-300** (per Ramakrishna); support **23,800** (important zone since June 15). Bank Nifty: resistance **~58,700**, support **~57,000**; RSI 63, above all MAs incl. 200-DMA.
- **PCR 1.13**, India VIX cooled to ~2.63(as stated). Highest OI: **24,000 calls**; Bank Nifty **58,300 calls / 58,000 puts**.
- **Macro positives**: GST collections **+14% YoY**, car sales **+24% YoY** avg, strong UPI transactions and freight loading — signalling economy resilience despite global "turbulence" (Trump-related trade noise).
- Market called a **"buy on dips"** market; stock selection should focus on **earnings visibility + management commentary/execution track record**, not just cheapness.
- **Market open**: Nifty +80, Sensex +230, Bank Nifty +110-120; breadth positive (NSE 1,446 adv/372 dec). Mid-cap standouts: **SCTL** (upper circuit again, band cut 10%→5%), **Sai Parentals** (+8% on a Rs 1,300-cr Australian "Nawmed" contract renewal, ~80-90% above its Rs 392 IPO price), **GNG Electronics** (near upper circuit, laptop-refurbishment play, new Redington tie-up). Losers: **Zee Entertainment** (-3%, promoter warrant issue), **HFCL** (-3%, profit booking), KPR Mills, Aegis Logistics, Apar Industries, Sai Life Sciences, RITES, GE T&D, Oil India.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao was absent today.** The anchor explicitly noted this on air ("I think Kutumba Rao garu hasn't come") before bringing in the two analysts who were present.
- **Kranthi** — fundamental analyst — covered the market view (FMCG/sector-rotation thesis, GST data) and answered the bulk of viewer stock Q&A.
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst — covered Nifty/Bank Nifty levels, OI, RSI/MACD, and intraday trade set-ups.
- Anchor also highlighted **SCTL** (ambitious 2-year growth roadmap, new data-centre order possible) and **Sai Parentals** (Australian order, Hyderabad pharma growth story) as thematic picks, and flagged **Invesco India Smallcap Fund** (a viewer's ~25% 3.5-month return) as evidence for a 50/50 direct-stock/mutual-fund portfolio split.

## Note on stock calls
Because Kutumba Rao did not appear on this episode, **no calls are attributed to him** — see `2026-07-02__Business_Breakfast_Stock_Market_News_July_2_2026_TV5_Money.buys.json` (`recommendations: []`) and the companion `kutumba_rao.md` file for the full list of calls made instead by Kranthi and Ramakrishna.
takeaways: Kranthi: 7 buys (Blue Jet Healthcare, Tenneco Clean Air India, LT Foods, Amara Raja, Hitachi Energy (Power India), GE Vernova, LG Electronics), 1 sell (Edelweiss Financial Services)

kranti.note: Highest short build-up in FMCG after 3 years of underperformance; bottom-fishing happening, but sustainability depends on Q1 results and management guidance.
Can be held for the long term without any doubt; worth adding slowly on any dip.
Can be held confidently for the long term; add slowly on dips.
Underperformed 3 years on FII selling; when FII flows reverse, such large caps can outperform again - hold for now.
FIIs sold over Rs 2,50,000 crore in 2 years after peak ~85% holding; once FII money flows back it can outperform - hold.
Same answer as PFC/HDFC Bank - holdable large cap, wait for FII flow reversal.
Up 150% but no need to book profits; Q1 results expected to be good - sell only if tired of waiting.
Decent quarterly earnings, expansion capex in pharma zone at Vizag; add on dips with a ~2-year timeframe.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 1 July 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/pVS12PjRBlc

The show opened with birthday wishes to TV5 / TTD Chairman **B.R. (Bolineni Rajagopal) Naidu**. It is the first day of **July**, of the new quarter, and of a new **F&O series**, all at once. **Gift Nifty** indicated a weak/lower opening, but the market actually opened flat-to-positive.

## Global backdrop
- **US markets** closed higher led by tech/AI: **Nasdaq +1.5%**, **S&P 500 +0.78%** — though ex-tech, **~60-70% of S&P 500 stocks fell**. The **"Magnificent 7"** (Amazon, Apple, Nvidia, Alphabet, Meta, etc.) are driving the tape.
- Gains driven by a positive **JOLTS jobs data** upside surprise, easing job-loss fears.
- **First-half (calendar) scorecard**: Nasdaq **+27%** (2nd strongest in 25 yrs); **Philadelphia Semiconductor Index +88%**; Dow one of its strongest H1s. In Q2, **Micron, AMD, Intel** added **~$2 trillion** combined market cap.
- **Crude oil** spiked to **~$120/bbl** on the war but has cooled back to **~$69-70/bbl**; a stabilised crude is seen as a big positive for India.
- **New Fed chairman** turned **hawkish** (possible rate hike in H2), reversing rate-cut hopes — a factor behind gold/silver profit-booking.

## Indian markets
- **June series** closed nearly flat: **-48 pts** on the series, Nifty **-45 pts** for the month (May was -82). April had surged **+1,600 pts** (war-settlement hopes); March **-3,000 pts** (war onset).
- **Positioning skew:** FIIs began the July series **~90% short** (record), rolling over **98% of shorts** vs only **55% of longs**; total rollovers **~1.79 cr contracts / ~80%**. In contrast, **client (HNI/retail) longs ~80%** — clients bullish, FIIs bearish on July.
- Anchor's guess: **July closes positive**; noted **Kutumba Rao's (prior-day) 24,000 month-end call**.
- **Small/mid caps outperformed**: Smallcap index **+4%** last month. H1 standouts — **HFCL +208%**, **Apar Industries +88%**, **Data Patterns +73%**, RR Kabel, Honasa, Finolex Cables, Angel One, Himadri Speciality +40%, Raymond +36%, Tejas Networks +35%, GE Shipping +33%, Nippon Life AMC +33%, Navin Fluorine. Laggards: Cyient, Alok, BLS Intl, Affle, IIFL Finance, Rites, NBCC.
- **IT cautious** — **KPIT profit warning** (revenue/EBITDA/margins to fall); stay selective (Oracle Financial Services, Coforge); Persistent's acquisition viewed negatively.
- **Pharma is the go-to sector** — Pharma index at **all-time high**; Dr Reddy's, **SMS Pharma +11%**, Laurus Labs, Lupin, Cipla, Sun Pharma strong; Hyderabad-based names leading. **SITL/SCTL** flagged as a pharma proxy expanding into **data-center cooling/piping** (billions-$ opportunity); stock hit **10% upper circuit** yesterday and was **+9%** today.
- **Auto sales (June)** expected strong: Maruti +26%, M&M +26%, Tata Motors PV +58%, Eicher/Royal Enfield +21%, CVs +13%, Ashok Leyland +9%. Early prints: M&M sales up, **Escorts Kubota +19% YoY**.
- **Technicals (Ramakrishna):** Nifty range-bound since ~April 8 (broad range); support **23,800**, resistance/target **24,000 → 24,261**. RSI ~51.7, MACD converging → consolidation/neutral. OI: highest call 24,000, highest put 23,400. **Bank Nifty** stronger (above all MAs); resistance 58,700, support 56,850; 58,000 call & put highest OI → mild upward bias. India VIX **~13.4**, PCR **0.8**. **Monsoon ~40% deficit** flagged as a near-term negative.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao did NOT join today.** Present were **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst), **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst), with **Vasanth** anchoring/analysing.
- **Kranthi's fundamental calls (H2 themes & viewer Q&A):** buy power stocks (GE Vernova, TD Power) on dips; chemicals attractive (Clean Science on dips, 2-3 yr); defense watchlist (Cochin Shipyard, BDL, Mazagon Dock); CG Power as an SIP watchlist stock; **SITL/SCTL his favourite**. Viewer Q&A verdicts: NCC hold (Sem India better), Nava add on dips, JB Laminations hold/no-average, Brigade hold-sell on rallies, Bharat Bijlee hold, Persistent hold (long term), Chennai Petro book above 1,200, Avanti Feeds & Eternal hold, Heritage Foods hold (long-term), Godfrey Phillips buy on the move (news/real-estate/family-settlement driven), JK Enterprises accumulate below 200, GOCL avoid, VA Tech Wabag/Laurus/SCTL/Polycab hold (book Polycab if desired), Infosys hold near 1,000 (contra).
- **Ramakrishna's technical calls:** long only above **23,970-980** (50-pt stop) or buy the **23,800** dip; SMS Pharma enter 50% now, add above 445; Cohance weak → buy only above 515-520; VA Tech Wabag hold (breakout above ~1,950).
takeaways: Kranthi: 6 buys (GE Vernova, TD Power Systems, Clean Science, Sem India, Godfrey Phillips, JK Enterprises), 3 sells (Chennai Petro, VA Tech Wabag, Polycab)

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 30 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/adftq8FwykE

## Global backdrop
- **US markets rose**: **Nasdaq +2%**, **S&P 500 +1.17%**, ending a five-day losing streak for the S&P 500; AI/tech/chip stocks led.
- **Dollar Index at 101.28**. **Japanese yen at its lowest vs the dollar since 1986** (a 40-year low). This morning **Nikkei +0.93%**, **Kospi +0.69%**.
- **Samsung + SK Hynix** announced a combined **~$1.2 trillion** AI/chip capex over the next five years to expand memory-chip capacity (memory demand now second only to GPUs). Hyperscalers collectively are on track for **~$800bn** of AI investment in 2026.
- **Crude ~$72**; a move below $70 would help Bank Nifty. **War impact seen as almost over.**

## Indian markets
- **Triple event today**: June F&O series expiry, month-end, and quarter-end all fall on the 30th. Volatility and rollover-driven trade expected; Gift Nifty pointed to a slightly positive open.
- **Yesterday**: Nifty **-110 pts**, Bank Nifty closed weak; FIIs sold **Rs 1,350 cr**, DIIs bought **Rs 2,800 cr** (net institutional buying, but negative sentiment).
- **Levels**: 50-DMA **23,846** and 100-DMA **24,146** converging (~250 pts apart — often precedes a big move). Support **23,785 / 23,800**, resistance **24,260**. June was flat/range-bound (**~400-pt range, 23,800–24,200**).
- **Open**: Nifty **+55**, Bank Nifty **+220**, Sensex **+230**; advances 948 vs declines 467 — broader market stronger.
- **Banking "musical chairs"** (ET): Bandhan CEO **Rajeev Anand → Axis Bank**; Axis CEO **Puneet Sharma → HDFC Bank**; **Rajiv Kumar** (ex-CEC) named HDFC Bank part-time chairman (3-yr term); Atanu Chakraborty allegations set aside. HDFC Bank uncertainty largely cleared (only MD & CEO post pending) — could give decent returns over 1–2 years.
- **Delhi EV policy**: mandates EV adoption, old ICE vehicles to be replaced by 2030 — watch impact on ICE makers, gas suppliers (**MGL, IGL**), and beneficiaries (**Electra Green Tech, JBM, Ashok Leyland, Tata Motors, Maruti**).
- **Macro**: monsoon is the **driest since 2009** with a **~40–43% June deficit** and **kharif sowing area down ~23%** (rural-economy risk); offset by **IIP at a 5-month high of 5.1%** (led by manufacturing/electricity). External debt at **$762bn (~20.8% debt/GDP)**. Rupee steady at **~94.51** (couldn't hold below 94). Gold fell a 4th straight month. **India again the 5th-largest market cap** — scope to outperform after two years of underperformance.
- **Themes/flows**: broad-based rally with sector rotation; pharma strong on a firm dollar (**Laurus +10%/mth, crossed 1,500; Dr Reddy's +8% in 14 days; Lupin/Cipla near 52-wk highs**); metals rebounded (Nalco, Vedanta). **Promoters sold ~Rs 24,000 cr of stakes to domestic institutions in May–June** — read as positive given strong domestic (MF/SIP/PE) liquidity.
- **Corporate actions**: Persistent Systems -11% on Nagarro takeover; Astral fell on chemical-division demerger; Pricol announced an auto-castings demerger; Netweb punished (>120 PE) on fund-raise while Apollo Micro Systems rewarded. **SCTL** opened strong (~255, heavy volumes). **Genus Power -11%** on a block deal.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (markets + viewer stock Q&A): cautious on July despite consolidation — Q1 results will show war/supply-chain impact; **stay invested but stock-specific**. Options: monthly calls shifted to 24,000 (from 24,500), few next-month rollovers; **sustaining above 24,000 is key**; puts building at 23,500; a close below 23,800 for a couple of sessions risks an aggressive down-move. **PCR fell 1.2 → 0.85**, **VIX ~13.61** — bull confidence easing. Speculative longs in Max Health, Radico, Paytm, Coforge, Persistent, Bharat Forge, Hero Motor; delivery buying in ICICI General Insurance, ICICI Prudential, Asian Paints, NTPC. Flagged expensive high-PE mid/smallcaps as risky; realty (Keystone, Lodha, Omaxe) showing hidden strength; Macpower (CNC) +15-16%.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst, 2nd segment): views promoter stake-sales to institutions as **positive** (3–5-yr MF horizons, strong domestic liquidity, marquee investors — Madhu Kela, Vijay Kedia — entering; watch *who* buys, not just that the promoter sells). Answered viewer calls on Ashok Leyland, Ten Eco Clean Air, CG Power and KIMS.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst): **absent today**.

## Kutumba Rao's viewer stock calls (see kutumba_rao file for detail)
- **Buy (long-term)**: Infosys, Bajaj Housing Finance. **Accumulate on dips**: Rainbow Children's Medicare (~1,275–1,300), Enter Technologies (after Q1). **Income InvITs**: Capital Infra Trust + PG InvIT (12–15%+ yields; review 6-monthly). **Hold**: Laurus Labs ("never sell"). **Watch (wait for dips)**: Aditya Infotech (~120 PE). **Avoid/Sell**: Just Dial (exit 580–590), Infollion Research Services (SME), SBI Cards (switch to SBI; exit 630–640 on relief rally), KCP Sugar.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 buys (Rainbow Children's Medicare, Infosys, Bajaj Housing Finance, Capital Infra Trust, PG InvIT, Enter Technologies), 3 sells (Just Dial, SBI Cards, KCP Sugar)
Kranthi: 4 calls (Ashok Leyland, Tenneco Clean Air India, CG Power, KIMS)

kutumba.note: Good long-term bet but won't outperform hugely; buy on correction.
kutumba.detail: Already trading above 50 PE and near 52-week highs, now modestly corrected to ~1,400+. Hospital sector has good FII interest pushing PEs higher. He'd pick it up on a dip to 1,275-1,300.

kutumba.note: Buy with a long-term view.
kutumba.detail: Fell along with other tech majors on the AI overhang, but some stability came after Nilekani's AGM speech. A good long-term bet, though not expected to give greatly outperforming returns.

kutumba.note: Long-term good returns as loan book builds.
kutumba.detail: Corrected after listing and has now stabilised; its loan portfolio is building up. For the long term it might give good returns.

kutumba.note: High-yield InvIT income play; review every 6 months.
kutumba.detail: A good highest-dividend InvIT with ~15.7% yield (anywhere above 12% likely), but not a 15-year instrument - typical InvIT life is 5-10 years and it may not exist after 10 years. Do it as income, review at least every 6 months, and diversify across 2-3 InvITs/REITs.

kutumba.note: Second income InvIT to diversify into (~12% yield, quarterly).
kutumba.detail: Suggested alongside Capital Infra Trust for diversification. Around 12% dividend yield works out and they pay quarterly dividends.

kutumba.note: Never sell; strong pharma name.
kutumba.detail: Holder is up ~6x from 292. 'The word sell will never come from my mouth for Laurus.' Pharma is strong on a firm dollar; Laurus is up ~10% over the last month and crossed 1,500.

kutumba.note: Valuations stretched but strong momentum; add on dips.
kutumba.detail: Valuations look a bit stretched, but there is strong momentum and emerging buying interest. Management gave huge guidance last quarter which drove the rally; watch this quarter's execution. Add more on dips after the Q1 results.

kutumba.note: Extremely strong chart but too expensive to chase now.
kutumba.detail: 52-week chart is extremely strong (higher-top/higher-bottom, jumped ~to 3,500 from May-last-week to June-first-week), but it is a not-cheap ~120-PE stock and any correction will be severe. Given at 2,000 at the Hyderabad workshop (now ~doubled). Don't chase here - be afraid to buy after such a jump; wait for dips.

kutumba.note: Ambani-owned, weak counter with AI/quick-commerce headwinds.
kutumba.detail: Ambani-acquired stocks trade weak (cf. Sterling Wilson); previous high (bought at 750) is doubtful. Search-dependent models face pressure from AI tools and quick commerce. A short-term weak counter - exit at 580-590 in rallies unless it aligns with Reliance Retail. Rule of thumb: sell on Ambani takeovers.

kutumba.note: Micro SME stock near 52-week low; not for small investors.
kutumba.detail: An SME stock with an extremely small market cap (under 70 crore), already near its 52-week low. Not a good choice and not advisable for a small investor. Better stocks exist; buy such names only after a proven track record - otherwise avoid.

kutumba.note: Weak since listing; switch to parent SBI.
kutumba.detail: Holder at 860, now 550 (-30%). Card companies lack valuations in India versus US peers (Visa); the stock has traded continuously weak right from listing. Better to shift to parent company SBI. Expect a relief rally at results - exit around 630-640.

kutumba.note: Sugar sector weak; avoidable.
kutumba.detail: Sugar companies are not performing and results aren't coming as expected. With a weak monsoon the crushing season is likely to be poor too. As of now it is avoidable - exit in rallies.

kranti.note: Hold for now; stock is stable, consolidating in the 160 range.

Thematic stock, view with long-term prospect; if the theme plays out strongly it can come into the limelight, else long consolidation - believe in the theme, track developments, be careful (caption said "Ten Eco Clean Air"; recently listed auto-ancillary = Tenneco Clean Air India).
Decent, buy-on-dips kind of stock; hold for the medium to long term.
Decent stock, performing well, recent QIP done; hold for the medium to long term.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 29 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/OOhlWWt5gOA

## Global backdrop
- **AI-trade reversal** across Asia. **Korean Kospi** ~**-2%** today (down ~**7%** last week, hit a **downward circuit breaker**); **Japan's Nikkei** off ~**7%**. Two chip majors — **SK Hynix** and **Samsung** — are ~**60% of Korean exchange market cap**, amplifying index moves.
- **SK Hynix + Samsung** to announce (before Korea's PM) a **USD 1.3 trillion / 10-year** chip investment — framing a widening **US-vs-Asia AI/chip race** (US still the stronger AI player, led by **Nvidia** and hyperscalers).
- Open question: does the AI-trade unwind **divert emerging-market fund flows into India**?
- **West Asia**: fresh US-Iran strikes over the weekend — watch for escalation. **Russia-Ukraine** flagged as possibly re-escalating. **Gold** weak, ~**USD 4,000/oz**, with softening ETF flows. **Rupee** stable at **~94.39**.

## Indian markets
- **Expiry/settlement week**: monthly Nifty options expiry (June series) **tomorrow**; month-end too. Roll-overs and sector leadership to be watched.
- Last 3 months: **Nifty +~4-5.4%**, **Mid-cap +15%**, **Small-cap +20%** — but mid/small-caps **underperformed last week** (small-cap flat, mid-cap ~-1%); possible "tiredness."
- **FII selling continues**; index lacks large-cap leadership (needs **Reliance / Bharti** type names to pull).
- Near-term direction set by **two factors: Q1 results + monsoon progress**; global impact seen as limited.
- **Banks in focus**: an external legal review gave HDFC Bank's board a **clean chit** (dismissing ex-director **Atanu Chakraborty's** allegations); reports of **Sashidhar Jagdishan's** MD/CEO term (ends Oct) being **extended**; renewed buying in **HDFC Bank**, positive tone on **Kotak Mahindra**. **ICICI Bank** has led private banks; question is whether HDFC Bank now participates.
- **Netweb Technologies** (India's #1 AI proxy) weighing **QIP / rights / possible FPO** fund-raising amid a broad capex wave.

## Market open (as noted on air)
- **Nifty +~35-46 pts**, **Sensex +77**, **Bank Nifty +115**. Breadth mixed (BSE ~1400 adv / 1344 dec).
- **Buying**: pharma — **Dr Reddy's +3.5%**; banks (**ICICI Bank, HDFC Bank**); Olectra +1.27%.
- **Selling**: AI/IT names — **Persistent Systems -7%** (acquisition announcement taken poorly), Netweb.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets): Nifty must hold **>24,000** and cross **24,200** for safety; highest call OI **24,500**, puts piled at **24,000** (breach on volume = "rough time"). **PCR 1.06** (flat), **VIX ~12-13** (below 12 = bull confidence). Speculative longs in **IndiGo** (crude/ATF hopes), **Hero MotoCorp**, **Bajaj Auto** (auto-sales week). Fresh shorts in metals — **National Aluminium, Hindustan Zinc** — but "temporary bottom / oversold." Conviction delivery-buying in **HDFC Life, Nykaa, Alkem, ICICI Lombard General Insurance**. Overall a **consolidative phase — watch results**.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): Markets in a **consolidation zone** since ~June 15; couldn't hold **100-DEMA** but hold above 20/50-DMA and a falling trend line; RSI/MACD **consolidation-to-positive bias**. Suggested **shorting rallies / below 24,100**. **Bank Nifty**: resistance **58,750** (Thursday high; above it = further rally), support **57,000-57,100** near 200-DMA; RSI 66-67, mild outperformance vs Nifty likely. Highest OI: Nifty **24,200 call / 24,100 put**; Bank Nifty **58,500** call & put. Watch for a **volatile settlement move** tomorrow.

## Special interview — SETL enters data centers
- **Standard Engineering Technology Ltd (SETL/SCTL)** — a pharma/chemicals/food-processing engineering firm (**~800 cr FY revenue, guiding ~1,200 cr / 40-50% growth** in existing business) — is acquiring a **51% stake in GScale Energy Pvt Ltd** for **Rs 190 crore** (first phase), entering the **data-center engineering** space. Guests: SETL promoter **Kandala Nageswara Rao**, GScale promoter / "data-center Baahubali" **Kasu Brahma Reddy** (ex-**Control-S**, ~**20-25%** of India's DC capacity), CFO **Anyal**, and Japanese ED **Mr. Esuki** (satisfied with performance, open to investing more).
- **Deal structure**: Rs 190 cr = **Rs 125 cr cash/capex into the company** + **Rs 65 cr share-swap** to the founder (per SEBI pricing rules; stock last closed ~**Rs 210**, ~doubled from Rs 110-120). Total planned **~Rs 500 cr**, balance ~300 cr milestone/phase-wise. Funded from **Rs 220 cr FD** + trade receivables; **no debt**.
- **Market thesis**: India DC capacity to grow from **~1.6 GW** (built over ~30 yrs) to **10+ GW** in 5 years; **~Rs 50,000-60,000 cr capex per GW**, of which **~50-60% is products/engineering** (a 1 GW contract ≈ **Rs 30,000 cr** opportunity). EBITDA **~25-28%**. Products offered: **RPPs, PDCs** (AI-rack loads up **15-20x**, to **150-160 kW/rack**), **liquid-cooling CDUs** (currently imported), heat exchangers, SS piping — end-to-end turnkey cutting customer project timelines **4-6 months**. Revenue from DC business expected from **Q4** (dispatch from ~Nov-Dec). Global focus after India: **Middle East and Southeast Asia**.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 9 calls (IndiGo (InterGlobe Aviation), Hero MotoCorp, Bajaj Auto, National Aluminium (NALCO), Hindustan Zinc, HDFC Life Insurance, Nykaa (FSN E-Commerce), Alkem Laboratories, ICICI Lombard General Insurance)

kutumba.note: Speculative longs building on lower crude / ATF hopes
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao noted speculative longs are mostly building in IndiGo, on the hope that turbine/ATF fuel costs fall as crude prices have come down recently. He flagged it as a positioning observation rather than a firm recommendation.

kutumba.note: Auto-sales numbers due this week
kutumba.detail: He grouped Hero Motor with Bajaj Auto as names in focus this week because good monthly auto-sales numbers are due. He wants to see how consumer demand and auto sales hold up despite higher oil prices.

kutumba.note: Auto-sales numbers due this week
kutumba.detail: Flagged alongside Hero MotoCorp ahead of this week's automobile sales numbers, which he expects to impact the market. Watching whether sales stay resilient despite higher oil prices.

kutumba.note: Fresh shorts building but possibly a temporary bottom
kutumba.detail: He observed fresh shorts being built in metal stocks including National Aluminium as global rates corrected recently. However he said these look oversold in the extreme short term, so a temporary bottom may be near.

kutumba.note: Fresh shorts building but possibly a temporary bottom
kutumba.detail: Named with National Aluminium as a metal counter where fresh shorts are building on softer global rates. He flagged them as oversold in the very short term, hinting at a possible temporary bottom.

kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said conviction delivery-based buying is visible in HDFC Life, along with Nykaa, Alkem and ICICI Lombard General Insurance. He cited it as a positive institutional-interest signal in his market-internals readout.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 25 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/CEhBnO4fB5g

## Global backdrop
- **Global cues positive** except Hang Seng; **Gift Nifty** pointing to a positive open. Today is **weekly + monthly options expiry** for the Sensex, ahead of a **three-day long weekend** (markets shut tomorrow).
- **Micron Q3 blowout**: revenue **$41.46B** (est. ~$35B), EPS **$25.11** (est. $20.6), gross margins **84%** on price hikes; **Q4 revenue guidance $50B** (vs ~$43.55B expected). Stock **+15%+ post-market**, up **~700% over 12 months**. Data-centre/HBM revenue **7x YoY**.
- Renewed chip buying lifted **Korea +5%+**, **SK Hynix +10%**, Samsung up, **Japan ~+4%**. Western Digital and other Micron peers rose in the US.
- **Brent crude ~$72** — lowest since Feb 27, **down ~42%** from the $126 April-30 peak; futures already **below $70**. Indian basket ~$74.34 (June 23). A supply glut as stuck Persian Gulf tankers move again. Big positive for India's current-account deficit.
- **Dollar index ~101.5**, sharpest monthly gain in a year, after new Fed chief **Kevin Warsh** turned hawkish (rates up, not down). **Gold at a 7-month low** (key level **$4,000/oz**); **silver halved** from its January peak; base metals (aluminium) weak.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday: **Nifty +200**, closed above **24,000**; **Bank Nifty +~1,000 (+1.69%)**, best close since the war began; **IT index +2%**. Large caps outperformed; **mid/small caps underperformed** (breadth only ~1:1 — 1,578 adv / 1,544 dec).
- Rally attributed to the **RBI Governor's dovish tilt** (continue rate regime, less inflation fear) — strong bank rebound. Infosys aided by **Nandan Nilekani** GBM comments (AI facilitates, not doomsday for services); but IT's Nifty weight at a record low.
- Data centre flagged as a **5-10 year theme** (energy, EPC, cables, servers, storage, cloud infra); a specific idea to be unveiled Monday.
- **Today's open**: positive — Nifty +~120 (24,124), Sensex +~400, Bank Nifty +~330. Metals weak (Hindalco -2%, Hindustan Copper -2%). Movers: Tata Chemicals +~5%, HDFC +4%, Emcure Pharma +2.6% (large trade), Embassy +7% (UP govt deal).
- **IRFC**: non-retail OFS portion 1.86x subscribed, green-shoe to be used; retail portion opens today (stock closed weak). Govt OFS collecting one of its highest amounts. **IDBI Bank**: Fairfax/Prem Watsa bid seen ~Rs 70-78; govt unlikely to sell below 90.
- Heavy **block-deal selling** in recent IPOs: Alpha Wave ~72 lakh shares (~460), Pine Labs ~2.4 cr, Mirae Asset in Shadowfax. Delivery-based buying in Delhivery (52-wk high in futures), Adani Ports, Sun Pharma.

## Derivatives / levels
- **Nifty**: 24,500 call OI building; 24,000 put pile-up = safety zone; below 24,000 = next leg of weakness. Resistances 24,200/24,500/25,700; support 23,800. **PCR 1.2** (peak ~1.6-1.7 → further steam). **VIX ~13.37**, cooled 4%; below 13 = more comfort.
- **Bank Nifty**: ~6% from all-time high. Highest OI at 58,000 strike; 58,200 call / 58,000 put writing; resistances 59,500-60,000; support 57,000. RSI >66, MACD supportive.
- Speculative longs: Oracle, Indigo, UPL, Oberoi Realty. Shorts: IRFC, Swiggy, Dmart, National Aluminium.
- Options tactic (Vasanth): if Nifty closes above 24,200 EOD, buy the 24,500 call for the weekend. Ramakrishna: intraday short below 24,100 (target 24,000-24,040); no big long trade, likely consolidation.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamental + viewer Q&A. Macro-positive on banks/financials (RBI-led), cautious on averaging IPO/momentum names; gold "wait and watch."
- **Kranthi** — separate fundamental analyst. Standard Engineering "game-changing" 11 a.m. announcement theme; likes Jyoti CNC (post corporate-governance dip), engineering/defence (Make-in-India), and chemicals Naveen Fluorine, Balaji Amines, Clean Science.
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst; levels, expiry guidance, Capston Services (~350 entry), Sandhar Technologies (add small).
- **Vasanth** — anchor.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Bharat Electronics (BEL), Fairchem Organics, Himadri Speciality Chemical), 1 sell (Devyani International)
Kranthi: 2 buys (Jyoti CNC, Clean Science)

kutumba.note: Add on every decline, even now
kutumba.detail: Caller holds BEL in 120% profit and asked whether to add. Kutumba Rao said one can add on every decline, even at current levels. He treated it as a stock to keep accumulating on dips.

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; not a stock to average
kutumba.detail: Caller is in 25% loss and asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said Devyani should only be sold on rallies and is not a stock to average, since no significant gains will come until the company turns around (it trades around 100-plus / 130-plus). If you ask him, it is better to exit the counter on rallies.

kutumba.note: Hold, don't sell at current levels
kutumba.detail: Caller is in 20% loss, having bought near the all-time high (Avanti touched ~1,400 around April 15-20) and is now forming lower tops/lower bottoms. The West-Asia crisis pressures raw-material prices and this quarter may be weak. Kutumba Rao said don't sell at current levels, hold; the stock only enters a safe zone if it crosses 1,040-1,050.

kutumba.note: Hold; average only after Q results, not now
kutumba.detail: Caller holds 500 shares at average 650, now ~600, and asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao said don't average now but hold, since it already closed 600-plus recently after testing a low near 400 and is now forming higher top/higher bottom. Consider averaging only after this quarterly result, no need to rush.

kutumba.note: Recently active and strong in chemicals
kutumba.detail: Asked for a chemicals pick, Kutumba Rao said nothing looks that strong as most are in mid-course correction, but the one that recently gained activity and looks strong is Fairchem, which he would buy (Fairchem Organics).

kutumba.note: Opportunity; will also perform, may take time
kutumba.detail: In the same chemicals discussion Kutumba Rao added that there is an opportunity in Himadri Speciality too; even if it takes a little time, it will also perform.

kutumba.note: Hold all banks; matter of time
kutumba.detail: Caller is in 5% loss. Kutumba Rao said hold all banks, it's a matter of time; yields have fallen and the RBI Governor is behaving pro-market, especially for banks. Hold, no worry.

kranti.note: Corrected from ~Rs 1,100 to ~600 on a European-subsidiary corporate-governance inquiry, now rebounding; company disclosed everything needed and there is no material impact on operations, so such companies can be looked at (engineering stocks good for medium to long term).
Can be held; a good stock (caller portfolio query answered by Kranthi).
A bit in consolidation/downtrend since listing for now, but can still be held.
Can be held; a good stock (caller portfolio query answered by Kranthi).
In specialty chemicals, looks attractive in this range; one can buy in this price range for the medium to long term.
Not a SIP-worthy stock for a 3-year SIP -- it only has momentum; a decent company, but not one to SIP into.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 24 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/sAvnVnZZ3UM

## Global backdrop
- **South Korea's KOSPI crashed ~9.99% (~910 points)** yesterday, triggering an index-level circuit breaker (a rare event) — falling from a recent record high of ~**9114 to 8203**; ~**742 lakh crore won** wiped out in one day.
- Drivers: extreme overheating; **Samsung Electronics + SK Hynix** (together >half of KOSPI value) fell hard after **SK Hynix** flagged a shift from high-end AI memory chips back to traditional memory chips, sparking global AI-chip demand fears. Also, **MSCI** confirmed South Korea stays in **Emerging Markets until at least 2029** (won's limited convertibility), and leveraged ETF positions unwound.
- **US tech/chip rout:** **Micron -13.2%**, SanDisk -13.6%, **Intel -6.1%**, **AMD -5.8%**, **Nvidia -4.1%**; SpaceX +1% (bounce after 3 down days). **Cerebras Systems** (IPO price $185, listed ~$350) fell to **$202 (-11% in a day)** despite strong Q1 (revenue **$193m, +96%** vs $181m est.) — market correcting AI-semiconductor bubble valuations.
- Trump touted 19 million barrels of crude transiting the Strait of Hormuz ("most beautiful place").

## Indian markets
- Yesterday: **Nifty -1.16%**, **Bank Nifty -1.3%**, amid weekly options expiry. Breadth very negative (**855 advances vs 2070 declines**); turned negative from ~10am.
- Flows: **FIIs bought Rs 18 cr, DIIs bought Rs 680 cr** — no AI play here; FIIs busy booking profits in overheated Korea/Japan/Taiwan. Selling here came from weaker/retail hands + mid/small-cap profit booking.
- This morning: GIFT Nifty positive; markets opened up (Nifty +10, Sensex +76, Bank Nifty +100). Pharma, IT, FMCG, Realty, Bank Nifty opened green; Metal, Auto, Capital Goods, Energy, Oil & Gas red. **IRFC OFS** opens for retail today (stock -5%).
- **Infosys:** at its AGM, signed an AI-driven transformation pact with Nasdaq-listed **Global Foundries**; **Nandan Nilekani** flagged a **$400bn AI opportunity** for Indian IT.
- **Pharma outperformed** (USFDA cancer-drug news); small-caps like **Cohance, Piramal Pharma +5–10%**.
- **Bajaj Auto** ex-buyback from today; tender-route buyback at **Rs 12,000/share** (July 1 onward).
- Movers flagged: **Pace Digitech (+12%)**, **Indo Borax (+67%, ~380)**, **Oriental Hotels (+18%)**, Mac Charles, **Kirloskar Oil (+4%)**, Capstone (+7%), **Standard Engineering (+8%, ~222)**, Rashi Peripherals (+6.5%). Vedanta -8% (block deal / shorts); shorts also in Waaree Energies, Muthoot Finance.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental / viewer Q&A) — see the dedicated `kutumba_rao` note. Key macro calls: **24,000 is the pivot/major resistance; below 23,800 → 23,500 possible; supports 23,200-400**. Biggest risk = **El Nino / weak monsoon spread** threatening H1FY earnings; watch monsoon daily. Precious metals in sharp correction (gold **-12%+**, silver **-18%**; decade-worst; gold's $4,000 level in question). Crude falling (~70s) = a positive; crude shocks unlikely. PCR fell 1.12 → 0.88 (nearing buy signal for positional traders); VIX +9%. Advice: avoid fresh buying this week if Nifty closes below 23,800; stay invested, trim profitable/weaker names before earnings.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — Opening gains can hold; **23,800 crucial support (held), next 23,500**; above falling trend line & 20-DMA. **Bank Nifty** held 200-DMA (single-day -750 pts); support **57,000**, resistance **58,100 / 57,500**. RSI cooling (Bank Nifty 60.97, Nifty ~52). **India VIX 14.21 (+~11%)**. Nifty: 23,500 puts / 24,000 calls highest writing; **24,000 crucial, 24,200 tough resistance**. Intraday: plan long-only — long near 23,750 or on a 24,000 break.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (Engineers India, AGS Transact Technologies, Pace Digitech, Naukri (Info Edge), Poonawalla Fincorp), 3 sells (Hindustan Copper, Hindustan Zinc, HBL Engineering / HBL Power)

kutumba.note: PSU, decent gains after recent correction
kutumba.detail: As a PSU it won't be a multibagger, but decent gains are visible after the recent correction. He called it a good company and one of the caller's better picks.

kutumba.note: Terminals demand up; good bet at current levels
kutumba.detail: A good company being promoted as an investment idea, with some ifs-and-buts. Logistics are fine and demand for terminals companies has risen. Good bet at current levels; after a decent June-quarter result the stock has signs of slowly gaining to 300-plus.

kutumba.note: Not a buying zone now; enter only after results
kutumba.detail: Broke out from the 800-850 band to 1,100, then corrected to ~955. A hyped but good new-gen stock. This is not a buying zone; the buying zone is 850-880, which it may not reach unless the quarter's result is weak. Enter only after seeing the results.

kutumba.note: BESS dark horse; buy near 200 with stop-loss ~180
kutumba.detail: Earlier flagged as a dark-horse idea; it is in the Battery Energy Storage System (BESS) segment with a developing order book, and execution is the key. The segment is being re-rated. Buy close to 200 with a stop-loss around 180.

kutumba.note: Metal cycle peaked; exit on bounce
kutumba.detail: A hyped PSU monopoly, but the metal cycle has peaked and it is already below 500, a clear sign of weakness. Better to exit once it comes to around 530-540; a close below 490 could see it fall to 430-440. Only traders should trade it.

kutumba.note: Silver correcting; exit on any rally
kutumba.detail: Silver, one of its key profit-making units, is correcting, and the stock has made lower tops and lower bottoms over the last six months, fizzling out from ~600+. Use any temporary rally closer to 560-570 as an opportunity to exit.

kutumba.note: VC/PE bets may materialise; accumulate below 1000 medium term
kutumba.detail: Yesterday's rally was driven by news that some of its VC/PE investments (over Rs 1,200 cr across firms) may materialise with good returns, with a possible AI-company windfall like Zomato earlier. Keep a watch and accumulate below 1,000 for the medium term.

kutumba.note: Needs patience; exit if impatient
kutumba.detail: No connection to the Jio Platforms IPO. The company is shifting from retail financing toward a BlackRock-type / fund-management model, which will take time. Wait if you have patience; if not, exit, since many other NBFCs (Shriram Finance, M&M Finance, L&T Finance) gave far stronger returns.

kutumba.note: Long-term hold; story just beginning
kutumba.detail: Hold for the long term; the story has only just begun. It presented at the Go India conference (alongside Standard Engineering) and reportedly drew strong mutual-fund interest. Small-quantity holders should hold; those with very large quantities may book a little profit.

kutumba.note: Best times over; exit on bounce
kutumba.detail: The best of times are over for this stock; he doubts an immediate return to its old glory of 1,000-plus unless dramatic events, product announcements or results appear. Stay cautious and exit once it comes close to 880-900.

kutumba.note: Preferred over Capri Global for 1-year hold; stronger promoter
kutumba.detail: For a one-year single pick between Capri Global and Poonawalla, stick with the stronger group, Poonawalla, because the promoter has financial strength. Capri Global outperforms operationally and is near its all-time high, but the stronger promoter is preferred.

kutumba.note: Weak stock near 52-week low; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: Flagged as a highly risky bet at listing. It is a weak stock near its 52-week low that didn't rise even in the two-month mid/small-cap rally; it should have been exited in the rallies. A hype-driven IPO listing.

kutumba.note: Stabilising; batteries space should see some movement
kutumba.detail: The stock was very volatile but is now slowly stabilising. As of now, hold, because the batteries space should see at least some movement in such stocks.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 23 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/4hpi0fOj-WU

## Global backdrop
- **US markets mixed; Nasdaq fell** on renewed selling in the AI/tech trade — **Alphabet -5%** (two VP-level AI researchers left, one to Anthropic, one to OpenAI), **Amazon -4.8%**, **Meta -2.3%**, **Microsoft -3%**.
- **SpaceX** (recent IPO) under pressure: **-16% yesterday**, ~**-32% over three days**, ~**$600B** market-cap lost cumulatively and **$225B wiped in a single day**, hit by a surprise bond-sale news. It fell from a lifetime high near **220** to almost **150** — within ~10% of issue price. Cited as a caution on large IPOs (NSE, Jio) — book listing gains, buy only at correct valuations.
- **Iran oil:** US Treasury cleared Iranian crude sales for a **60-day window**; pre-1995 Iran exported **~1.5M barrels/day**, so more supply may weaken crude further. **Brent below $78**; some see crude going **below $70** (positive for India).
- **Dollar Index ~101**; **USD/INR ~94.67** (recovered from ~97). Rupee slipped slightly to ~94.63.
- **Gold** may rebound off support near **$4000/oz** per some chartists.

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty** pointed to a slightly negative open; Nifty stuck in a **23,900–24,200** range for three sessions; **Tuesday NSE weekly options expiry**.
- **FIIs sold ~Rs 636 cr; DIIs bought ~Rs 1036 cr** — light institutional activity; selling tapering.
- **Bank Nifty strong**; **Defence index +1.47%** (BDL, BEL, HAL; PSU + private defence names, Astra Microwave, Apollo Micro Systems).
- **Pharma in focus** on US FDA request to Indian firms for cancer drugs (Natco, Emcure, Granules, Sirma SGS moving).
- Heavy **mid/small-cap stock-specific action**: **Kirloskar Oil** hit upper circuit (192 MW / 96-unit data-centre power-systems order; Rs 1400 cr capex; Cummins rivalry) — whole **Kirloskar group** (incl. Kirloskar Brothers) outperforming. Black Rose Industries +17%, Menon Pistons ~+20%, Kaveri Seeds +11-12%, Subros/Themis Medicare +15-18%.
- **CRED** raised **$900M from Meta** (valuation now ~**$4.5–5B**); promoter **Kunal Shah** named **global CEO of WhatsApp** — positive for Indian startup ecosystem.
- **Reliance** re-rating theme (Jio platform ~35% of embedded value; targets up to **1600**) — needed for index to advance, alongside HDFC Bank, Bajaj group, Bharti Airtel.
- **Manufacturing data at a seven-month low** — a macro negative.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental / markets + viewer stock Q&A) — present and named. View: **buy on declines / buy on dips**, no panic; broader range **23,800–24,600**; hunt for value in the broader market rather than the index. Fielded most caller/mail stock queries (see kutumba_rao file).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) — positive bias; Nifty above falling trend line and 10/20/50 MAs, falling-wedge breakout; **doji** on Nifty/Bank Nifty/Sensex (indecision) but further upside likely after consolidation. **RSI ~60** (Bank Nifty 69), **PCR 0.97**, **VIX ~12.84** (fear reduced). Nifty OI: 24,150 call / 24,100 put highest writing → consolidation; Bank Nifty 58,000 put writing → upward bias. Intraday: trade a 5-min confirmed break of the day's high (~24,481/82) or low (~24,440). Gave entry-point calls on **VA Tech Wabag** (wait for pullback ~1800-1825), **Radico Khaitan** (50% at ~3798, balance ~3600), **Union Bank** (~175 for 1-yr; better above 180).
- **Anchor** (Vasanth) — handled the Capital Infra Trust (InvIT, ~15% dividend yield, ~Rs 73) mail from a viewer.

## Market open
- Nifty ~**-43 pts**, Bank Nifty ~**-50 to -75 pts**, Sensex ~**-170 pts**; advances 91 / declines 462 initially, but **mid-cap, small-cap and pharma indices opened positive** — breadth positive. **IT index -1.6%** (front-line + mid-cap IT selling). Vedanta block deal (~1.8% equity, -4.6%).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (Viyash Scientific (formerly Sequent Scientific), CG Power, Bajaj Finance, Bajaj Housing Finance, Finolex Cables), 1 sell (Vedanta (post-demerger))

kutumba.detail: Nippon India AMC is definitely one of the outperforming AMCs, no doubt about it. Hold; there is no hurry to sell at all.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 22 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/G-FtMH8Jw4E

## Global backdrop
- **Asian screens positive**; **GIFT Nifty** indicating a positive opening of **60-70 points** after Friday's losses.
- **US-Iran tensions** flared again over the **Lebanon** issue; Trump warned Iran over funding proxies. **Pakistan and Qatar** mediated in Switzerland; Iran's Foreign Minister signaled agreement. A **60-day window** is in play but public posturing remains aggressive.
- **Strait of Hormuz** was closed and reopened over the weekend; **55 commercial ships** carrying **17 million barrels** of crude reportedly passed through.
- **Brent crude** back to **$79** (had spiked to **$82-83** on the Hormuz scare). Sustained $70s rather than $80s would be a relief for India.
- **US dollar index** near an ~11-month high post-Fed; dollar likely dominant for a couple of months until US mid-term election clarity.

## Indian markets
- **FIIs bought Rs 4,859 crore** on Friday (boosted by **FTSE rebalancing**, ~$600m inflow); **DIIs sold Rs 1,160 crore**. Net FII buying ~**Rs 3,386 crore** last week.
- Nifty closed last week **+390 points**; FIIs net buyers in **3 of 5** sessions.
- **Rupee** roughly unchanged Friday; had touched **96.96**; a move below **94** (~94.34) would be more positive. FCNR liberalization could aid debt-market inflows.
- **Mumbai monsoon rain** arrived on the 22nd — seen as positive — but an overall **30-40% rain deficit** remains the biggest question mark for rural demand and inflation.
- Focus is on **mid- and small-caps**, where last week's index gains (Nifty/Bank Nifty +1.5%) were dwarfed by **Midcap/Smallcap +3-4%**.

## IPO pipeline (key theme)
- **Jio Platforms DRHP filed** (announced at Reliance AGM): ~**Rs 37,700 crore** (~$4bn), the **largest IPO in Indian history**. Expected valuation **$110-120bn** (~Rs 11.64 lakh crore, on par with **Bharti Airtel**). ET pegs potential market cap at **Rs 14-17 lakh crore**.
- Mukesh Ambani also announced a **LEO satellite constellation** launch. Existing Jio backers: Reliance 66%, **Meta 10%, Google 7.73%**, PIF/KKR/Vista ~2.3-3% each.
- **Reliance + Jio combined** could reach ~**Rs 31 lakh crore**, lifting their share of India market cap from ~4% to ~7%.
- Also pending: **NSE IPO** (high PE), and next year **Carlyle**-linked healthcare names (RCM/Assets).

## Technicals & derivatives
- **Nifty range** seen ~**23,500 (down) to 24,800 (up)** for the next couple of months; key intraday pivot **24,000 / 24,100**.
- Nifty **24,500 calls** and **23,500 puts** heavily piled; highest call writing 24,000, highest put writing 23,950. **Bank Nifty** max calls ~**58,000** (put writing 57,500), expected 500-600 pt range.
- **PCR** fell 1.12 → 0.9; **India VIX** ~**12.82** (below 30) — favorable for bulls.
- Speculative longs: **Motherson, Bharat Forge, Bajaj Finance, JTL**. Persistent shorts: **Muthoot, Tata Elxsi, Infosys, Coforge** (software shorts rebuilding). **Kaynes Technology** the only F&O ban-list stock.
- Volume movers: **Garware Technical Fibres**, Bhagiratha Chemicals, Aksharchem, **Berger Paints** (+10%, ~556), Panama Petrochem, NCL Industries, Transformers & Rectifiers (+10%).
- Ramakrishna: Higher-High/Higher-Low setup intact; support **24,800**, resistance **24,700/24,500-24,600**; Bank Nifty above 200-DMA, RSI ~67-68, MACD/histogram positive. Intraday long above **24,100** with stop **24,050**.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamental view + viewer Q&A. Answered the **Igarashi Motors** and **Ind Swift Labs** calls (see kutumba_rao file).
- **Kranthi** — fundamental analyst (separate). Handled CG Power/Jain Resource Recycling, HFCL, defense stocks (Cochin Shipyard, BDL).
- **Vasanth** — anchor/analyst; led Jio Platforms IPO discussion.
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst; levels and intraday calls.

## Stock calls (across all analysts — see kutumba_rao file for attribution)
- **Igarashi Motors** — wait for results, don't rush (Kutumba Rao).
- **Ind Swift Labs** — near 52-wk high; wait for quarterly results or buy with stop ~158-159 only if daring (Kutumba Rao).
- **CG Power** — add on every decline / monthly SIP (Kranthi).
- **Jain Resource Recycling** — speculative, low margins; only for high-risk appetite, wait 1-2 quarters (Kranthi).
- **Kirloskar Oil Engines** — buy on declines (opened +12%).
- **IRFC** — short-term positional only, stop ~90, target 115-120; not an investment.
- **Jupiter Wagons** — avoid/exit on rallies, 64 PE.
- **Finotex Chemicals** — wait, buy only after correction.
- **Envira Infra** — avoid till ~200.
- **HFCL** — take initial capital out (FOC), hold rest; defense subsidiary momentum (Kranthi).
- **Zee Entertainment** — hold with stop loss only.
- **IRCTC** — wait for higher levels post-results, exit on rally.
- **Suzlon** — hold, activity just starting; **YES Bank/NCC** — hold/wait.
- **Defense (Cochin Shipyard, BDL)** — fairly priced, add slowly on dips (Kranthi/Vasanth).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 calls (Igarashi Motors, Ind Swift Labs)
Kranthi: 3 buys (CG Power, Cochin Shipyard, Bharat Dynamics (BDL)), 1 sell (HFCL)

kutumba.note: Auto-component micro-motors; wait for results, don't rush
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said Igarashi is not one of the good stocks in the auto-component space. It makes micro-motors, such as those that operate windshield wiper blades, but its performance over the last two-three quarters has not been inspiring. He advised waiting to see this quarter's results and not rushing in.

kutumba.price: stop loss near 158-159; else wait till ~160
kutumba.note: Near 52-week high after doubling; wait for results or buy with stop only if daring
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao noted Ind Swift Labs has roughly doubled recently and is near its 52-week high. He prefers to wait and watch and buy after the quarterly results. A momentum buy would need a stop loss, but the stop (near 158-159) is far away, so he would only buy at current levels if one dares; otherwise wait till around 160 to buy.

kranti.note: Add without any doubt on every decline, even as a monthly SIP stock; good progress in semiconductors and core power-equipment business doing well.
Speculative activity, huge turnover but low margins; fresh position only with high risk appetite, wait 1-2 quarters of results before serious investment.
Withdraw initial capital and hold the rest free-of-cost; strong momentum plus defense-subsidiary news, huge order book gives a long-term story.
Among defense stocks (mostly fairly priced after rallies), attractive on valuations; add slowly on dips.
Along with Cochin Shipyard, attractive on valuations within defense; add slowly on dips.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 19 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/XcXKMiaRjaw

## Global backdrop
- **Gap-down opening expected.** Many Asian markets (China, Taiwan) closed for a local festival; GIFT Nifty signals weakness. It's Friday, last day of the week.
- **US markets closed positive** — Nasdaq strong on AI/chips: **Nvidia +3%, Intel +10%** (on news of a chip-manufacturing deal with Apple).
- **But Accenture took a beating** — cut FY revenue guidance (to 3–4% from 3–5%); West Asia war cost ~$100m of consulting revenue; raised acquisition spend to $9bn (from $5bn). Negative read-through for Indian IT: **Infosys ADR −10%, Globant −11%, EPAM −12%, Cognizant −10%, Capgemini −9%.**
- **Brent ~$79.55**, settled — won't rush to $70/$65 because strategic reserves (incl. US) must be replenished.
- US–Iran interim peace deal effective from today; key is Strait of Hormuz goods flow returning to pre-war levels. Kutumba Rao notes the "Treaty of Versailles 2.0" parallel (one-sided deals can sow future conflict).

## Indian markets
- **5th straight gaining session** — Nifty +82 yesterday; ~1,000-point rally from 23,000 to above 24,000. Profit-booking session likely (IT drag + crude disappointment).
- **FII selling has eased** — only ₹1,000 cr sold yesterday (vs ₹4,000–5,000 cr earlier); **DIIs bought ₹3,517 cr.** Mid-cap index at all-time high, small-cap near it.
- **Reliance 49th AGM today** — market awaits Jio Platforms listing/IPO timeline commentary.
- **Direct tax collections +15%** YoY — a positive for the government despite headwinds.
- **NSE IPO sellers/beneficiaries:** SBI (could net ~₹5,000 cr "jackpot"), Morgan Stanley/Mauritius, CPPIB, Bank of Baroda, Stock Holding Corp, GIC, New India Assurance (~₹2,157 cr — was a top Nifty-500 gainer). **LIC (10.72%, ~₹5,400 cr) not selling**; nor are Radhakishan Damani (~₹8,032 cr) or Sunil Kant Munjal (~₹2,000 cr).
- Curiosity: a shareholder (Kashmiri Lal Rana) had 5,000 NSE shares wrongly credited (Dec 2023), refuses to return them; sold 3,685 — NSE now in a legal fight to recover ₹1.43 cr.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Kranthi & Vasanth** — discussed **Olectra Greentech** (capacity expansion, electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, old-bus replacement tailwind → potential 2–3-bagger; "picked it first" from ~90 to 2,200); plus some viewer Q&A (Divi's Lab, NHPC/NCC/IRFC, HDFC, SEMA India).
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — Nifty bullish toward **24,700** (support 23,800); **Bank Nifty toward 60,000 zone** (RSI 70.97, first >70 since Nov; resistance only at 60,400–500). Buy-on-dips; go long intraday only above 24,120–130.
- Market opened weak — **Nifty −200, IT index −6%**, breadth negative; HFCL hit 5% upper circuit, Natco Pharma +2.5%.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Olectra Greentech)
Kranthi: 1 buy (HDFC)

kutumba.note: NSE stake ~Rs 5,400 cr (=10% of LIC mkt cap); signs of +10% once NSE issue factors into NAV.
kutumba.detail: Speculative-longs / watch list. NSE stake value alone is ~Rs 5,400 cr, which is 10% of LIC's market cap; once the NSE issue happens that gets factored into NAV, so LIC has signs of rising 10%. Stock may go up to 460 or 470 in the near-to-medium term; don't ignore it.

kutumba.note: Multibagger / 2-3 bagger potential; capacity expansion, electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, old-bus replacement.
kutumba.detail: Sees more than 15-20%/yr - multibagger returns; from here a likely 2-3 bagger. Capacity expansion underway, entering electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, and India's hard line on China plus old-bus/vehicle replacement (Delhi, Mumbai) are tailwinds. Picked it first from ~90.

kutumba.note: Not a great/wealth-creating stock; rises seasonally then falls. Hold.
kutumba.detail: I'd say hold; recently activity in the stock has increased. But these aren't great stocks to buy and not wealth-creating - they rise seasonally when the market is good and then fall.

kutumba.detail: Only for positional trading; the stock moves very swiftly (1,000+ to ~750 then back above 1,100). Parent-controlled with fixed margins so the valuation holds, but if you ask me IOC, BPCL or HPCL would be better bets in the future.

kutumba.detail: In such large quantity, not immediately. The company is stabilising slowly but not showing expected performance, so the stock is range-bound; it has held above 200 for many days. I would wait for the June-end result before deciding.

kutumba.note: On-and-off performer; dividend play. Don't enter with great hopes.
kutumba.detail: ITC and IOC perform on-and-off and can lie flat for a couple of years. In IOC there's a dividend play, especially as crude prices keep moving back and forth; my advice is don't step in with great hopes on these.

kutumba.detail: Fundamentally looks fine; put it as a slightly speculative bet. Rode the recent CCL rally to 180 then corrected to ~150; being a Rs 10 paid-up stock some activity continues, so hold for some time. Buying fresh is at the operator's mercy - it's in his fist.

Decent stock, 1-2 year horizon; sentiment improved, can hold.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 18 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5pa0Yll0Hm4

## Global backdrop
- Modi and Trump met in France after a 16-month gap. Little of substance, but Trump was warm on India ("great friend," called Modi "an angel" and "a killer" negotiator). An Iran–US ceasefire MoU was signed; the formal agreement is due "tomorrow." India–US relations, frosty three months ago, look back on track.
- **FOMC:** Kevin Warsh chaired his first meeting and was hawkish — rates held, but a hint of **one rate hike in 2026** (9 of 18 officials expect it; markets eye ~40–50 bps, possibly October). 10-yr yield ~4.5%, Dollar Index back above 100.
- **US equities fell**, led by tech/AI (Nvidia, hyperscalers); Nasdaq down. Focus shifting to the ~$700bn AI capex cycle (Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta, Oracle) and the debt-vs-equity question behind it. Brent ~$78.

## Indian markets
- 4th straight gaining session: Nifty +~100, Sensex +350, **Bank Nifty the standout (+~1.3%)**. Nifty poised near a breakout around 24,088 (next resistance ~24,247 / 100-DMA ~24,232).
- **Heavy IPO/primary-market pipeline:** NSE IPO filed (~₹30,000 cr, likely the largest ever), Jio Platforms (~₹38,000 cr) papers expected this week, plus ~$26bn of lock-in shares releasing over coming weeks. Reliance AGM tomorrow.
- India became the **6th-largest market cap (~$5T)**; South Korea edged ahead, Taiwan still above (~$5.55T).
- Theme: large-caps are institution-driven (valuation-sensitive); mid/small-caps are retail-driven (story-driven, valuation-blind) — watch the hype.
- **CtrlS** (Hyderabad/Telugu-promoted data-centre firm) got a ₹7,000 cr investment from Canada Pension Plan. India–UK trade deal effective **15 July** (positive for liquor, auto, textiles/garments, chemicals).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — Nifty bullish, Bank Nifty stronger with targets toward ~59,726; intraday short near 24,140–24,160 resistance.
- Market opened flat/slightly weak but breadth strongly positive; mid/small-caps and Bank Nifty firm.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Natco Pharma), 1 sell (Praj Industries)

kutumba.detail: speculative, recently listed, hard to judge; would not take big risk.

kutumba.detail: exit on rallies; selling appears on every bounce; exit near 400.

# Uploaded: 2026-06-17

# Summary — Business Breakfast | Stock Market News | June 17, 2026

## Global backdrop
- Brent crude cooled to about $79/barrel, slipping below $80; it had recently been around $83-$84 and earlier above $90.
- Trigger: prospect of the US allowing Iranian oil exports / easing sanctions, with buyers like China potentially resuming Iranian oil purchases — bearish for crude, positive for India.
- FOMC meeting in focus over the next couple of days; watch whether the Fed (Jerome Powell, vs. Donald Trump pressure) holds its mandate or turns dovish. Expectation: likely no rate change at this first meeting, possible change at the next.
- US markets: Nasdaq showing a correction setting in (around -1.2%); SpaceX listing referenced (entry ~$25/share), watch for post-IPO hype fizzling out. American corporate valuations described as at "skyrocketing" levels; one net-worth/valuation reference of $15 billion (as of June 15).

## Indian markets
- Monsoon is the key domestic watch item; next two weeks crucial. Historical reference: 2014 deficient rains, 2015 ~15% rainfall deficit; current flooding/drainage stress in Mumbai (Bombay Municipal Corporation). Clear monsoon indication expected over coming weeks.
- Nifty: immediate resistance ~24,088 (recent high); 100-day MA ~24,247 is next resistance. 24,000 is the most important level (highest put + call open interest); pre-open showed Nifty +~50 pts, then ~+23 pts after open.
- FII data: short covering yesterday — index-futures long positions down to ~13%, shorts ~87%; June shorts cut to ~2.3 lakh contracts from ~40k+; rollovers into next-month options.
- Bank Nifty strong, closing above 200-day MA; max call OI shows resistance; range/gap ~800-900 points; high writing ~57,300.
- Breadth positive at open: advances 1059 vs declines 429; Midcap index outperforming Nifty/Sensex/Bank Nifty.
- Oil-marketing companies opened positive on weaker crude.
- IT index up ~1%: HCL Tech top Nifty gainer ~+3.55% (ServiceNow ~10% stake mention); Coforge ~+3.77%/+4.4% after analyst meet (target $5 billion revenue by 2030).
- Metals weak: aluminum names NALCO and HINDALCO down ~5% (lower circuit) on import/supply (Gulf) fundamentals — do profit-booking.
- Reliance Industries strong for two days (1330 call premiums +~50%).
- Brigade announced a 1:3 bonus (ex-bonus trade); Tarsons listing referenced. Caution advised ahead of quarterly results, especially banks (one-time provisioning, asset-quality, June-quarter results).

## Analysts & segments
- Two analysts featured: Kutumba Rao (fundamental/stock calls) and Ramakrishna (technical). Kranti also thanked at close.
- Ramakrishna (technical): flat-to-slightly-positive open; RSI 59.02 with positive MACD crossover, rising histograms; 24,000 pivotal; consolidation/doji formation; advises fresh buys on a confirmed break above.
- Kutumba Rao (fundamental): stay invested; favors IT and select power names; cautious on autos and metals; answered caller stock queries (Tejas Networks, VA Tech Wabag, Affle/BLS, hospital stocks, NTPC, Anoop Engineering / Solar Industries).
- Caller topics: Tejas Networks, VA Tech Wabag, Affle 3i & BLS International, Aster DM Healthcare/KIMS/Fortis, NTPC, Anoop Engineering & Solar Industries, plus workshop requests (Pune/Nagpur).

## Key numbers
- Brent crude: ~$79 (below $80); recent range $83-$84; earlier $90+.
- Nifty levels: 24,088 (immediate resistance), 24,247 (100-day MA), 24,000 (key OI level).
- FII futures: longs ~13%, shorts ~87%; June shorts ~2.3 lakh contracts.
- Bank Nifty: high writing ~57,300; range ~800-900 pts.
- Breadth: 1059 advances / 429 declines.
- HCL Tech +~3.55%; Coforge +~3.77% to +4.4% (target $5B revenue by 2030); NALCO/HINDALCO ~-5%.
- Brigade 1:3 bonus; Reliance 1330 call premiums +~50%.
- Tejas Networks ~1100 (avg ~145 noted by caller), support ~180/300 levels, trailing stop ~160.
- VA Tech Wabag ~1770, recommended from ~1500, trailing stop ~1600.
- Solar Industries / Anoop Engineering down ~30%; Solar high ~18,850, ~17,000 area.

# Business Breakfast | Stock Market News | June 16, 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-06-16
# https://youtu.be/MCht7Xs4bL8

# Summary — Business Breakfast, June 16, 2026 (TV5 Money)

## Global backdrop
- Global cues mixed: selling in China's Hang Seng; Gift Nifty pointed to a mildly positive open.
- Crude oil stable near 83 dollars; the anchor commentary calls stabilization near 80 dollars favorable for India. Brent below 83, and ideally below 80, seen as a positive.
- US/Iran/others to formally sign a deal Friday in Geneva (JD Vance attending); the Strait of Hormuz — through which ~50% of India's crude imports and LNG/LPG flow — said to be partially reopening. Israel still refusing to withdraw from Lebanon, so tensions persist.
- Trump framed as having struck the deal under domestic pressure ahead of midterm polls.
- Wall Street closed at record highs: Dow +0.9% (near record high), S&P 500 +1.7%, Nasdaq +3%. Japan, Korea, Europe also rallied.
- G7 meeting underway — watch for fresh Ukraine/Iran statements from Trump or Putin.
- SpaceX IPO: highly valued; rose from listing to ~200 dollars, then 224 after-market; ~2.3 trillion dollar market cap; reportedly created ~4,400 millionaire and ~400 billionaire shareholders. Cited as proof of huge global appetite — feared crash did not materialize.

## Indian markets
- Indian markets underperformed the prior day: Nifty/Bank Nifty raised hopes (Bank Nifty up ~900 points intraday) but failed to hold.
- Nifty resistance at 24,488 (prior swing high); crossing it could bring a 500–600 point rally. 24,000 is a stiff resistance with profit booking expected; 23,500 is major support.
- Bank stocks pivotal. RBI rate view eased: earlier feared up to 2% hike, fresh estimates now suggest 1% suffices — would spare banks from repricing recently raised deposit rates.
- Monsoon negative: IMD says monsoon reaches Maharashtra only after June 20 (vs early June in 2024/2025); El Nino effect discussed.
- New revamped WPI series printed 9.7% (multi-month high) on a higher fuel index — a caution.
- New Service Provider Price Index launched (~7 sectors incl. banking, exchanges, insurance, railways, telecom); exchanges grew 2.8% QoQ despite F&O turnover decline, signaling active cash-market participation.
- Unemployment rate at 11-month high (~5.x%), especially rural — watch for government steps.
- Rupee at ~5-week high of 94.45 vs dollar; a move below 94 seen as psychologically supportive.
- Flows: FIIs turned to net cash buying (~200 crore) after a declining sell trend (8,000 → 6,000 → 4,000 → 2,000 → 1,000 crore); DIIs bought ~3,000 crore. FM said India "will take more steps to draw in capital"; equity + bond market reforms could attract FPIs. April current account surplus of 4.7 billion dollars noted as macro positive.
- Pre-open/open positive: Nifty +~40 to +118 points, Sensex +190 to +259, Bank Nifty +115 to +120; advance-declines weak (141 advances vs 318 declines) — "not a great opening."
- Key catalyst ahead: Indo-US trade deal — an India-favorable announcement seen as a big booster.

## Stocks & sectors in focus
- IT/AI is the morning's big theme: HCL Tech investing 150 million dollars (~1,400 crore) for a 10.46% stake in startup Sarvam AI (India-specific foundational LLMs / sovereign AI). Sarvam raising 300 million dollars total; valuation ~1.5 billion dollars — a new unicorn. Seen as a positive trigger for IT.
- Five Indian unicorns this year, including Sarvam, Nisa, and Hyderabad-based Skyroot Aerospace (first space-tech unicorn).
- Coforge annual investor meet in Noida today; RIL AGM on Friday (data centers / AI announcements expected).
- Vedanta demerger entities listed: Aluminium (closed >520; opened day down 5%), Power (~42, flat), Oil & Gas (~38–39; down 5% circuit), Iron & Steel (~21–22; later 5% upper circuit). Price discovery ongoing — be cautious.
- QIP watch: Waaree Energies, Craftsman Automation (floor 8,966); KIMS priced at 799 — watch floor prices.
- GIC: Offer For Sale, stock ~4.2% down.
- Other movers at open: HCL Tech +2.5% (1,147), Bajaj Finance, SBI Life, Tata Consumer, Coal India up; Tata Steel, Infosys, JSW Steel, HDFC Life selling. Mid-caps: Devyani International +6%, Apar, RR Kabel, Sapphire Foods, HFCL, Le Travenues Tech +2.33%.
- Recurring coaching theme: stay invested, "buy on dips," let profits run; avoid panic selling after the ~800–900 point two-day rise (a 150–200 point correction would be normal).

## Analysts & segments
- Analyst Kutumba Rao: market commentary on expiry-day dynamics, options positioning, WPI/services index, FII flows, and answered viewer stock queries (see dedicated extract).
- Technical analyst Ramakrishna: Bank Nifty above 200-DMA and outperforming Nifty; momentum (RSI ~56 Nifty / ~67 Bank Nifty, MACD above signal) positive; PCR 0.96, VIX cooling to ~14.35; intraday levels — long above 23,960/23,970 (5-min hold), short below ~23,860; technical "hold" on Inox India (entry ~1,700 on pull-back).
- Mr. Kranthi did not join today.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, June 10, 2026 (TV5 Money)

## Global backdrop
- **Geopolitics:** The US carried out fresh "self-defense strikes" on Iran. Iran's Foreign Minister responded aggressively, saying "the US has chosen to test our determination." Tensions seen rising around the Strait of Hormuz (helicopters circling); Iran objecting strongly.
- **Trump:** Claimed a deal would be reached within 1–3 days, with "complete victory" to follow.
- **Crude oil:** Trading around **92.35**; prices have NOT spiked despite tensions (earlier reached 94–96). Markets, for now, not treating this as a major challenge.
- **US equities (overnight):** Renewed selling in technology stocks. **Nasdaq** intraday range was the **4th largest in the last 3 years** — very volatile. Nasdaq later recovered; **Dow Jones** closed slightly positive. Theme: "old economy vs tech stocks."
- **S&P 500:** Tech stocks (incl. semiconductors) now account for **more than one-third** of weightage/movements.
- **IPO supply:** No new highs due to an investigation/"situation" crisis, but heavy IPO supply incoming. **Google** raised ~**$80bn / $75bn** recently. **SpaceX** IPO near (issue price/listing pending). **OpenAI** (ChatGPT) filed a **confidential** IPO with the SEC. **Anthropic** also in the queue. A hundreds-of-billions-of-dollars market; SpaceX IPO seen as marking the **peak of AI euphoria**.
- **Asia this morning:** KOSPI (Korea) down ~**2%**; Korean index had risen >100% over the past year and is now correcting. Taiwan index down ~**1.5%**.

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty:** Indicating a slightly **negative / weak lower opening**, tracking Asian markets and global cues.
- **Prior session close (referenced):** Sensex up ~forty-points-odd (caption noise around level), **Bank Nifty +1100 points**, Nifty closed strong.
- **Driver of weakness:** Renewed Iran-related geopolitical tension.

## Analysts & segments
- This episode is a **market-news / global-cues monologue**. No named market analyst (no Kutumba Rao, no technical analyst Ramakrishna), no caller Q&A, and **no specific stock buy/sell calls or levels/targets** were given.
- Coverage: global markets sweep (Korea, Taiwan, Japan), US tech selloff and IPO pipeline, oil/geopolitics, and the expected weak Indian open.

## Key numbers
- Crude: ~**92.35** (prior spikes to **94–96**)
- Nasdaq intraday range: **4th largest in 3 years**
- S&P 500 tech weight: **>1/3**
- Google IPO/raise: **~$80bn / $75bn**
- KOSPI: **−2%**; Taiwan: **−1.5%**; Korean index: **>+100%** over past year
- Bank Nifty (prior close): **+1100 points**; Gift Nifty: **negative open** expected

# Business Breakfast | Stock Market News | June 9, 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-06-09
# https://youtu.be/Ej_vut-F9ng

# Summary — Business Breakfast, June 9, 2026 (TV5 Money)

## Global backdrop
- After Friday's heavy sell-off, US markets closed higher overnight. Nasdaq +0.86%; the semiconductor index rebounded ~6% after a 10% Friday fall.
- GIFT Nifty still pointed to a weak/lower opening despite the global rebound.
- Brent crude eased below $94 (high was $98 the prior day). Crude cooling is seen as the key positive trigger / "game changer" for Indian markets.
- Iran–Israel direct conflict paused, though Israeli strikes on south Lebanon continue; geopolitics remains a swing factor (Netanyahu resisting Trump).
- Asian markets (Korea, Taiwan) had led the sell-off; SK Hynix-type names dragged South Korea.

## Indian markets — previous session and today
- Prior session: Nifty -243 pts (closed ~23,123, slightly below the April 8 / 23,150 support); Sensex -719 pts (~0.9–1% loss). India relatively outperformed Korea/Taiwan.
- FIIs sold ~Rs 5,556 cr; DIIs bought ~Rs 5,165 cr (net mild selling). FII index shorts built up from 1.53 lakh contracts (May 26) to 2.78 lakh — ~92% shorts vs 8% longs.
- Put-call ratio fell to 0.78 (from ~0.83) — at the low end, leaving room for a relief rally. Weekly options expiry / settlement day today.
- Heavy selling in mid/small-caps (profit booking + some panic selling on AI-trade reversal fears).
- Rupee weakened 76 paise to ~95.70/$ despite expected bond inflows; RBI continued forex swap measures.
- Today's open: Nifty +~80 pts; Sensex up ~200–300 pts; Bank Nifty +0.6%, slightly outperforming. Advance/decline favored advances (BSE 931:349; NSE 1,350:333). Gains continued and strengthened intraday (Bank Nifty later +1.1%, +500 pts).

## Key news items
- MSCI EM top-10: For the first time in 26 years no Indian company is in the MSCI EM top-10. HDFC Bank now 11th, Reliance 12th, as Samsung (+146% YTD) and SK Hynix (+193% YTD) weightings surged.
- Telecom: Bombay High Court struck down the ~Rs 22,000 cr retrospective spectrum levy (reversing a Madras HC judgment). Positive for BSNL, Bharti Airtel, Vodafone Idea — telecom stocks expected to rebound.
- Current account: India posted a surplus of ~$7.2 bn (Jan–Feb period) on stronger services receipts/remittances — a positive vs prior deficit worries.
- Mazagon Dock: Reportedly in talks to set up a shipbuilding cluster in Andhra Pradesh (East Coast) after being passed over in Tamil Nadu (Tuticorin/Thoothukudi went to a foreign player). Per analyst Kutumba Rao, a ~Rs 28,000 cr cluster project; Korean and possibly Russian firms may also participate.

## Sector / stock action noted by the desk
- Telecom firm on the levy ruling (Vodafone Idea, IRB Infra, Tata Communications, IDFC First Bank, Bharti Hexacom among mid-cap gainers).
- Pharma and infrastructure/engineering showed resilience in the prior session.
- Notable movers cited: CCL Products (+~10%, near 52-wk high), Thomas Cook (+~5% two days running), Exicom (~+10% on heavy volume), OCL (+15%, sulphur price tailwind).
- Nifty/mid-cap gainers at open: InterGlobe, Tata Motors, Grasim, Bajaj Finance, Bharti Airtel, Wipro, Ajanta Pharma, Emcure, INOX India, RVNL, ICICI Lombard, Jubilant Ingrevia, Five-Star Business Finance, Netweb. Minor losers: Aptus Value, CCL Products, HEG, Anand Rathi Wealth, Torrent Power, Happiest Minds, Saregama.

## Analysts & segments
- **Kutumba Rao (analyst):** No major recovery expected; market range-bound with hidden weakness — caution the watchword. Watch 23,000 (support) and 23,300 (upside trigger); no fresh positions until 23,300 crosses. Answered viewer stock queries (see dedicated extract).
- **Ramakrishna (technical analyst):** Settlement/expiry day; mild volatility. OI: highest call writing at 23,250, highest put writing at 23,000 — Nifty to oscillate in that zone; Bank Nifty in a 300–400 pt range (54,200–54,500). Long attempt only above 23,259 with stop 23,170–23,180. Also gave entry levels for TD Power and Ather Energy. Plus a Friday live workshop session (2:30–3:30) on closing/intraday strategies (BTST/STBT).
- **Kranti (analyst, dialing from Mumbai):** Expects a pullback/relief rally if crude falls decisively below $90. It's a "traders' market" — Nifty roughly range-bound 23,000–24,750 for three months. Advocates buy-on-dips/sell-on-rallies for traders, with separate core vs satellite portfolios and no leverage; portfolio investors should not disturb the core.
- **Mowniika Halan chart (cited by Kutumba Rao):** Even investing Rs 1 lakh/year at the Sensex's annual peak for 35 years grows ~Rs 35 lakh into Rs 3 crore+ (~11% CAGR) — better than "safest" options like Sukanya Samriddhi; SIPs may disappoint over 3–4 year down cycles but outperform long term.
- **Viewer portfolio case (Bachu Santosh):** Rs 1.5 lakh/month SIP since 2023 (Invesco India Smallcap, Bandhan Smallcap, SBI Contra, Motilal Oswal Midcap, Parag Parikh Flexi Cap, HDFC Flexi Cap) returning only ~2.5–5% over ~3 years — attributed mainly to non-participation of large-caps (Reliance, HDFC Bank, TCS, HUL, Infosys, Maruti, M&M flat for years); Motilal Oswal Midcap a notable underperformer.

# Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 8th June 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-06-08
# https://youtu.be/IZlgpRUahC4

## Summary — June 8, 2026 (Monday)

**Market backdrop: a "double whammy."**
- **Geopolitics:** Iran–Israel war re-escalated (Iran fired missiles directly at Israel over the Beirut strikes); Trump publicly restrained Netanyahu. Crude back near $96.
- **AI sell-off:** Nasdaq −4% Friday (worst day since Apr 2025). Strip out AI names and the S&P 500 was flat — the sell-off is concentrated in AI/semis. Korea's Kospi −8% (touched lower circuit), Japan −4%, Taiwan weak. Gift Nifty pointed to a >250-pt gap-down open.

**Domestic cues.**
- RBI Friday measures (FPI tax relief, EFCNR/ECB relaxations, forex swaps to PSU banks to Sep 30) aim to attract ~$65–70bn over 3–4 months to plug the ~$75bn CAD; should support banks/financials, but global turmoil capped the move.
- FII −₹8,776 cr Friday; DII +₹9,134 cr. Rupee recovered to ~94.94 from near 97; may stay below 95 into September.
- Expected weakness: metals (commodities −2–4%), power. Resilience: pharma (only Sun Pharma & Dr Reddy's green in Nifty) and sugar (positive ethanol-mixing news).

**Kutumba Rao's view:** Odds favour further weakness. Mega-IPO history is cautionary (Visa 2008 → S&P −38%; Agri Bank of China 2010 → Shanghai −22%) ahead of the SpaceX IPO. Calls the RBI/FII debt-market concession "a very bad decision" (ignores domestic investors; rupee strength may fizzle back to 96–97). Broader market is the strength: only 12% of sub-₹10k-cr stocks were positive before March vs 70% now — an earnings-and-breadth-driven rally, not pure liquidity. **Stock picking is paramount.** Holds 23,300 (EOD) = safer; near 23,000 = prepare for more weakness. PCR ~0.77 (positional trades possible), VIX ~16. Cash turnover at 22-month high → distribution risk; don't chase "cats and dogs," sell weak stocks but don't trade them speculatively. **Caution is the watchword.** Workshop themes: best four-quarter earnings growers, "magnificent seven sectors" (tourism/hospitality, real estate outperforming), plus a new sector from this quarter.

**Technical (Ramakrishna):** Below all moving averages; 23,000 support, 23,500/23,700/24,000 resistances; Nifty momentum negative (RSI ~40), Bank Nifty relatively better (above 20-DMA, RSI >50) but may follow down. Possible double-bottom near 22,200 if the fall continues. Avoid the first hour. Positional longs: KIMS, Federal Bank (7% SL).

## Stock calls
- **Kutumba Rao:** Hotel Leela Ventures (dark-horse only / avoid), Veeto Switchgears (hold, trailing SL), Visakha Industries (hold, SL ~60), D Development (strong order book, upper circuit — emerging mid-cap), Sai Parenterals (buy on dips), Affordable Robotics (avoid for now — financials suspect), Epigral (hold), Gujarat Pipavav/GPPL (hold).
- **Excluded (Ramakrishna, technical):** KIMS, Federal Bank long trades; Nifty/Bank Nifty levels.
