# Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 5th June 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-06-05
# https://youtu.be/GXazKdGxJs8

## Summary — June 5, 2026 (Friday)

**Market backdrop.**
- **AI-trade reversal in Asia:** semiconductor/AI names that drove Korea and Taiwan are selling off. Kospi −4% (−7% at one stage; Samsung + SK Hynix are >50% of the index), Taiwan −1%, Broadcom −12% on a results miss; Nasdaq underperformed while Dow (+~900) and S&P closed at/near record highs. AI theme cooling (Anthropic blog on "recursive self-improvement" risks flagged).
- **Geopolitics/crude:** Israel's Lebanon strikes continue; Iran won't move to a ceasefire while they continue, keeping oil sticky (~$95, −$1). Bitcoin in a multi-month low at ~$61k (>50% off ATH) — a risk-aversion flag.
- **Domestic events:** RBI policy at 10am (no rate change expected — likely a "non-event," commentary-driven, esp. on rupee weakness); Q4/FY26 GDP after hours (market reacts Monday). Economic Times lead: Centre may give FIIs more sops (MFN treaty etc.) — a possible RBI + Centre "coordinated action" trigger. Monsoon landed in Kerala (3 days late).

**Kutumba Rao's view.** RBI likely a non-event (VRR 75,000-cr notified, only ~17,000-cr subscribed → decent liquidity; WAR 5.26% → no repo change likely); watch commentary on rupee weakness. Doubts a major Nifty move today. Nifty held 23,300 all week; with FIIs in record shorts (~2.6-2.7 lakh contracts) and heavy underperformance, any small trigger could spark a 500-600 pt Nifty rally and a revisit of 24,000 — conditions favour taking some long risk. The real action is mid/small-caps (portfolios +20-40% since early March; some SMEs +30-50%); large-cap participation may start soon as valuations are attractive. Be nimble; favour long-term portfolio over chasing geopolitical short-term moves. Stay invested in good long-term names; exit weak small/mid-caps that rallied and rotate into new quality stocks. Watch IPO supply (MNC IPOs, Walmart) as a secondary-market drag, and corporate-governance flare-ups (Rajesh Exports). Institutional-radar names to watch: Go Digit (bulk deal, new high), Goldman buying "Million Brains/Garuda(?)", CG Power (switchgear commissioned), HDFC AMC (paused gold-ETF lumpsum).

**Kranti's view.** AI/chip valuations overheated — like the 1990s TMT bubble (HFCL historical valuation; Infosys 1995-peak buyers took ~10 yrs to recover). India is a hedge against an AI-bubble burst (no AI theme, stable domestic/consumer economy) — a stable long-only theme for FIIs (per Citi's Jane Fraser). But FIIs play Nifty 50/BSE 100; retail's edge is the BSE 500 / micro-mid-small-cap, stock-specific market (HFCL +3x in a year). Know when to "pluck the fruit" — D Developers (Diwali pick, 300→650) shows the cost of exiting too early; carry winners.

**Technical (Ramakrishna).** Range-bound consolidation; support 23,150 (April-7 gap fill), resistances every ~300 pts (23,600/23,900/24,100; heavier call-side selling = strong resistances). Below all MAs (10/20/50/200) and the falling trendline, momentum weak — needs to hold above 24,000 for a few days for real strength. IT heavily sold (one-day-wonder bounces hammered next day); Bank Nifty outperforming within the zone. Prefer Mid-cap Nifty for directional trades (light positions); straddles/strangle-selling for index. No trades till 10:30; then long >23,536/540, short if 23,300 cuts for 5 min. Pre-open: Nifty +62; opened +45-50 (Sensex +187, Bank Nifty +100), positive breadth.

## Stock calls
- **Kutumba Rao:** Aptus Value Housing (stable/dividend play, no multibagger), Flair Writing Industries (avoid / exit on rallies), KPI Green & Waaree (hold), VST Industries (hold, range-bound, no big gains), Reliance / TCS / HCL Tech / L&T (hold — long-term, patience), Idea Cellular & Suzlon (hold ~1 yr with trailing SL, high-risk), SBI Cards (underperformer, wealth-creation doubtful), ITC & HUL (hold). Plus positive momentum flagged: Go Digit, Adani Enterprises (big-volume), CG Power.
- **Excluded (Kranti):** GRM Overseas (exit on rallies, re-buy on dips; +200% already), BEL (add on dips, 15-20% compounder); D Developers (carry the winner).
- **Excluded (Ramakrishna, technical):** Meghmani Organics/MOL (technically weak); Nifty/Bank Nifty/Mid-cap levels.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 4 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/7wEL3-SBnc0

## Global backdrop
- US markets closed lower; **Nasdaq -0.89%** after a nine-day winning streak, with selling in tech.
- **Broadcom -13%** after a small revenue miss, though guidance set a **full-year AI-chip revenue target of $100 billion** — signalling sustained AI-chip demand over the next 3-5 years. **Nvidia -3.6%**, **Microsoft -3.1%**.
- West Asia crisis ongoing: Israel striking Hezbollah targets in Lebanon; **no progress** reported in Iran nuclear-disarmament talks. Uncertainty drove profit-booking.
- Asian markets mostly in the red; **Gift Nifty down ~200 points**, pointing to a negative open.
- The **dollar is at a two-month high** against many currencies; a large **SpaceX IPO** is pending, watched for post-IPO market behaviour.

## Indian markets
- **Gap-down opening** expected. Yesterday Nifty lost **76-77 points** but **Bank Nifty closed up ~470 points** (outperformer), with IT dragging the index.
- IT pack in a **bear market**: **TCS -7.5%** yesterday; 2300 call premium fell 72% while put premium rose 600%. Advice: avoid large-cap IT; selectively buy mid/small-cap IT (Coforge, Oracle Financial, Persistent).
- **Nifty technicals**: touched **23,150** (filled the 8 April gap); support **23,000-150 / 23,270**, resistance **23,800**. Lower lows forming (23,922 on 25 May → 23,151 on 3 June), but mid/small-caps stay resilient despite FII selling.
- **RBI monetary policy tomorrow** is the key trigger (repo rate, yields, rupee commentary). RBI **rejected treasury-bill bids** (weak demand); **rupee weakened to ~95.71** (near 96).
- News flow: government proposal to **mix petrol into ethanol** (positive for sugar/ethanol makers); rumours of **STCG/LTCG relief for FPIs** and a cut to the **20% tax on overseas bond-investor interest** (Bloomberg-reported), which helped bonds recover — "buy on rumours, sell on news."
- **Rajesh Exports**: SEBI interim order alleges **Rs 15 lakh crore of fictitious transactions (2021-25)**; repeatedly flagged as untrustworthy. Stock rose to Rs 109 (+2.2%) but likely heading to lower circuit. LIC and two FIIs each hold >10%.
- Cabinet decisions: **Rs 10,000 crore Aviation/ATF relief fund**; Delhi old-vehicle replacement (positive for electric buses); Telangana highway expansion.
- Bloomberg "RBI sold ~12 billion dollars of gold" story called false; **RBI issued a rejoinder** (reserves ~880 tonnes "as on date"); Kutumba Rao noted the undated wording fuels confusion.
- Stock-specific moves at open: **Trent** ex-bonus; **JBM Auto +5%** (claims 49% e-bus market share, 157 buses delivered last month); **Olectra +2.5%** (Rs 500 crore expansion); IdeaForge and Skipper announced QIP/preferential issues; Savita Oil and Akshar Chem +20% on results.
- At open: **Nifty ~-130**, **Sensex ~-450**, **Bank Nifty -200+** (underperforming early); **VIX +6% to 16.28**.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets, viewer stock Q&A) — see the dedicated Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): contra setup between weak Nifty and strengthening Bank Nifty; Bank Nifty 54,500 OI strength; Nifty support 23,000, resistance 23,700-800 then 24,000; reiterated the **22,600 month-end put** (bought ~110, trail stop to Rs 90, target 160-170). On viewer Qs: **NLC India** fresh entries near 325 (~68% pullback); **Laurus** strong, dips to 1250-1280 buyable.
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst): FPI-taxation speculation but no concrete notes; government in "realisation mode" on FII outflows; "buy on rumours, sell on news." On viewer Q: **Godavari Bio Refineries** — hold if investor (clinical trial-2 molecule), sell near 350 resistance if trader; flagged its branched-alcohol patent.

## Workshop
- Vijayawada workshop **June 13-14** (one-to-one sessions Sat, workshop Sun), with extra **Friday 12 June** one-on-ones and a live closing-strategy session (2:30-3:30) by Ramakrishna; Kutumba Rao to present risk-management/accumulation in crisis markets.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Laurus Labs, Heritage Foods, Vimta Labs)

kutumba.note: High-conviction; buy partial now instead of waiting for lower level
kutumba.detail: In stocks with strong conviction, buy about 25% upfront rather than insisting on an exact entry level. The awaited 1100 never came; it is already at ~1400. Start buying some now and add in staggered fashion.

kutumba.note: Best-in-class medical equipment but turned speculative near 52-week low
kutumba.detail: One of the best in medical equipment; corrected from ~2300 with decent results (growth maintained) and is now debt-free. But it became a speculative counter at 89 PE near its 52-week low with no buying coming in. Hold; if it rallies to 510-520, exit.

kutumba.note: Not a great stock; hold existing position
kutumba.detail: Grouped with Ramky as 'not very great stocks.' Advised holding the existing holding rather than acting.

kutumba.note: Not a great stock; hold existing position
kutumba.detail: Grouped with Protean as 'not very great stocks.' Advised holding the existing holding rather than acting.

kutumba.note: Smart-meter story; expect only steady returns
kutumba.detail: A smart, better story that has consolidated and trades steadily around 300, with good-enough results. Only steady returns expected from here; could attempt 400 again if the smart-meter revolution continues. (Originally suggested near 78; it ran to 500-600.)

kutumba.note: Hold if already bought; no fresh buying
kutumba.detail: Company is doing well overall, but watch whether the regulatory framework for solar panels changes and how quickly government changes come. Hold for now if already bought; no fresh buying.

kutumba.note: Only ever a 'dark horse' on volumes, never a recommendation; tainted group
kutumba.detail: This was never an investment recommendation, only a 'dark horse' mention when volumes appeared. The promoter group is tainted (a sitting MLA and former MP-minister). Be very careful with such counters and do not go near them.

kutumba.note: Overpriced since listing; heavy distribution
kutumba.detail: Overpriced since listing (a 'Lokesh-type', Gujju-batch-operated counter) with heavy distribution at 1300-1200. Exit on rallies around 675-700. A reasonable buy price for the company would be 360-400.

kutumba.note: Milk prices rising, ice-cream season strong
kutumba.detail: Milk prices are jumping and the ice-cream season is running well, so accumulate at this rate (~322). Patience is needed; some slow results improvement may be seen next quarter.

kutumba.note: Attractive valuations; needs to hold 530-540 for sustained interest
kutumba.detail: No fresh news, but attractive valuations bring intermittent buying. For sustained interest it must hold above 530-540 for two-three days. Accumulate continuously whenever the stock is below 500.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 3 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5DPMstSE2yo

## Global backdrop
- US markets closed higher again; **Nasdaq and S&P 500 near record highs**, AI trade continuing. Selling in traditional IT (e.g. **EPAM**), buying in AI names like **Nvidia**.
- **Marvell Technologies jumped 32%** after Nvidia's Jensen Huang called it the "next trillion-dollar company." Note: Marvell has a significant India presence (Hyderabad).
- **Google/Alphabet** announced an **$80bn capital raise for AI** despite a **~$4.5tn market cap** and ~$160bn annual profit ($62bn last quarter). Uday Kotak's tweet: all Indian listed companies combined are only ~$4.84tn — roughly equal to Google alone — urging Indian corporates to do capex.
- A **Korean ETF tracking SK Hynix** reportedly collected ~900tn yuan, highlighting the global craze for AI/chip names.
- Market-cap league table: **Taiwan $5.15tn, South Korea $5.04tn, India $4.84tn** — India has slipped from 5th to 7th (host later says effectively 8th as Korea overtook).
- Caution flagged on AI-driven **job losses** and the end of the H-1B route hurting Indian IT optimism.

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty pointed to a ~130-point gap-down** open; pre-open showed Nifty -67, Sensex -142, Bank Nifty -173. Losses deepened intraday (Nifty -180, Bank Nifty -445/-450 later).
- Yesterday's rally was a **short-covering / mean-reversion bounce led by IT** (call premiums rose 5–10x). IT reversed sharply lower today (**TCS down >4%**, Infosys, LTIMindtree, Wipro, Tech Mahindra all weak; IT index -2.5%).
- **Market breadth stayed positive** (prior day 1,875 advances vs 1,247 declines; at open BSE 829/538, NSE 1,004/650). Strong **mid/small-cap bull market** continues even with Nifty ~3,000 pts off its all-time high — many retail portfolios at all-time highs.
- Midcap IT post-results surges cited: **Tata Tech +40%, Coforge +50%, OFSS +66%, Firstsource +30%, Xchanging +30%, Newgen +15–20%**.
- Lifetime highs touched by: Aditya Infotech, Universal Cables, Venus Remedies, Rubicon Research, Sterlite Technologies, Acme Solar, Apollo Micro, Thangamayil Jewellery, D Development Engineers.
- **Block deals**: Lenskart ~Rs 2,873 cr at ~Rs 5,008; GMR Airports. **Newgen** flagged as ~85% speculative (3.5cr volume, only 27 lakh delivery).
- Favoured pockets: **metals, pharma, data-center theme** (Nomura calls data centers "the next big theme").
- Macro watch: **FII selling intense** (~Rs 400 cr/hour; ~Rs 3,00,000 cr net over ~5 months); **RBI sold gold** (quantum unknown — possible panic trigger if large vs 880t reserves); **WPI revision from June 15**; **RBI policy / commentary Friday = most important day**. El Niño / weak-monsoon and **MSME early-stress** risks flagged.
- **NHPC OFS**: institutional portion only 1.6x (muted vs Coal India's 8x); retail portion opens today; stock +4% on green-shoe news. **Concord Biotech +3.5%** on USFDA approval.
- **Nomura**: Nifty target **25,900 by March '27** (~11% upside) on robust earnings — a rare bullish note amid bearishness.
- Crude warning: one institution sees **crude up to $150** if West Asia tensions persist — a severe risk for India.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamentals/markets, viewer Q&A): Nifty stuck **23,300–23,800**; below 23,300 = "big fall," possibly **600–700 pts in a couple of days**; above 24,000 on volume + positive macro/deals = big rally. Sees more scope for weakness (~80% chance of El Niño effect). Stay invested but treat as a trading market; small positions; PCR 0.7 = buy zone, 1.2 = book. Gave viewer stock calls (see Kutumba Rao file).
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst — separate from Kutumba Rao): Nomura's target is "very conservative," crude is the key swing factor; data-center theme bullish. Answered ETV Networks (buy pre-split if at all), Axxela (stretched, risk-appetite only), OKD Pharma (add on dips, fully priced). Noted John Cockerill (workshop pick) up ~120%.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst): markets range-bound, below all moving averages; momentum (RSI/MACD) weak; possible **double bottom near 22,200**; 23,300 break → short with strict stop. Entero entry ~6,700; Apollo Micro buy on pullback ~395–400; 22,600 put idea.
- **Vasanth**: anchor.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 7 calls (Mass Chip (Maschip), Good Luck India, Suzlon, Honeywell Automation, MRF, Bata India, Cello World)

kutumba.note: Not for short term; long-term hold with good potential
kutumba.detail: On a viewer holding 2,500 shares at 215 for a one-month horizon, Kutumba Rao said one shouldn't buy this for the short term at all. He recalled it went to 280-290 last Sep-Oct then fell to 145, rose again to 230 with a higher top. He advised a trading stop-loss to limit losses and said a big rally would only come if it crosses 250 again, which may not happen easily; short-term holders can sell into a small rally, but it is a good long-term (1-3 year) hold.

kutumba.note: Hold long term; book partial profits and trail stop if bought lower
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao advised holding Good Luck India for the long term ('lock-down'), noting results were not as great as expected but the stock has held up at higher levels (touched 1,400+, now around 1,300). If bought at lower levels, book partial profits and put a trading stop. He said the low P/E supports continuing to hold for the long term.

kutumba.note: No risk; SEBI penalty is routine, company doing well
kutumba.detail: On the recent SEBI report/penalty (~Rs 28 crore), Kutumba Rao said there is no risk and advised holding. He noted the penalty has already been appealed to SAT and such cases are routine (compared it to an old Reliance Petrochemicals case dismissed in court). For a company posting quarterly profit above Rs 2,000 crore, a ~Rs 20 crore SEBI notice is nothing; the company is doing well, so hold.

kutumba.note: Not a stock to watch daily; hold high-priced quality name
kutumba.detail: For a viewer holding 12 shares of Honeywell Auto bought at 37,000, Kutumba Rao said it is not a share to look at daily and advised simply holding. He noted it is a high-priced share that has frequently given good returns over time and is not a cheap stock either.

kutumba.note: Put in the locker; review after a year or two
kutumba.detail: For a viewer at a 6% loss asking hold or sell, Kutumba Rao said once you buy MRF you should put it in the locker and look at it after a year or two; it is not a stock to track daily.

kutumba.note: Won't buy despite discount; competition up, weak results
kutumba.detail: On whether to buy Bata at a discount, Kutumba Rao asked why it is available cheap, pointing to rising competition and results not coming as great as expected. While peers like Relaxo and other footwear stocks rose a bit, Bata is still close to its 52-week low (around 650-660). He said he would not buy it; better to buy Bata's footwear than the share now.

kutumba.note: Can't withstand competition; near 52-week low, could fall to 250
kutumba.detail: A viewer planned to invest Rs 1 lakh each in Cello World and MV Photovoltaic on the thesis that China-import restrictions would benefit them. Kutumba Rao said that reasoning is not correct: Cello World cannot withstand competition and has been making lower-tops/lower-bottoms since listing. Even after a somewhat impressive recent quarter, the stock sits near its 52-week low, and expecting it to rise on an unrelated macro development is foolishness; he feels Cello World could touch even 250.

# Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 2nd June 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-06-02
# https://youtu.be/0xkhls60gM0

## Summary — June 2, 2026 (Tuesday, Nifty expiry; Telangana Formation Day)

> **Kutumba Rao did not appear in this episode** (he messaged that he was at a temple). Analysts present: Kranti and technical analyst Ramakrishna. The episode also carried CM Revanth Reddy's Formation Day address.

**Market backdrop.** Gap-down open (~200 pts) on elevated crude — Brent touched $98 overnight, eased to ~$94-95. ~700-pt Nifty fall over three days (24,300 → ~23,100). India underperforming again: South Korea ($5.04tn) is now the 6th-largest equity market; India slipped to ~7th/8th as Korea/Taiwan/Japan led by a few AI names (Samsung, SK Hynix) pull ahead. FIIs −₹7,850 cr (cash+futures); DIIs buying (net +~₹1,200 cr cash), but index-stock selling keeps sentiment negative. FII shorts jumped from 1.53→2.23 lakh contracts in four sessions. Breadth turned negative for the first time — risk of spreading to mid/small caps.

**Economy is fine (data cited).** GST +3.2% MoM to ₹1.94 lakh cr (up YoY); PMI mfg ~55; UPI ~761m daily txns; May auto sales strong across nearly all makers (Tata PV +42%, Maruti +35%, TVS/Atul +31%, Bajaj +21%) — only Force Motors fell. IT supported by strong HPE results (rev $9.3bn, +18% YoY); Nvidia strong on a new PC chip; Anthropic filed its IPO draft (racing OpenAI).

**Kranti's view.** Track crude; "buy-on-dips / sell-on-rallies" texture; market swings like a pendulum between extreme optimism and pessimism — book profits into optimism. US delegation visiting (3-day bilateral talks) and MPC meeting due — likely status quo; a rate hike would be "frying pan into the fire." Buy strong-outlook earnings names on dips; keep a satellite/trading portfolio. YTD outperformers cited: HFCL +168%, Hitachi Energy +100%, Schneider +72%, Welspun Corp +70%, Thermax +65%, BSE +55%, Adani names 40-60%.

**Technical (Ramakrishna).** Settlement-driven volatility; prior-gap fill ~23,150, support ~23,175; longs only if it holds/rises there, else shorts. By late morning losses narrowed (Nifty <100 pts down).

## Stock calls (all by Kranti / Ramakrishna — NOT Kutumba Rao)
Siemens Energy (add, far better than Advait Energy), Archean Chemicals (hold), Olectra Greentech (consistent compounder — add on dips/book on rises; Vasanth more bullish, sees possible 4-5x in 3 yrs on 10,000-bus capacity), NHPC (exit on rallies — slow low-beta PSU), Netweb (add 25% ~4,700), Oriana Power (weak SME; small attempt near ~1,520 support only), MTAR Technologies (wait, add ~6,200), HFCL (data-centre theme strong but institutional-holding overhang; make capital free-of-cost), Persistent Systems (buy, add ~5,300-5,350 / >5,600), RateGain (hold/add on dips), BLS International (hold). Nasdaq-100 ETF — start an SIP.

# Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 1st June 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-06-01
# https://youtu.be/ZyfiZWuRJfA

## Summary — June 1, 2026 (Monday, start of June; results-heavy episode)

**Market backdrop.** Friday saw heavy losses (Nifty −360, Sensex −1,100) on intense end-of-day FII selling tied to an MSCI rejig. Brent crude ~$93. Severe Indian underperformance: in May Nifty −2% while Dow +3%, S&P +5%, Nasdaq +8%, Kospi +26%, Nikkei +12%, Taiwan +12% — the worst relative showing in two decades. Drivers: FII selling (~₹21,000 cr Friday; ~a year's quota done in 5 months), rupee weakness, crude/war, El Niño rainfall worry (~90% of average). Govt must decide soon on FII/capital-gains relief or risk more damage. Q4 earnings mostly good; Q1 may be softer. Mid/small caps still see stock-specific action (index near ATH); rally limited to Pharma, Capital Market, MNC Energy (Hitachi, GE Vernova, Siemens Energy), some Industrials. Crude must fall + war recede for the rally to broaden.

**Results.** Olectra Greentech strong (FY rev +28% to ₹2,312 cr, PAT ₹180 cr; energy/insulator division high-growth ~38% margins). PTC Industries blockbuster (top line ₹121→₹225 cr, high operational leverage, QoQ profit ~doubled; ₹25,000 one-year target cited). OAD/Ocard +16% on US FDA approval ("Zenix").

**Kutumba Rao on Natco Pharma:** weak optics from absent one-time income, but EBITDA margin held at 25.1% despite the South Africa (Adcock ~35.5%) acquisition — a one-time glitch; strong US pipeline; API revenue rising. **Hold and accumulate between 900-1000.**

**Technical (Ramakrishna).** Friday's fall mainly an MSCI-rejig pretext (heavy IT selling); net inflows actually positive. Monday volatile early; intraday longs only above 23,733; avoid shorts. Market opened strongly positive (Nifty +140, Sensex +500); breadth positive — mid-cap-dominated day.

## Stock calls
- **Kutumba Rao:** Natco Pharma (accumulate 900-1000), Laurus Labs (buy — top pharma pick), SMS Pharma (buy after correction), Sai Parenterals (buy), CG Power (buy), Adani Power (buy — invest equally with CG Power), Siemens Energy (buy on dips/SIP), Ashok Leyland (hold), NBCC (hold, trading stock), Suzlon (hold), Canara Bank (accumulate slowly), SBI (accumulate slowly), MCX (good co., wait for ~2,725-2,750), Gold & Silver ETFs (exit — 10-15% correction overdue), PTC Industries (avoid for now — wait for correction), Oword Pharma (avoid for now), Campus Activewear (exit/sell), Sai Silks Kalamandir (avoid), Emmvee Photovoltaic (wait for correction).

# Business Breakfast | Stock/Share Market News | 29th May 2026 | TV5 Money
# Uploaded: 2026-05-29
# https://youtu.be/M7lkqfRENTU

## Summary — May 29, 2026 (Friday)

> Note: Analyst **Kutumba Rao was ABSENT** in this episode. Analyst commentary was provided by **Kranti**; technicals by **Ramakrishna**.

**Market backdrop.**
- Last trading day of May; weak open expected (Gift Nifty ~−100 pts after being −300 intraday yesterday on renewed Iran–US strikes — Iran fired a ballistic missile at Kuwait, intercepted). But global cues were positive: Nasdaq at a record high (AI rally), Korea/Taiwan led by tech (TSMC ~45% of Taiwan's cap), crude low at ~$93, dollar index ~99.
- **AI momentum:** Dell raised AI-server revenue guidance from $10bn to $60bn (6x); Anthropic raised funds at ~$965bn valuation (run-rate ~$47bn) vs OpenAI ~$860bn; Korean 12-layer HBM4 sold out. Wipro ADR +18% on a ServiceNow agentic-AI tie-up. India = large AI *user*, not maker; ~5 GW of data-centre capacity expected in 4-5 years → plays in servers, network tech, electrical equipment, cables, gensets.
- **Domestic:** FII selling easing (Mar 1.18 lakh cr → Apr 61k cr → May ~34k cr; Wed only ~1k cr). Record FY26 listed-bank profits (~4.1 lakh cr). Strong results season (Business Standard Q4 sectoral scorecard). Financial Express: steps to boost capital inflows likely within a week; possible review of 12.5% LTCG for foreign investors. Defence action shifting from PSU (HAL, Cochin Shipyard, BDL) to private names (Data Patterns, Astra Micro, Apollo Micro). Coal India OFS institutional portion ~8x subscribed (floor 412, cut-off ~447).
- **Caption Services:** FY revenue +21% (831 cr), EBITDA +43%, PAT +57% (28.13 cr); entering home services (Hyderabad launch June 19) — TAM ~51-52k cr but initially loss-making; 12-month target 480-500 (bull), 400-450 (base).

**Kranti's view (in lieu of Kutumba Rao):** There IS a bull market in India — but in breadth (bottom of the pyramid, mid/small-caps up ~25-30% off lows), not the top 50/100. A stock-picker's market; run profits, stay vigilant (not a "buy-and-sleep" market). Crude is "the joker in the pack" — decisive cool-off (toward $80) would be a big relief; above $100 turns bearish. If results stay positive next quarter, India could turn attractive to FIIs again.

**Technical (Ramakrishna):** Market stuck in a symmetrical triangle; 24,100 the key upside trigger (then 24,600), downside support 23,706 (OI suggests 23,900). Momentum indicators (RSI/MACD/histogram) positive; holding 20-day EMA; VIX cooled ~7% (~14.98), PCR ~1.02; breadth strong; net strong-hand flows positive (~+2,800). Breakout either side likely by June 2.

## Stock calls
- **Kutumba Rao:** None — he was absent this episode.
- **Answered by Kranti (analyst, excluded):** Morepen Labs (sell/exit — junk), Orchid Pharma (hold 2 quarters), M&M (add on dips), Frontier Springs (hold/good), Sai Parenterals & Senores Pharma (buy small — new listing), Ashok Leyland (hold), Groww (sell on rallies), Laurus Labs (hold), ICICI AMC (hold), HAL (hold), Marico (sell on rallies), Piramal Pharma (hold), Embassy Office Parks REIT (hold) / Embassy Development (exit — weak), Raymond Realty (preferred real-estate pick), Adani Green/Power (hold — high-risk).
- **Answered by Ramakrishna (technical, excluded):** Frontier Springs (buy 1,600-1,650, momentum >1,800), Sai Parenterals (buy ~470, >520 momentum), Hitachi Energy (add ~34,250-300 / 20-DMA), MedPlus (add ~883 in tranches); Nifty/Bank Nifty levels.

## Summary — May 27, 2026 (Wednesday; Sensex expiry; Bakrid, holiday Thursday)

**Global / AI theme.** US mixed — Nasdaq-100 closed above 30,000 for the first time (was ~10,000 at ChatGPT's Nov-2022 launch). **Micron** hit a $1tn market cap (+19%; HBM memory chips sold out through 2026; ~+800% in 12 months); **SK Hynix** also crossed $1tn (+250% YTD, supplies HBM to Nvidia). India's market cap slipped to **6th** (Taiwan overtook): US ~$77tn, China ~$15.6tn, Japan ~$8.7tn, HK ~$7.25tn, Taiwan ~$4.95tn, India ~$4.92tn — we're "zero in semis/AI."

**Domestic wealth creators (where the money is).** Capital market (Adani group strong over 2 months), pharma, energy/electrification & data-centre supply (grid upgrades), metals at multi-year highs (Nalco, Hindalco, Hindustan Copper), aerospace (MTAR). 1-yr +100% names cited: Hitachi Energy, MTAR, GE Vernova, Sterlite Tech, Netweb.

**Kutumba Rao's view.** Pre-holiday nervousness; Nifty below 24,000 slightly negative — must protect 23,800; rupee weakening again (~95.7) is a negative; FIIs net bought (relief). 24,000 is the inflection/resistance. India's premium near 24,000 may slowly slide unless Indian firms become multinationals. **Technical (Ramakrishna).** Below 24,000 & 50-DMA but 20-DMA protected; 23,800 support, 24,100/24,600 resistance; momentum turning positive (MACD crossover) but OI worry (highest call 24,000, highest put 23,200). June-series ideas: metals (Nalco #1, Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc) and PSU banks (Bank of India, Union Bank). Avoid trading today; longs only positional above 24,000 with hedging.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 26 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/3DoN7lYjtNI

## Global backdrop
- **Weak global cues** at the open. Most of Asia (except Korea) saw selling after the prior day's gains; **Gift Nifty** pointed to a soft start, down about **20-25 points**.
- **US markets closed** for **Memorial Day**, so no overnight lead. World attention fixed on **US-Iran talks**.
- **Brent crude** below **100**, trading near **98**, roughly **15-16% below** its recent peak — seen as a settling factor for markets. "Friendly/defensive strikes" on southern Iran continued even as Trump touts near-final peace talks; Trump demands Iran's **enriched uranium** be destroyed or removed.
- **Taiwan's market cap** reached almost **5 trillion dollars** (TSMC ~**42%** of it), now above the **UK's** market cap despite Taiwan's GDP being under 1 trillion vs the UK's ~4.3 trillion.
- **Trump's "new twist":** pushing Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Pakistan, Turkey, Egypt, Jordan to join the **Abraham Accords** (friendship with Israel). **Pakistan has already rejected** it.

## Indian markets
- **Today is monthly options expiry** for Nifty, Bank Nifty and all NSE indices — a "very, very important trading day."
- Prior session strong, **led by Bank Nifty**; Nifty closed near the day's high. Drivers: crude under 100 and a **recovering rupee** at **95.23** (up **73 paise**, third successive day of strengthening).
- **FM's three F's** — **Fuel, Fertilizer, Forex** — flagged as the economy's biggest risks. Caution on **fertilizer subsidy** possibly crossing **3 lakh crore**, pushing overall subsidies up **40-50%**.
- **Flows:** FIIs bought **822 crore** (now ~**16% longs**, up from 9% earlier in the series, covering shorts); DIIs bought **3,857 crore** — net buying ~**4,700 crore**. (Part of FII positivity tied to a **Premier Energies** bulk deal.)
- **Roll-overs:** Nifty **60%**, Bank Nifty **64%** — watch roll into new series. **PCR 1.26**. **VIX** fell **6.8% to 16.7**.
- **FM** signalled openness to reviewing **capital gains tax on foreigners** ("we will take their inputs").

## Results season / stock action
- Stock-specific, result-driven action dominant.
- **Apollo Micro Systems** (Hyderabad defence theme) the **stock of the day** — rumours it will acquire **Premier Explosives** and/or **ideaForge** (both hit upper circuits); Apollo up 11-16%, touched **420**. No official confirmation yet — watch for exchange clarification. Apollo is also targeting UAV/ISR/drone/loitering-munition entry via acquisition (has DPIIT licence).
- **Hitachi Energy** ("Power India") — standout quarter: revenue **1,840→2,700 cr** YoY (2,000→2,700 QoQ), PAT **184→330 cr**, order book ~**30,000 cr**.
- **Amara Raja** result essentially **flat** once stripping ~**209 cr other income** (insurance claims: **186 cr** fire-accident full-and-final from Chittoor 2023, plus **122 cr** business-interruption in Sept). Stock rose ~8%, opened above 900.
- Other good numbers: PACE Digitek, Oriana Power, Aaron Healthcare, Harrisons Malayalam, Travel Food Services, TVS Supply Chain, IFB Industries, Uniparts, Sigma Advanced Systems (large receivables — caution). **Kalyani Forge** disappointed; **Container Corp** weak (-5%).
- ~20% movers: Premier Explosives, Hariom Pipes, Cords Cable, Sundaram Brake Linings. Also Suprajit Engineering (+12-13%), Gufic Bio (+10%), railway-wagon names (Jupiter Wagons) on talk of **40,000 cr wagon orders** over 3-4 years.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamentals + viewer stock Q&A. Sees expiry day **range-bound**; a close above **24,000** sets up a good new series. Watch roll-overs. Stay cautious; buy only fundamentally sound results on declines. Bullish on **Amara Raja** (toward 1,000+). Took viewer calls on Motherson/Cummins/Aeroflex, Renuka/Bajaj Hindusthan, NTPC.
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst — kept in a different folder) — answered several viewer mails (addressed to anchor "Vasanth"): Surya Roshni, Torrent Power/Trans Rail, CDSL/BDL, Godavari Bio. Strongly bullish on a recently-discussed small/mid-cap ("Russel Taxes"?) with long earnings cycle. Endorsed Amara Raja's eventual upside (Giga corridor commissioning post-Q2) but flagged patience needed.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — Nifty back above 20-DMA and 50-EMA, momentum indicators (MACD, RSI 54.35) turned positive; next hurdle **24,600**. Bank Nifty +2.3%, next hurdles 56,300-400 then 57,340, support 53,200. Long attempt today only above **24,550-60**. Gave KRN entry **980-1,000**.
- **Vasanth** — anchor/analyst running the desk.

## Key levels
- **Nifty:** breakout above **24,050** near-term; next hurdle **24,600-620**; close above **24,000** key for new series.
- **Bank Nifty:** hurdles **56,300-400** / **57,340**; support **53,200**.
- **OI:** Nifty highest writing 24,000-400 (400-pt call/put gap → volatility); Bank Nifty 55,700 call / 55,000 put writing.

## Other
- **REITs** flagged as a good option — ~**2,500 cr+** distributed this year, **10-12% yields**.
- **Monsoon** onset over Kerala on **25 May**.
- Ahead of **RBI**: watch rising inflation (CNG up 4 times, successive fuel hikes) → WPI/CPI and RBI commentary; new US Fed chief (Warsh?) commentary also key.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 4 buys (Amara Raja Energy & Mobility, Samvardhana Motherson International, Cummins India, NTPC)

kutumba.note: Buy on every decline; headed toward 1000-plus
kutumba.detail: His repeated conviction call: buy Amara Raja on every decline, the stock is on its way to 1000-plus in the coming quarter. He flagged the latest result as essentially flat once ~209 cr of other income (insurance claims of 186 cr for the 2023 Chittoor fire and 122 cr business-interruption) is stripped out. Stay invested and accumulate on dips.

kutumba.note: Near 52-wk high; results discounted, buy only on dips
kutumba.detail: Trading near its 52-week high, and auto-ancillary companies are doing well on better-than-expected results. But results are already discounted, so for fresh buying he would buy only when a decline comes rather than chase at current levels.

kutumba.note: Strong MNC engineering name; buy on a decline
kutumba.detail: A strong multinational engineering company, no doubt about it. Already near its 52-week high at 5,370. Strong summer or a weaker monsoon lifts demand for such companies, so it is a good bet when a decline comes.

kutumba.note: Heavy speculation; book profits, don't buy fresh here
kutumba.detail: Recently listed and has a lot of speculative activity; trade it carefully. If raw-material cost impact shows up, there are signs it could come back to 350-360. If you already hold some, book some profits, and don't buy fresh at this level.

kutumba.note: Sugar = trading sector; regulatory overhang, big returns unlikely now
kutumba.detail: Regulatory interference has become too much on both the ethanol and export sides, with exports temporarily banned to control inflation and ethanol offtake insufficient. The recent 1-1.5 month rally in ethanol-based sugar names was bubble-like and big returns are unlikely now. Sugar is a trading-only sector, not wealth-creating.

kutumba.note: Same sugar overhang; only a dark-horse counter
kutumba.detail: Same regulatory overhang as the broader sugar space, with exports banned and weak ethanol offtake. He sees it only as a dark-horse counter (there are better counters in sugar) and treats sugar as a sector to trade in and out of, not hold.

kutumba.note: Disinvestment of subsidiaries to lift stock over ~6 months
kutumba.detail: He would buy NTPC because two-three more subsidiaries are being prepared by the government for disinvestment. Over the next six months, either for that disinvestment or the gains it brings, the stock should rise. It is close to its 52-week high (414) at 391, hasn't fallen much, so stay invested and fresh buying can also be done.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 25 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/Rstqoq_UEyU

## Global backdrop
- **US–Iran**: Strong reports a deal is being reached. Trump's Truth Social post says "the framework of the deal is largely negotiated," though US negotiators are "not in a rush." Israel's PM insists the deal needs Iran's **nuclear threat fully removed**. Positive statements also came from Iranian officials.
- **Crude**: Brent fell sharply on the de-escalation — from **104–105** on Friday to near **99** (quoted at **98.72**). Crude below 100 is treated as the single biggest cue for the day's direction.
- **US/Asia**: US markets closed positive Friday; Asian markets and **Gift Nifty (+200 pts)** point to a gap-up open.

## Indian markets
- **Open**: Gap-up confirmed — **Nifty +250**, **Sensex +800**, **Bank Nifty +750**; advance/decline strongly favoured advances (**1,569 adv / 250 dec**). All indices (Nifty, Sensex, Bank Nifty, midcap, smallcap, IT) green.
- **Rupee**: Recovered to **~95.69** Friday close (Ramakrishna also cited ~94.29 vs USD).
- **Flows (Fri)**: DIIs bought **Rs 6,000 cr**, FIIs sold **Rs 4,440 cr** — net positive (DII figure inflated by block deals). Read as a buy-on-dips market.
- **RBI dividend**: Record **Rs 2.86 lakh crore** special dividend to the government (vs ~Rs 2.1 lakh cr two yrs ago, ~Rs 2.7 lakh cr last yr) — to materially narrow the fiscal deficit.
- **Fuel**: Petrol +**61 paise**/litre, diesel +**71 paise**; ~**Rs 7.50** rise over ~10 days. Positive for OMCs, which were bleeding ~**Rs 13/litre on petrol** and ~**Rs 38/litre on diesel**.
- **Short week**: Only **4 sessions**; Thursday closed for **Muharram**.

## Earnings season (weekend results)
- **Hindalco** — excellent. **Eicher Motors** — strong; **Goldman Sachs target Rs 8,400**. **Sarda Energy, Precision Wires, Century Plyboards** — good. **NTPC** — weak (a laggard). **Torrent Pharma** — good.
- **Divi's Labs** — excellent Q4; margins dipped but H2 capex on track. Peptides market leader; **Morgan Stanley target Rs 7,900**; valuations rich; made up for a weak Q3.
- **SMS Pharma** — disappointing Q4 (war effect). FY: revenue **Rs 887 cr (+13.3%)**, EBITDA **Rs 179 cr (+22.5%)**, PAT **Rs 87.6 cr (+28%)**; but Q4 PAT margin fell **11.09% → 8.79%**, EBIT margin **14.65% → 11.34%**. Cause: ibuprofen raw-material/gas price spikes (SMS is a top global ibuprofen producer); MD Ramesh expects recovery over next ~2 months.
- **HBL/HPL Engineering** — negative reaction. FY26 revenue **Rs 1,967 → Rs 3,303 cr**, EBITDA **Rs 393 → Rs 1,113 cr**; Q4 sales good but profitability fell on lumpy **kavach** (railway safety) contracts. Management guides **FY27 significantly better than FY26**.
- **Sai Parenterals** (first results post-listing) — see promoter interview below.

## Analysts featured
- **Ramakrishna (technical)** — Kutumba Rao and Kranthi had not arrived at the open, so he opened the show.
- **Anil Kumar** — promoter/MD, Sai Parenterals (in-studio interview).
- **Kutumba Rao** — joined later remotely (Zoom/phone); answered some viewer stock Q&A.
- **Kranthi** — joined later; answered Sai Parenterals and one viewer call.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

### Ramakrishna — technical view
- Nifty breaking out of the **23,400–23,800** range to the upside. **24,000** is the hard level; above it, next resistance **24,600**. The **2-April bottom** can be treated as the seasonal bottom (war "settled").
- OI: highest put writing **18.5 lakh contracts at 23,750** Friday → support shifted up from 23,400 to **23,750**.
- Nifty small positive candle (daily + weekly); closed above 10 EMA; 20/30/50/200 EMAs to be crossed gradually. Bank Nifty +1.15% / +600 pts Friday, above 10 EMA. **PCR 1 → 1.08**, **India VIX flat at 17.82** (comfort zone). Buy-on-dips; take intraday entries on dips toward ~23,900.

### Sai Parenterals (Anil Kumar interview)
- First post-listing results; first quarter of consolidation with **Newmed** (Australian marketing co, acquired 12 Nov).
- **Consolidated FY25-26**: income **Rs 381 cr (+133%)**; EBITDA **Rs 19.9 → 11.9 cr (-40%)**; PAT ~flat; PAT margin **8.8% → 3.7%**. The drop is a one-off consolidation/merger effect (Newmed runs ~10% EBITDA vs Sai standalone ~22%); not comparable YoY since no Newmed last year.
- **Standalone FY26**: revenue **Rs 162 cr (+31%)**, EBIT **Rs 18.8 cr (+31%)**, PAT **Rs 16.8 cr (+63%)**, PAT margin **8.3% → 10.4%**; EBITDA held ~22%.
- **Backward integration**: Newmed's manufacturing being shifted in-house (captive supply to Australia) → margins to rise toward **21–22%** vs current 10%. Margin upside expected from **Q1/Q2**.
- **Capex Rs 440 cr** total (Rs 303 cr Australia — 80% done, balance by Sep 2026; Rs 130–138 cr India — done by December), to complete before March 2027; revenue impact in **FY28/FY29**. Best quarters Q3/Q4; Q1/Q2 lean.
- **Guidance**: **Rs 750 cr** topline (conservative) and **~17% EBITDA margin** for the year; hopes for a four-figure topline. 88 product dossiers added in regulated/emerging markets, 67 in pipeline, all to be commercialized FY27.
- Balance-sheet note: **Rs 118 cr "other financial liabilities"** is the OFS amount paid 2 April (cash shown but OFS settled post 31 March) — a balancing entry. Higher inventory (Dec–Feb buying) is cushioning raw-material price rises for the next two quarters.

## Stock calls (viewer Q&A — attribution noted)
Calls clearly answered by **Kutumba Rao** (captured in his file + buys.json):
- **52-week-high strategy (Radico, Sansera Engineering, Hitachi, Bans)** — strategy validated; use trailing stop-loss; for long term do SIPs / buy on dips, not just at highs.
- **Bondada Engineering** — **Hold** (entered high at Rs 435; huge order book, solar EPC reviving).
- **Waaree Energy & CDSL** — long-term **Buy/Hold**; 5-yr 100% returns plausible (capital markets + renewables).

Calls answered by **other analysts** (kept OUT of Kutumba Rao's buys.json):
- **CG Power** — Ramakrishna: buy on pullback ~840.
- **Jubilant Foodworks** — Kranthi: exit on rallies (QSR under pressure).
- **Enter Technologies (ENTR)** — Ramakrishna: entry ~7,300 on further dip. Q Power / TD Power / CG Power / Adani Power — buy all four equally (anchor/Ramakrishna).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 buys (Radico Khaitan, Sansera Engineering, Hitachi (Hitachi Energy), Bans (Bansal Wire / Bans), Waaree Energy, CDSL)

kutumba.note: 52-wk-high buying is fine; protect profit with a trailing stop-loss
kutumba.detail: A viewer who buys names like Radico whenever they hit a 52-week high asked if the strategy is sound. Kutumba Rao said it is typically a good strategy because a 52-week high happens with a reason (results/sector change), but one must confirm the change is positive and protect profit with a trailing stop-loss (e.g. Radico near its 3-year high ~Rs 3600, trail ~3250-3350). He noted this breakout method suits short/medium term; for the long term, also buy on dips.

kutumba.note: Good wealth-creator; do SIPs, do not buy only at highs
kutumba.detail: As part of the 52-week-high strategy question, Kutumba Rao named Sansera as a good long-term wealth-creating company. He advised buying on dips and doing SIPs rather than only at highs, while using a trailing stop-loss to protect profits on breakouts.

kutumba.note: Good wealth-creator; SIP-worthy on dips
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao named Hitachi as a good long-term wealth-creating company in response to the 52-week-high strategy question, recommending SIPs and buying on dips rather than chasing only the highs. He paired it with a trailing-stop-loss discipline for the breakout/short-term portion.

kutumba.note: Named as SIP-worthy long-term wealth-creator
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao listed 'Bans' among the names worth doing SIPs in (alongside Hitachi and Sansera) when validating a viewer's 52-week-high buying strategy. He advised accumulating on dips for the long term rather than only at highs. (Company name approximate from auto-captions.)

kutumba.note: Hold, do not book the loss; huge order book, solar EPC reviving
kutumba.detail: A viewer at a ~25% loss (bought at Rs 435) asked whether to book the loss or hold. Kutumba Rao said hold and do not book the loss. He cited a prior interview with promoter Raghvendra showing a huge order book, and noted solar EPC companies are picking up again. The viewer entered high (~Rs 100 above the current price), so patience is needed.

kutumba.note: Long-term buy; renewables space; 5-yr 100% return plausible
kutumba.detail: Asked by a viewer wanting ~100% returns in five years on Waaree Energy and CDSL, Kutumba Rao said that kind of return is plausible over five years going by the last three years' performance. He noted Waaree is in the renewable energy space, supporting the possibility of strong long-term returns.

kutumba.note: Long-term buy; steady capital-markets business, no problem
kutumba.detail: In the same answer (Waaree Energy and CDSL, targeting 100% in five years), Kutumba Rao said CDSL is fine for the long term: capital markets keep generating volumes and new listings, and CDSL has a steady (paid) business with no problem. He judged a 5-year 100% return possible.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 22 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/I9fernV4SRg

## Global backdrop
- It's Friday (weekend factor). US markets closed positive last night; Nikkei, Kospi and Taiwan trading up.
- **US–Iran peace deal** near a final stage but stuck on two issues: (1) Iran's ~440 kg of **enriched uranium** (Iran wants to keep it but "not build a weapon"; US/Israel refuse), and (2) Iran's proposal for a **permanent toll system on the Strait of Hormuz** (via Oman) — Trump says "we don't want tolls."
- **Crude volatile** — swung $106 → $102 → back above $105; markets trading watching crude. Dollar at a six-week high.

## Indian markets
- Nifty couldn't hold higher levels; volatile sessions (±233 points). Three drivers cited: US bond yields, dollar–rupee, crude.
- **Rupee strengthened** to ~96.2 (from 96.96) after RBI's $5bn dollar–rupee swap auctions (on the 26th); NITI Aayog chairman dismissed rupee weakness ("100 is just a number").
- **PM held marathon meetings** (govt's 12 years in office); Commerce Minister Goyal flagged measures to control the CAD — possible weekend news (e.g., FPI taxation?).
- **Strong stock-specific action** despite a flat index, especially around the **data-centre theme**: power/electricals (Schneider +43%, Hitachi Energy, GE Power +40%, GE Vernova +21%), cooling (KRN Heat Exchangers, Aeroflex +68%, DEE Development +60%), cables/networking (Sterlite Tech +153%), system integration (Dynacons +60%, Black Box +84%); pharma CDMO (Neuland, Laurus, Gland +29%, Divi's, Syngene).
- **Crude/Reliance story (ET):** Venezuela now India's 3rd-largest crude supplier; UAE pipeline bypassing the Strait of Hormuz under construction. Reliance/Jamnagar is the main (85–95%) processor of thick, high-sulphur Venezuelan crude — a key advantage; tied to a US refinery deal.
- Cell Health (Dr Ravi Kumar Chinta, naturopathy/wellness) flagged as a possible upcoming IPO.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Don't go short; stay invested; exit weaker stocks in the rally. Even a bounce to 24,500 wouldn't surprise.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — market range-bound since May 11 (23,900/24,000 resistance, 23,400/23,350 support); breakout likely Mon/Tue on settlement pressure, chances tilt to the upside. Don't short positionally. Momentum dull but histogram bars shrinking → weakness completing, big green candle possible. Nifty closed ~flat (-4 pts); Bank Nifty -123, below all MAs.
- Market opened flat/+40, recovered toward +90/100 in Nifty; breadth positive; LIC +3.5%, Honasa +8% on results; ITC/Titan negative reactions.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 8 buys (ITC, LIC, SBI, Adani Ports, IDBI Bank, Info Edge (Naukri), TD Power Systems, TVS Motors)

kutumba.note: Only for extreme long term; accumulate staggered. Has disappointed/corrected over decades.
kutumba.detail: Only for the extreme long term (children's-money horizon); accumulate in a staggered manner. ITC has disappointed over 30-40 yrs and has corrected. Not for 1-3 months.

kutumba.note: Only for extreme long term; accumulate staggered. Spectacular quarterly result.
kutumba.detail: Only for the extreme long term; accumulate in a staggered manner. Subdued since listing but gave a spectacular quarterly result, a reflection of capital markets. Not for 1-3 months.

kutumba.note: Attractive at this level; start buying with fresh money, good returns in 1-2 years.
kutumba.detail: Looks attractive at this level; start buying from here, good returns likely in the next one to two years. (Told caller to hold Bajaj Finance & L&T Finance rather than shift, but buy SBI with fresh money.)

kutumba.note: No big result expectation (flat/slight uptick); GLP-1 angle. Buy after results.
kutumba.detail: No big expectations for tomorrow's result (flat/slight uptick, no big surprise); Citi report on GLP-1 obesity products (18 MT capacity) is the angle. Buy after the result.

kutumba.detail: Looks good on every decline; add to it. Part of a good list (with Tata Power, M&M, Tata Steel, NSDL, CDSL) the caller should add to rather than buy new names.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 21 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/4-9QKYGSX5U

## Global backdrop
- **Gap-up open expected** (GIFT Nifty +120–130). US closed strong, Asia positive. Main driver: **crude fell ~5% overnight to ~$105–106 (Brent $105.96)** — an "oil-relief rally." Trump says Iran peace talks are in their "final stage" (credibility doubtful).
- **US 10-yr yield cooled from 4.67% to 4.57%** (positive for equities).
- **Nvidia blowout Q1:** revenue $81.62bn (est. $78.86bn), EPS $1.87 (est. $1.76), dividend raised 1c→25c, buyback raised $60bn→$80bn, Q2 guidance $91bn (est. $87bn) — despite no China exports ("handed China to Huawei"). Stock dipped after-hours on "not high enough" guidance. CEO: "compute capacity is revenue/profit; Nvidia is the platform of this era."
- **Bezos:** future data centres on the Moon, solar-powered. **SpaceX filed IPO papers** (potentially the largest ever); plans a space data centre by 2028. Long lament that India is "far behind" on tech/AI/innovation vs Korea, Taiwan.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday choppy but **good second-half recovery; Nifty closed above 23,650 (+40)**. RBI announced a **$5bn dollar–rupee swap auction for 26 May**, checking the rupee (touched 96.96, closed ~96.86). **FII selling eased (~₹1,600 cr)**.
- Nifty resistance ~23,800–900; action concentrated in **broader/mid-small caps** (advance–decline came to ~1:1 at close yesterday, 4:1 at today's open). Nifty IT closed above 20-DMA.
- **Strong results** flagged: Apollo Hospitals, Samvardhana Motherson, Grasim, IGI, Lenskart, MedPlus, Metro Brands, Cosmo First, Honeywell Automation, Sansera, JNK. Today's results: ITC, Max Healthcare, Aurobindo, GAIL, Nykaa. Whirlpool disappointed; Ola Electric, Jubilant FoodWorks negative reactions.
- **Power/energy theme hot** (record 260 GW demand): GE Vernova, Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy, JSW Energy active.
- **SEBI considering allowing salary to be paid in mutual-fund units** — called a "radical move."
- **Melody/Parle frenzy:** Italian PM Meloni news led retail to search "Melody chocolate share price" and rush into unrelated penny stock "Parle Industries" (upper circuit) — a cautionary tale (cf. Reliance Chemotech in 2007, Tata Motors DVR confusion).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna (Kranti)** (technicals) — market oscillating in a 500–600 pt range (23,400 support / 23,800 resistance); needs to clear **24,000** to move freely; "run your profits, book on rallies"; Bank Nifty still below all MAs but relatively better. Intraday short suggested near 23,859 (SL), target ~100 pts; entry ~23,797–800.
- Market opened strongly positive (Nifty +170, Sensex +530, Bank Nifty +480), only TCS and ONGC red in Nifty; gains faded to +110 by close of segment, action in mid/small caps.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Varun Beverages (VBL), Natco Pharma, SMS Pharma), 3 sells (Birlasoft, Yatra Online, Standard Glass Lining)

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 20 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/0Yr-XseHAiY

## Global backdrop
- **Crude near $110–112/bbl**, driven by the unresolved **West Asian / Iran crisis**. Mixed signals: Trump and VP **JD Vance** talk of "fruitful" talks, but Iran isn't committing; **NATO** now says it may escort ships through the **Strait of Hormuz** by **early July** if it isn't reopened.
- **US debt at $40 trillion** — roughly equal to the rest of the world's combined. **Bond yields surging**: 5-yr **~4.32%**, 10-yr **4.66%** (highest since Jan 2025), 30-yr **~5.18%** (a **22-year high**, back to 2007 GFC levels). A move to 6% would be disastrous for global equities (seen as low-probability).
- **FPIs exiting most emerging markets** (Korea, Japan, Taiwan) on rising US yields; **not selling China** (AI/semiconductor exposure). Anchor frames rising yields + strong dollar + weak EM currencies as the worst combination for economies like India.
- **SpaceX IPO** pegged as the **largest in history** — targeting **$75bn raise at a $1.75tn valuation** (vs Saudi Aramco's $29bn at $1.7tn in 2019); **Goldman Sachs** lead banker. **Nvidia earnings** due tonight (No. 1 in the Magnificent Seven).

## Indian markets
- **Nifty range 23,300–23,800**; a break of **23,300** opens the door to **22,800 → 22,500 → potentially 22,000**. Pre-open weak (Nifty **−170**, later **−194**; Sensex **−530/−600**; Bank Nifty **−500, >1%**).
- **Rupee at ~96.9–97/USD**, signs of drifting to 97–98 and possibly 100; pressures the **current account deficit**.
- **Breadth weak at open** (584 adv / 973 dec, ~**1:3**), after ~2:1 positive the prior day. **FIIs sold Rs 2,450 cr**, **DIIs bought Rs 3,800 cr** the previous session; DIIs not yet in big-wave selling, retail SIPs still supporting mid/small-caps (Mid-cap index near ATH, +0.9% prior day; Small-cap +1.17%).
- **Q4 earnings** mostly good. Positives: **BEL, Mankind Pharma, Karnataka Bank (+5%), Dredging Corp, ASK Auto, Godavari Power (+2%), Prince Pipes, Hindalco**, and local **Vyash/VR Scientific** (PAT and EPS up; amalgamation/exceptional items noted). Negatives: **PI Industries (−7%), PTC India, BPCL**. **Karnataka Bank +5%, PI −7%** on result reactions.
- **Sector view (anchor):** favour **pharma** (many near 52-wk highs — Laurus Labs, Sun Pharma, Zydus), **capital-market theme**, **mid-cap IT**; be careful with **rate-sensitives** (banks, autos, NBFCs, real estate). Themes flagged: **electric buses/trucks** (Keto Motors listing via reverse merger; ~Rs 10,000 cr govt incentive scheme), **AI / HVDC / EMS** (e.g. Avalon Technologies, broker target 1,775).
- **Oil refiners** still loss-making (BPCL chairman: Rs 10–14 losses; breakeven needs ~$85/bbl crude).

## Analysts featured
- **Vasanth** — anchor; delivered the macro open and moderated.
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental) — sees a possible **global recession / crisis bigger than COVID** once bond yields weaken; rupee to **97–98**; flags **Tata group** weakness (>Rs 10 lakh cr market-cap erosion since Sep '24, governance/trust issues). Trade setup weak; **PCR back to ~1, VIX ~19**. Answered all the **viewer Q&A** stock calls (see `kutumba_rao` file).
- **Kranthi** (fundamental — separate analyst) — on **IT**: large-cap IT PEs **below 20**, so some **bottom-fishing / contrarian, staggered buying** by HNIs/institutions on AI "feelers"; but **avoid IT until clarity** (notes Standard Chartered cutting 8,000 jobs to AI). *(His calls are not Kutumba Rao's.)*
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — narrow-range consolidation; **resistance 23,800**, **support 23,400** (series), break → **23,100**; intraday low **23,397**. Weak momentum (RSI <50, MACD negative crossover). Trade cautiously with strict stop-loss/hedge.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Ujjivan Small Finance Bank, Canara Bank, State Bank of India), 3 sells (Asian Hotels (North), Morepen Laboratories, Titan Company)

kutumba.note: Earlier 'dark horse'; hold long-term
kutumba.detail: He had flagged it earlier as a dark horse and it performed reasonably well. Even in a volatile market it held above 40. Presently you can hold; it was a long-term pick.

kutumba.note: Good bank-space stock; add carefully, no hurry
kutumba.detail: He said you can add but there is no hurry since the market is weak and may fall further. It is a good stock in the banking space; watch the entry point carefully.

kutumba.note: Good stock; add carefully, no hurry
kutumba.detail: He said all three of the caller's stocks are good and you can add, but there is no hurry given market weakness. Buy but watch the entry point carefully.

kutumba.note: Start slowly near 900, no rush
kutumba.detail: Definitely start slowly but with no rush. The broader market will be weak and bank stocks usually suffer in higher inflation. After it comes close to 900, start accumulating SBI slowly.

kutumba.note: Hold if already bought; no fresh buy now
kutumba.detail: If already bought, hold. But fresh buying immediately is not necessary.

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; Delhi-centric, litigation
kutumba.detail: Exit on rallies. It is not a pan-India hotel chain, only Delhi-centric (Asian Hotels North). It already ran to about 400 and is forming lower tops and lower bottoms, with news-driven activity and a litigation on the hotel's shopping arcade. Sell around 320 to 330; you should exit this counter.

kutumba.detail: Hold; all power companies irrespective of short-term performance he says hold. Decent Gujarat-based large group. It corrected modestly from around 1,800 to the support level near 1,450 on a weak result, but the correction is likely short and it could jump again. Don't sell at this level.

kutumba.detail: Caller bought near the peak (~2,000) after a ~1,000 rise in a month. Hold with a stop-loss around 1,500; if the stock rebounds, exit close to his price. It is a good stock without doubt but very richly valued at about PE 90.

kutumba.detail: Fancy could come and many treat it as a pure-AI company. He said it would be a good bet only below 900.

kutumba.detail: If you ask him, just hold; no fresh buying and exit on rallies. It stood strong and some buying comes in the counter at lower levels. If you have it, hold.

kutumba.note: Infra space could rebound; hold
kutumba.detail: Overall the infra space could see a rebound, there is a possibility, so he said hold.

kutumba.note: Very weak; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: Morepen is very weak. He said exit on rallies.

kutumba.note: Exit; jewelry best-of-times over, can fall
kutumba.detail: He thinks jewelry companies have seen the best of times. Near a recession, even if margins are protected, sales improvement may not come. If you ask him, exit, because the stock can go down to 3,600 to 3,700 in the next one to two months.

kutumba.note: Avoid; near 52-wk low, margins weak
kutumba.detail: Good debt-free result but top line grew while bottom line and margins did not, so the stock did not rise and is near its 52-week low. He would not invest; the fintech field is competitive and unless they upgrade services the stock is hard to sustain. If already bought put a 200 stop-loss, else exit on rallies at 240 to 250.

kutumba.note: Hold if held; no fresh buy, fully priced
kutumba.detail: One of the larger, near-monopoly players in wheels, but now fully priced and the overall market is dull. If you have bought the stock, hold; fresh buying, no.

kutumba.note: Operated counter, PE ~100; would not buy
kutumba.detail: More of an operated/event-driven counter than a fundamental one; one should only hold if you follow the news regularly. It was once a penny stock and now trades close to 200 at about PE 100. He would not buy.

kutumba.note: Branded, low-cost maker; hold
kutumba.detail: A company with branding and good prospects, one of the lowest-cost cable manufacturers, though the industry is a bit dull for now. He says hold.

kutumba.note: Weak numbers; avoid amid volatile crude
kutumba.detail: Numbers came yesterday and were weak. For now, avoid. With volatile crude prices it is doubtful HPCL can perform.

kutumba.note: Margins down, spreads to fall; near 52-wk low
kutumba.detail: Margins fell a lot and a regulator order will compress their spreads further from the coming year. Despite higher volumes the stock trades near its 52-week low. He would avoid.

kutumba.note: Wait and watch; treasury-loss risk on yields
kutumba.detail: As of now, wait and watch. As yields rise, treasury losses for banks could also rise going forward.

kutumba.note: Short-term weak but can rebound; hold
kutumba.detail: Hold. Only because of short-term concerns there is a bit of weakness, but it can rebound. On the rebound, when it comes close to the caller's price, exit.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 19 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/SOI0mRnZij8

## Global backdrop
- **Iran:** Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the UAE asked the US to hold off; Trump halted planned strikes "from today" — another back-and-forth. Iran put an updated peace proposal to the US and is negotiating tough; it set up a "Persian Gulf State Authority" to keep control of the Strait of Hormuz.
- **US markets** closed mixed — memory-chip stocks sold off, Dow positive. Asian markets mixed. **Crude below $110** (had hit ~$112 on the halt-strikes news); India raised diesel +91 paise, petrol +87 paise.
- US gave a **third extension of the Russian-oil waiver**; India firmly reaffirmed it will keep buying Russian oil for energy security.
- Conditions remain unstable; markets very volatile.

## Indian markets
- Sharp intraday recovery: **Nifty +~300, Sensex +~1,000**, closed green, **led by IT** (Coforge, Persistent, then Tech Mahindra, Infosys, Wipro) plus Bharti Airtel and the Bajaj twins.
- FIIs net +Rs 2,682 cr, DIIs +Rs 2,814 cr — but largely block deals (GQG took 1.5 cr JSW Steel shares at Rs 1,260 ≈ Rs 1,900 cr; SBI MF 1 cr shares at 1,260), so underlying buying was thin.
- **Small-caps the worry:** advance/decline 785 vs 2,355; small-cap index −1.26% (−1.95% MTD) after a 30–50% run over ~45 days — profit-booking.
- **Adani group in the limelight:** US court threw out the fraud allegations (clean chit); Adani to pay ~$2.5m penalty for Iranian LPG shipments — big relief; group stocks opened up.
- Today is **Nifty weekly options expiry / settlement day** → volatility expected. Market opened positive (Nifty +70, Sensex +~300, Bank Nifty +110).
- **Result season:** >50–55% of results comfortable. Good: GE Vernova, Samhi, JK Paper, Puravankara (+14%, revenue-recognition change), Indian Oil, Visaka, Garuda Construction, Apollo Micro (+4%, but watch negative operating cash flow), Astral, Sheshasayee Tech. Poor: Afcons Infra (−5–6%). Due today: BEL, BPCL, Mankind Pharma, Zydus Life, PI Industries.
- Notable macro: a year ago 3 Indian firms were in the world top-100 by market cap (RIL 57, HDFC 73, TCS 84); now none (RIL 106, HDFC 190, TCS 314). Many IT stocks near 2008 sub-prime-crisis levels.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Headline view: stay invested, be stock-specific, avoid cats-and-dogs, keep exiting weaker stocks; banks/NBFCs/autos attractive for a 2–3 year view.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — markets bouncing back inside the Bollinger Bands; Nifty reversed at the 50% retracement near 23,300, target 24,000 (20-DMA); 23,400 immediate support; expects to stay above 24,000 into monthly settlement. RSI ~45 (needs to cross 50). VIX +4.47% to ~19.63; PCR 1.24. Triple-whammy macro risk (bond yields up, crude up, rupee weak at 96.36) → a Nifty 23,000 retest is highest-probability if it persists 2–3 weeks. Suggested index longs with stop near 23,650.
- Side note: an extended on-air discussion about IT firms hiring "forward-deployed engineers" (Palantir/Coforge) and the anchors/analysts learning to use AI tools (Claude, Anthropic Academy courses, Gemini, Perplexity).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 8 buys (Vimta Labs, Finotex Chemicals, Jupiter Wagons, Mahindra & Mahindra, Blue Star, MTAR Technologies, Netweb Technologies, Garden Reach Shipbuilders (GRSE))
Kranthi: 3 buys (Blue Star, MTAR Technologies, Netweb Technologies)

kutumba.note: Buy immediately, accumulate slowly; serious 2-year wait, don't panic on interim corrections.
kutumba.detail: Buy immediately / accumulate slowly from here; needs a serious 2-year view, don't panic when corrections come in between.

kutumba.note: Decent March results; ran 24-25 to 32 in 5 days, wait for a correction then buy a little; fully paid-up.
kutumba.detail: Decent March results, ran from 24-25 to 32 in five days. Wait for a correction (~30) then buy a little. Fully paid-up stock, don't dismiss as cheap because it's Rs 30. Worth investing.

kutumba.note: Asked alongside Vimta/Finotex for 2-3 year view; covered under accumulate answer, no negative flagged.
kutumba.detail: Asked for a 2-3 year view alongside Vimta Labs and Finotex; covered under the general accumulate answer, no separate negative raised.

kutumba.note: Weak results, rising luggage competition, near 52-wk low; needs reinvention to regain share.
kutumba.detail: Weak results, competition rising in luggage with new brands pulling share, stock near 52-week low. Difficult to regain lost market share unless it reinvents/rebrands. I would avoid it.

kutumba.note: Buy immediately, at least a 1-year view.
kutumba.detail: M&M - buy immediately, at least with a one-year view.

kutumba.note: Good last-quarter and full-year results; data-centre/new segments opening up. Add slowly on dips.
kutumba.detail: Add slowly on dips. Good last quarter and full-year results. Now caters to data centres / new segments beyond just retail and corporate; markets opening up.

kutumba.note: Hold as dark horse with stop-loss; Rs 1L cr funding + SBI package, Birla back in mgmt, but Bharti/Jio pressure.
kutumba.detail: Hold (bought at 1.50). Rs 1 lakh crore funding plan over 3 years + Rs 35,000 cr SBI-led package; Kumar Mangalam Birla back in management is positive. But Bharti/Jio competition (Jio IPO ahead) means tough times. Treat as a dark horse with a stop-loss.

kutumba.note: Short-term impact only; long term much better; near 52-wk low, negatives discounted. Hold; accumulate ~1,750.
kutumba.detail: Short-term impact on market share/product range; long term company will do much better. Already near 52-week low, most negatives discounted. Hold if owned; if buying, start accumulating close up to 1,750 (figure as spoken).

kutumba.detail: Avoid. Stock shows some momentum on the government betting policy/guidelines but the initial euphoria/exuberance has fizzled. Only for those with high risk-appetite and capacity; such business-model stocks not suitable for everyone.

kutumba.note: Hold for 3 years; the safer choice vs Edelweiss.
kutumba.detail: Definitely hold (3-year view). The safer one between this and Edelweiss.

kutumba.detail: Good company, no doubt; Rashesh Shah a veteran. But gets treated on-and-off as a speculative contract. Poonawalla is the safer choice.

kutumba.note: Stay invested; slight recovery path, sustains above 475; no clear rationale to switch to Kaynes.
kutumba.detail: Stay invested with Newgen. In a slight recovery path; recently went 500-plus and stays above 475 even after correcting. Doesn't understand the rationale for switching from Newgen to Kaynes.

kutumba.note: Top pick of the four 'Technologies'; at a temporary peak, buy only on corrections.
kutumba.detail: Number one choice among Nazara/Kaynes/Netweb/MTAR. Don't buy at current levels - reached a temporary peak. Buy after a correction; best on dips.

kutumba.note: Second choice; accumulate on every dip along with MTAR.
kutumba.detail: Second choice after MTAR. Accumulate nicely on every dip, along with MTAR.

kutumba.note: Decent March-quarter and full-year results; add slowly on declines around 2,500, Rs 100 below, staggered.
kutumba.detail: Add slowly on declines in the 2,500 range. Decent March quarter and full-year results. Around 2,500 and Rs 100 below, in a staggered manner on dips.

kutumba.note: A bit weakish currently but hold.
kutumba.detail: Currently a bit weakish, but still hold.

kutumba.note: Fell ~50% from high; fertiliser imports tough; better only if raw-material sourcing right, else likely stays weak.
kutumba.detail: Fell by half from its high. Fertiliser imports getting tough. Would perform better only if raw-material sourcing is correct; otherwise likely stays weak. Phosphate is largely imported, processed here and sold.

kutumba.note: Doubts for a 2-3 month bet; profit more than halved; needs deeper study, don't buy just because it fell.
kutumba.detail: Has his own doubts for a 2-3 month bet. Fell 20%; profit dropped more than half (top line held). Needs deeper study whether one-off or sustained. Don't buy just because it fell; could fall further.

kranti.note: Add slowly on dips; good last-quarter and full-year results; new demand from data centres beyond retail/corporate.
Top pick among four 'Technologies' stocks; accumulate nicely on every dip.
Second pick among the four; accumulate nicely on every dip.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 18 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5o6ZlW1Xdh4

## Global backdrop
- **US markets closed lower Friday**: Dow ~**1%**, S&P **1.24%**, Nasdaq ~**0.5%**. Asian cues negative.
- **Geopolitics**: West Asia **US–Iran war** uncertainty persists; a fire at the outer perimeter of a **UAE nuclear reactor** over the weekend undermined the ceasefire — more negative developments than positive.
- **Crude oil** back to ~**USD 108–110/barrel** (Kranthi flags a move toward **USD 110–112**) — the single most-watched driver; bad for large importer India.
- **Rupee** depreciating sharply (**USD-INR ~96**), widening the current account deficit.

## Indian markets
- **Friday close**: Nifty **-46 pts at 23,644**; Sensex **-160 pts at ~75,237**. Nifty Midcap **-0.45%**, Smallcap **-0.61%**. India VIX up ~**1%**.
- **Weekly**: Nifty **-2.2%**, Sensex **~-2.6%**, **Bank Nifty -2.89%** (underperformer, bearish daily candle).
- **GIFT Nifty** ~**-200 pts**; negative opening expected (and confirmed — Nifty opened down ~**240 pts to 23,400** during the show).
- **FII selling** heavy: ~**USD 3bn** over two weeks; ≥**USD 1bn/week** through April — mostly Nifty 50 names, hence index drag.
- Friday gainers: Auto, M&M, Dr Reddy's, Infosys, Tech Mahindra, Coal India (+1.5% to >5%). Losers: Hindalco, Nestle, Tata Steel, Eternal, Reliance (-1.5% to -3%).

## Options / technicals
- **PCR fallen to ~1** (from 1.17); VIX rose to ~19 — bull confidence fading.
- **Resistance 24,000–24,100** (highest call OI now at 24,000, dragged down from 24,500/24,800); **support 23,000–23,100** (highest put OI at 23,000). Critical Nifty level **23,400/23,300** — break opens 23,000 → 22,600.
- Possible **double-bottom toward 22,200**; April gap at **23,200**. RSI ~45, MACD below zero/reference line — range-bound to negative bias.

## Results & stock-specifics
- **Tata Steel** weekend result: profit ~**+150%**, but stock **-4%** post-results. **ITC Hotels** steady; **Power Grid** flat; **SAIL** strong (all steel cos good). **Godfrey Phillips** profit ~**+90%** (book profits after recent run). **Gland Pharma +12%** (3x volume). **ICICI Prudential +8%**, **Amber +6%**.
- Today's results to watch: **Indian Oil/IOC** (refining margins hit by high crude), **Strides Pharma, JSW Cement, Apollo Micro, Ola Electric**.
- Nifty reshuffle (~September): **BSE in, Wipro likely out**. Banned list: **Kaynes, SAIL**.
- Watch: **Solara Active, Nazara, Shadowfax** (+15% post-listing). **Coal India** — accumulate at lower levels (coal gasification). **Aurobindo Pharma** active near 52-wk high (biosimilar permissions).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamentals + viewer Q&A) — see dedicated note; gave own calls on IRFC, R Power, Kalyan Jewellers, KPI Green, Salzer, Capstone, Belrise + MFs, Kitex, Suzlon, Vedanta, Brigade, Warree Renewables, Anantraj, Piramal Pharma, Ipca.
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst) — stock-pickers' market; calls on Network Tech, SML Isuzu, Avio (avoid), Tilak Nagar/Tejas, Indo Farm/Sika, Avantel, Inox Wind/Adani Power, A Polymer, IT-to-pharma switch.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — range-bound weakness, stick to quality; calls on Danish Power, HAL, Bank of India/EPL/NHPC, Kirloskar Oil Engines, KP Energy vs Waaree Energies, ITC Hotels.

## Overall advice
- Stay in fundamentally sound / time-tested stocks; exit weaker names on rallies. Reduce positions on any bounce toward 23,500. Avoid freshly-listed/new stocks. Follow results closely — good results rewarded, bad results punished severely.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 15 calls (IRFC, Reliance Power, Kalyan Jewellers, KPI Green Energy, Salzer Electronics, Kitex Garments, Capstone, Belrise Industries, Suzlon Energy, Vedanta, Brigade Enterprises, Waaree Renewable Technologies, Anantraj, Piramal Pharma, Ipca Laboratories)

kutumba.note: Hold temporarily; weak now but stable March results and dividend
kutumba.detail: Weak as of now but the March results were stable and there is a dividend. No need to sell in a hurry in this weakness. If you must sell, sell ex-dividend.

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; weak
kutumba.detail: Weak as of now. Exit on rallies.

kutumba.note: Exit on a technical rally near 400 / 400-plus
kutumba.detail: Weak in the short-medium term; jewellery stocks got over-played and face government pressure, with lower sales likely pressuring profitability despite protected margins. On a technical rally closer to 400 or 400-plus, exit. The stock is testing a 52-week low; a couple of days' close below 350 could open a move below 300.

kutumba.note: Hold; decent March results, can recover to 480-500
kutumba.detail: Temporarily weak but decent enough March results. If the market gets some stability, KPI Green should improve again with clear signs of moving closer to 480-500. No need to panic.

kutumba.note: Exit/reduce on rallies; peak reached, flat result
kutumba.detail: The stock has reached a peak; it touched over 1,100 and is forming lower tops and lower bottoms, and the result was flat. It already ran from ~500 to 700-plus recently, so it is better to reduce the position. Exit on rallies.

kutumba.detail: A good Kerala-based company (with a Warangal plant) but textiles are weak since US tariffs began, and it is already a heavyweight/expensive, stable-results name. The stock has likely bottomed, but a rally from here depends on cotton prices. Exit on rallies at 180 for a re-entry at lower levels.

kutumba.detail: Stabilizing/slightly declining only because of impending results (date thought to be around the 24th). The new subsidiary Capstone Home Services Pvt Ltd's impact will not be felt immediately. Hold until results; no need to be hasty.

kutumba.note: Hold; hyper-speculative activity, protect profits
kutumba.detail: There is hyper-speculative activity and huge continuous volumes in the stock, which has to be understood. Hold as of now; if bought at lower levels, keep it as a trading stock to protect profits.

kutumba.note: Hold without doubt; stabilizing above 50
kutumba.detail: Interest will return to it as the range opens up. The stock is now stabilizing above 50, in the 54-55 range. Hold, without any doubt.

kutumba.note: Buy only on dips below 300 / in 260-280; now a dividend play
kutumba.detail: Recently demerged and now trading standalone. Treat it largely as a dividend play, since all the important units have been demerged. If at all you want to buy, buy below 300, or in the 260-280 band closer to it.

kutumba.note: Hold; reduce on a technical rally near 800
kutumba.detail: Realty stocks are weak now, but Brigade has the advantage of regular lease income apart from construction income. The stock is at low levels, so no need to sell here. On any technical rally closer to 800, reduce the position. Hold as of now.

kutumba.note: Exit on any rally to 1,000-plus; continuous weakness
kutumba.detail: The stock has continuously fallen and displayed weakness even after results. It failed to cross the previous high of 1,125 (and the ~1,300 high), and fizzled out near 1,100. It is already at 925 with 1,000 breaking; on any rally to 1,000-plus, definitely exit. It could stay flat for a few days.

kutumba.note: Hold; good fundamentals but won't outperform now
kutumba.detail: A good stock with good fundamentals, but not one that can outperform the market at this time. It has the financial strength to perform over a period. Hold, but do not panic-sell in weakness.

kutumba.note: Hold; good fundamentals but won't outperform now
kutumba.detail: A good stock with good fundamentals, but not one that can outperform the market at this time. It has the financial strength to perform over a period. Hold, but do not panic-sell in weakness.

kutumba.note: Wait for results (~29th this month) before buying
kutumba.detail: One of the good stocks, trading near a 52-week high, though expensive and not a high-volume name; it continues with stable results. Results are nearby (date ~29th this month), so look after results before buying.
