# Summary — Business Breakfast, 13 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/lJQfVkH5d_g

## Global backdrop
- **US markets** closed lower last night; recovered somewhat from the lows. **Gift Nifty** signaled a slightly positive open.
- Core driver of the turmoil: **crude oil** disruption at the **Strait of Hormuz**. Travel through it is shifting from "free" to "paid," pushing logistics/transport costs up. Kutumba Rao thinks **80–90 dollars/barrel** could be the "new normal" for crude even if the war stops (old Rs 50–60 won't reset).
- **LNG cost** flagged as the next danger — roughly **70 dollars per MWh**; a sharp rise would hit LNG-using industries.
- **Trump visiting China today** — watched as a potential catalyst; any trade deal / tariff announcement could give global markets some relief.
- "AI fear" (intangible) plus tangible-asset monopolies (crude) seen as a double squeeze on countries like India with little presence in either.

## Indian markets
- **Yesterday: Rs 12 lakh crore (~115 billion dollars)** of wealth wiped out; market down ~**2%**.
- Breadth brutal: **435 advances vs ~2,700 declines (1:6)**. Nifty closed below **23,300** and below the **10/20/50/200-DMA** — described as the **steepest fall this financial year**.
- **Mid- and small-caps fell more than benchmarks** — complacency unwinding after extreme outperformance (15 pages of 52-week highs, none of lows earlier).
- **Inflation**: March **3.4%**, April **~3.48%/3.08%** — still "under control," helped by petrol/diesel prices not being raised; gold/silver and food added pressure.
- **Sectors**: Nifty Bank, Nifty IT, Reliance, gold & jewellery, consumption basket all hit; **only pharma** defensive. **Nifty IT index in "three-year" zone**; OpenAI (now offering enterprise software services, tied up with **Infosys** while also becoming a competitor) re-spooking IT.
- IT companies returned **Rs 1.3 trillion (Rs 1.30 lakh crore)** to investors via buybacks last FY despite AI headwinds.

## Policy / events
- **Gold & silver import duty raised to 15%** (from 6%) — ~9% duty + 1% cess. Gold up ~**Rs 12 lakh/kg** (~Rs 12,000/tola), silver up ~**Rs 22,000/kg**.
- **PM (Sunday, Hyderabad)** called for austerity: cut energy use, use public transport, postpone gold buying for a year, defer foreign travel/destination weddings — advisory, not bans.
- **Fuel price hikes** likely soon (oil marketing companies losing crores/day).
- **MSCI Standard Index** rejig: out — Hyundai, RVNL, Jubilant Foodworks, Kalyan Jewellers; in — Federal Bank, MCX(?), Indian Bank.
- Kutumba Rao expects a **gold policy** from the government — notes PSU banks' ~Rs 1.9 lakh crore profit leans heavily on a ~Rs 13 lakh crore gold-loan book (~Rs 35–40k crore profit), with the interest burden falling on semi-urban/rural borrowers.

## Today's open
- Indices opened **positive**; mid/small-caps positive too. Advance-decline positive (**1,450:750** BSE; **1,619:613** NSE).
- **Texmaco Rail +11–12%** (order win), **Berger Paints +8%**, **Dixon Technologies** positive (hammered prior day). **Tata Power ~-5%**, **Cohance Lifesciences -6%**.
- **Gold ETF opened up to +6%**, MCX strong; **jewellery stocks** (Kalyan, Titan, Senco Gold) selling off.
- **Bank Nifty** slipped into the minus intraday — flagged as a caution.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (joined by phone; **not on screen** — Vasanth on screen): broad market call + viewer Q&A (Infosys, Dixon, SIP/retail strategy). Levels: 23,500 cut is "very negative"; risk of slipping into **22,600–23,500** band; another **1,000–2,000 Nifty points** down possible if monsoon disappoints (El Nino). Mode flipped from "buy on declines" to **sell on rallies** (esp. near 24,000). Says stay cautious, trail stop-losses, only token buys in value stocks.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals): next support **23,100**; broke 23,800 & 23,500; sell-on-rise; MACD negative crossover, RSI below 40; 23,500 calls / 23,000 puts highest OI. Small bounces possible in beaten-down mid/small-caps.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst — separate from Kutumba Rao): answered **Sula Vineyards** (hold at this range / exit on rallies — lifestyle stock).
- **Vasanth** (anchor/analyst).
- **Guest interview**: **Valluripalli Prabhu Kishore** (Varun Group), on his book "The Vinnerian" — discipline/consistency, invest more in downturns, no public-issue plans (family-owned, no fund need, clean 50-year profit/regulatory record).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 sell (Infosys)

kutumba.note: Exit IT; sell Infosys on a rally and diversify
kutumba.detail: He repeated his long-standing call to come out of IT stocks and to sell Infosys on a rally. The viewer has held since 2004 (~300 shares) through a prolonged period and can't catch the sector shift in time, so he advised lightening the position on rallies and widening/diversifying the portfolio. IT won't behave as before unless companies reinvent their business models; he preferred redeploying into a little Adani Group (e.g. Adani Ports), a couple of power stocks, and some new-generation stocks.

kutumba.note: Weak result already discounted; hold above 10000
kutumba.detail: He noted the stock rose after a weak result because the result was discounted in advance; a sharp net-profit miss would normally drop a stock 8-10%, but it instead recovered with ~1% gains, so the market is discounting the future. He said hold as long as it holds the 10,000 mark, with potential to rally to 11,400-11,500, where he would exit.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 12 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/phea-bjR6vc

## Global backdrop
- **GIFT Nifty** pointing to a weak open of roughly **180 points**; US markets fell overnight.
- **US-Iran conflict** in a "no peace, no war" stalemate. US officials (e.g. **Kevin Hassett**) making provocative comments — "Iran's economy has almost collapsed, no hurry to do a deal."
- **Brent crude settled above USD 105**, heading back toward USD 100/bbl — a clear negative for emerging markets.

## Indian markets
- **Weekly options expiry** today (Tuesday).
- Big domestic overhang from the **PM's Sunday Hyderabad speech** urging **austerity measures**; sparked fears of being "on the edge of an economic crisis." Industry circles pledged support (work-from-home, restricting non-essential travel).
- Govt reassurance: **60 days of crude/natural gas reserves**, **45 days LPG rolling stock** — no need to rush petrol pumps.
- **Rupee** touched an all-time low, closing near **95.31/USD**.
- **FIIs sold Rs 8,438 cr** (~USD 1 bn) in cash, second straight day; **DIIs bought Rs 5,940 cr**.
- Mutual fund equity inflows resilient: **Rs 42,700 cr in April** (vs Rs 42,800 cr March), absorbing FII selling; retail confidence intact.
- **Last 1 month:** Nifty **-1%**, Bank Nifty **-2.5%**, but **Mid-cap +5.9%**, **Small-cap +10%** — broader market clearly outperforming as inflows rotate into mid/small caps.
- **Groww**: Rs 4,750 cr block deal (pre-IPO/QIP sellers, Billion Brains) at Rs 177/share vs Rs 193 close — steeper discount; expected to be absorbed given MF cash positions (some schemes 15-20% cash).

## Results season
- Good prints noted: **Fractal Analytics, Shyam Metalics, Shriram Pistons** (revenue up sharply, EBIT margin hit by acquisition adjustment), **Mold-Tek Packaging, JTL Industries, Privi Speciality Chemicals, Satin Creditcare** (profit +200%), **Manorama Industries, Indian Hotels, Anant Raj, Sonata Software** (PAT Rs 425cr → Rs 464cr).
- Watch today: **Dr Reddy's, Dixon Technologies** (both could be weak), **Tata Power, Max Financial, MTR**. SBI disappointed over the weekend.

## Technicals (Ramakrishna)
- **Nifty:** support **23,800** at risk; next crucial support **23,500**, then gap-fill to **23,100**. RSI 46, MACD negative crossover, first red histogram bar since early April.
- **Bank Nifty:** big down candles below all moving averages; at ~53,439, next **52,800** over a few sessions. RSI 43.74, MACD below signal/zero line.
- Expiry: Nifty **24,000 call** & **23,600 put** highest writing; unusual ~**400-pt** range (vs usual 100). Bank Nifty highest call writing shifted from 57,000 → **55,000** (rising pressure); **54,500 put** support fragile. **PCR 0.76** (weak, may go <0.7). **VIX +10% to 18.55** — panic risk, hurts option sellers. Advance/declines ~3:1 negative.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — fundamental/markets + viewer-stock-relevant calls (Biocon, MTR, Satin Creditcare, Laurus, Sai Parenterals; EV theme via Olectra/JBM Auto). Range view 23,600-24,500; stay invested in quality, no aggressive buying.
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst (levels, OI, momentum above).
- **Kranthi** — fundamental analyst; "buy on dip, sell on rally" below 24,000, stock-pickers' market, accumulate quality on the down-trend.
- **Vasanth** — anchor (also discussed semiconductor/EV themes, MosChip, Micron forward PE ~8, overseas exposure via MFs).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 4 buys (Biocon, Satin Creditcare, Laurus Labs, Sai Parenterals)

kutumba.note: Buy on any dip into 360-380; succession set up, returns to old glory over time
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said to watch Biocon and buy it on any fall, whenever the stock comes into the Rs 360-380 band. He reasoned that the promoter has set the stage for succession and will clearly nominate a successor, so over a period Biocon will return to its old glory.

kutumba.note: His Dec investment idea; results today key to justify the sharp rally
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said he has more interest in MTR, which rose very sharply recently, because they have been promoting it as an investment idea since December. He said its results today are key to see whether the rally was justified, so he flagged it to watch around the result.

kutumba.note: Terrific result, profit up over 200%, pan-India presence; might jump
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao noted Satin Creditcare, which he had mentioned earlier, delivered a terrific result with profit jumping over 200% and a pan-India presence. He said the stock might jump, framing it as part of staying invested in fundamentally sound, cash-rich stocks.

kutumba.note: His first choice for CDMO / overseas-exposure theme
kutumba.detail: When discussing CDMO and overseas-exposure ideas, Kutumba Rao said his first choice would be Laurus. He presented it ahead of his second choice (Sai Parenterals) as a preferred play on the CDMO story.

kutumba.note: Second choice CDMO play; recently listed, add on dips
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao named Sai Parenterals, a recently listed company, as his second choice, citing a good CDMO story. He advised putting it on the watch list and adding to it whenever dips come.

kutumba.note: EV/energy-transition theme; possible sustained mid-term rally
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao flagged that companies like JBM Auto and Olectra may have a sustained mid-term rally because of the emphasis the PM placed on the EV space. He said if the crude-oil crisis continues, demand will lean toward EV vehicles and EV public-transport buses, making it a theme to follow carefully.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 11 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/N2JHF4W4hY0

## Top theme — PM Modi's forex-saving call
- The show opened with a clip of **PM Modi's Hyderabad speech** urging citizens to save foreign exchange: postpone **gold** purchases for **one year**, cut petrol/diesel use, use **EVs** and car-pooling, revive work-from-home/online meetings.
- Context numbers cited: FY25-26 **import bill ~$135 billion**; crude/petrol/diesel imports **>Rs 10 lakh crore**; gold imports **Rs 6 lakh crore**; foreign tours **Rs 3 lakh crore**; fertilizers **Rs 1.5 lakh crore**.
- If crude holds ~$100-105, India's **current account deficit ≈ 2.5% of GDP**, pressuring forex reserves, inflation and fuel prices.

## Global backdrop
- **Brent crude trading above $105** — the dominant market driver.
- **West Asia / Strait of Hormuz** is the real swing factor for crude; deadlock continues, Iran responded again, Trump called Iran's stance "totally unacceptable."
- **Russia-Ukraine ceasefire** looks shaky despite Putin saying "matter is coming to an end."
- Global tech/AI bubble: **South Korea and Taiwan** led world rallies — **Kospi +78%** YTD; **Intel +~250%**, **SanDisk +~558%** in 2026; India's index is **~9% in the minus**. FIIs see India as unattractive vs other emerging markets (cited Samir Arora video).

## Indian markets
- Expected **gap-down open of ~200 points**; at open Nifty -236, Sensex -860, **Bank Nifty -670 (-1.25%)**. Pre-open: Nifty -200, Sensex -690, Bank Nifty -478.
- **SBI Q4 numbers very weak**, hurting bank sentiment (vs strong ICICI/HDFC earlier).
- **Q4 earnings strong overall (70-75% beat)** but **Q1 expected weaker** due to crude, geopolitics, supply-chain and inflation.
- Negatively-impacted sectors: **aviation** (USA return fares up from <Rs 1L to ~Rs 1.5L), **jewellery** (Titan top loser, Kalyan), **hotels, OMCs, fertilizers**.
- Positively-positioned sectors: **power/energy, renewables/solar, EVs**.
- Advance-decline strongly negative: NSE 674 up vs 1845 down; BSE 958 vs 1840.

## Earnings flagged
- **Good Q4:** MCX (outstanding), Advanced Enzymes, Gujarat Ambuja Exports (+6%), Bank of Baroda, Niva Bupa, Tata Consumer (+3.5%), Oberoi Realty, CreditAccess Grameen, KPI Green, Ujjivan SFB, **Atlanta Electric** (rev +82%, EBIT +118%, PAT +129%), **Rain Industries** (EBITDA +83%, PAT +189%, rev +20%, stock +13-14%). Day movers: Sasken +14%, Quick Heal +10%, CMS Info +5%, Hyundai Motor +4%.
- **Weak Q4:** Swiggy, ABB (-9%, strong order book but weak numbers), Urban Company (-6.5%), SICA (-13%, flattish results).
- Watching today: Canara Bank, UPL, **Fractal Analytics** (pure-AI listing), SJS Enterprises.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — fundamental/markets view, OI levels, and viewer stock Q&A (see kutumba_rao file).
- **Kranti** — separate fundamental analyst; deferred detailed result review to after the 15th; flagged Aeroflex Industries (vertical rally off 150-170 range).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst: range **23,800-24,600**, Nifty lost 50-DMA (held by 20-DMA), RSI ~52 (Bank Nifty ~48), highest OI at 24,200; VIX rising; trade with strict stops.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

## Kutumba Rao — key levels & view
- Stay invested; market driven by crude/geopolitics. **24,000 is the inflection point** — above positive, below negative; sideways range **23,600-24,500**.
- Strong call OI at **24,200-24,500**; VIX below 17; PCR jumped to 1.28 (near peak ~1.4).
- Suggested a **24,000 monthly call** for risk-takers (available at low premium after the sharp fall).
- Gold likely to moderate (stalled US-Iran talks) — mildly positive for stocks.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (NTPC, Olectra Greentech, Siemens Energy)

kutumba.note: Hold; possible surprise move as investment-banker peers jump.
kutumba.detail: Hold definitely; after the recent correction the stock is looking good again. Investment-banker companies are all jumping, so JM could too. Corrected from near 200 and is now stabilising near 140; if it crosses 150 levels a good move could come.

kutumba.note: Coal India subsidiary; only one result so far, definitely hold.
kutumba.detail: This is a recently listed company, a subsidiary related to Coal India. Only one result has come so far, so it must definitely be held.

kutumba.note: Not very positive on agro-chemicals now; monsoon/El Nino risk.
kutumba.detail: Agro-chemical companies with higher raw-material costs will see margins fall; companies with lower raw-material costs will rise. This quarter's performance could be weak because of monsoon and the El Nino effect. So not very positive on agro-chemical companies as of now.

kutumba.note: Top energy pick; ~Rs 1 lakh crore value unlock from subsidiaries in 6 months; slow mover.
kutumba.detail: My energy choice now is NTPC; accumulate it. Two-three subsidiary investments (incl. HURL, alongside Coal India) will see value unlock - maybe about Rs 1 lakh crore over the next six months. It moves slowly, like 2% or 1% (a tortoise), not 10% like other power stocks, but it's one of the best companies in the power sector.

kutumba.note: Preferred EV play; rose well over the past month; buy EV + renewables now.
kutumba.detail: We've been saying for many days, buy Olectra; it rose well over the last one month. There's no other distinct EV stock to buy - if you ask me, combine EV plus renewables and buy now. Adani Power and such have already risen well.

kutumba.note: Some revival after touching bottom; hold for now.
kutumba.detail: For now I'd say hold Trent, because we're seeing some revival in the stock after it touched bottom.

kutumba.note: 100% hold for long term; buy more on dips.
kutumba.detail: 100% hold for the long term, and buy more when dips come. It lags Hitachi Energy / GE Vernova only because companies don't move equally - there are cycles of under- and out-performance; in that segment they are excellent companies.

kutumba.note: Weak, making losses; don't average, wait for turnaround.
kutumba.detail: We're tracking it, but the stock is weak - a chemical-space company that, after re-listing, saw institutional activity rise then fade, and it is now making losses too. Don't average; wait for a turnaround in performance. It's a large company (almost a couple of thousand crores turnover).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 8 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/BoQ6Zygyxhw

## Global backdrop
- **US markets**: Tech stocks performed well overnight, but the other two indices saw negative/loss reactions. Ongoing **rotation between AI/chip-manufacturing stocks and traditional software** — profit booking in chip names, recovery/buying in traditional software.
- **Geopolitics**: Fresh **US-Iran firing in the Persian Gulf**; both sides trading blame. Trump tweeted the ceasefire is still in effect, calling the latest exchange "just a **love tap**." Markets treat it as a small skirmish, not escalation.
- **Crude**: **Brent ~101-102**; dipped to ~98 intraday before ticking back up on tensions. Analysts note a further **~10-dollar fall (toward 80-90)** would be a positive trigger for Indian markets.
- **Nvidia**: Cited as a model of R&D-driven growth — last year's profit ~**25bn USD** projected to **~250bn USD in 2026** (10x), backed by **~75bn USD R&D** spend; contrast drawn with Indian firms that "return money instead of doing R&D."

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty** pointed to a **weak open (~105 pts down)**; **Friday/weekend caution** flagged (no Mon-reaction window). At the open: **Nifty -125, Sensex -400, Bank Nifty -390**, but advance/decline stayed positive (NSE ~1028 adv / 775 dec).
- **Mid/small-cap space remains the action zone.** Mid-cap index hit an **all-time high** despite flat benchmarks; small-cap index near its all-time high. Driven by **retail/HNI** (low FPI presence), so insulated from FPI selling. Repeated advice: be aggressive on **quality mid/small-caps with a 2-3 year view**, enter on dips, avoid FOMO chasing.
- **Beaten-down names rebounding**: **Godrej Industries** top Nifty-500 gainer (+20%, upper circuit); **Tejas Networks +15%** (Arnab Ray re-appointed MD — confidence move despite weak results); Jain Resources and others — mostly **technical rebounds**.
- **Results season**: Strong Q4 from **BSE, Pidilite, Usha Martin, Thermax, Thyrocare, Vijaya Diagnostics, Globus Spirits, Gravita, Kennametal**. "Sell-on-news" caution where stocks ran up pre-results (e.g., **Paytm** numbers weak, **Laurus Labs** fell pre-result then rose). Open-day reactions: **positive** — Thermax, Pidilite (+4%), Vijaya Diagnostics (+6%), Saregama, Thyrocare, Netweb (+3.5%); **negative** — Aditya Birla Lifestyle (-9%), Gravita, Mahanagar Gas, Britannia, Dr Lal PathLabs.
- **Paint/crude-sensitive names** (Asian Paints, Pidilite) flagged as having valuation comfort if crude settles in 80-90.

## Technicals (Ramakrishna)
- Nifty in **consolidation**; a 24,450 breakout attempt yesterday was a **false breakout**. **Support 24,000 / resistance 24,600**; a decisive break needed for a real trend.
- Momentum improving (higher-highs); **RSI ~55.33**. OI (12 May expiry): **24,400 highest call writing, 23,800 highest put writing** → range likely holds into Tuesday. **Bank Nifty**: 57,000 call / 56,000 put highest writing → base forming, 55,000-56,000 zone defended.
- **Pharma index at all-time high** — re-rating theme; positions encouraged.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao was NOT present** in this episode (anchor noted he "still isn't appearing"). No Kutumba Rao stock calls were made.
- **Vasanth** (anchor/analyst) — AI-adjacent picks (Netweb, E2E, Black Box, Sterlite Tech, HFCL, MTAR), aerospace commentary.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst, joined via Zoom) — Raymond Ltd & Raymond Realty (aerospace/realty themes), continuation of conference picks; viewer Q&A (Semco, Hitachi Energy, Ashok Leyland, Shipping Corp, Cochin Shipyard).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) — TD Power caution, pharma theme, Natco Pharma.

## Other notes
- **Skyroot** (Hyderabad-based launch-vehicle startup) became a **unicorn (~1.1bn USD valuation)**, targeting **Vikram-1** launch this year — but it is **unlisted**, so not investable yet.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 6 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/-AMjFakzGFQ

## Global backdrop
- **GIFT Nifty** indicated a **gap-up open of ~150 points**; a "sandwich session" between two expiries.
- **Iran/US**: Trump's "**Project Freedom**" (post-Operation Epic Fury) passed to ease vessel transit through the **Strait of Hormuz**, but the blockade on Iranian vessels continues; **ceasefire to continue**. Seen as a positive de-escalation; war seen as "almost over."
- **US tech roaring**: Nasdaq/S&P 500 at **all-time highs**. **Micron** crossed **$700bn** mkt cap; **SK Hynix** crossed **$1 trillion**. Trillion-dollar club: Nvidia ~$5T, Alphabet ~$4T, Apple $3.8T, Microsoft $2.8T, plus Amazon, TSMC, Broadcom, Meta, Tesla, Berkshire.
- **AI/semiconductor revolution**: leaders growing **35-40% annually** even on huge bases; **Intel up ~13%** (multibagger since US govt stake). Apple investing in Intel; inviting Intel/SK Hynix to make device processors in the US.
- **Cloud backlogs huge**: Google Cloud **$460bn**, AWS **$364bn** Q1 backlog; **Anthropic-Google ~$200bn / 5-yr deal**. Crude cooled to **~$108** (from $112-113).

## Indian markets
- **Govt stimulus**: Cabinet approved a **Rs 2.55 lakh crore** credit lifeline — **100% credit guarantee for MSMEs**, **90% five-year loans** for non-MSMEs/airlines, **1-year moratorium** (Rs 100 cr for small cos, Rs 1,500 cr for airlines). Drove **banking stocks** and **IndiGo/InterGlobe**.
- **Reform**: **Google** got India's **first private discom (distribution) license** for its Andhra Pradesh data center — a notable reform.
- **Nifty levels**: closed above **24,000** (positive); **24,500** = major resistance, **24,000** = key benchmark/support. PCR jumped to **1.08**. Opened +200; **Bank Nifty +700 (~1.2-2%)**. Advance/decline ~**7:1 to 8:1**. FIIs **+Rs 3,600 cr**, DIIs **+Rs 2,600 cr**.
- **Smallcap momentum**: ET banner story — smallcap-100 **+18.4% in April**; momentum favoring small/mid caps (historical 15%+ months: 2020, 2014 +22.4%, May 2009 +47%).

## Results season (broadly strong)
- **Blockbuster**: **Lloyds Metals & Energy** — record revenue **Rs 631 cr** (5x YoY), PAT **Rs 1,530 cr** (~7-8x YoY, +50% QoQ), cash flow Rs 920 cr.
- **Good**: Hero MotoCorp, M&M (SUV king), Poonawalla Fincorp (rev Rs 215 cr, ~2x; PAT Rs 255 cr Q4), Coforge (EBIT/margins up, +8%), Aadhar Housing Fin, Raymond Realty, Aeroflex, JNK Electronics, SJS Enterprises.
- **Disappointing**: **L&T** — margins/EBIT weak (stock -3%), though order book very strong at **Rs 7.4 lakh cr / $80bn** (beat FY26 guidance). **TCS** down ~10-15% post-results — IT called "wealth destroyers."

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): proactive govt + reforms keep India "most resilient among emerging markets"; markets likely range-bound, breakout possible above 24,500-25,000. Buy dips; protect profits. Detailed results rundown and viewer stock calls.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental): mid/small caps outperforming; their con-calls confident long-term despite short-term headwinds; large caps (esp. **IT**, "old jackals" / *vriddha jambukalu*) stuck in high-capex consolidation, need earnings revival. Answered Hindustan Copper / Bharat Coking Coal.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): Nifty in narrowing **healthy consolidation** (buy ~23,900, sell ~24,300); momentum indicators (RSI/MACD) weak. Bank Nifty below MAs, resistance 56,500 / support 54,000. Long only above 24,250. Chart picks: Sinema STS, Manorama Industries, Apollo Micro Systems, Sun Flag Iron, Omax Autos (20% UC), Natco Pharma, Karur Vysya Bank, BSE, GE T&D/Hitachi/TD Power, Bajaj Consumer.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 calls (Rico Auto, Coal India, Vimta Labs, Leela Palace (Schloss Bangalore), Alu Fluoride (Alufluoride), ITC Hotels)

kutumba.note: Already ran to 140, now stabilizing; can cross 52-week high but depends on pending March results.
kutumba.detail: Rico Auto has already risen a lot - it went up to 140 and is now stabilizing around 116-120. Hold. It can cross its 52-week high again, but that depends on results; the March results haven't come yet, so take a decision after they come.

kutumba.note: Recently-listed Coal India subsidiary; got listing hype, pulled back, now recovering slowly. Good product demand.
kutumba.detail: This is a recently-listed Coal India subsidiary (the caller's 'Bharat Coal'). At listing it got good hype and rose swiftly, then came back, and is now slowly recovering. Their products all have good demand, so hold it for some time.

kutumba.note: Last result flat (not bad); one-of-its-kind, little competition; FMEG turnover rising. Expects a remarkable turnaround.
kutumba.detail: Last result wasn't bad, it was flat (top and bottom line flat). I think the stock will definitely do a remarkable turnaround because it's one of its kind with little competition; the fast-moving electrical goods unit's turnover is slowly rising and the company can cross Rs 500 cr turnover next year. Hold a bit longer; don't look only for the result.

kutumba.note: Stuck in 400-450 range; real action needs cross of 430-440. One of the better premium hotel stocks.
kutumba.detail: Hold as of now, with a stop loss around 380. The stock has been fixed in roughly a 400-450 range since listing (it spiked to 465-470 once); real action needs a cross of 430-440 levels. It's one of the better hotel stocks - extreme-premium segment, so the economy has little effect on them - and this high-PE stock can surprise.

kutumba.price: 590 (buy price); buy on dips, not at current rate
kutumba.note: Has risen a lot; don't add at this rate, accumulate on dips. Good turnaround performance visible.
kutumba.detail: Alu Fluoride has risen a lot; don't add at this rate - take it when some dips come. A good turnaround performance is visible in the stock.

kutumba.note: Looks poised for 200 but will take time; don't fixate on a hard 200 target.
kutumba.detail: It will take time, because if you fix in your mind that it must reach 200 it becomes hard. But definitely, as of now it looks poised for 200. Hold.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 30 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/Xxxn-P60WE4

## Session setup
- **Long weekend**: markets shut **tomorrow (May 1)** for Maharashtra Formation Day (all exchanges HQ'd in Mumbai). Today also a **Sensex weekly expiry**.
- **GIFT Nifty** signalling a **140-150 point gap-down** open after yesterday's 180-point gain.

## Global backdrop
- **Brent crude above $121** (up ~**8% overnight**, later quoted **$122.45**) — repeatedly called the "main villain/enemy" for equities.
- Trump reaffirmed (on Truth Social, with an AI image) that the **Strait of Hormuz blockade continues**; matching negative signals from Iran. No sign of a peace/agreement; crude could push toward **$140-150**.
- **US markets**: Dow lower, Nasdaq flat, S&P 500 slightly down. **Magnificent Seven results** (Microsoft, Google, Meta, Amazon) came **in line**, but after-market: **MSFT -2%, Meta -5%, Amazon -2%**, Google marginally positive.
- **US Fed**: held rates; West Asia clouds the inflation call. Speculation now leans toward a possible **hike before year-end** rather than a cut. **Four dissents** (highest on record) — divided FOMC.
- Striking stat cited: **Magnificent Seven market cap ~$22 trillion** — larger than all the rest of the world's stock markets combined. FIIs globally crowding into **AI stocks**; **South Korea's market cap has overtaken the UK's** on semiconductor/AI gains.

## Indian markets — yesterday & today
- Yesterday: strong first half on exit-poll optimism, then **profit-booking** in the second half. **Nifty** off the **24,335** high to close ~**24,170** (~190-200 pts off high). **Bank Nifty -700** (56,178 → 55,403). Mid/small-caps also booked profits.
- **April was historic**: small-cap index **+18.56%** (best month in **14 years**); mid-cap index **+13.84%** (best in **6 years**, best since Covid); Nifty **+8.5%**, Sensex **+8%**.
- **Rupee weakness**: opened **~95.02** (prior 94.85), near record low — a new low would keep **FIIs away**.
- **Today's open (weak)**: Nifty **-220**, Bank Nifty **-730**, Sensex **-650**. Breadth poor (NSE 828 adv / 1,400 dec). Only ~6-7 Nifty names green (Infosys, TCS, Coal India). Almost every sector index red.

## Exit polls (state elections)
- Poll of polls: **Bengal** — BJP **43.7%** vs TMC **41.9%** (markets prefer a BJP win for industry-friendliness). **Tamil Nadu** — most polls DMK; Axis My India sees Vijay's **TVK**. **Assam** — BJP; **Kerala** — UDF. Anchor view: largely **already discounted** by the market.

## Results season
- **Good**: Granules India, RPG, Navin Fluorine, IIFL Finance (gold-loan growth), Force Motors, SEMA India (+7%), RPG Life Sciences, Bajaj Finance (in line, +2.5-5%).
- **Mixed/weak**: Motilal Oswal weak; **Waaree Energies** PAT ~flat QoQ (Rs 1,126 cr vs Rs 1,107 cr), +75% YoY, but **QoQ margin contraction** — result reaction negative; Reliance soft in O2C but good in retail/telecom; Time Technoplast disappointed. No outright "howlers" so far; ~80% of companies report by **May 15**.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): see dedicated file. Headline — exit weaker/high-PE stocks, but **buy on dips, stay invested only in good stocks**; be **very cautious on fresh buying unless Nifty stabilises above 24,500**; market broadly consolidating **23,800-24,300/400**.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst, separate from Kutumba Rao): discussed **BlackRock staying bullish on India** (5-10 yr horizon; valuations down ~18 months, intact demographics/reforms/digitalisation; crude is the key near-term risk). Cited Larry Summers calling Europe "a museum", China "a jail", Japan "an old-age home", with India the long-term opportunity. Handled **EPL** (Hold), **360 ONE** (Hold), **Fractal Analytics** (buy on declines), **Salzer Electronics** (Hold).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): be cautious into the long weekend/events; Nifty consolidation **~23,800-900 support / 24,600 resistance**; Bank Nifty 54,500 support / 56,500 resistance with MAs clustered (confusion); intraday **short ~24,050-60, 100-pt target, 50-pt SL**. Hedge any carried positions.

## Other mentions
- **MTAR Technologies** (+4%, ~Rs 5,915): a fresh **1-GW Bloom Energy order** translates to a ~**Rs 1,000 cr** order (MTAR makes the hot-boxes); flagged as a past workshop idea (~30% return, target exceeded).
- **Mutual-fund picks** (anchor): small-cap **Bandhan Small Cap**; mid-cap **Edelweiss Mid Cap / ICICI Pru Midcap / Nippon India Growth Mid Cap**; flexi-cap **Parag Parikh Flexi Cap / ICICI Pru Flexi Cap**. Note: some AMCs have **paused lump-sum** small-cap subscriptions (SIPs still open).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Laurus Labs), 3 sells (DCX Systems (DCX India), Exide Industries, Tamilnadu Petroproducts (TN Petro))

kutumba.note: Exit on the recovery rally after this quarter's results
kutumba.detail: Will recover from the recent fall. After this quarter's results, when a rally comes (perhaps ~40% recovery), take the capital out and exit.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 29 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/oRx5AEdjqvo

## Global backdrop
- **US markets closed lower**, dragged by tech: **Nvidia -1.6%**, **Oracle -4%**. **OpenAI/ChatGPT** results missed even its own estimates (weak new-user and revenue numbers) amid rising competition from **Grok, Claude, and Google Gemini**.
- Pre-results nervousness ahead of tonight's **Magnificent 7** numbers — **Microsoft, Amazon, Meta** (and Google) report after-hours.
- **Gold** back near **$4,500/oz**; both gold and silver are now **range-bound** — no big move expected.
- **Brent crude settled above $110**; **USD/INR near 95** (94.54 close) — both flagged as macro negatives.

## Macro / events
- **UAE to exit OPEC from May 1** (after 60 years), citing friction with Iran. UAE is ~**12% of OPEC output** (current capacity **4.85 mn bpd**, targeting **5 mn bpd by 2027**); freed from quotas, this could ease oil prices — though impact is capped until Strait of Hormuz flows normalize.
- **State assembly elections** (West Bengal focus; 142 seats; also Tamil Nadu, Assam): **exit polls tonight**, **results on 4th**. A BJP win in Bengal would be short-lived positive news. Today/tomorrow/Monday seen as crucial.
- **May F&O series begins light**: open interest **2.11 cr → 1.45 cr contracts**. Rollovers: **Nifty 71%**, **Bank Nifty only 78%** (subdued → Bank Nifty underperformance).
- **RBI's revised forward-looking provisioning norms** weigh on banks, especially **SBI** and treasury-heavy PSU banks (**Union Bank** corrected sharply).
- **US Fed meeting** expected to be a non-event (no rate change; outgoing chair).
- Severe nationwide **summer/heatwave** and **monsoon uncertainty** are key seasonal themes.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty -12% in March, +7.5% in April** (recouped about half the March loss). Mid/small-caps strongly outperformed: indices **+15–18%**, individual stocks **+30–50%** in the April series (capital markets, energy, metals led).
- **Nifty opened ~+99–107 pts at 24,094**; **Sensex +350**; **Bank Nifty +133** (underperforming). Advance/decline strongly positive (NSE **1,810 adv / 379 dec**).
- **Defense theme**: DAM Capital sees a **~Rs 15 lakh crore** potential opportunity; mixed/"some fear" in defense pack at open.
- **Power/IEX volatility flagged**: per-unit spot power crashed to **3–5 paise** (from ~Rs 10 a few weeks ago) on a solar-power glut — caution that not all power companies will post great Q1 results.
- **Vizag data-center boom**: **$50 bn** total investment incoming — **$15 bn** data center (foundation stone laid), **Reliance ~$17–19 bn**, plus Sify and others. Anchors and Kutumba Rao defended state concessions (GST reimbursement Rs 2,245 cr, land at 25% below market, stamp-duty waiver, power/water subsidies) as a strategic investment, not a loss.

## Results / corporate news
- **Dr. Reddy's**: Canadian regulator approval for its **semaglutide** (obesity/diabetes) drug — positive open.
- Good prior-day numbers: **Piramal Finance**, **Kirloskar Pneumatic**, **CEAT** (PAT **+145%**, **Rs 35 dividend**, margin expansion), **Skipper**, **MV Photovoltaic** (revenue **+62%**, PAT **+89%**), **Tamilnad Mercantile Bank**, **AU Small Finance Bank**.
- **Today's results**: Bajaj Finance, Vedanta, Mphasis, Federal Bank, Adani Power, Force Motors, Indian Bank, KFin Technologies, Motilal Oswal, Waaree Energies.
- Strong opening reactions: **CEAT +10.5%**, **Bandhan Bank ~+7%**, **Garden Reach +10%** (~8 warships to be delivered this year), **Maruti Suzuki +4%**, **Star Health +12%**, **Eternal +3.7%** (brokerage targets up to 500), **Skipper +3.5%**. Selling in REC, Power Finance, Hindalco; Metals index slightly negative.
- Summer-beneficiary names highlighted: **Voltas, Blue Star, Amber Enterprises, Dixon Technologies, PG Electroplast**; ice-cream/seasonal stocks.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A) — present and named. Sees the **May series staying in consolidation**; cautious on banks (RBI provisioning); cautions on new SIF fund offerings; flags FIIs still selling, DIIs buying modestly. Gave several viewer stock calls (see kutumba_rao file).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) — Nifty consolidation/pullback likely arrested; support **23,800** (20-DEMA 23,950), worst case **23,500**; resistance **24,600** (cross needed for next swing high); intraday R1 **24,130**. Bank Nifty bounce likely. PCR 0.98, VIX ~18. Trade with a positive bias.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

## Trade setup (Kutumba Rao)
- **Nifty 24,000** is the key level — a close/sustained trade below it signals further weakness; calls piled at **24,500**, puts at **24,000**. For an exit-poll play, a **straddle/strangle below the 24,000 strike** suggested.
- **PCR ~0.98** (near 1), **VIX ~18** flat. Speculative shorts ahead of results in **Eternal, IDFC First, APL Apollo, ICICI Bank**. Delivery-with-conviction buying in **Nestle** (near 1,500; closed 1,444), **Sun Pharma, SBI Life, HDFC Life, Hindalco, Divi's** (possible move toward 7,000). Highest rollovers (97–98%) in low-volume counters: Motilal Oswal, Marico, Idea, Grasim, CG Power, Phoenix, Nykaa.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 10 buys (Larsen & Toubro (L&T), NCC, Jio Finance, Adani Power, Tata Power, NTPC, Canara Bank, Siemens Energy, Garden Reach Shipbuilders, Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders)

kutumba.note: Put the modest amount into one name rather than four diversified
kutumba.detail: Asked by a caller with Rs 50,000 and a 3-year horizon to pick one of four (L&T, Infosys, Vikram Solar, MCX), Kutumba Rao said the amount is very modest and he would rather put it all into a single name, L&T, than spread it across four diversified stocks.

kutumba.note: Wait for results; range-bound, very high PE
kutumba.detail: Results had not yet come; he expects no great surprises and a range-bound stock between 180 and 230. With a very high PE (~800+ pre-consolidation), he said this set of results will be key and could dictate the future, so wait for the results.

kutumba.note: At bottom valuation; good for one-year-plus horizon
kutumba.detail: Asked alongside Jio Finance, he said NCC is at bottom valuation and is a buy. He noted both NCC and Jio are good for an investor with a one-year-plus waiting horizon.

kutumba.note: Clear-cut outperformer; buyable at current levels
kutumba.detail: He said Jio looked like a clear-cut outperformer on both 1-week and 3-4-week performance, and can be invested in at current levels for a one-year-plus horizon.

kutumba.note: Do not average a penny stock; large opportunity loss already
kutumba.detail: A caller holding 8,000 Unitech shares bought at Rs 60 asked about averaging. Kutumba Rao agreed one should not average; he noted the stock sat below about five rupees for four-five years and that simply holding so long was already a large opportunity loss, implicitly advising against adding.

kutumba.note: One of his two explicit long-term power picks
kutumba.detail: Asked to name two power stocks in his view, he said his choice would be Adani Power and Tata Power, calling them long-term, low-hype counters. He warned against blindly chasing hot momentum power names (Atlanta Electricals, Quality Power, High Voltage), advising small position sizes and no over-allocation to one sector.

kutumba.note: One of his two explicit long-term power picks
kutumba.detail: Named as his second preferred power pick alongside Adani Power for the long term, as a low-hype counter. He also mentioned Tata Power earlier as a suggestion for a viewer exiting Tech Mahindra to rotate into energy.

kutumba.note: Long-term energy suggestion; buy quality power names on dips
kutumba.detail: For a viewer exiting Tech Mahindra to buy energy stocks, he suggested NTPC for the long term, along with quality transmission/power companies to be bought on dips, and noted nuclear-energy stocks can also be bought.

kutumba.note: Bank stocks stable but entry timing is key
kutumba.detail: Asked about Canara Bank and Siemens Energy, he said both can be added on declines. PSU banks that corrected were recovering; bank stocks are stable but the time of entry is key, and he would accumulate Canara closer to 130 or below 130.

kutumba.note: Add on declines
kutumba.detail: Asked alongside Canara Bank, he said both can be added on declines.

kutumba.note: Split a Rs 5 lakh long-term investment equally with Mazagon Dock
kutumba.detail: A viewer asked which of Garden Reach or Mazagon Dock to invest Rs 5 lakh in for the long term. Kutumba Rao said comparing two same-sector PSUs is the wrong framing and advised putting the amount equally in both.

kutumba.note: Split a Rs 5 lakh long-term investment equally with Garden Reach
kutumba.detail: Paired with Garden Reach in the same viewer question; he advised splitting the Rs 5 lakh equally between the two PSU shipbuilders for the long term rather than choosing one.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 28 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/ppF0Xz0YMek

## Top story — Telugu-states data-centre boom
- **Andhra Pradesh (Vizag)** is becoming the data-centre hub of India. A **Google data centre (1.5 GW)** with a **$15 bn** investment had its foundation-stone laid today.
- **Reliance Industries** is planning a larger data-centre cluster (**$19 bn**); the two together ~**$34 bn** (~Rs 3.5 lakh crore). Reliance reportedly asked for **935 acres** (300 in phase 1, then 635, plus 1 acre for a cable landing station and 80 acres for a desalination plant for water-cooling).
- **Bharti Airtel** is also showing interest; **Sify** data centre coming to the same region. CM hosted a dinner/discussions in Vizag.

## Global backdrop
- **US equities:** tech led again; semis lagged but "played out." **Intel +40%** this month. AI + semiconductor themes driving US markets (Micron, Intel, Nvidia). **Nasdaq/S&P near new highs**, sustaining, but wait-and-watch downstream.
- **Earnings week:** Google, Microsoft, Amazon, Meta report tomorrow night; **Apple Thursday**.
- **FOMC** meeting begins today — **last for Jerome Powell**.
- **Geopolitics fragile:** Iran–US stalemate; Trump rejecting Iran's proposal to postpone nuclear talks; Strait of Hormuz reopening watched. **Brent crude ~$109** (negative for India).

## Indian markets
- **Today = Tuesday monthly expiry** (Nifty, Fin Nifty, Mid Cap Select); **Sensex expiry Thursday**. Gift Nifty pointed to a negative open; markets opened down (**Nifty -24/25, Sensex -135, Bank Nifty -350**).
- **West Bengal final polling tomorrow**; exit polls tomorrow evening; **results Monday**. Friday is a holiday. A BJP win seen as a positive market trigger. Post-election fuel-price hike speculation (Rs 25-28/litre).
- **April = broader-market outperformance.** MTD: **Nifty +7.9%, Midcap +14.4%, Smallcap +18%, Nifty Next 50 +17.3%**. YTD: Nifty ~-8%, Midcap -0.4%, Smallcap +1%.
- **Sector winners (CY):** Energy is a mega theme on data-centre power demand — **Hitachi Energy +75%, Schneider +63%, Adani Power +52%, GE Vernova +48%**. Aerospace emerging: **Azad Engineering +50%, Belrise +68%, Unimech +40%, Dynamatic/Raymond +35%, Sansera +21%**. Capital-market and EMS/AC themes flagged (hot "super El Niño" summer).
- **Yesterday's Nifty +200** was Reliance-driven (67 pts); Sun Pharma 33, M&M 15, TCS 11; dragged by Axis, ICICI, Shriram Finance.
- **RBI revised ECL norms** → selling in PSU banks (SBI, Bank of India, Canara). **UltraTech** posted multi-quarter-high results (+2%). **Cohance Life Sciences +10%** on new CEO.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental) — market view + viewer stock Q&A (see kutumba_rao file).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — buy-on-dips; long near **23,975 / 24,000** intraday with 60-pt SL; OI highest at **24,100/24,000**; Bank Nifty **56,800 call / 55,200 put**, high volatility expected; advised **hedged positions** into the events. On NTPC: cup-and-handle not complete until it crosses **Rs 445** (~8-9% away), but technically strong/buyable.
- **Guest interview — Bondada Engineering** promoter **Raghavendra Rao** (solar EPC): FY26 turnover **Rs 1500 cr → 2843 cr** (~90% growth), net profit **115 → 211 cr**; Q4 revenue **712 → 914 cr**, profit **54 → 63 cr**. Order book **~Rs 7200 cr** (execute in ~20 months); ~75% revenue from solar EPC; moving into **battery energy storage (1.1 GWh orders)**, signed ~5 GW with Adani (Khavda, 1 GW/yr). Turned **debt-free**, op cash flow swung **-125 cr → +125 cr**; guides **~65-70% growth** for FY27. Q4 margin dipped to **6.88%** (from 7.77%) on diesel/cable/steel cost rise and billing deferred to FY27. Stock opened ~Rs 355, sell-on-news reaction (52wk range Rs 215-510, now ~382).

## Kutumba Rao's market view (brief)
- **Risk-on in mid/small caps** clearly visible — beaten-down names recovering on huge volumes (Mahindra Financial +10%, TMB +8%, others). But this may be a **short-term trade**; watch West Bengal exit polls/results.
- **Sun Pharma's acquisition** (biggest since Tata Steel/Corus) was the major mover; expects pharma impact and possible **domestic takeover targets** among small pharma.
- Summer/beverage/AC/EMS/power-equipment results likely good for **Q1 FY27**.
- **Nifty levels:** fresh buying only above **24,500** (critical resistance); **24,000 support**, below it **23,500** downside; **PCR** improved 0.78 → 1.0; **VIX fell** (confidence improving). Don't take excessive risk on settlement day; watch rollovers.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Schneider Electric / Techno Electric / GE Vernova, Ambuja Cements, State Bank of India (SBI))

kutumba.stock: Schneider Electric / Techno Electric / GE Vernova
kutumba.note: Staggered buy on a long-term power/electrification theme
kutumba.detail: Asked about buying all three (already risen a lot), Kutumba Rao said don't wait for a dip. If investing about Rs 5 lakh, deploy about Rs 1 lakh immediately and add on every dip. This is a long-term theme, not short-term; these companies can perform well over the next two to three years, so avoid FOMO buying at the highs later.

kutumba.note: Something is cooking in Ambuja and ACC; definitely buy
kutumba.detail: A viewer holding 100 shares at a 20% loss asked about adding another 100. Kutumba Rao said to buy, noting that something is cooking in Ambuja and ACC and that at some point he would definitely recommend buying.

kutumba.note: Accumulate
kutumba.detail: On a viewer query, Kutumba Rao said to accumulate SBI.

kutumba.note: Wait before buying
kutumba.detail: A viewer wanted to accumulate Sanhi Hotels; Kutumba Rao advised to wait rather than buy now.

kutumba.note: Hold patiently; bought at high rates
kutumba.detail: A viewer holding 100 shares of HDFC bought at Rs 600-800 (plus Lara around Rs 960) asked whether to hold or sell. Kutumba Rao said to hold with patience, since they were bought at high (not cheap) rates and these are good stocks.

kutumba.note: Good company doing well; hold, can average on results
kutumba.detail: A viewer at large losses across several names asked about Natco Pharma (also Powermech, CMS Info, Avro). Kutumba Rao said Natco is a good company doing very well (around Rs 1100) and to hold patiently, watch the results, and one could even average. He noted Powermech depends on whether it was bought pre- or post-split and the buyer's average.

kutumba.stock: CG Power / GE Vernova / CAMS / Sirma SGS / Kaynes
kutumba.note: Hold; CG Power back at 52-week high, transformer-capacity shortage a tailwind
kutumba.detail: A viewer holding these at 25% profit asked whether to hold. Kutumba Rao said hold all of them. CG Power has come back to its 52-week high and the 840-850 levels are likely to be crossed; there is an endless shortage of transformer capacity, making it a great opportunity for these companies. Good stocks bought, patience needed.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 27 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/SN8-NsBMa7c

## Global backdrop
- **Global cues positive** Monday morning — **Kospi, Nikkei, Shanghai** up; **Hang Seng** slightly red. **Gift Nifty** indicated a ~**200-point gap-up**.
- **Iran–US talks in Islamabad cancelled** over the weekend (US delegation stopped before boarding; Iranian Foreign Minister left Pakistan early). Iran has since floated a **new proposal** (Strait of Hormuz / lifting blockade first, nuclear deal later), which US officials say they are examining.
- **Brent crude ~107** (range 100–107) — flagged as the main market negative.
- **US tech strong** Friday: **Nasdaq +1.63%**, **S&P 500 +0.8%**; **Intel** a star performer (Nvidia invests in Intel); **Nvidia market cap crossed $5 trillion**.
- This week brings the **US Federal Reserve rate decision** — expected to hold status quo regardless of Trump pressure (per Kranthi).

## Indian markets
- **IT sector wrecked last week** — IT index **-10%**; Wed–Fri indices fell heavily on disappointing results (**TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech, Wipro, Tech Mahindra**). Better mid-cap IT: **Oracle Financial Services, Persistent, LTIMindtree**.
- **Nifty** formed lower highs/lows: day's high slipped from **24,602 (Apr 21)** to **24,206 (Fri)**. Supports: **23,635 (20-DMA)**; **50-DMA 24,284**.
- **FII sold Rs 8,828 cr** Friday; **DII bought ~Rs 4,700 cr**. **Rupee weakened to 94.24/USD** (from ~92.5 earlier).
- **Results season strong** — "best double-digit profit growth in 10 quarters" (Business Standard); ~**65–70% of companies** beating. Strong from banks, non-bank lenders (**Shriram Finance, M&M Finance**), metals (**Hindustan Zinc**).
- **Weekend results:** Reliance — weak O2C, strong retail, record Jio subscriber adds (best in 7 quarters), first Indian co. to post **$10bn annual profit**, but weak overall numbers. **Axis, IndusInd** fine; **IDFC First** weak. Good: **Supreme Petrochem, Jaswal Nico, Can Fin Homes, Atul, Adani Green**.
- **Today's results due:** UltraTech Cement, Coal India, AU Bank, SBI Cards, Varun Beverages.
- **Sun Pharma** confirmed its **~$4bn (enterprise value $11.7bn) all-cash acquisition of Organon** (Merck spin-off) — biggest Indian pharma deal; stock **+2.5%** at open, seen as a positive signal to FPIs.
- **Power/energy as a major theme:** peak power demand hit a record **~256–260 GW** (vs 243 GW last year) amid 47°C heat wave; grid designed for only **270 GW** (96% utilization). Power-equipment / EHVDC / transformer names flagged (see Kranthi note below).
- **Market opened strongly:** Nifty **+117**, Bank Nifty **+247**, Sensex **+370**; advance-decline strongly positive. **Paytm -7/8%** (RBI cancelled its payments-bank license). **M&M Finance +10%**, **India Cements +7.5%**, **Tanla +12%** on results.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao was NOT on the show today** (anchor stated he is not joining). **Stock Q&A was handled by Kranthi (fundamental analyst — kept in a separate folder) and Ramakrishna (technical analyst)**, with **Vasanth** anchoring. Per project rules, none of these calls are attributed to Kutumba Rao.
- **Ramakrishna (technical):** Friday's put trade closed successfully; pullback complete, possible reversal up. Nifty immediate resistance **~24,600** (cross → ~+1,000 pts / +4%); support **23,500**, then **23,100** gap-fill. Bank Nifty to outperform (relative strength >0; offsets Reliance drag). OI: Nifty 24,000 call / 23,900 put highest writing (monthly settlement tomorrow); Bank Nifty 56,000 call / 55,000 put. **PCR 0.78**, **VIX 19.71**. Intraday: buy Bank Nifty near **56,200**, Nifty near **23,940–950** with strict stop-loss.
- **Kranthi (fundamental):** Market needs to reclaim **24,000** decisively; **24,750** the key upside trigger (needs crude to cool). Stock-specific action over broad rally; banks (e.g. **RBL Bank**) standing out on strong results. Highlighted Sun Pharma–Organon as a positive FPI signal; **Hemadri Speciality Chemicals** an under-the-radar cyclical with good results; **Paytm** sustaining after RBI action. Numerous viewer Q&A calls (see Kutumba-Rao file's closing note).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 21 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/3MGecfbr-R8

## Global backdrop
- **US-Iran talks** scheduled for "tomorrow"; JD Vance left for Pakistan and an Iran delegation is expected (per WSJ). The ceasefire deadline expires around Thursday morning IST — markets watching whether a deal is struck or the ceasefire is extended.
- US markets closed in the red, but Dow/Nasdaq futures and GIFT Nifty point to a positive open (~60-70 pts).
- **Crude:** Brent ~$95, Nymex ~$88-89; physical market eased from $140-145 toward ~$120 as nations cut consumption ("demand destruction") and focus on energy security.
- **Tech:** Apple — John Ternus becomes CEO, Tim Cook (15 yrs, ~20x market-cap growth) moves to executive chairman. Amazon to invest $5bn in Anthropic (maker of Claude AI); Anthropic to use $100bn of AWS in return.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday volatile, closed with small gains (Nifty +11-12 pts after a negative open and intraday +120-130). **Today is the Nifty 50 expiry.**
- RBI eased the April-1 derivatives restrictions → banks can offer offshore derivative contracts again; **rupee saw its steepest one-day fall** and weakness may resume.
- Bank Nifty faced a **200-DMA rejection**; heavy put writing means Nifty faces strong resistance higher up.
- **FIIs net buyers (~Rs 1,000 cr); DIIs net sellers (~Rs 3,000 cr)** — but a low-volume day.
- **Vedanta** to split into five companies (Oil & Gas, Aluminium, etc.); record date May 1.
- Results: PNB Housing, Bank of Maharashtra, Navkar Corp, E2E Networks, Aditya Birla Money all reported good numbers; PN Gadgil positive Akshaya Tritiya update. HCL Tech, Persistent, 360 One reporting today.
- Open: Nifty +39, Sensex +208, Bank Nifty +315 (later Nifty +118, Sensex +400); Nifty IT down 4%, mid/small-caps strong.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Kranti** (new co-analyst, joined late) — market in a consolidation phase around 24,000; no disappointments in results so far (IT subdued); momentum to pick up; crude ~$85 favourable.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — higher-high/higher-low structure intact; 24,415 gap filled, next gap ~25,100; chance to cross 50-DMA. Brent below $100 digested; above $100 = danger bells. Bank Nifty trying to fill the March-6 gap (57,000 put). Buy-on-dips; Nifty supports 24,271 / 24,100 / 23,900; expiry range bounded by 24,500 call / 24,300 put.

## Theme
Energy/power a multi-year theme (Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy, Quality Power, Atlanta Electricals, TD Power); identify early. Mid/small-cap season — "pick your right stock and invest." Avoid getting tempted into illiquid real-estate bets.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 4 buys (SBI, HDFC Bank, Syrma SGS, Bandhan Small Cap Fund)
Kranthi: 1 buy (Bandhan Small Cap Fund)

kutumba.note: Good bet on every decline; confidently targets servicing 25% of GDP by 2030.
kutumba.detail: Even being a PSU bank, SBI confidently reports it will service 25% of GDP by 2030 — shows how aggressively it thinks. A good bet on every decline; no need to think too much.

kutumba.note: Value-unlocking signs (HDFC Sec, AMC, Life); at not even 11-12 PE, will attract buyers.
kutumba.detail: Largest private bank, competitively placed; value-unlocking signs given subsidiaries (HDFC Securities, HDFC AMC) and already-divested HDFC Life. Including investment value it's available at not even 11-12 PE; will attract buyers at some point.

kutumba.note: Up ~150% from earlier call; high-PE (~86-87) but performing; buy near 900 on any post-result dip.
kutumba.detail: At 52-week high, ~150% return from his earlier call; now high-PE (~86-87) but performing in line with results (profit Rs 65 cr Sep to ~Rs 94 cr Dec; Q4 to come well; new France JV). Forms higher tops/bottoms — any post-result correction close to 900 is a good entry.

kutumba.note: Segment leader among small-cap schemes; start investing (also Nippon India, Invesco Small Cap).
kutumba.detail: For a caller wanting the best small-cap fund: named Bandhan Small Cap (segment leader), Nippon India Small Cap and Invesco Small Cap as outperformers; advised starting with Bandhan.

kranti.note: Segment leader among small-cap schemes; told caller to start investing in it (also liked Nippon India Small Cap, Invesco Small Cap).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 20 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/v-fgk-foH5w

## Workshop recap
- The TV5 weekend investor workshop was a big success (~400 attendees met one-on-one). Hosts thanked analysts (Kutumba Rao, Ramakrishna, Kranti) and the back-end team; best-12 attendees got prizes/books ("Mango Millionaire").

## Global backdrop
- **Action-packed weekend on crude:** Brent swung from $96 → $86 (Fri evening) → back to ~$95–96 this morning. Whole story revolves around the **Strait of Hormuz**: Iran said it reopened it, but the US attacked an Iranian-flagged cargo ship; Iran threatened retaliation on any vessels in the Persian Gulf / Sea of Oman.
- **US–Iran peace talks** set for "tomorrow" in Islamabad — outcome very uncertain. US team (JD Vance et al.) en route; Iran reportedly not sending its team; fresh-war risk remains. This morning Iran did a fresh drone attack on US vessels.
- GIFT Nifty pointed to a gap-up; Asian markets (Hang Seng, Nikkei, Kospi) positive.

## Indian markets
- **Domestic cues very positive:** ICICI Bank Q4 net profit +8.5% (NII +8.4%), HDFC Bank net profit +9.1%, NPAs down; Yes Bank result also positive.
- **Mid/small-cap rally continuing** — NSE Midcap 100 near its all-time high. Stock-picking + patience to withstand volatility emphasized.
- FIIs net buyers (+₹683 cr); DIIs modest sellers (~₹4,700 cr) but buy ₹25–30k cr/month overall — not a concern.
- Watch **gold** (likely correction once Akshaya Tritiya effect fades); **dollar** may strengthen back above 93.
- The market opened **negative** (GIFT Nifty / pre-open "deceived"), then **recovered** to positive by close of segment (Bank Nifty +~390 from low). Mid-cap/small-cap firm.

## Analysts featured

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 17 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/Z-QULxrJM_4

## Global backdrop
- A 10-day **Israel–Lebanon ceasefire** was reached (Trump tweet); not hugely significant in itself, but relevant since the recent Iran–US ceasefire violations centred on Lebanon. Crude (Brent) ~$98, up a touch.
- **US markets up for a 12th straight session** (Nasdaq, S&P 500) — the longest such run since 2009, led by tech. Dow modestly weak. Inflation seen as the biggest coming risk for US markets, though tech tends to perform well in inflationary periods.
- **Taiwan's market cap reached ~$4.14T, overtaking the UK** — cited as proof that tech-led economies create global records.

## Indian markets
- Nifty couldn't hold **24,300** yesterday; trend still positive and pro-long. Stabilisation likely around **24,000**. HDFC Bank + ICICI Bank results tomorrow (33% of Bank Nifty) → some banking nervousness.
- **Breadth strongly positive** — ~2:1 advances (1,900 up vs 1,000 down), a sharp reversal from Nov–March (March A/D ratio just 0.79). Mid/small-caps outperforming after a 5–6 month underperformance stretch.
- **Results season:** Wipro disappointed again (FY26 CC revenue -1.6%, guidance cut, promoters tendering in the buyback — a concern); **Angel One Q4 excellent** (rev +9% QoQ, EBITDA +16%, PAT +19%); a renewables firm posted FY26 rev ₹3,331 cr / PAT ₹479 cr (>2x, 112% rev CAGR FY23-26); VST Industries strong.
- **Mining/metals rallied** on the government opening rare-earth corridors — NMDC +19%, MOIL +16%, Ashapura Minechem +9%; Hindalco, Nalco, Vedanta up. MosChip +~20% on an acquisition.
- FIIs bought a token ₹382–400 cr (no longer selling); **DIIs sold ₹3,400 cr** (profit booking on old low-priced holdings, kept recent momentum buys). Passive equity buying has risen sharply (year-end allocations).
- Workshop weekend (Akshaya Tritiya Sunday): Sat one-on-ones only, Sun one-on-ones + workshop.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — 24,500 is key resistance (multiple-support-turned-resistance) and a "crossroads"; 23,900/23,700 support; markets held the 50-day EMA, above 10/20-day MAs. 24,800 call (bought ~90, closed 78) — hold, SL ~57–60. Bank Nifty comfortable, crossed 200-DMA.
- Market opened slightly weak then turned flat/positive; IT/Auto/PSU banks/metals down; breadth positive; capital-market theme (Angel One, BSE) gaining.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 buys (TVS Motors, Adani Enterprises, State Bank of India (SBI), Vedanta, Canara Bank, MCX)

kutumba.note: First choice in EV/two-wheeler space; captured the market, better bet even in declines.
kutumba.detail: For the EV space, first choice is TVS Motors, because in two-wheelers they've captured the market; better bet even in declines. Ola/Ather can be looked at; watch for a turnaround.

kutumba.note: Preferred data-centre supplier play; makes parts for US partner, feeding data centres indirectly.
kutumba.detail: More bullish on data-centre suppliers than operators (operator business risks becoming commoditised). MTAR on the radar — makes some parts for its US partner, indirectly feeding data centres.

kutumba.note: Buy big conglomerates/holding companies; up 3%, can base around 2,000.
kutumba.detail: Buy big industrial groups/conglomerates/holding companies. Adani Enterprises performed well, up over 3%; can now base around 2,000. GKG-Partners-into-Adani report boosted Adani stocks.

kutumba.note: Stabilising/recovering from lows, crossed 50; institutional selling stopped, will rise on performance.
kutumba.detail: Hold; slowly stabilising and in a recovery process from the low; already crossed 50 and stabilising; institutional selling has stopped; from here it rises depending on performance.

kutumba.note: Switch to State Bank; couldn't cross 25-26, trades back near 20.
kutumba.detail: Repeatedly advises switching to State Bank instead. Despite recent developments (even a Middle-East major bank stake), the stock couldn't cross 25-26 levels and trades back near 20.

kutumba.note: Preferred over Yes Bank; shift Yes Bank holdings into SBI.
kutumba.detail: Recommended as the shift destination from Yes Bank.

kutumba.note: Buy/add; small negative (fire-accident court matters) will pass; restructuring + demerger ahead; big commodity player.
kutumba.detail: Buy / can add. Small negative today (court matters from the fire accident) will pass over time; more corporate restructuring and a demerger exercise ahead; one of the bigger commodity players amid a strengthening commodity rally.

kutumba.note: Caller at 500% profit; hold, no need to panic.
kutumba.detail: Caller holding ~3,000 shares at 500% profit; hold for now, no need to panic.

kutumba.note: Buy on declines, not now; June-ending could be weak (treasury yields fell, large PSU treasury books).
kutumba.detail: Buy on declines, not now. Results maybe a bit better but June-ending could be weak; treasury yields fell and PSU banks hold large treasury portfolios — buy on a dip, no rush.

kutumba.note: Buy on declines, not now; trading near 52-week highs, gauge its strength.
kutumba.detail: Buy on declines, not now. Trading near 52-week highs, so must understand how much strength it shows.

kutumba.note: Earlier suggestion; result good, stock +16-17% - a good development.
kutumba.detail: Earlier suggestion by Kutumba Rao; result was good and the stock jumped 16-17% - a good development.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 16 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/RWMRAt-zRoc

## Global backdrop
- US markets at records: **Nasdaq closed above 24,000** (first time) and **S&P 500 above 7,000** (first time). Nasdaq logged its **11th straight up-day** on tech/semiconductor/Magnificent-7 momentum.
- **Tesla +8%** on Elon Musk's chip-development announcement; Nasdaq +1.6%. Dow relatively weak.
- Geopolitics easing: a rumored **two-week US–Iran ceasefire extension** was rebutted by the US, but a **second round of talks possible in ~48 hours (Islamabad)**. Mood compared to the now-ignored Russia–Ukraine war.
- US Treasury Secretary **Scott Bessent**: "we will not renew general licenses to import oil from Russia and Iran" — a risk to India's oil bill (India's basket had fallen from **$150 to $110/bbl** on cheap Russian crude).
- **Gift Nifty** pointed to a ~**90-point positive open**.
- Obituary: legendary EM investor **Mark Mobius (89)** passed away — the "Indiana Jones of Emerging Markets," long a 20–25% India-weighting bull. Tributes throughout the show.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty** rallied ~**1,900 points** off the late-March low (~22,331 → ~24,231 close), broad-based.
- **FIIs net buyers** again: ~**Rs 666 cr cash** + **Rs 1,243 cr index futures** (~Rs 2,000 cr total). Dollar Index slipping toward **98** (from 101) seen as supportive for EM/India flows. Rupee flat/narrow band.
- Extremely strong breadth: ~**9:1 advance/decline** (2,700+ adv vs 358 dec on BSE; ~2,827 vs 431 on NSE prior day).
- Volume breakouts flagged: **Kirloskar Electric +11.64%**, **Quality Power ~+11%**, KR Heat Exchanger, Thermax, Welcorp, Sarda Energy.
- **Today's results due:** Wipro (with buyback decision), HDFC Life, HDFC AMC, Angel One, renewables. **HDB Financial Services up ~9%** on good numbers at open. **ICICI Lombard** flat/mixed. **Tejas Networks -3%** (weak as expected, multiple management changes).
- Weekend results watch: **HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, SBI**.
- **Defense stocks** in focus on a pending Cabinet decision; **IT** participating after US tech strength.
- Caution: **Tata group stocks** (Tata Trusts/Mehli Mistry squabble) and **TCS** Nashik harassment case flagged as overhangs.
- At the open: Nifty +~110–150 points; defense buying; Mid Cap Index +1% (outperforming).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): present and active. Tech read bullish — **PCR 1.12**, **VIX ~18.67** (down ~8–9%, target 15–16). Resistance **24,300–24,800**, attempt at **24,500** likely; "buy on dips, not sell on rallies" until 25,000. Spec longs: Godfrey Phillips, Motilal Oswal, Siemens. Delivery buying: Nykaa, United Spirits, Marico, Biocon, Cipla. Advice: stay invested, buy on declines, use this rally as the **last chance to exit weak stocks**, rotate into stronger/fundamentally good names. (See dedicated Kutumba Rao file.)
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): trading idea — buy Nifty near **24,280**; **24,800 CE (28 Apr)** at ~Rs 90. Working on a breakout-stocks list. Shared a personal Mark Mobius / Tata Investment anecdote (bought ~Rs 200–250, exited Rs 800).
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst): tribute to Mark Mobius; discussion of **Howard Marks** ("Mastering the Market Cycle," "The Most Important Thing") vs Warren Buffett value investing; noted some value funds closing.
- **Vasanth** (anchor): macro framing, Mobius obituary, workshop logistics (book prizes — Howard Marks titles; registrations closing).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Ather Energy)

kutumba.note: Prefers Ather over Ola as of now
kutumba.detail: Asked Ola vs Ather (viewer Surya), Kutumba Rao's preference is Ather as of now. Ola may see a short-term move but Ather looks fundamentally better. On Ola, wait for results before gaining confidence since management always speaks bullishly.

kutumba.note: Long-term dividend play; hold, don't panic-sell
kutumba.detail: Viewer Sheshasai bought at Rs 449. Buy/hold only with a 2-5 year outlook; if you panic over a 10-20 rupee fall, don't touch it. It is a dividend play plus some capital-appreciation expectation, suited to low-risk-appetite investors. It rallied strongly and pulled back with volatility but will slowly recover - no worry if 5-10% down. Hold for the long term, not for short-term moves.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 15 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/FHYenKW9D0Q

## Global backdrop
- **Risk-on mood** over the last 36 hours (Tuesday was an Ambedkar Jayanti holiday). Trump said the West Asia second-round talks resume within days and told Fox News the **"war is closer to over."** The Israel–Lebanon hurdle to the ceasefire looks set to be resolved; both sides ready for direct talks.
- **Brent crude crashed ~$10**, from $103–104 to ~$94 (futures near $91 this morning) — a big positive for India.
- **US equities exuberant:** Dow +302 then +318; Nasdaq up ~700 points over two sessions; S&P 500 within 10–20 points of its all-time high. Magnificent Seven and semiconductors led; software stocks (incl. Infosys ADR +5% one night) staged a technical/positioning-led bounce — not a fundamental shift. Anthropic announced **Opus 4.7** (new tool for designing websites/presentations), a reminder of recurring disruption to software firms.

## Indian markets
- Markets set to open strong (Nifty pre-open +~320; opened above 24,000). The "algo trade" in play: **Brent down → banks/financials up**, so a multi-sector up-day expected (banks/financials, IT, autos, realty, metals).
- **Stock/news flow:** ICICI Pru AMC (market share up, revenue flat, PAT down sequentially); Poonawalla Fincorp QIP allotted at ₹370; **LIC 1:1 bonus + FPO prep** (counter to watch); Ujjivan SFB universal-banking-licence application **rejected by RBI**; Cyient buyback expected (~23rd). Q4 earnings season under way.
- **Key macro risk: the monsoon.** IMD/Skymet disappointing; Skymet flagged **~80% drought probability**; severe April heat. Would hit consumer and agri-related stocks.

## Opening session
- Strong open: Nifty +~380, Sensex +~1,300, Bank Nifty +1,000+, mid-cap +~1.9%. Breadth very positive (BSE advances 2,141 vs declines 322). Gainers: oil marketing (HPCL), InterGlobe Aviation, Hindalco +3.3%, L&T, IT pack, RailTel +6.5%, Hindustan Copper, Swaraj Engines +10%, Enviro Infra +9%, LG Electronics +7%. Losers (minor): Dr Reddy's, Oil India, Indus Towers, ICICI AMC, Vedanta, ONGC.
- **Hindustan Zinc** in limelight (successful Rajasthan bidder for a rare-earth/halite exploration; silver above $80).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — markets bottomed at the 20-day EMA Monday and reversed; war fear easing. Nifty immediate resistance 24,500 (gap-fill 24,450–24,500, 50-DMA there); supports 23,800 (OI) / 23,500. Bank Nifty +1,400 from low, resistance 55,700, supports 54,500. Breakout "kings" cited: Aether Industries, Lumax Auto, Godavari Power & Ispat, Groww, Adani Power, SMS Pharma, TD Power. VIX +8.7% on the holiday, likely to cool. FIIs sold ₹34,000 cr of index options Monday → first-hour volatility; intraday long near 24,120 after the first hour.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 7 buys (Manorama Industries, Bandhan Small Cap Fund, Invesco Small Cap Fund, Nava, Sansera Engineering, Shilchar Technologies, DCB Bank)

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 13 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/bYZ_KjhKWF8

## Global backdrop
- **US–Iran talks failed** over the weekend (negotiations led by JD Vance and the Iranian Parliament Speaker). The two-week ceasefire still holds, but Trump again threatened to "block" the Strait of Hormuz (already effectively shut). Markets fell across Asia.
- **Crude spiked ~9%:** crude crossed $104, Brent above $102 (vs $95–96 over the weekend). Markets now move directly with crude. S&P 500 futures -0.75%, Dow futures ~-360.
- A **prolonged war** is a real risk to India: crude/LPG supply disruption, higher diesel/ATF export duties hurting refiners, and a possible shortfall in the ~₹78,000 cr dividend the government expects from non-financial CPSEs. Rating agencies/brokers see GDP growth cut to ~6–6.5%.

## Indian markets
- After Friday's strong rally (Nifty had recovered ~70% of war losses; last week Nifty +~6%/1,300 pts, Bank Nifty +8%), markets set to **gap down ~350–400 pts** today. By open, Nifty was down ~490, Sensex ~1,640, Bank Nifty ~1,500; breadth very weak (NSE adv/dec ~1:10).
- **Tomorrow (Ambedkar Jayanti) is a market holiday**; today also has weekly/Sensex expiry → extra volatility.
- **FIIs bought ₹672 cr Friday** (first buy since Feb 26); DIIs +₹410 cr — but sustained FII buying is doubtful.
- Oil refiners/OMCs hit by crude + higher export duties (diesel duty raised sharply; ATF 29.50→42 paise/litre). Adani Energy top loser (-9%). Oil India a rare gainer (+1%).
- Results season on (ICICI Pru Life AMC, Elecon, HDB Financial, etc.).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Range-bound 23,500–24,500; buy quality on dips for the long term; large-caps to stabilize first, then mid/small. Cement and steel as war-resilient core sectors; contrarian view that large-cap IT fear (TCS hiring 25,000) is overblown — though he personally advised selling large-cap IT for great returns. Suggested shifting from Motilal Oswal Midcap (PE ~40, underperforming) to HDFC Midcap (PE ~23).
- **Kranti** (commodities/strategy) — Brent erratic (1-month high $119.50); news/emotion-driven market. Long-term holders need not worry; short/medium term "buy on dips, sell on rallies," buy the panic.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — knee-jerk reaction; supports at 23,500 (20-DMA) and 23,680–700 (Thursday low); RSI >54, MACD positive, VIX cooled. Positional long near 23,500 with 60-pt SL; intraday traders should avoid. Direction hinges on crude.

## Other notes
- Heavy coverage of the weekend TV5 investor **workshop** (one-liner contest, portfolio prizes); next session this weekend at Cyber Gardens.
- Caution flagged on **NSE unlisted shares** (~₹1,950 via InCred) — pricing is everything; "love before marriage" analogy on IPO euphoria (LIC, Tata Capital, Nykaa, Eternal, Paytm all faded post-listing).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 buys (Nifty BeES / Bank Nifty BeES, GMR Airports, JSW Infrastructure, Vedanta, TCS, Infosys)
Kranthi: 2 calls (NetWeb Technologies, Rasul Textiles)

kutumba.note: Hold; add only after Q4 results. Stabilizes ~540-550; electrographite EO duties rationalized, decent quarter likely.
kutumba.detail: HEG can be held; addition only after Q4 results. As of now stabilizes around 540-550. Product is electrographites; earlier EO duties rationalized so performance has decent/'good' possibility this quarter. Decide on fresh buying or extra adding only after results.

kutumba.note: Buy on dips; good attractive broad-market exposure; can attempt buying on a correction day like today.
kutumba.detail: Definitely buy on dips. To take exposure to the whole broader market, Nifty BeES and Bank Nifty BeES are good attractive bets; buy on the very day a correction comes, like today.

kutumba.note: Buy/accumulate in this weakness at these levels.
kutumba.detail: GMR Airports can be bought in this weakness; buy at these levels, accumulate.

kutumba.note: Accumulate; emerging among top-five infra players, definitely buy.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 10 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/yAI0v7YT5QQ

## Global backdrop
- **Ceasefire looks fragile**; US/Israel vs Iran tensions persist. Talks set for **Islamabad tomorrow** (JD Vance to lead US side, Iran's parliament speaker the other). Story now centred on **Lebanon (Hezbollah) and the Strait of Hormuz**.
- **US markets up a 7th straight session.** Brent eased to ~$96–97, but **physical oil is at a record premium** (Dubai spot ~$124–126 vs futures) — uncomfortable for importers like India. Only **8 ships moved through the Strait of Hormuz** (vs 100+/day pre-war); Iran charging a ~$2m/ship toll.
- India should keep buying Iranian/Russian oil; basket touched ~$150 earlier.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday Nifty **fell ~220 pts** on ceasefire cracks, but **mid/small-caps outperformed and closed positive** (breadth positive). Gift Nifty indicating ~+50–60 open today.
- This week: **Nifty +4.7%, mid-cap +6.2%, small-cap +5.9%**; real estate, defence, autos, capital markets led.
- **TCS results** (FY26 ~$30bn revenue) came in-line in rupee terms but **constant-currency growth ~ -0.5% YoY** — large-cap IT "lost its charm." Infosys ADR -2.7%, Wipro ADR -1.1% overnight; Nasdaq up.
- **Wipro announced a ~Rs 16,000 cr buyback** (April 16 board) — Kutumba Rao called it "a very bad move" (should conserve cash for AI reinvention).
- **FII selling easing** (~Rs 2,500 cr → ~Rs 1,700 cr); DIIs bought ~Rs 956 cr. Rupee strong (~92.47) partly on RBI forex-long restrictions whose deadline (April 10) is today.
- Calendar: **Tuesday market holiday → Monday expiry**; today (day before expiry) could be volatile. Hyderabad workshop this weekend (one-to-one sessions; venue Cyber Gardens near Shilparamam, Sunday).
- Active/strong stocks flagged: Ola Electric (+20% upper circuit, then +5% to ~38), ITI (+11%), Hitachi Energy (near 52-wk high ~27,000), Meesho, Thermax, Ather, Honasa, HFCL, Zen Tech, Angel One; ITDC, Apollo Micro on big volumes. BSE rising on approval to launch electricity derivatives.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 9 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/yNEANjBQzJE

## Global backdrop
- **Iran–US ceasefire in doubt.** Within hours of the ceasefire deal, Iran re-closed the **Strait of Hormuz** (on reports Israel kept striking Lebanon); Iran's parliament speaker Ghalibaf accused the US of violating 3 of its 10-point proposals (immediate ceasefire incl. Lebanon, civil uranium-enrichment right). Trump's stance has clearly softened — he wants the war to stop, having found it too costly. Anchor's read: Iran effectively "won" the war despite losing leaders. Even if Hormuz reopens, Iran may charge ~$1/barrel (≈Rs 20 cr per ship) toll.
- **Brent crude back above $97**, up 2–3% on the morning; staying above $100 would squeeze corporate margins. Decisive relief only below $85.
- **RBI** held rates (status quo); GDP growth seen ~6.9%; **World Bank** nudged India to ~6.6% (from 6.3). RBI Governor's commentary called rational/realistic; banking system deemed safe (IDFC episode bank-specific, not systemic).

## Indian markets
- Yesterday: **huge relief rally — Sensex/Nifty/Bank Nifty all +3%+**; Nifty +880 pts, broad-based, breadth very positive. Over the 40 days since the war began, Nifty had lost ~12% (PSU banks −11.2%, Bank Nifty −8%, Auto −7.7%); beaten-down sectors bounced hardest.
- **Today opened weak** (Nifty −140, Sensex −500, Bank Nifty −350) — a small gap-down, not a collapse; mid-caps relatively better (NSE A/D ~2:1).
- **Q4 earnings season is the main event:** TCS and GM Breweries report today (TCS after hours); TCS tipped for its highest revenue growth in 9 quarters — but mostly on rupee weakness. Bata reportedly eyeing a ~Rs 9,000 cr acquisition.
- Stock action: **KEC International** (+~7% on a big order), **Honasa Consumer** (+ on Q4 update), **Bosch** (+~2%, acquiring "Chassis"); losers incl. Force Motors (−3%, after yesterday's spike), AIA Engineering, HFCL.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna / "Kranti garu"** (technicals) — yesterday's gap-up is a bullish breakaway gap (won't fill quickly); RSI back above 50 (53.89); Bank Nifty up ~6,000 pts off the swing low, set to outperform; Nifty resistance ~24,500, supports 23,900/23,800; Bank Nifty resistance 57,000, support 55,000; buy on a dip to ~23,750. Also handled crude outlook and the Apollo Micro / Ashok Leyland / Waaree calls.
- Net view: stay invested, avoid "cats and dogs," watch company commentary on war/margin impact; FIIs still selling (~Rs 8,700 cr) though net cash+futures positive and short-covering seen — DIIs buying as usual.
