# Summary — Business Breakfast, 8 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/EotU_6H4Kag

## Global backdrop
- **Iran–US ceasefire announced** (brokered via Pakistan; Israel not in the picture). Both Iran and the US came forward, so the war has all but ended (a "two-week" ceasefire). Talks set for the 10th in Islamabad (J.D. Vance likely). Iran's terms (lift all sanctions, control of Strait of Hormuz, civil uranium enrichment) seen as unlikely to be fully accepted.
- **Brent crude crashed ~14% to ~$95** (from $115). Global risk-on: Taiwan +4%, KOSPI +6%, Dow futures +2%; Dollar Index at a two-week low; euro/yen/AUD/kiwi and the rupee rallying.
- GIFT Nifty signalled a **~680-point gap-up**. Anchor's lesson: India must prioritise energy security — deepen ties with Iran, buy more Russian oil. Watch Strait of Hormuz shipping recovery (was 100+ ships/day) and possible fall in Gulf remittances.

## Indian markets
- **RBI policy day:** rates expected on hold; the focus is commentary — inflation projection (3.5–4%?) and GDP forward guidance (Moody's cut to 6%; ~6.5% would be comforting). The RBI Governor, not the gap-up, is seen deciding the close.
- Markets had **bottomed and risen four straight sessions** on a building view that the war would end; today set up for a large **short-covering rally**.
- **Opening:** Nifty +~750 pts (pre-open ~23,855), Sensex +~2,550, **Bank Nifty +4% (outperformer)**, Auto index +5%. Advances ~2,450–2,519 vs ~50–161 declines; India VIX down ~19%. Up: InterGlobe Aviation +10%, L&T +8%, Shriram Finance +6.5%, UltraTech +5.5%, LIC +5.5% (bonus-issue talk), TBO Tek/Petronet LNG +10%. Down: ONGC, Oil India, Coal India −3%.
- **Beaten-down sectors set to bounce:** banking, real estate, infra, OMCs (BPCL, IOCL), Asian Paints, Pidilite, InterGlobe; crude-dependent names had been hammered on Middle-East exposure.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Market "discharged from ICU to general ward"; crude at $95 still 50%+ above old levels; use the rally to dump weak/dud stocks before earnings season and rotate into strong ones. FIIs sold ~Rs 45–46k cr in April (Rs 1.17 lakh cr in March); DIIs buying.
- **Ramakrishna / Kranti** (technicals) — crude must fall decisively to $70–80 (long-term green signal); Nifty about to cross its first swing high since Feb 3; 23,500 resistance likely cleared, next target ~50-DMA near 24,500; treat the gap-up positively (we're near the 52-week low, not the top). Trade gap-ups only with strict stop-losses.

Kranthi: 1 call (Laurus Labs)

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 7 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5XNyutWROjg

## Global backdrop (war footing)
- The Iran–US (West Asia) war is in its **sixth week** (~37–38 days). Trump set yet another deadline ("8 pm Eastern tonight") threatening to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges. Anchor treats it as empty bluster — Trump declared an unsolicited ceasefire ~12–15 days ago and may do so again; "a well in front, a ditch behind" — either he escalates (crude spikes) or backs down (looks weak).
- Why a quick end matters: Iran has been hitting Gulf energy infrastructure **and AI/web infrastructure** — already destroyed "Amazon servers," now eyeing the **$500bn Stargate AI project in the UAE**; the AI revolution could take a blow. Iran's ultimate weapon is **closing the Strait of Hormuz**.
- FM **Nirmala Sitharaman**: the war has "evolved into a systemic tremor," but India has "enough fiscal space to support war-hit sectors."

## Indian markets
- Tuesday = **Nifty options expiry**. Yesterday the Nifty bounced ~255 pts (led by HDFC Bank, Axis, ICICI, L&T) despite **Reliance dragging it down ~90 pts**; closed just below 23,000.
- **FIIs relentless sellers:** Rs 8,167 cr yesterday; **Rs 1.17 lakh cr in March** (~Rs 60,000 cr of it financials), continuing into April. DIIs bought only Rs 889 cr. Domestic funds/MFs are positioned for a relief rally.
- Recent higher highs: 22,679 (Apr 1) → 22,713 (Apr 2) → 22,968 (yesterday).
- Markets opened **very weak today**: Nifty ~-220 (22,750), Sensex ~-940; only Wipro & Hindalco green in the index. VIX still elevated >25; crude not cooling — **NYMEX (~113-115) now trades above Brent (~111)**, a war-driven reversal.
- Macro red flags: Moody's cut GDP to **6%**; govt planning a **Rs 2.5 lakh cr credit-guarantee (stimulus-like) scheme** for MSMEs; fertilizer shortage / rabi-crop uncertainty; induction-stove subsidies hint at a worsening **LPG crisis**; election-driven **labour crunch** (Assam, West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu) stoking inflation. **RBI policy meeting tomorrow.**
- Q4 business updates strong in jewellery (Titan, Kalyan, PC Jeweller) and Jubilant FoodWorks. Notable single-day pops: Senco Gold +10-12%, Lux Industries +20%, Venky's +12%, Banka BioLoo 10% UC, Vishal Mega Mart +10%.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Net stance: yesterday was a one-off; rally continuation doubtful; stay invested but no aggressive bets, sell weak counters on rallies; a relief rally (15-25% in oversold value names) possible but wait ~24h; trade stock-specifically with small positions.
- **Ramakrishna / "Kranti garu"** (technicals) — market stuck in a wide ~1,300-pt range (22,200–23,500); painful consolidation; 23,500 the swing-high to clear; closed above 10-DMA (positive); RSI >40, MACD positive crossover/green bar; VIX 25.47 (fear high). Trade light, strict stops. Gave a **1:3 ratio spread** (buy one 23,000 call, sell three 23,500 calls, hedge with far OTM 24,200).
- **Options strategy** (Kutumba Rao): straddle/strangle at 22,800 strike (buy both call and put) for the expiry — but premiums are high.

## Note on roles
The anchor is **Vasant/Vasanth garu**; he repeatedly presses Ramakrishna ("Kranti garu") for a directional call on whether Trump will attack — Ramakrishna refuses to predict Trump ("a shameless person... can do anything").
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (Vishal Mega Mart ("Walmart") Retail, Ather Energy, Kalyan Jewellers, Pidilite, SBI)
Kranthi: 3 buys (SBI, Canara Bank, Kotak Mahindra Bank)

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 6 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/izfEMVPK85I

## Global backdrop
- **US–Iran war in its 37th day (week six).** Trump posted obscene Truth Social threats over the weekend ("Power Plant Day and Bridge Day"), setting an 8 pm Tuesday ET deadline to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges — but has changed deadlines five times, so markets are taking it less seriously. Iran reportedly downed six US aircraft (unconfirmed); US rescued an F-15 airman.
- **Crude not reacting as feared:** Brent ~$110 (should be ~$120 on these statements); rare situation where **WTI/Nymex (~$112) is above Brent.**
- US: S&P 500 closed below its 200-DMA; **VIX cooled from 32 to ~23.** Trump told Fox News there's a "good chance" of a Tehran deal "as early as Monday" — not to be taken seriously.

## Indian markets
- Markets have fallen **six straight weeks** (Nifty down 10%+). Thursday saw a big gap-down (Nifty 22,330, Bank Nifty 49,980) then a sharp recovery near day's high; A/D ratio ~2:1 positive.
- Signalling a slightly negative open (~90 pts on Gift Nifty), but opened roughly flat/positive intraday. India VIX still high at ~25.5 — uncomfortable for bulls.
- **Heavy FII selling** (~₹9,930 cr; >$1bn) vs DII buying (₹7,200 cr); markets still closed up on short-covering.
- **Q4 updates strong** (HDFC Bank, RBL, Bank of Baroda, Bank of India, MOIL, L&T Finance, Dabur, DMart, Senco Gold +8%); bank credit growth strong (HDFC ~15%), but deposit growth lagging → CDR near all-time high (~83%).
- **RBI MPC meeting 6th–8th** (outcome 8th) — rates expected unchanged; commentary on war/crude/inflation is the focus. TCS results Apr 9, then Infosys etc.
- **Section 232: 100% tariff on patented pharma** — hits Sun Pharma; generics (Dr Reddy's, Aurobindo, Biocon) largely unaffected.

## OFS / IPO warning (Kranti + Kutumba Rao)
- Business Line data: of 300 IPOs in 5 years, **68 pure offer-for-sale deals raised ₹1.76 lakh cr** (money went only to promoters, not companies), and nearly all now trade below listing-day price — ~₹95,000 cr of investor wealth eroded. Exceptions: Mankind Pharma, Anand Rathi, ICICI Pru AMC, KFin. Be very careful with pure-OFS IPOs.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Market in "sell-on-rise" mood; stay invested if holding, bottom-fish select sectors via valuations if already out; long-term Nifty TRI doubles every ~6–7 yrs.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — after six-week fall, set for a possible relief/dead-cat bounce. Key resistance/swing-high **23,500** (needs a close above for a new setup); supports 22,000 / 22,200; Bank Nifty calls 52,000, puts 51,000.
- **Kranti** — flagged the OFS investor-wealth erosion; will detail it in the upcoming seminar.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 1 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/VwB7ZJs-GMY

## Global backdrop
- **First day of FY2026-27.** A Trump statement that the US will withdraw from the Iran war "in two or three weeks" lifted global markets: US equities closed very strong (S&P's best day of 2026, though still its worst quarter in 4 years), crude fell from ~$112 to ~$105.
- Trump said the US has "achieved its objective" in Iran, won't seek regime change, and will pull back "without reopening the Strait of Hormuz." For the first time Iran's side signalled willingness to cease fire if Israel/US give firm guarantees. China and Pakistan floated a 5-point peace plan. Trump to address the nation (6:30am IST) on the war.
- Caveat: even if the war ends, after-effects will be "pretty big" — Iran is expected to start charging tolls (Rs 15-20 cr per large ship) at the Strait of Hormuz; global rates uncertain for 5-6 months; US midterms ahead.

## Indian markets
- **Gap-up opening** (~1.5% for Nifty vs 3-4% for Korea/Japan). At open: Nifty +~500-570, Sensex +~1,600-1,800, **Bank Nifty +~2.6% (slight outperformer)**. 49 of 50 Nifty stocks up (only ONGC down on lower crude). BSE breadth: 2,349 advances vs 335 declines.
- **FY26 was bad:** Nifty -5% for the year, with March alone -11.5% wiping out the year's gains; rupee -9.88% (to ~95). FIIs sold ~Rs 1.6 lakh cr (Rs 11,000 cr on Mar 30 alone); DIIs bought ~Rs 8.5 lakh cr (Rs 14,815 cr on Mar 30) — that support is the only reason the index isn't near 17,000-18,000.
- **Historical hope:** since 2004, every year the Nifty fell, it rose the next year (FY09 -36% → FY10 +73.7%; FY20 -26% → FY21 +70%). April has been positive in 9 of last 10 years.
- IPO market raised a record Rs 1.79 lakh cr in FY26, but 71% of IPOs trade at a loss; Sai Parenterals managed only ~1x subscription.
- Disinflation in food (potatoes -40%, onions -50%, tomatoes -80%, wheat -10%, rice -5-6%) due to lack of exports to the Gulf. ATF prices +15% (only foreign carriers, not domestic — InterGlobe +3-4%).
- HDFC Bank active (~1.25 cr volume, +2.5%) after Atanu Chakraborty's resignation news; could lead a rally.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Expects FY27 to be a "relief year," ~15-20% returns; favours PSUs/pharma at lower valuations; careful stock/sector selection is key.
- **Kranthi** (technicals, first segment) — current move is a "dead-cat bounce" until crude decisively cools; markets are cyclical, stay invested, continue SIPs. Likes Ather Energy on dips.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals, second segment) — weak setup but bounce expected; 22,000 big support, 22,800 then 23,465 / ~23,500 resistance. VIX very high (~28, cutting 9% today) → do put-selling (22,000 put, SL 100).
- Workshop ("Ideas Unlimited") promoted heavily — TAM-growth sectors (CDMO pharma 50k cr → 1.5 lakh cr in 5 yrs; EVs).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Suzlon)
Kranthi: 1 buy (Ather Energy)

kutumba.detail: Hold, no need to panic and sell. Even if the last quarter was slightly weak, it is doing extremely well; valuations for all hospital stocks are improving, so the stock won't take long to recover.

kutumba.note: One of the better stocks; benefits from semiconductor recovery and Gujarat plant commissioning.
kutumba.detail: Definitely one of the better stocks; with the semiconductor recovery and once the Gujarat plant is commissioned, definitely hold.

kutumba.detail: At current levels you shouldn't sell, because recently the stock has seen a lot of ups and downs; positive on the data-centre story, and it is now available at a decent valuation (~436).

kutumba.detail: Hold; worst almost over. Railway monopoly (reservations + catering) but single-customer type; already ~518; unless results dramatically improve, becoming a stable dividend player from here on; don't expect outperforming gains (PE already 25-26).

kutumba.detail: Hold; for range-bound players definitely better times ahead, especially after the oil crisis. Institutional selling absorbed, stock holding ~40-42; at 52-week low it's in fact a fresh buy, even as a surprise bet, since the stock at least shows profits.

kutumba.detail: Definitely hold.

kranti.note: EV two-wheeler; very resilient. Add slowly on dips; petrol price hikes post-election could boost EV space.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 30 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/hiScytDt1Us

## Global backdrop
- **West Asia war enters its fifth week** and keeps escalating. **Yemen's Houthi rebels** have joined, launching ballistic missiles at Israel and threatening the **Bab-el-Mandeb** strait — a second chokepoint on top of the **Strait of Hormuz** (~**20% of global oil supply**), where traffic has nearly halted (~**20 million barrels/day** disrupted).
- **Brent crude ~$115-117/barrel**. Houses are flagging extreme tail risks: a **Macquarie** report warns that if crude hits **$200** it would be hard to come back, likely settling near **$150**. **Goldman Sachs** three-scenario framing: 6 weeks of disruption → ~**$120**, possibly back to **$80** if conditions change; 10 weeks without infrastructure damage → ~**$140** then **$95**; >10 weeks with production wiped out → **$160+**, staying above **$100**.
- **All asset classes under selling pressure** (equities, debt, currency, commodities) as hedge funds face margin calls; even **gold and silver** are being sold. **Only the dollar (DXY >100)** is a safe haven.
- **US 10-year yield ~4.4%**, possibly heading to **4.5-4.6%**. **Trump** is anxious ahead of **November mid-terms**; Iran refuses to back down after its leadership was killed. **Recessionary global conditions** would not be a surprise. Color: Trump floated a "next attack on Cuba" remark while the US simultaneously allowed Russian oil tankers to Cuba ("no sanctions").

## Indian markets
- **FII selling severe**: ~**$12.313 billion** sold in March; Friday net FII sell ~**Rs 4,367 cr**, DII buy ~**Rs 3,566 cr**. More than selling, there's now a **"buyers' strike"** plus **HNI tax-harvesting** weighing on the tape (continuing today/tomorrow).
- **RBI forex action (Friday):** capped banks' net open forex positions at **$100 million** (no cap existed before), **deadline April 10**. Forces banks to unwind dollar-long/overseas-short trades — could trigger up to ~**$40 billion** of dollar selling, lifting the rupee. Rupee closed Friday near **94.81** (~90 paise fall in a day; 90→95 in two months); expected to recover toward **93.5-93.8** at the open. A rupee recovery could give equities a relief bounce, but the forex-unwind losses hit bank P&L this quarter.
- **Opening 30 March:** very weak — Nifty down **~270-300+ pts**, Sensex down **~950-1,000 pts**, **Bank Nifty down ~2-2.5%** (near **50,960**), led by forex-exposed banks (Axis, Kotak, BoB, Union Bank -8%). Advance/decline a poor **328 vs 1,680**.
- **Today is Nifty monthly + fiscal-year-end expiry**; tomorrow market closed for **Mahavir Jayanti** → high volatility expected.
- **Gainers (war/commodity driven):** **ONGC** (crude play), **Hindalco/Nalco** (aluminium prices spiked after a Bahrain aluminium plant was attacked), NTPC; plus Jubilant Ingrevia, Shyam Metalics, GSK Pharma, Thermax, Ipca, Dr. Lal PathLabs, Page Industries.

## Technical view (Ramakrishna)
- All indicators **negative / weak**. Nifty key support is the recent low **22,471**; a break opens the **52-week low** zone — April 2025 levels of **~21,900 / 21,744**, i.e. another **~4% downside**.
- Resistance: Nifty swing high **23,465**; Bank Nifty resistance **50,146** swing high, support **51,323**. **100-DMA broke 200-DMA**; Bank Nifty's **50-DMA cutting 200-DMA**.
- OI: highest call/put writing clustered at **23,000 and 22,000**.

## Analysts featured
- **Vasanth** — anchor (hosting via Zoom from Vijayawada; no live calls, mail-based Q&A).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst (on screen; chart-based entries, OI/levels).
- **Kranthi** — fundamental analyst (joined after the break; handled all fundamental viewer Q&A).
- **Kutumba Rao — NOT present today** (explicitly: "Kutumba did not join today"). His only mention: a **23,000 put** he had given on an earlier day is now in big profit (~930 vs ~500 cost) — a past call referenced by Ramakrishna, not a call made on this episode.

## Workshop note
- Capital-protection / hedging / basket-investing workshop pushed: **Batch 1 April 11-12**, **Batch 2 April 18-19**; analysts Kutumba Rao, Kranthi and Ramakrishna to present. Repeated "**Protect your capital first**" theme.

## Stock calls today (all by Kranthi / Ramakrishna — see kutumba_rao.md note; none are Kutumba Rao's)
- **Defence (Kranthi):** Cochin Shipyard (top pick, attractive valuations) > Garden Reach (higher risk/beta) > Mazagon Dock; BHEL/HBL last (large-cap, pricey, gives stability).
- **Pharma:** Granules (Ramakrishna — at multiple tops, breakout → all-time high, 10-20% upside from ~624); Natco Pharma (both — strong, bottoming, restructuring ahead, defensive).
- **Hold/Accumulate (Kranthi):** Archean Chemicals (hold; accumulate below 550-500), CG Power (hold; accumulate on dips — semiconductor theme), Sanofi India (add slowly; PE ~20-21; accumulate below 3,000), Telecon Engineering (add on dips), Kaynes Technology (add slowly, long-term), Coal India (buy — "safe place"; 50% at 447, balance above 475 — entry via Ramakrishna), BEL (accumulate slowly).
- **Hold, no averaging now (Kranthi):** Jaggle Prepaid, Transrail (IPOs testing issue prices).
- **BDL vs Midhani:** BDL the better stock (add on dips; book some profit 1,700-1,800); Midhani lagging on tech investment.
- **Bosch (Ramakrishna):** wait for ~1,500-pt dip toward 26,500-27,500 for first lot.
- **Anant Raj (Ramakrishna):** don't enter now; weak/multiple bottoms — only after it crosses ~520-530.
- **Likhitha Infra (Kranthi):** add (AP piped-gas tailwind); Deep Industries / Epack Durables — accumulate slowly after results.
- **Caplin (Caption) (Kranthi):** OK to buy — defensive manpower-services stock, low war exposure.
- **Mutual funds:** switch out of underperforming Tata Smallcap to Nippon India / Bandhan / Invesco / HDFC / Quant Smallcap; continue good-performing SIPs.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 27 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/Q1bLzhvcfa0

## Global backdrop
- **West Asia war into its 5th week.** Trump announced another **10-day ceasefire/truce** (to April 6) "at Iran's request," but after US market close — the show is deeply skeptical (trust deficit; talks with unnamed parties; a WSJ report says the US may send 10,000 ground troops). Trump also claimed a "gift" from Iran: 10 Pakistan-flagged tankers allowed through the Strait of Hormuz.
- **US equities fell:** S&P 500 −1.74%, Nasdaq similar; S&P and Nasdaq technically in **correction (−10% from highs)**. Chip/storage stocks hit after Google announced a new chip that cuts AI storage needs ~17%. Futures later up 0.5–1%.
- **Crude is the key risk:** Brent ~$106 (rose 5.5% over two sessions, did NOT fall on ceasefire news). Gold −3.3%, silver −6.8–8%. Bond yields rising globally → inflation signal. OECD cut world GDP / raised inflation for 2026.

## Indian markets
- Nifty recovered 800 pts over two sessions (from ~22,500 to ~23,300); resistance ~23,380–23,850, support ~23,000. Markets set to **open weak** (~130–150 pts down) on the war news and a record-low rupee.
- **Rupee at 94.23/$ — an all-time low, above 94 for the first time.** Forex-loan hedging risk premium up 200% in a month.
- **Goldman Sachs & HSBC downgraded India.** Goldman cut CY2026 GDP from 6.5–7% to 5.9%, earnings growth to ~8%, and the **Nifty 12-month target from 29,500 to 25,900**.
- FIIs sold ~₹1,800 cr (Wed); DIIs bought ~₹5,430–5,500 cr.
- **At the open:** Nifty −~230, Sensex −764, Bank Nifty −680 (−1.38%, the worst index; all 14 constituents red). IT positive (TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech) on rupee weakness; Coal India and OMCs positive. Excise duty cut on petrol (₹13→₹3) and removed on diesel (→0) — relief to OMCs, not consumers. Hyderabad saw petrol/diesel queues from a botched advance-payment policy by OMCs.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Headline call: buy a **Nifty 23,000 put** as portfolio hedge; expects a broad 22,500–24,000 range; "new normal" of $100 crude is most probable.

- Workshop (April 11–12 and 18–19, identical, 400/week) heavily promoted — focus on war-resilient sectors and basket investing.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 25 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/4JiNu4E-f_s

## Global backdrop
- **"De-escalation" is the word of the morning.** Peace efforts are under way on the Israel–Iran/US war from both sides; Iran signalled willingness to talk and said it will keep the **Strait of Hormuz open to "non-hostile" (friendly-nation) ships**. The US is pushing a **one-month ceasefire**; Turkey, Egypt and Pakistan are active mediators. Trump spoke with Modi by phone. Caution: tensions could flare again — "Trump cannot be trusted."
- **Brent crude fell to ~$98** (saw $94 in early hours; was $104–105 the day before) — a positive. Gold ~$4,600, silver ~$74, both firm. Story still revolves around crude; no risk to markets if crude sustains below $100 toward ~$80.
- US markets closed lower (Nasdaq −180, Dow ~−100) but **US futures strongly up (~+280 Dow futures)**; Asian markets firm (Nikkei +2.6%, Hang Seng +0.5%). GIFT Nifty signalled a ~150-point gap-up.

## Indian markets
- Set to open up for a **second straight session**. Yesterday Sensex and Nifty closed ~1.78% positive; breadth ~1:3 positive (2,480 adv vs 805 dec). PCR jumped to 1.07 (from 0.82); VIX fell over 8% (26+ → ~24) — a "bottom-out" feeling.
- **FIIs relentless sellers** — ~Rs 8,000 cr in cash yesterday, ~Rs 1.16 lakh cr over 18 straight sessions (exiting emerging markets broadly). **DIIs buying** (~Rs 1,500–5,800 cr) but less than FII selling.
- **Oil & gas / gas pipelines in focus** — PM announced fast-tracking "accelerated pipeline infrastructure development" (positive for pipeline/city-gas cos, Engineers India). Pharma strong.
- **IPL-team theme:** United Spirits selling RCB for ~$1.8bn (~Rs 16,700 cr); early team investors made 40–57x. Listed plays: Sun TV (+3.5%), **RPSG Ventures (+16%; Lucknow team ~250% of its market cap)**.
- News: **Manipal Hospitals ~$1.2bn IPO** (likely India's largest healthcare listing). SEBI consultation paper to **allow "gifting" mutual-fund investments via PPIs** (effective ~after April 14). RVNL–Ircon merger talk; PSU-bank merger watch; IDBI divestment uncertainty. Zerodha/brokers raising F&O fees. Avantel +11% on a Rs 460 cr order; PG Electroplast +9% on AC production normalising.
- Holiday-heavy stretch: Sensex expiry today; tomorrow Sri Rama Navami; Nifty monthly expiry next Monday; Good Friday after.

## Analysts featured

Bank Nifty resistance ~54,382, support ~51,438; RSI oversold (31–34). 23,000 has highest call + put writing → likely closes above 23,000. Trade only with hedging/stop-losses given holidays.
- Market opened strongly: Nifty +200, Sensex +670, Bank Nifty +700; every Bank Nifty stock positive a second day. IT index the lone loser (TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech, Wipro, Tech Mahindra).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 24 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/kT8ZzpKdSdY

## Global backdrop
- **Trump flip-flop on Iran:** After a 48-hour ultimatum, **Donald Trump** reversed course within 24 hours and announced a **five-day ceasefire/pause**, saying no attacks on Iran's energy infrastructure and that talks are "making good progress" (posted on Truth Social).
- **Iran pushback:** Iran's Foreign Minister called it **fake news**, alleging Trump is **manipulating oil and financial markets**; the Iranian Parliament Speaker also denied any talks. **Netanyahu** said Israel is "trying to stop the war."
- **Pre-open anomaly:** Around **6:50am US time** (before the 7:00 statement at ~7:04am), billions of dollars of shares were bought in **S&P 500 pre-open** — raising **insider-trading suspicions**.
- **Market moves:** S&P 500 futures **+2.5% to +5%**; **NYMEX crude -10%** (from ~USD 98 to USD 86, below USD 90); **Brent** from ~USD 113-114 to below USD 100, now ~USD 104. **Dow** gained as much as **1,100 points**, closed **+600**.
- **Asia mixed:** Hang Seng **+1.3%**, Nikkei **+0.7%**; **Dow futures ~250 points negative** by morning. Anchor coined the **"TACO" (Trump Always Chickens Out)** trade.
- **Key signal to watch:** normal vessel/cargo traffic resuming through the **Strait of Hormuz** = real end of conflict.

## Indian markets
- **GIFT Nifty** had been up **800-900 points** overnight, cooled to **~300-360 points** gap-up by morning.
- **Wealth destruction:** **Rs 15 lakh crore** of investor wealth lost in a single day yesterday; **Rs 50 lakh crore** eroded over the last 24 days (since Feb 28) on BSE market cap.
- **Yesterday's flows:** **FIIs sold > Rs 10,000 crore**, **DIIs bought > Rs 12,000 crore**; Nifty closed **600 points negative** (below 22,500) after a late recovery during the PM's Parliament speech.
- **Open (pre-open trade):** Nifty **+370 points**, Bank Nifty **+947 points**; cash open **~22,815 (+~300)**. Advance/decline strongly positive (BSE 2,034 adv / 315 dec; NSE 2,148 adv / 127 dec) — reverse of yesterday's close.
- **Volatility:** **India VIX spiked to 26.73** (highest in nearly two years); FII **put-call ratio 0.84**. **Rupee** near **93.94-94/USD**.
- **Gold:** fell to ~**USD 4,200**, recovered to **~USD 4,350** with Trump statement and rising crude; analyst doubts precious metals will keep acting as a safe haven.
- **Sector/stock action at open:** gains in consumer durables, auto, metals, PSU & private banks, oil & gas; **Asian Paints +3%** (price-hike news); buying in **Electra Greentech, JBM Auto, GR Infra** (order-win). **HDFC Bank down ~4%** despite the rally (bank-specific concerns not being digested).
- Two IPOs opening today: **Pavarika** and **Sai Parenteral**.

## Sai Parenteral IPO (featured)
- **Hyderabad-based injectables/CDMO maker**, promoted by MD **K. Anil Kumar**. **Raise Rs 409 crore**, price band **Rs 372-392**, **listing April 2**.
- **Valuation:** FY25 EPS **Rs 5.43**, **PE ~72x** (anchor called it ~70x), P/Rev **6.4x**, P/B **10.9x** — "expensive" on trailing, but anchor's forward case sees **EBITDA ~Rs 305 crore by FY28-30 (50% CAGR)**, **fair value ~Rs 700/share (~78% upside by FY30)**.
- **Anchor book ~Rs 146 crore** (oversubscribed vs Anil's Rs 122 crore portion); names include **Kotak (incl. 100% pharma fund), Morgan Stanley, India Emerging Fund (Dubai), Blue Lotus Fund**.
- **Story:** acquired **74.6% of Australian Newmed Pharmaceuticals** (451 IP dossiers) for Rs 131 crore; backward integration in Australia with a **non-repayable USD 20m grant** (40% of USD 51m plant cost), targeting **~3x margin** uplift. Pre-IPO/early backers: **Mohandas Pai (Aarin Capital), Nikhil Kamath (Gruhas), Polycab family office, KIMS' Bhaskar Rao**. Promoter stake 61% pre → ~52% post.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — fundamental/market view + viewer Q&A (Bata, Protean). Endorsed the Sai Parenteral IPO. (See dedicated file.)
- **Kranthi** — answered viewer Q&A on L&T / Home First / Jio Financial / BEL (and a hold-list).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst; flagged extreme volatility, recommended a trading halt / tax harvesting near year-end.
- **Anil Kumar** — Sai Parenteral MD (IPO interview).
- **Vasanth** — anchor.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 calls (Bata India, Protean eGov Technologies)

kutumba.note: Not a great choice; better strong stocks available
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said Bata is not a great choice as of now. The stock's valuations are attractive after the recent correction, but there are much better financially strong stocks to be purchased. He said avoid Bata.

kutumba.note: Speculative, risky bet as of now
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao called Protean a speculative bet that becomes a bit risky as of now. He did not recommend buying it at this time.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 19 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/C4xYRrKdukg

Ugadi special episode (Sri Parabhava-nama year). Despite the festive opening, the market begins sharply negative on a triple shock: Middle-East war escalation, an HDFC Bank boardroom crisis, and a hawkish US Fed.

## Global backdrop
- **War escalation:** Israel attacked Iran's **South Pars** gas field (described as the world's largest, ~**730 million cubic metres** of gas, on the Iran–Qatar border). Trump claims it happened "without US involvement." Iran threatens Saudi Aramco, UAE and Qatar energy fields; Iran's intelligence minister reportedly killed.
- **Energy/imports:** India imports ~**48% of its LNG from Qatar**; Qatar recently halted LNG supply citing "force majeure." **Brent crude back above $111** (futures already at **$112**; high-sulphur regular crude ~**$97**).
- **US Fed:** FOMC held rates (currently **3.5%–3.75%**) with **hawkish** comments from departing chair **Jerome Powell** (higher gas prices hurt personal incomes). Vote **11:1**. Market now prices **at most one rate cut in 2026** (possibly none). **Dollar index above 100** (~100.11). US jobs weak, unemployment unchanged but signs of slow rise; AI impact a side risk.
- **Asia:** **Nikkei -2.5%**, **Hang Seng -0.5%** at open. US markets fell overnight on Fed.

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty** indicated a **~450-point gap-down** (peaked at 550–560 down, recovered ~100).
- At open: **Nifty -430 pts**, **Sensex ~-1470 pts**, **Bank Nifty ~-1360 pts**. Sensex **expiry** day. Pre-open Nifty showed ~580-pt loss.
- **HDFC Bank crisis:** Part-time chairman & independent director **Atanu Chakravarty** (ex-IAS, Gujarat cadre, joined May 2021, oversaw HDFC Ltd–HDFC Bank merger) **resigned** citing "moral/ethical reasons." **KK Mistry** (former vice chairman, age 71) appointed interim chairman for 3 months. Reported personal differences with MD **Sashidhar Jagdishan**. ADR fell **7–8%** overnight; stock down ~**8–9% pre-open**, recovered to **~5%** after a 9am analyst call where management said nothing is wrong. HDFC Bank = **11.9% of Nifty**, **18.7% of Bank Nifty**, near **52-week low**; 5-yr return ~2% (2% CAGR), 10-yr ~12% CAGR.
- **Rupee** at a record **92.63/USD**. **Gold** firmly below $5,000 (~**$4,850**), down **14% from high**; Kutumba Rao sees a possible freak move to $4,100–4,200.
- **FII/DII (prior day):** FIIs sold **~Rs 2,700 cr**, DIIs bought **~Rs 3,250 cr**.
- **Three-day "unloved rally"** (Sensex +~2,000 pts, Nifty to ~23,800) seen fizzling; portfolios saw little gain.
- **Options/technicals:** Heavy call OI at 24000/24500 (panic unwind expected); puts piled at 23000; key support **23000 / 22955**; sustained move below 23500 → further weakness. **PCR** 1.14 (could fall below 1); **VIX** below 19 (could spike to 21–22). Death cross (50-DMA below 200-DMA); analyst expects vertical fall could mean a faster recovery (3–4 months) if a ceasefire comes.
- **Active/themes:** Yesterday's gainers — EV names (**Olectra Greentech +Rs150, JBM Auto, Ola Electric**) on Maharashtra 5-yr fuel-cut announcement; capital-market stocks (**NSDL, CDSL**); **Urban Company**. Speculative longs in builders, REC, Waaree Energies, Tata Elxsi, Persistent. Possible active names today: PFC, Bharat Forge, APL Apollo.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A) — present and central; gave his own stock calls (see kutumba_rao file).
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst) — handled BEL on dips and discussed HDFC Bank (no position; would buy on dips, eyeing ~700).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) — death cross, support 23000/22955, possible double-bottom; avoid trading on volatile expiry day; HDFC Bank not yet a buy contrarian-wise.

## Overall stance
Stay balanced "like the Ugadi pachadi" — don't celebrate or panic on one-two day moves. Hold existing positions; retail investors stay on sidelines / wait-and-watch. Lengthen investment horizon to 2–3 years. Good IPO stocks may give decent 1–2 year returns. Avoid gold/precious metals short-term. No decoupling from global weakness while crude is high, rupee weak and FIIs selling.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 buys (HDFC Bank, Laurus Labs, Natco Pharma, Bank of India, Manorama Industries, Precision Wires)

kutumba.note: Bought himself today on cheap valuation
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said he bought HDFC Bank today, around Rs 795 (it came twice), purely on valuation - a rare ~15.4 PE and price-to-book of ~2.6. He noted management is confident, with interim chairman KK Mistry and MD Sashidhar Jagdishan giving positive assurances after the boardroom resignation.

kutumba.note: Definitely buy in this correction
kutumba.detail: He said Laurus Labs is definitely a buy in this correction, with results that keep appearing better and better. He grouped it with Natco as a buy.

kutumba.note: Turnaround story, something cooking
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said both Laurus and Natco can be bought. Natco has fully turned around and now pays a dividend (a token 10% interim dividend was approved). A board meeting on the 24th will separate/split the agrochemicals business. He is trying to meet management again and believes something else is cooking in the company.

kutumba.note: Good working company
kutumba.detail: Asked by the anchor whether Bank of India can be bought, Kutumba Rao said Bank of India and Manorama are both good working companies.

kutumba.note: Niche segment, QIP underway
kutumba.detail: He called Manorama a good working company in a niche segment (e.g. making butter from nuts), with a QIP underway now. He recalled the last QIP preceded a ~200% return, and this QIP comes after a long time.

kutumba.note: Start accumulating at these levels
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said Precision Wires can definitely be accumulated at true levels and advised starting to accumulate.

kutumba.note: Hold; exit on relief rally after Q4
kutumba.detail: For a caller down 25% on LTTS, he said 25% is not a big loss in current conditions. The whole IT-services sector is in a transformation stage so it is hard to call, but he advised holding because management backing gives flexibility. One could exit on a relief rally after Q4; review again in a few days.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 18 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/6BEGlwp63Ng

## Global backdrop

- **Asian markets mixed** Wednesday morning; **GIFT Nifty** pointing to a slightly positive open. **US markets** came off highs but closed with gains; **Nasdaq** weakened below a technical level (under 22,500). **Dow futures +~120 pts**, **Nikkei strong (~2%)**, Hang Seng flat.
- **War continues** with no sign of de-escalation. Trump criticised NATO members for not supporting the US; his **Xi Jinping summit postponed ~5-6 weeks**. Iran continues strikes targeting UAE/Dubai; Israeli forces announced killing **Iran's security chief / intelligence officer** ~3pm — yet markets showed resilience.
- **Crude:** Brent near **102** (102.09); but India's relevant **heavy/high-sulphur crude is below 95**, down ~2% this morning. India barely uses Brent (only ~2-3% for specific purposes).
- **Indian crude basket** (Brent + Oman + Dubai blend) doubled in ~15 days: **70.9 (Feb) → 127.2 → 136.56 → ~142.69** (Oman ~150, Dubai ~130, Brent ~103), with future risk premium included (~138-140 in papers). If sustained near 130, a **threat to the economy**.
- **Macro warning (CEA Nageswaran):** if oil stays high, **GDP growth could fall ~1% to ~6.4%** (from ~7.4-7.5% estimate) and **inflation could rise to ~5.2%** — coming just as earnings are improving.

## Indian markets

- Two positive sessions: Nifty up ~**420-430 pts** off the low, **Sensex +1,500 pts**; mid-/small-caps led the up-move.
- **Breadth positive** (open: 1796 gainers / 1224 losers). Higher highs / higher lows in Nifty and Bank Nifty.
- **VIX cooled to 19.79** (−8%), back below 20. **PCR 1.09** (above 1).
- **FII short-covering** two days running: short contracts 2.6 lakh (Mar 13) → **2.4 lakh** (20k covered). FII selling eased to **Rs 4,741 cr** (vs Rs 6-7k avg); **DII bought Rs 5,225 cr**.
- **Rupee near 92.40**, close to all-time low, on crude + sustained FII outflows.
- **Sector rotation in the war environment:** strength in **metals, energy, autos, industrials/capital goods, defence**; selling in **IT** (Nvidia's new AI/CPU tools reviving doubts on labour-based IT billing) and parts of **FMCG**.
- Notable moves: **MOIL +20%** (investor presentation; disinvestment/QIP signals), **Shaily Engineering +19%**, **Webel Solar +15%**, **ideaForge +12%** (1cr+ shares), Jyoti CNC +5% (1.2cr shares), Raymond Realty +15-16%, OCTL ±20% on alternate days.
- **Open:** Nifty +~100-128 pts (hit a "century"), Sensex +~300-390 pts, **Bank Nifty underperforming**; mid/small-caps outperforming, breadth very positive (2131 adv / 376 dec on NSE later). **IT pack strong** at open on Nasdaq lead; autos and InterGlobe +2% firm.

## Analysts featured

- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental): bounce was mostly **short-covering**; **23,600 is the key level** today, then a 23,400-24,000 zone. Stay invested but **exit weaker stocks** on relief; **avoid gold** (risk of 600-700 dollar drop below 5,000/oz). Likes ore/metal PSUs (Coal India, MOIL, NMDC). Answered viewer calls on Persistent/Coforge (avoid averaging), Cams/Dixon (wait-watch), Cyient DLM (watch), NCC/Suzlon/Vimta (accumulate), NBCC (hold). **His Zoom froze midway**, so other analysts took over several calls.
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst): Adani Power (slow add, resilient), Heritage Foods (slow add, Shameerpet ice-cream plant), HAL (slow accumulate, consistent compounder), Jio Financial (add, Bajaj Allianz tie-up), Servotech (hold, governance issues).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): still a **weak/cautious setup** — death-cross (50DMA below 200DMA), below all key MAs, lower-high formations. **Resistance 24,000, support 23,000**; long-viks formed at bottom is a positive. RSI ~33, MACD recovering. **Cautious trading, strict stop-losses, hedge overnight**. Calls: Netweb (add), Gabriel (accumulate). On open: long-attempt possible if Nifty holds ~120 pts above for 15 min.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (NCC, Suzlon, Vimta Labs)

kutumba.note: Stop averaging down; mid-cap IT outlook weak
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said headwinds in IT are clearly high and Persistent and Coforge are among the weakest on the weak side. He advised the caller (who was averaging down at a 20-30% loss) to stop adding for now, saying averaging only on a 5-10% price fall is wrong; reduce exposure into any dead-cat-bounce / intermittent rally. The business-model re-invention and AI-adoption story will decide the stock, which may stay range-bound for now.

kutumba.note: Stop averaging down; mid-cap IT outlook weak
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao grouped Coforge with Persistent (and LTTS) as mid-cap IT names where doubts about the business model are being expressed. He said this is not the time to add and one should cut exposure on any rally. The stocks may stay range-bound; how the companies re-invent/transform and adapt AI by next year is the key.

kutumba.note: Wait and watch; not a bad stock but temporarily weak
kutumba.detail: On the EMS/financial-infra names, Kutumba Rao said Cams and Dixon are the two weak ones; once aggressively bought by institutions, institutional interest has now fallen. He told the caller to stop trying to add for now and wait-and-watch, calling them not bad companies but going through a temporarily bad phase.

kutumba.note: Wait and watch; EMS supply-chain fears
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said EMS companies face fears that the logistics chain will be upset, and a weakening economy from the crude crisis could indirectly hit sales of fast-moving electrical goods, with Dixon especially exposed. As of now he advised wait-and-watch, saying it is not a bad stock but in a temporarily bad phase; do not average down.

kutumba.note: Wait and watch; check if institutional exit or a bottom
kutumba.detail: On Cyient DLM (the defence/aerospace-focused EMS name), Kutumba Rao noted interesting volume action the previous day - over 3 crore shares traded, far above recent times - yet the stock still fell around 2.5% after that volume. He said one should watch whether this marks a bottom or whether it was an institutional exit before acting.

kutumba.note: Accumulate for medium term; promoters buying
kutumba.detail: For NCC, Suzlon and Vimta Labs, Kutumba Rao advised accumulating all three with a medium-term perspective. On NCC specifically he noted promoters have been buying on and off / continuously. (His Zoom audio was breaking up; the anchor relayed that the gist heard was 'all three can be bought / accumulated for the medium term'.)

kutumba.note: Accumulate for medium term
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao grouped Suzlon with NCC and Vimta Labs as medium-term accumulate ideas. (Said while his Zoom audio was breaking up; the anchor confirmed the accumulate verdict was attributed to Kutumba Rao.)

kutumba.note: Accumulate for medium term
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao included Vimta Labs among the three (with NCC and Suzlon) he advised accumulating with a medium-term view. (Said with audio trouble; the anchor relayed the accumulate verdict as Kutumba Rao's.)

kutumba.note: Hold; only realty stock in PSU space, good dividend
kutumba.detail: A caller holding NBCC at Rs 107 asked whether to continue. Kutumba Rao said hold/continue - it is the only realty stock among PSUs, no doubt about that. It corrected with recent problems along with others, but has good dividend, consistent business, and recently new payments and fresh work orders have come; the caller's cost price will easily be recovered at some point.

kutumba.note: Stay away from precious metals for now
kutumba.detail: In his market view Kutumba Rao said gold has fallen below 5,000 dollars an ounce on higher dollar prices, and speculators warn that if it stays below 5,000 a sharp correction of 600-700 dollars could come over the next 2-3 days. As of now he advised staying away from gold and precious metals; no need to chase it as a 'safe haven'.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 16 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/-waGSBIMC24

## Global backdrop
- **War / crude dominates.** Over the weekend the US-Israel coalition struck **Kharg Island**, Iran's main export hub (~**90%** of its crude exports). Iran is readying retaliation and launched a new "Ashura" missile, signalling a possible shift from a short war to a **prolonged conflict**.
- **Brent crude above 104 dollars**; vessel movement through the **Strait of Hormuz** has halted, with **11 vessels stuck since March 1**. The IEA is preparing to release **400 million barrels** of reserves. Experts warn that if crude holds above **100 dollars for ~10 days**, **150-180 dollars** is likely.
- **Dollar index above 100**; **gold ~5000 dollars/oz** (dipped below 5000 this morning), **silver above 80 dollars**.
- **Fertilizer supply risk** flagged as the next worry: China has halted phosphate exports, Europe's nitrogen supply is near a standstill; could force US crop rotation (corn → soybeans) and hit the global food chain.
- **Bond yields tightening globally** — 2-year yields up **34-52 bps** across US, Canada, UK, France, Italy, Spain, Portugal — raising the risk that central banks **hike to fight inflation** (negative for equities).

## Indian markets
- **FII selling relentless:** **Rs 52,704 cr** sold in March so far (3rd-highest monthly figure; Oct 2024 was Rs 94,000 cr, Jan 2025 Rs 78,000 cr); **Rs 56,000 cr** over the last nine sessions; **Rs 10,000 cr** cash (Rs 17,000 cr incl. F&O) on Friday alone. FII **shorts at 90%, longs 10%**.
- **Sentiment extreme:** RSI oversold, **PCR ~0.74** (near buying zone), **VIX 22.64** (3rd successive rising session). **Market breadth below Covid lows** — pages of 52-week lows on BSE; private banks near 52-week lows.
- **Nifty levels:** support ~**22,700** (below 23,000); risk to **22,000**, even **21,000** if sentiment persists; a dead-cat bounce toward **23,800** possible if 22,700 holds. Top-10 firms shed ~**Rs 4.5 lakh cr** market cap (SBI, HDFC Bank, TCS each down Rs 50,000-90,000 cr).
- **Open interest:** highest call OI at **23,500**; highest put OI at **23,000** (key to sustain). Longs building only in NHPC, Swiggy, Manappuram; shorts building in Bharat Forge, M&M, Supreme Industries, L&T.
- **Opening:** Nifty opened ~+30, slipped to ~+11; Sensex +27-30; **Bank Nifty weak (-25-60)**. Breadth negative (NSE 838 adv / 930 dec). Oil marketing names (IOC, HPCL, BPCL, Chennai Petro -7%) and **IDBI Bank (-11%)** down; VA Tech Wabag +3% on an order; pharma, cement, FMCG firmer.
- **Disinvestment/IPO:** **IDBI** disinvestment (~Rs 30,000 cr) postponed after bids came below reserve price (Kotak didn't bid). **CMPDI** (Coal India subsidiary) IPO opens **20th**, price band **163-172**. Mega-IPO float rule eased — **2.5%** dilution now allowed (vs 5%) for Rs 5 lakh cr+ firms — paving the way for **NSE (year-end)** and **Jio (next 3-4 quarters)** IPOs. Petrol/diesel prices unlikely to rise ahead of 5-state elections (Assam, Kerala, Tamil Nadu, West Bengal, Puducherry).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental) — gave the lead market view (crude/Nifty/OI) and fielded several viewer Q&A.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental, separate analyst) — IPO outlook, promoter-buying thesis, and viewer Q&A.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — entry levels and sector technicals.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

## Kutumba Rao — view & calls
- **Market view:** Don't short; bottom-fishing / extreme-long-term entries can start, but otherwise **wait and watch** for confirmed moves until the geopolitical picture improves. Market likely to move on geopolitics, not technicals; expects a few days of **lull**, no significant further fall **unless** US-Iran dramatically escalates. **Warns Nifty could touch 17,000 if China attacks Taiwan** (TSMC makes ~90% of advanced AI chips).
- **Stock calls:** **Paradeep Phosphates — Avoid** (until clarity; fertilizer crisis, imported ore); **Jindal Saw — Accumulate slowly** (promoter buying, long-term only; worst not over); **ADSL — Avoid/don't average**, **Blue Jet — Hold**, **Olectra — Hold** (mild bottom-fishing only); **Cement (UltraTech/Grasim/Ambuja) — positive/Watch**; **Transformers & Rectifiers — Hold** (no fresh buying); **Hindalco — Buy**, **L&T — Buy** (marriage-gift large caps).

## Other analysts' calls (not Kutumba Rao's — for reference)
- **Kranthi:** GVT & Hitachi Energy (electrical components, aggressive growth); oil/gas defensives incl. **IGL** (contra, 3-yr horizon, multibagger potential); **DCX Systems — Hold, don't average**; **Copper — Buy, Gold/Silver — Wait**; **IDFC First Bank — Hold**; **GRM Overseas — Hold** (already +100%, bonus-adjusted), **Daiichi Karkaria — Hold**; **Nifty Bees→L&T switch only with high risk appetite**; **Nestlé — Buy on dip (~1000)**. Also urged participation now (cites NCC, Bajaj, IRB Infra promoter buying).
- **Ramakrishna (technical):** **Bajaj Finance — Buy 50% position** (~854, at multiple supports); Nifty long >23,255 / short <~23,46x; Friday put-buy call gave 100%; **Pharma focus — Natco (Buy), Biocon (Buy)**, Nifty Bees in two tranches (~12-15% long-run, ~10% downside risk to ~22,000).
- Mutual funds: Nippon Mid Cap / HDFC Flexi Cap — invest via **SIP** or split into 3-4 tranches on big down days.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Jindal Saw, Hindalco, Larsen & Toubro (L&T))

kutumba.note: Avoid until clarity; fertilizer crisis hurts imported-ore converters
kutumba.detail: He said it is unfavourable because India mostly imports phosphate ore and then converts it, so even this resilient stock has started falling sharply, sliding from a stable Rs 130-140 in February to about Rs 110. Kharif season is starting and a good monsoon could help, but an uneven monsoon would hit fertilizer usage. As of now he would avoid until stability and clarity come.

kutumba.note: Long-term holders only can slowly accumulate on promoter buying
kutumba.detail: He flagged it as one of the promoter-buying stocks from his morning remarks: promoters made a significant purchase one day with big volume, pushing the stock up nearly 20% that day. But he stressed the worst is not over, though the December result is better. Only long-term-view investors should slowly accumulate, for that one reason.

kutumba.note: Don't average; should have exited in December on weak result
kutumba.detail: He said the stock has already fallen to about a third of its price, but this weakness came from a weak December result, not just the current market. He noted the investor could have exited safely at Rs 150-160 in December. Not exiting then and reconsidering averaging now is not correct; average only after the market settles.

kutumba.note: Not a bad stock; average only after market settles
kutumba.detail: He grouped it with ADSL and Olectra as not bad or penny stocks, but said there is nothing much to do now. Definitely do any averaging only after the market settles.

kutumba.note: Mild bottom-fishing only; oil crisis could lift EV demand
kutumba.detail: He noted ongoing execution and repeated CEO-change issues and a lack of expected company news. But one advantage now is that the oil crisis could make EV demand jump, so EV companies may perform a bit better. A bottom-fishing attempt could pay off, but only in mild quantity.

kutumba.note: Cement sector firmer on volume; infra boost and pre-monsoon demand
kutumba.detail: He said weekend press (Economic Times and a couple of papers) covered the cement sector, and this morning cement stocks are performing better on volume. UltraTech is the leading gainer at +3%, followed by Grasim, with Ambuja Cements also working resiliently. The Government may boost infra and cement consumption could improve ahead of the rainy season.

kutumba.note: Hold; no fresh buying, stock became very speculative
kutumba.detail: He said the stock became very speculative, touching 52-week highs easily on a steady uptrend and then falling by half. The latest result was good enough for a small rebound. He advised hold; fresh buying he would not advise.

kutumba.note: Looking much better; large-cap marriage-gift pick
kutumba.detail: Asked to name marriage-gift stocks, he picked Hindalco, saying it is definitely looking much, much better right now. He grouped it with L&T as two solid large-caps to take.

kutumba.note: Solid large-cap; marriage-gift pick alongside Hindalco
kutumba.detail: He named L&T as the second of two large-cap picks for the marriage-gift question, alongside Hindalco. He advised taking both as solid large-caps.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 13 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/oN9d27n28T8

## Global backdrop
- **War situation continues.** Global equity markets trading very negatively; **Gift Nifty pointing to a 150-160 point gap-down** (later indicated ~270 down).
- **Iran has reportedly closed the Strait of Hormuz** — Supreme Leader announced it, and Iran is said to have planted **more than a dozen underwater mines**, halting ship/vessel movement. US Treasury Secretary **Scott Bessent** said the US would escort vessels "when militarily possible" — the show treats this as not realistically doable.
- **Crude oil above $100** — at **$101.05**; closed above $100 for the first time since **2022**. Touched **$119/$119.5** intraday recently before snapping back to $90. Goldman Sachs and others forecast **$150**; 2008 GFC peak was **$147.5**; Iran is threatening **$200**.
- **Russia is a beneficiary** (sanctions effectively lifted on buyers of Russian oil; US now supplying Iran with arms previously meant for Ukraine; can sell oil at a premium). **China and India are the losers** — China buys ~**90%** of Iran's crude; India already seeing **LPG/gas supply** strain.
- **US tariffs** reimposed against **16 major countries** (India, China, EU, Korea, Vietnam, Taiwan, Bangladesh, etc.); a **global recession** is now seen as "on the cards." US fuel rates **$2.60-$3.90/gallon** in some states.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty lost 650 points** over two days; another ~150 expected at open. Downside targets cited: **22,000**, **20,000**, even **18,000** if very pessimistic. Kutumba Rao reaffirms **22,000 "on the horizon"** (called Monday), framed off the 17,000 Ukraine-war base.
- At open: **Nifty -170**, **Sensex ~-570**, **Bank Nifty -500+**. NSE breadth **668 advances / 1,461 declines**; BSE **760 / 1,220**.
- **Rupee at a lifetime low of 92.37**; analysts warn 95-96 wouldn't surprise. **FIIs sold >Rs 7,000 cr; DIIs bought Rs 7,500 cr** — sustainability of DII matching in doubt if redemptions start.
- **Banks/financials very negative** — re-rating underway. **ICICI Bank** 52wk high 1500 → **1250**; **HDFC Bank** 52wk high 1020 → **820** (warned it could hit **700**). FII selling of Rs 5,000-6,000 cr/day hits large private banks first. **Coal India strong**, up >15% in a month.
- **Bank Nifty**: calls at **56,000**, puts at **55,000** — breach of 55,000 implies more aggravated bank selling. **Nifty options**: calls building 24,000-24,300, puts heavy at 23,000; failure to sustain 24,500 risks 23,000. **PCR ~0.886**; **VIX ~21.5-22** (S&P 500 VIX 27). Temporary bottom expected **23,200-23,500**.
- **Energy/power is the standout theme** — peak power demand cited at **270.1 GW** this summer; gas-fired output may be cut ~**10 GW**. NTPC Green +12% (to 97), JSW Energy +7% flagged. At open, broad **power/energy rally**: NTPC Green +4.7%, Adani Gas/Power, HFCL, Siemens Energy, CESC, NHPC, Adani Transmission, ABB, GSPL, JP Power, SJVN, CG Power, Power Grid, Torrent Power.
- Strategy: **wait and watch**, no daredevilry until externals settle; exit weaker stocks on rallies, stick to **stronger / pharma / home-staples / consumption** names; **stay invested**, avoid panic.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — fundamental/markets view plus viewer stock Q&A (Reliance, M&M, dead-stock query). See the dedicated Kutumba Rao file.
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst: 23,400 crucial intraday support; extremely oversold (RSI 28.02, weak MACD), only relief/dead-cat bounces likely until the war pauses. Trade cautiously with hedges/stop-loss. Technical longs flagged: **Tata Power, Mazagon Dock, NTPC, Union Bank, Coal India**; technical shorts: **Amber, Supreme Industries, Eicher Motors, HPCL**. Said **Redington** is fundamentally strong (no stop-loss needed, range-bound, ~10-15% swings, targets possible before next quarterly numbers). Suggested a **17-Mar put at ~22,750** (buy if it crosses 63, SL 40) and a contrarian **23,500 intraday call** (cut if Nifty cash hits 23,350).
- **Kranthi** — answered the **HDFC → Bajaj Finance** switch query: stay in HDFC for now (Bajaj is higher beta; both need a financials rally); HDFC could fall to 700 on continued FII selling. Also remarked the fancy car-number trend is nationwide.
- **Vasanth** — anchor; also flagged Hyderabad's RTA fancy-number auctions (e.g., Mega Engineering paid Rs 10,50,000 for TS09L) and Caption Services' new training academy in Gachibowli (target: train 50,000 people by 2030 across 15 cities).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 buys (Reliance Industries, Mahindra & Mahindra)

kutumba.note: Accumulate slowly for the long term on sum-of-the-parts and AI value
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said the US $300 billion investment is not the reason to buy Reliance. The case is long-term sum-of-the-parts value (Jio divestment/possible IPO, retail) and AI developments relevant here that will have more impact on the stock. He said start accumulating slowly because it is somewhat resilient versus several other stocks, but with a long-term horizon.

kutumba.note: Hold/accumulate; treat as a diversified engineering/defense stock, not just autos
kutumba.detail: Asked by a viewer holding M&M at Rs 1700, Kutumba Rao said keep it because M&M also has defense exposure and is a diversified engineering stock, not just automobiles. He said hold, fresh buying is fine, and if asked, he would accumulate.

kutumba.stock: Gitanjali Gems / Kingfisher Airlines / Mercator / GVK Power / Lanco / Alliance
kutumba.note: Dead/untradeable stocks with no liquidity
kutumba.detail: Adding to the anchor's answer on a viewer's old holdings, Kutumba Rao said these have no liquidity at all and are completely dead, not even trading. There is no way to sell them, so they cannot be used for an LTCG set-off either.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 12 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/NuZGYmCbXJY

## Global backdrop
- **Gift Nifty** pointed to a gap-down of **200+ points** after Monday/yesterday's **~400-point** Nifty fall; a gap-down open materialized.
- **Brent crude crossed $100** (~$100.53-100.55, up ~55c overnight), having been ~$89-90 during the prior session. Whipsawing wildly — $120 a few days ago, then $80, back to $100. Anchor sees a chance of a move back toward **$120**, with $150 still possible if the war escalates.
- **IEA pledged to release 400 million barrels** of reserves, but that covers only ~4 days of world demand and physical commodity logistics take time, so prices stayed firm.
- Only ~**20% of global supply** disrupted (Strait of Hormuz vessels), yet crude is up ~**50%** (from ~$70 to ~$100).
- Reserve days by country: **Japan ~220 days** (highest), **India ~70 days**. US is insulated as the world's largest oil producer; Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, China relatively safe.
- **Inflation sensitivity** flagged: current ~**2.1%**; could reach **~5.1%** if oil holds above $100 for a sustained period, **~4.2%** near $80, **~4%** near $70 (RBI's target zone).
- **Iran** told mediators it will halt the war only if attacks on it stop; a **drone strike on Dubai International Airport** injured four. Trump claims "nothing left to target" in Iran but is reviving tariffs via **Section 301** (investigations on 16 countries including India and China) after the US Supreme Court struck down his earlier IEEPA tariffs.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty** opened with a 290-300 point loss (~23,562), gradually slipping; closing-segment levels showed Nifty **-263**, Sensex **-800+**, Bank Nifty **-770**. **23,700/23,500 supports broke**; next major support seen at **~22,500-22,000**.
- **Bank Nifty** (55,735 prior close; ~55,270 Monday low) now falling faster than Nifty (yesterday -2.13% vs -1.6%), losing its prior resilience.
- **Midcaps under-performing** (-1.8% vs Nifty -1.2%); advance-decline ~**592 / 1,889** (≈85% declines).
- **FII/DII flows**: FIIs sold **Rs 6,267 cr**, DIIs bought **Rs 4,965 cr** — DIIs seen pulling back somewhat.
- **India VIX ~22**. Sensex printed a **death cross** (50-DMA below 200-DMA); both indices trading ~**6% below the 200-DMA**. Anchor frames it as a **bear market since Sep 2024** and a **man-made "Black Swan" / manufactured supply-chain disruption** — urges capital protection over fresh longs.
- **Sectoral churn**: stove/appliance makers **TTK Prestige, Butterfly Gandhimathi, Stove Kraft up 7-10%**, Bajaj Electricals +3.2%, Adani Total Gas up ~13-20% on panic LPG-substitution buying; crude consumers (InterGlobe Aviation, Asian Paints), gas distributors (IGL, MGL), M&M (-3%) and L&T (-3%) weak.
- Anchor's thematic ideas: favorable — **oil exploration, pharma, energy/renewables (solar, wind), manpower-services**; avoid — **oil marketing, gas distribution, crude-consuming** names.

## Policy / corporate
- **Central cabinet eased FDI via Press Note 3**: investments from land-border countries up to **49% to be approved within a time-bound 60 days** (vs prior 6-12 months); funds (PE/VC) with up to **10% Chinese LP exposure** now cleared via the automatic route — removing a long-standing bottleneck. Indian majority ownership still required. Seen as a positive for **electronics, semiconductors, solar manufacturing** and tech transfer/jobs (India targeting **500 GW** renewables).
- Cited example: **Dixon Technologies–HKC JV (~Rs 7,624 cr)** for display modules (Dixon subsidiary 74% / HKC 26%); MeitY recommends, **DPIIT** gives final approval.
- **LPG crunch**: National Restaurants Association (Hyderabad) flags commercial LPG supply effectively halted; ~50% of members ordered gas with no delivery, ~40% have 2-3 days left. Commercial 19 kg cylinder rose from ~**Rs 1,750 (28 Feb)** to ~**Rs 2,150 (9 Mar)**; black-market rates **Rs 4,000-5,000**. Hyderabad F&B revenue ~**Rs 10,750 cr/yr** (~Rs 30 cr/day); industry employs **~8 million** directly. Restaurants moving to limited menus / bulk biryani; NRAI seeks "essential service" status and **~50-65% rationed supply**.

## Analysts featured
- **Anchor: Vasanth** (macro/markets monologue; conducted all interviews and viewer Q&A).
- **Kranthi** (క్రాంతి) — fundamental analyst; answered most viewer stock Q&A.
- **Ramakrishna** (రామకృష్ణ) — technical analyst; opening-trade read, death-cross/support levels, one trading idea.
- Guests: **Sandeep Balasubramanyam** (NRAI Hyderabad president), **Pankaj Mohindru** (ICEA chairman), **Guru** (Selcon Group promoter).
- **Note:** Kutumba Rao did **not** appear in this episode. The stock calls below were made by **Kranthi and Ramakrishna**, not Kutumba Rao — hence buys.json is empty (see kutumba_rao.md closing note).

### Stock calls (by Kranthi / Ramakrishna — NOT Kutumba Rao)
- **Redington** — Ramakrishna: positional **Buy** near ~240, 10-15% target (IT relief tailwind; range-bound).
- Stove makers (**TTK Prestige, Butterfly Gandhimathi, Stove Kraft**) — Kranthi: short-term/speculative only, strict stop-loss; not core products, supply ramp ~2-3 months.
- Oil exploration **Hindustan Oil Exploration** (+5%); refiners **Chennai Petroleum** — Kranthi: watch for opportunity on LPG/refining ramp.
- **Vishnu Prakash** — Kranthi/anchor: **Avoid** (Q-loss ~Rs 30 cr; only high-risk 2-3yr horizon; promoters infused ~Rs 285 cr to repay debt).
- **Zomato** — Ramakrishna: **book profit / weak**; 200 is key support.
- **TIPS Music, HUL, Transformers & Rectifiers, TCS, KPIT** — Kranthi: **Hold** (decent names, bear market).
- **Krishna Phosphates** — Kranthi: not tracked / **Avoid** (flattish results).
- **Madhya Bharat Agro** — Kranthi: **Watch** (numbers improving, margins not).
- **Sahasra Electronics** — Kranthi: **stay put / tax-harvest** the 50% loss.
- **Raymond Ltd** — Kranthi: **Hold** (now engineering/aviation/automobile); Raymond Lifestyle — book profit on rallies.
- **Axis [GATES]** — Kranthi: **Hold**, add only on dips below ~1,000.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 10 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/6D_mhcXnMRU

## Global backdrop
- **Crude is the dominant driver** of every market right now (Middle East / Strait of Hormuz crisis). Brent spiked to ~**119-120 USD/bbl** at one stage, then cooled to ~**88-89 USD** and was bouncing around **91-94 USD** through the show — extremely volatile.
- Trigger for the morning's positive swing: a **Donald Trump statement** that the Iran war is "almost over, very complete." But the analysts flag his statements as contradictory and not credible ("we could go further and we are going to further") — Iran is countering with hard threats to attack vessels passing the Strait of Hormuz and says it is "prepared for six months / one year."
- **Korean index fell ~10%** the prior session on the crude shock. **Dollar index ~98.81** (tried for 100). **Gold stable**, dollar showing some weakness.
- **Dow futures very volatile** — swung from -100 to -164 to -250 points during the show.
- If the conflict drags ~6 months, analysts expect a **global recession**; ECB/analysts already flag **stagflation** risk, with European nations moving first.

## Indian markets
- Friday Nifty closed **-400 pts**; Nifty technically entered the **correction zone** (>10% down from the **5 January** high). Today opened **gap-up** (GIFT Nifty +220, briefly implying +300).
- Pre-open: **Bank Nifty +564, Nifty +252, Sensex +810**. After open: Nifty +180 then faded to **+110**; breadth strongly positive (~**1,717 up vs 170 down**, ~10:1). Only ONGC, Cipla, HCL Tech opened red in Nifty.
- **FIIs net sold ~Rs 6,345 cr** cash + ~**Rs 3,000 cr** in futures (added **21,000 short contracts** vs 1,480 longs; long ratio fell from **14.6% (Mar 5) to 12.4%**). **DIIs bought ~Rs 9,000 cr** — yet market fell, showing sentiment trumps flows.
- From **5 Jan**: 13 Nifty stocks positive, 37 negative; **22 stocks** down >10%, **7 stocks** down >20%; ~**Rs 43 lakh cr** market-cap wiped out. Outperformers: **ONGC +16%, BEL +9%, Power Grid +7%, SBI, Apollo Hospitals**. Underperformers: **Wipro -26%, Jio Finance, Asian Paints (-20-25%)**.
- **52-week lows: 847 stocks vs only 20 at 52-week highs** (BSE) — very negative breadth.
- **Today is monthly expiry (NSE)** — settlement factor strong; **India VIX ~23.36**, highest close since **June 2024** — major discomfort.
- Options: highest **call OI at 24,500**, **put OI at 23,500 / 23,800**; **24,000 holding as base support** (yesterday's low **23,700** the key support); resistance **24,400-24,500** (gap-fill to ~24,200). PCR ~1.
- **F&O additions from 1 April**: **Adani Power, Cochin Shipyard, Vishal Mega Mart, Hyundai, Nippon Life, Motilal Oswal** (Vishal Mega Mart saw a recent large block deal).
- **Rupee ~92/USD** (recovered from record ~92.33 close); analysts warn a weak rupee means India gets **no benefit even if crude falls**, plus current-account-deficit and inflation pressure on fertilizers, paints, exports (egg/grape/mango-pulp exports already hit).

## Strategy view
- **Stay invested; don't panic.** Continue SIPs regardless of the war. Use spikes/rallies to **exit weaker stocks**, keep/add **relatively strong stocks** (SBI, RIL flagged as resilient large caps). Buy on dips in small tranches (~5% of target) — don't try to catch the bottom (called "extremely dangerous"). Be a bit on the sidelines until clarity; buying slightly higher after clarity is fine.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental) — opening macro/crude view + several viewer stock calls (Dixon, Asian Paints, SMS Pharma/Fizility, Cupid, Eternal).
- **Second analyst** (OI/technical-heavy market read — VIX, FII shorts, option levels). 
- **Kranti** (separate fundamental analyst) — answered Orchid Pharma/PG Electroplast/HDFC Bank, Karur Vysya Bank/Tata Steel, RVNL, Denta Water/IDFC First/Jio Financial.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — intraday strategy, moving averages, 24,000 long with 100-pt SL, support 23,700 / resistance 24,400-500.
- Plus a community-interest segment: NRIs (organizer **Murthy Indrakanti**, London) conducting a **Sata Chandi Maha Yaga** in Moti Nagar, Hyderabad, praying for an end to the Iran-Israel war.

## Kutumba Rao stock calls (viewer Q&A) — see kutumba_rao file
- **Dixon Technologies — Buy** (long-term only; 74% Chinese JV for display modules, ~1% margin uplift est.)
- **SMS Pharma / Fizility — Hold / Buy** as a "dark-horse bet"
- **Asian Paints — Avoid / wait** (crude up = big negative; old glory gone, only steady returns; competition from Birla Opus, Jindals)
- **Cupid — Hold** (resilient, flat result, "operators stronger")
- **Eternal (Zomato) — Add on dips** (now an institutional stock; competition from Swiggy/Blinkit/Instamart noted)
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 buys (Dixon Technologies, Eternal (Zomato))

kutumba.note: Buy on the JV news but only with a long-term view; patience needed
kutumba.detail: He said one can buy Dixon, but only with a long-term view, not short-term. It is one of the largest producers of fast-moving electrical goods and an OEM for many leading brands, with good turnover and PLI advantage. Today's news: a JV with a Chinese company in which Dixon holds 74 percent to make display modules, with broking estimates of a roughly 1 percent profit-margin uplift.

kutumba.note: Dark-horse bet; hold if already bought, buy as a dark-horse bet if fresh
kutumba.detail: He repeatedly frames it as a dark-horse bet rather than a sure-shot winner. It showed decent working though profits fell a bit last quarter. If already bought, hold it; if buying fresh, buy it as a dark-horse bet.

kutumba.note: Rising crude is a big negative for paints; wait until crude cools
kutumba.detail: He said rising crude prices are a big negative for paint companies, so wait until crude cools before buying. New aggressive competition from Birla Opus and Jindals (from about 1.5 years back) means market share and leadership can shrink. A good sound company, but it will not create wealth like before and should give only steady returns, not the old glory.

kutumba.note: Resilient, flat result but rose; operators stronger in the counter
kutumba.detail: He said Cupid showed good strength and stayed resilient even in weak markets, near its 52-week high. The result was flat (not great) yet the stock rose, maybe on the bonus/equity action. He advised hold because operators are stronger in this counter; later he added it is a strong but speculative stock.

kutumba.note: Can add on dips; now an institutional stock that corrected from highs
kutumba.detail: He said one can add Eternal. It has become more of an institutional stock and is among the names that corrected from highs in this weakness. He noted competition with Swiggy, Blinkit and Instamart before the caller's audio froze.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 9 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/h_y7uq0iswA

## Global backdrop
- A worrying Monday: **Dow Futures down ~1,100 points**, **Gift Nifty pointing to ~800-point loss**, equity markets turbulent worldwide.
- **Brent crude up ~28% overnight, Nymex ~30%, now near 118 dollars** (vs ~90 on Friday). Anchor frames it as heading toward **150 dollars**; **dollar index near a 3-month high**.
- Driver: the **Iran war / Strait of Hormuz** standoff — ~**20 million barrels/day** of crude supply effectively halted; **UAE, Kuwait, Qatar curbing production** for lack of storage. US reportedly hit an Iranian water-desalination plant; Iran reportedly hit a Bahraini one.
- The show argues the conflict is now a **prolonged war (3-6+ months)** between two strong states, not a short skirmish; **Iran's late Khamenei succeeded by his son**, ~80% domestic backing claimed.
- **Japan -7%, China -3%+, US futures -1,000+ pts; gold ~-2% to ~5050 dollars/oz**. Risk of world recession flagged by some economists.
- **Qatar invoked force majeure** on gas contracts; **GNFC halted production** for want of LNG.

## Impact on India
- India imports **~5 million barrels/day (~200 million dollars/day, ~70 billion dollars/year)**; oil bill could **cross 100 billion dollars**.
- Rule of thumb cited: **every 10 dollars on crude ≈ ~0.3% on CAD and ~1% GDP-growth hit**; crude up ~60 dollars implies **~3% GDP impact**. Rupee, forex reserves, and stocks all pressured.
- India holds only **~25-35 days of oil reserves** (vs China's ~3 months); Russian oil is the cushion.
- **Indo-US trade deal pushed out 3-4 months** (US Supreme Court tariff judgment, refunds/revisions); March deadline effectively shelved.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty** slid from ~25,400 (war start) to ~24,500, opened ~547 down (-2%), later **-730 pts (>3%)**; **Sensex -2,400**, **Bank Nifty -4.1%**.
- **Pre-open**: Nifty -580, Sensex ~-1,800, Bank Nifty -1,660. Breadth very negative; on NSE only **1 of 500 stocks at 52-week high**, most near 52-week lows.
- **India VIX near 19.88-20** (vs bull comfort zone of 12-13); put-call ratio ~**0.84**.
- **Key support 24,300/24,000** (puts accumulated at 24,000); a breach opens **23,000** then potentially **22,000**.
- **Banks weak**: shorts built up in **Federal, SBI, ICICI, Union, Canara**; today PNB, BoB, Canara, IDFC First, SBI, Union down; ICICI -3%, HDFC -3.5%, Axis -2.9%.
- **OMCs hit** (HPCL -7.5%; "7-8%" across OMCs as they absorb crude); **InterGlobe Aviation ~-5%**; JSW Infra -4.67%; **Meesho -8%** on a ~1,500 crore IT notice.
- Some **selective short-covering** in large-caps: Reliance, National Aluminium, Mazagon Dock.
- Flows: **DIIs bought >50,000 crore in the past week**; **FIIs sold >20,000 crore in last 4 sessions** — recovery seen as difficult until FIIs return.

## Strategy themes
- **Be defensive, stock-specific, strict stop-loss, protect gains; cash is king.** Avoid catching the falling knife; **sell on rallies / dead-cat bounces** (the earlier "buy on dips" stance is over).
- **Pockets of safety: pharma** (Natco, Laurus named), **essential-commodity manufacturers**, **defence**, **upstream oil**, and partly **sugar** (oil-proxy).
- **Avoid pressure sectors** when oil rises: aviation, paints, chemicals, logistics; also fertilisers (gas-dependent), ceramics, autos, EMS at risk on supply-chain disruption.
- **Hedging tip (Kutumba Rao):** hedge the portfolio with **Nifty puts** (illustration: ~1 crore portfolio → ~26,000 put / ~8 lots, held till the market bottoms), or stock-level options if experienced; don't hedge "for the sake of it."

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamentals + viewer Q&A. Defensive stance; named the pharma/defence/upstream-oil/sugar safe pockets; gave portfolio-hedging guidance. His stock calls: **KIMS** (preferred among hospitals), **JSW Infra** (Hold), **Utkarsh SFB** (Hold/be alert), **Avantel** (Buy; accumulate below 140).
- **Kranthi** — separate fundamental analyst. Handled the **gold-ETF / Titan-Tanishq SIP** answer and the **shipbuilding** ranking (**Cochin Shipyard** long-term, **GRSE** medium/short-term, then **Mazagon Dock** — accumulate on dips).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst. Skipped levels today ("meaningless" after a 2-3% cut); no big supports, only minor ones; advised most traders to stay out — only experienced scalpers should trade; warned option sellers hurt by high VIX.
- **Vasanth** — anchor; read viewer mails (fuel/water saving, IPO losses) and ran the cricket segment (**India won the T20 World Cup**, beating New Zealand by 96 runs in Ahmedabad).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 buys (Krishna Institute of Medical Sciences (KIMS), Avantel)

kutumba.note: Top pick among hospital stocks for growing pan-India presence
kutumba.detail: Between KIMS, Rainbow and HCG his choice is KIMS, because it is expanding its pan-India presence. He flagged a potential merger of KIMS with a chain of hospitals (Quality Care, AsterDM Healthcare and an overseas company), which would increase size substantially. There is uncertainty over the merger swap ratio, but otherwise he said KIMS is definitely the better choice.

kutumba.note: Hold; ran to 52-week high then corrected with the market
kutumba.detail: JSW Infra recently went up to a 52-week high and corrected along with market conditions. For a long-term investor, since the company is reaching a more mature stage and is doing well, he advised holding now rather than selling.

kutumba.note: No need to sell at this rate, but stay alert
kutumba.detail: He called it one of the better small finance banks operationally, with decent performance. But it is small (market cap ~2,300 crore), penny-ish, and showed losses in the last two quarters with a turnaround in doubt. He said there is no need to sell at this rate, but to be a bit alert.

kutumba.note: Start accumulating below 140; defence order book improving
kutumba.detail: Despite two or three disappointing quarters and negative market sentiment, its defence exposure limits downside at this price. He noted a recent major order announcement (about Rs 122 crore) and said the future is rosy. He advised starting an accumulation attempt, ideally below 140, and said definitely buy.
