# Summary — Business Breakfast, 6 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/YMA1uoeOJJU

## Global backdrop
- **Geopolitics dominate.** The **Strait of Hormuz** is effectively frozen — no vessel traffic — and may stay disrupted for weeks, breaking supply chains and logistics. Framed as a Trump–Netanyahu-driven escalation now drawing in Qatar, Bahrain and others; the war is in its **7th day**.
- **Crude:** Brent hit **86.28** intraday yesterday, now near **84.15**. A mid-session rumour (via Sky News Arabia) that Iran's deputy foreign minister offered to pause Iran's nuclear program under conditions briefly pushed crude to **82** and lifted equities, before being denied.
- **Positive for India:** The US granted a **temporary 30-day waiver allowing Russian oil purchases**, easing pressure on refiners (Reliance, HPCL, BPCL). India is thought to hold ~25 days of reserves and aims to build 1-2 months' worth. Russia has also agreed to supply somewhat cheaper.
- **One theory** floated: the war is really aimed at **China** — choke Iranian oil (China buys ~90% of it) to weaken China, which holds leverage via **rare-earth magnets / critical minerals**. Renewed talk of US controls on **advanced AI chips**.
- US Supreme Court reportedly struck down Trump's tariff orders and ordered refunds — now largely forgotten amid the war. China growth projected at **4.5-5%**, lowest in decades, a drag on global commodity prices.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty** down **1.6%** on the week; **mid/small caps** down **2-2.5% to 5%**. Gap-down open of ~150-200 points expected; pre-open Nifty was ~110 pts down at **24,656**, and the cash open showed Nifty -~140, Sensex -~550, Bank Nifty -450.
- **FII selling** eased slightly — sold **Rs 3,753 cr** yesterday; **DII bought Rs 5,153 cr**. FIIs pulled ~**Rs 17,000 cr** from IT in February. DII mutual-fund net equity buying fell **75% MoM** to **Rs 10,381 cr** (3-yr low), signalling fund caution.
- **Levels (Ramakrishna, technical):** Support at the 4 March low **24,305**; below that, **24,000 / 23,800** in play. Resistance / gap-fill around **25,000-25,150**; yesterday's high **24,854** now a resistance. Indices below all key moving averages incl. 200-DMA. Options: highest call writing **25,500**, highest put **24,600** (at risk of breaking). Bank Nifty closed above 100-DMA, showing relative strength, but possibly only because IT-selling support is rotating in; OI clustered at **59,000**.
- **Domestic news:** Andhra Pradesh to give **Rs 25,000 birth incentive for a 2nd child from April**, targeting a rise in the fertility rate from **1.5 to 2.1** — part of a wider demographic-dividend push (Tamil Nadu and others moving similarly).
- **Stock-specific moves:** Ramky (+ on a Maharashtra order), NSC +12%, L&T Foods +16%, Hatsun Agro up; Reliance +3% (rare for the largest cap) propped the index. IT weak (TechM, HCLTech). Aluminium firm on Gulf disruption (Nalco ~400, Hindalco near 1000). VIX cooled from ~17.8-18 to ~15.

## Analysts featured
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — intraday levels and Capstone ex-bonus note (opened ~Rs 275 vs 400, purely ex-bonus).
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental) — answered caller Kiran: **DC Infotech — Avoid** (low-margin trading company), **Sam Metalics — Watch/buy near 700**.
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst) — Balu Forge (add slowly), Indo Farm (~130, stagger in), Denta Water/water-infra (downtrend, wait), SG Finserv (avoid), Electrosteel/KNR/IRFC (hold all three), MOSt Defence & PSU Bank ETFs (good long-run bets), Dynamatic vs Kaynes (enter Dynamatic with fresh money), Syrma SGS (buy on declines), Fractal Analytics (+3-4%, only listed pure-play AI).

## Trading takeaways
- War-time: hour-to-hour swings; intraday only, with strict stop-losses and hedges. **Do not carry weekend positions**; if Nifty closes below **24,500**, avoid fresh positions until Monday.
- Investors: basket approach, lean toward domestic-focused / resilient names (e.g. PSUs); don't be tempted by price into weaker stocks. Precious metals not rallying despite uncertainty because gold/silver trade in dollars and the dollar is strong — a rally needs dollar weakness; risky to trade short-term.
- Markets react strongly to geopolitics but eventually move past it ("speed-breaker" phase); new highs likely, but maybe only after 3-6-9 months.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 calls (DC Infotech and Communication, Sam Metalics)

kutumba.note: Low-margin distribution/trading company; don't buy
kutumba.detail: Answering caller Kiran (who addressed him by name), Kutumba Rao said DC Infotech has low volumes - so much so that one has to check for possible insider trading - and only modest profits, with net profit margins extremely low at about 3%. Quarter-on-quarter the top line grew but the bottom line did not grow as much. It is essentially a distribution/trading company (distributing products of equipment makers like D-Link), which is why margins are so thin, so such companies should be avoided unless they are fundamentally very sound with huge volumes like Redington. He advised wait-and-watch and not to attempt a buy.

kutumba.note: Higher-PE stock; buy only on dip to ~700 / 52-week low
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said Sam Metalics shows more strength than DC Infotech but is trading in a narrow range around 800. Its recent result came slightly weak - the top line held but the bottom line dipped a bit - and it remains a higher-PE stock. If asked, he would buy it only when it comes down close to 700, near its 52-week low.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 5 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/fCmNWdJ1P2k

## Global backdrop
- **West Asia war in its sixth day** — US vs Iran is the single biggest driver of markets now; tariffs have dropped off the agenda. The anchor frames the "old world order" as broken under Trump.
- Asian rebound this morning: **KOSPI up ~10%** (after losing 10-12% / lower circuits yesterday), **Nikkei +4%** (after 4 straight down days of 3-4%), **Hang Seng ~+2%**, Taiwan strong. Gift Nifty pointing to **~+100 pts**.
- **Crude ~$83-84** (stabilised); brokerages peg a **$90-100 medium/short-term target**. **Gold ~$5179/oz**, silver ~$84-85.
- US overnight: Dow & Nasdaq each only **~+200 pts**, propped largely by some **Bitcoin-linked stocks**; viewed as euphoric/technical, not broad.
- **Qatar reportedly halted LNG production** — India imports heavily; **Gujarat Gas** has warned of supply hits and gas-supply cuts to industrial consumers.
- **China cut GDP growth estimate to 4.5-5%** — its lowest since 1991, attributed to the war; negative for metals/infra demand globally.

## Indian markets
- **Rupee breached 92** yesterday (ET headline), opened today at **91.57** (~60 paisa recovery) on **RBI intervention** (also selling bonds). Rupee weakness is a tailwind for **IT** (EPS +12-22%).
- Yesterday only **IT and sugar** rose; everything else sold off broad-based (metals, autos, cement, pharma, even consumption like HUL).
- **FIIs sold Rs 8,753 cr; DIIs bought Rs 12,000 cr** — DIIs (buying ~1.5x FII selling) are holding the market up.
- **FII shorts at 1.63 lakh contracts** (vs 1.03 lakh on Feb 26; +60k in 3 days) — only ~14% longs.
- **India VIX 21.14** (~10-month high), up ~23% yesterday and ~50% over two days — fear near peak; may cool as market rises.
- Pre-open: **Nifty 24,615 (+135)**; opened positive — **Nifty +100+**, **Sensex +320**, **Bank Nifty +150-160 (underperforming)**. **Breadth ~4:1 positive** (1435 adv / 332 dec on NSE), mid/small caps ~+1%.
- **Sensex expiry** today.
- Sector colour at open: **Realty top gainer**, plus capital markets, metals; **Coal India +2.3-3%**, **Reliance ~+2%**, **Bharat Forge +3%** (defence buying), Solar Industries strong. **Gas stocks (Gujarat Gas) weak** on Qatar; Mahanagar Gas trying to recover.

## Theme: war and equities
- **Kranthi**: markets may "learn to live with war" — in WW2 equities trended up as wartime economic activity/production rises. But **crude is the "joker in the pack"**; a sudden spike jeopardises expected rate cuts and could force a 360-degree reversal, hitting CAD, inflation and margins (double-whammy on already-slow earnings).
- **EV adoption slowing** post Trump's "drill baby drill"; Tata Motors cited (ran to ~800 on EV theme, struggled back toward ~300).
- **Ethanol/sugar**: govt notified **20% ethanol blending** last week (was resisted by OEMs on engine-readiness) — bullish trigger; **Renuka** (largest producer) **+7%**, but recovery not broad across sugar names. **Praj Industries** also bouncing off bottom.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): joined via mobile (power outage). View — **no sustained rebound**, "rebound ruled out"; recovery will be intermittent/news-driven and fizzle unless the war gets a clear closure (e.g., Iran regime change). **Stay sidelined, wait-and-watch**; only ~25% buys in deep-value names with a 1-1.5 year horizon. Sees ~91.5 rupee close as okay. Answered the buy-table calls below.
- **Kranthi** (separate fundamental analyst — NOT Kutumba Rao): "habituate to war" thesis; markets must reclaim **25,000** or it's a medium/short-term bear market. Q&A: **Dynamatic Technologies** (add slowly on dips), **Shakti Pumps** (add slowly on deeper correction), **Sika** (add slowly ~900).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst — NOT Kutumba Rao): markets sustain above **24,000-24,100** (multi-month support; else deep cut); resistance **24,800**, oscillation 24,100-24,800. RSI oversold ~30.37, MACD weak, red histograms. **Bank Nifty** may bounce faster (above budget lows; 59,000 OI). Intraday long with strict stops in 24,550-560. Q&A: **Lumax Auto** (fresh entry ~1475), **Uno Minda** (buy near ~1200).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Netweb Technologies)

kutumba.note: Hold; QIP-driven recovery, will take time but comes back fast
kutumba.detail: Said it must be held as of now, nothing else to do, but it will come back quickly. It is already below even the Government of India's earlier divestment rate, and a new QIP should bring the price back. Cited Bank of Maharashtra, which sat below its QIP rate for ~6-7 months before crossing it to a new high; PSU banks reward on QIPs, only a matter of time.

kutumba.note: Hold; may take a bit of time
kutumba.detail: For the caller down ~45%, he said hold for now. Unlike UCO he expects this one to take a bit more time to recover.

kutumba.note: His pick of the four; good returns from current levels
kutumba.detail: Among the caller's four stocks he would take Netweb. Called it a slightly risky bet but one that should give good returns from current levels. Even at a high PE (~110-112), once recovery kicks in the stock should definitely do much better.

kutumba.note: At highs; wait for a significant correction before buying
kutumba.detail: Already at highs, so watch carefully. Momentum is good and it has performed well over a period, but he would not buy at current levels; be wait-and-watch and buy only on a significant correction.

kutumba.stock: Old Tamp (exact name unclear in captions)
kutumba.note: Avoid; sector appears to have peaked
kutumba.detail: He would avoid this stock as of now, saying the sector has somewhat peaked. The exact company name is unclear in the auto-generated captions.

kutumba.note: Fully priced; wait for the NSE IPO
kutumba.detail: Said BSE is fully priced and there is no need to buy before the NSE IPO; wait for that.

kutumba.note: Hold; realty under pressure from IT job-loss risk
kutumba.detail: Realty stocks are under pressure, and metro-centric realty like Brigade (Hyderabad/Bangalore) could be hit if IT weakness brings job losses; Brigade and Prestige are connected to that risk. But as of now he says hold.

kutumba.note: Wait; no need to rush in
kutumba.detail: For the caller asking about Kohans / Suven Life, he said wait; there is no need to rush in and buy.

kutumba.note: Hold; only PSU realty name, regular order book
kutumba.detail: Said hold. It is the only realty company among PSUs and has a regular order book, but do not expect outperformer returns like the private realty stocks.

kutumba.note: Hold; needs patience, renewables could rally if crude rises
kutumba.detail: Said hold; it needs patience. If crude prices rise he expects a rally across renewable-energy stocks, Suzlon included.

kutumba.note: Hold; disinvestment news could trigger a rally
kutumba.detail: Said hold for now. Disinvestment news could come any time; by and large the stake is likely to go to Fairfax / Prem Watsa (Canadian investor), who also holds CSB / Catholic Syrian Bank, with RBI waiting on approval. If that news comes there is scope for a rally.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 4 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5r0wMSmsxgY

## Global backdrop
- **War-driven risk-off.** Israel + US "Operation Roaring Lion" against **Iran** has widened — Iran is now reportedly striking targets across **Dubai, Saudi, Qatar**, and US embassies (incl. Pakistan) have closed/burned. Feared to become a **prolonged, multi-dimensional war**.
- **Strait of Hormuz** was closed by Iran, snarling crude/LNG tankers. Brent spiked above **$85**, cooling to **~$82-84** during the show after US pledged tanker insurance (via "USDF") and naval escorts — which the desk called largely a "sham."
- **Crude route dependence:** ~20% of global crude transits Hormuz; **India ~45-50%**, Japan 70-80%, S. Korea 65-70%, Taiwan 60%, China 40-50% — India among the highest-risk.
- **Global equities red.** South Korea **KOSPI hit a 10% circuit** (still +26% YTD after the drop); Taiwan +18%, **Nikkei +9%**, Shanghai +4% YTD. US fell **~1,200 pts** intraday, closed ~400 negative; Asia down 2-4% this morning.
- **Gold** slipped from **$5400 to ~$5100** — even safe havens saw selling. **Dollar strength** is driving markets; "cash is king" regime risk if gold breaks down (margin calls, central-bank reserve drawdowns).

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty** signalled a **500+ pt gap-down**. Nifty opened ~**476-538 pts (~2%) lower**, trading near **24,350**; **Sensex -1,700 pts** (~78,520); **Bank Nifty -1,000+ pts** (~58,745).
- **Breadth brutal:** ~1:5 advance/decline on BSE (~496 up vs ~2,525 down). Already **~900 stocks at 52-week lows** Monday; ~3,000 stocks at/within 20% of 52-wk lows. Index only **-6.6% from ATH**, but portfolios far worse.
- **Rupee** at **~92.14/USD**, near all-time low (vs 75 during the Ukraine-war shock) — positive for IT; some IT gains.
- **Flows resilient:** Monday FIIs sold only **~Rs 3,300 cr** vs DII buying **~Rs 8,600 cr** — strong domestic support at lower levels.
- **Key Nifty levels (Ramakrishna):** crucial support **24,150-200**; below 24,000 a faster fall risk, but likely arrested near 24,000; next real support only at **22,000** — an air-pocket in between. Bank Nifty relatively stronger (100-DMA holding); most momentum indicators (RSI, MACD) negative below 200-DMA.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): **Wait-and-watch** until clarity (a "known unknown"); too early to call the bottom. Sees gas/LNG supply risk (Qatar) as bigger than oil for India; inflation spike risk if war prolongs. **Tranche buying** (¼ of intended capital per ~5% leg down). Sectors that look safe: **defense, upstream oil, banks/financials/PSU banks**; **avoid precious metals for now**; warns small defense stocks are over-played given India's limited export capacity. Buy only deep value with a **1-1.5 yr** view. Expects significant recovery **after March**.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst — separate from Kutumba Rao): Few weeks of volatility = a **good long-term opportunity**; invest **fearlessly but slowly**, don't stop SIPs. Favours domestic-oriented / crude-insulated names — **large-caps, defense, infrastructure, healthcare/hospital, pharma**. Recalls Ukraine-war pessimism points where stocks later rallied ~30% off the bottom.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst): Trending markets are easier for traders; trade per own setup with strict stop-losses (e.g. long attempt with SL ~24,334, ~100-pt target). High-volatility days make OI/levels less reliable.

## Viewer Q&A (stock calls)
- **Tejas Networks** — *Kutumba Rao:* **Hold**; worst is over (ANC deal drove +60-70%), will do extremely well long-term; even Rs 1,000 buyers profit if held 1-1.5 yr. Stock +7% (~Rs 524, ~doubled off low).
- **IDEA/EDA (at 165)** — *technical analyst:* weak counter, lower-top/lower-bottom — **sell into rallies**, don't hold.
- **Eternal (at 290)** — *Kranthi:* **Hold**, no averaging; valuations stretched (~Rs 3 lakh cr mcap) as market re-rates "futuristic" names.
- **CDSL / Jeena Sikho** — *Kutumba Rao:* **Not a great choice**; Jeena Sikho has good results but weak markets drag everything — be careful, follow technicals.
- **Sangvi Movers** — *Kranthi:* **Avoid** in these conditions.
- **L&T, Russell Tax, Sai Life Sciences** — *Kranthi:* **Add on dips slowly** (Russell Tax entry near 650 if 700 breaks; L&T hammered on Middle East exposure).
- **Good Luck, Balu Forge** (ammunition) — *Kranthi:* **Add slowly on dips**; only proposals, no immediate production.

## Other notes
- Tata Group **Founders' Day** (Jamsetji Tata) honoured; group flagged as a long-term wealth creator despite recent underperformance.
- Bottom line: cash preservation, wait-and-watch, selective tranche/dip buying with 1-1.5 yr horizon.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 calls (Tejas Networks, CDSL, Jeena Sikho Lifecare)

kutumba.note: Worst is over; hold 1-1.5 yr, even Rs 1000 buyers will profit
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said the worst is over for Tejas, it is only a matter of time. Even before BSNL money arrived, one deal drove the stock up 60-70% off its low, showing how important that deal was. The stock will do extremely well over the long term; held another one to one-and-a-half years, even those who bought at Rs 1000 will see profits.

kutumba.note: Not a great choice
kutumba.detail: Asked about investing in CDSL now, Kutumba Rao said it is not a great choice. He cautioned to follow technicals carefully given the weak market.

kutumba.note: Good results but weak market drags everything; be careful, follow technicals
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said Jeena Sikho keeps announcing good results, no doubt about that, but in a weak market any stock falls. He advised being careful and following technicals carefully rather than buying now.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 March 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/pmOYZLE86C4

## Global backdrop
- **Major geopolitical shock over the weekend**: a US-Israel strike on Iran, with the anchor reporting the killing of Iran's Supreme Leader **Khamenei**. Iran retaliated with missile strikes on US-aligned Gulf states — **UAE (Dubai/Abu Dhabi), Bahrain, Oman, Qatar, Kuwait**.
- **Strait of Hormuz** in focus — carries ~**20%** of world crude; Iran is reported moving to close it, having already hit ~**3 ships**. A prolonged closure could push **Brent to $90-100**.
- One read: the strike is partly aimed at hurting **China**, which sources ~**9-10%** of its crude (of ~70% total imports) from Iran.
- **Commodities/global equities**: Brent ~**$76-77** (not yet past $80). Gold at a 1-month high **~$5,332/oz**; silver near **$93.5** (peaked ~$95). UAE capital markets **suspended 2 days**; **Hang Seng -2.7%**, Nikkei and Taiwan indices lower. **Dubai airport closed indefinitely**, disrupting India-Gulf flights.
- Historical framing: like Gulf War, Kargil, Russia-Ukraine, last year's Iran-Israel flare-up — markets react negatively first, then recover over 1-2 months as fundamentals reassert.

## Indian markets
- **Gift Nifty** indicated ~160-170 pt gap-down; **pre-open showed Nifty ~-800/-880 pts**, Sensex ~-2,743 pts (~-3.4%), Bank Nifty ~-1,324 pts. **Rupee very weak at ~91.25** vs dollar.
- **Actual open milder than feared**: Nifty opened ~**-1%** / ~245-290 pts; Sensex ~**-1.2%** (-929); Bank Nifty relatively better, defending **60,000**. Heavy selling in **mid/small-caps** (indices ~-2%).
- **Breadth deeply negative**: ~**159 advances vs >1,000 declines** (~1:7); on NSE only **68 stocks** advancing vs ~2,000 declining. **India VIX +14-15%**.
- **Sector moves**: gainers — **ONGC, Oil India** (upstream, crude up), **defence (BDL, BEL)**, **Hindustan Zinc** (silver), **Muthoot Finance** (gold), Sai Life, Hindalco, Sun Pharma. Losers — **OMCs -4-5% (IOC, HPCL)**, IT stocks, tyre/paint (crude raw material), logistics (**Allcargo -7%**), InterGlobe Aviation, Reliance Power/Infra.
- **Macro is supportive**: strong **revised GDP series**, **excellent GST collections**; FIIs turned net buyers (~**Rs 22,000 cr** in Feb) post US-India deal. Domestic funds flush with cash. Key risk flagged: if crude breaks **$80→$100**, India faces a "double whammy" (FPI selling + rupee depreciation + CAD pressure).
- **Note**: tomorrow is a **Holi holiday** — low volumes expected. Today's close is technically pivotal.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets): "**known unknown**" event, already largely priced in; "**not the time to panic / liquidate**", wait-and-watch. **25,000 = inflection point** (highest put OI; call OI at 25,400/25,500). Bank Nifty band **60,000-61,000**; below 60,000 = alert. VIX ~13 (slightly elevated); PCR ~1.25-1.3 (no panic yet); all indices below EMA and rollover levels. Favours a **diversified portfolio**; flags **defence, upstream oil, autos** (positive Tata Motors/Maruti sales) as limelight themes. Doubts gold/silver can sustain higher levels without central-bank buying.
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst — separate from Kutumba Rao): India domestic story still strong; advises accumulating **domestic themes — infra, capital goods, defence** (defence stocks down 40-50% from peaks, consolidating). Warns crude $80→$100 is the key risk. Markets eventually ignore prolonged wars (cites Ukraine-Russia).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): expects open ~-1.5% to -2%; first 30 min / first hour and the **close** are decisive (panic-day low-close = worst case; early recovery = possible bottom). Cites Trump-tariff-day V-recovery as a template; long attempts only on recovery path with strict 50-60 pt stop-loss.
- Anchor: **Vasanth**.

## Note on opening commentary
- Anchor and Kutumba Rao both condemned **deep-fake videos targeting TTD Chairman B.R. Naidu** (Kutumba Rao is a TV5 employee under Naidu), calling the perpetrators "digital terrorists / white-collar jihadis." India also won its T20 World Cup quarter-final (Sanju Samson) to reach the semis.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (RR Kabel), 1 sell (RVNL)

kutumba.note: Confident of ~25-30% more upside; hold in profit with trailing stop, buy fresh only on a correction
kutumba.detail: Trading near its 52-week high; he recommended it in a newspaper today as an investment idea. He is confident of another roughly 25-30% returns from here, but warns against overconfidence in uncertain times. If in profit, put a trailing stop; to buy fresh, wait for a correction.

kutumba.note: Avoid for now; Gulf-facing exports vulnerable in this conflict
kutumba.detail: A meat-exporting company whose exports are largely Gulf-side, so it is vulnerable in this conflict. Already near its 52-week low (a paid-up share around Rs 23). Only below 20 might one take a chance, and only with patience that it bases first then grows; no need to chase immediately.

kutumba.note: Dark-horse outsourced-services play at 52-week low; hold for now
kutumba.detail: Previously called a dark horse; an outsourced-services provider now at its 52-week low. Last result was slightly weak (good top line but bottom line couldn't hold), though it still showed a profit. A 10-paise paid-up share around Rs 36-37. Hold as of now.

kutumba.price: around Rs 70; exit on rallies near Rs 100
kutumba.note: Weak chart; seasonal result; hold, don't sell, exit on rallies near 100
kutumba.detail: Already near its 52-week low around Rs 70 and the chart looks completely weak. The recent turnaround was a seasonal result that shouldn't be read into. The price is supported partly by government disinvestment expectations. Hold and don't sell; on rallies, when it nears Rs 100, exit.

kutumba.price: around Rs 80 would be better; below OFS price
kutumba.note: Buy only for the dividend; weak optimism, trading below OFS price
kutumba.detail: Optimism is weak; even the recent OFS got no response, and it trades about 7-8% below the OFS price. Railway stocks have become dividend stocks. If it comes near Rs 80 it would be better; watch the March-ending result, and if it sustains the company can report around Rs 7,000 crore-plus net profit and possibly raise the dividend. Buy only for that dividend; otherwise nothing else.

kutumba.note: Good demand; buy on any small decline, don't chase near 52-week high
kutumba.detail: Demand is good and it can definitely be taken, but it has already risen a lot and is near its 52-week high (positive even on a weak day). The major support is around 1420-1430 from the recent take-off. Buy on any small decline rather than chasing the strength at the top.

kutumba.note: Don't switch out; largest private bank has to perform eventually
kutumba.detail: Caller has been stuck for three years and asked about switching out. Kutumba Rao says don't shift and keep it. It is certainly an underperformer, no doubt, but being the largest private bank it has to perform at some point, so hold.

kutumba.note: Underperformer; best of times for railway stocks are over, exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: RVNL and IRCTC/railway stocks should have been shifted out long ago; he has called them underperformers for almost a year. The best of times for railway stocks are over. Whenever there are rallies, exit ex-dividend and shift out.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 27 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/r2QviK2e4_c

## Global backdrop
- **US closed lower**: Nasdaq **-1.18%**, S&P 500 **-0.5%**.
- **Nvidia down ~5-6%** despite fine Q4 numbers — strength wasn't enough to lift the price; CEO commentary positive (AI capex cycle "intact", markets misreading IT/enterprise-software negativity).
- **Software stocks rebounded** in the US: **Infosys ADR +3.2%**, **Wipro ADR +2.2%**; **Accenture, Cognizant, Globant, EPAM up 5-8%**. Market now splitting tech into AI vs software — reversal/unwinding on both sides.
- **Gold/silver firmed** ahead of next-week **US-Iran nuclear talks**.
- **Block (Block Inc.)** cited as the model: stock **+27%** after signaling ~50% staff cuts via AI tools — markets will reward AI-efficient firms, punish laggards.

## Indian markets
- **GIFT Nifty** indicated a **~80-point gap-down**; market in a painful "one day up, one day down" zig-zag, no follow-through.
- **Sell-on-rallies mood**: strong first-15-min openings (100-120 pts) that fade; **Nifty stuck below 25,500** for successive days — **inflection point 25,500**, downside risk to test **25,000**.
- **Flows**: **FIIs sold Rs 3,465 cr**, **DIIs bought Rs 5,000 cr** (relentless DII support; FII mood swinging).
- **Breadth** decent earlier (mid/small-cap closed positive, selective accumulation), but **markets opened weak** today: Nifty -60, Sensex -215, Bank Nifty -250/-260; only ~8 Nifty stocks green (the five IT majors + few). NSE advance/decline ~748:816 then deteriorated to ~849:1375.
- **IT index top gainer** intraday **+1.6%** (short-covering); IT still flagged as weakest structurally.
- **Today's data**: **Q3 GDP** (first print on 2022-23 base, est. **7.6-7.8%**), **Feb auto sales** (clarity by Monday).
- **Sectors**: positive — **PSU banks, auto, pharma** (Laurus Labs, Natco near Rs 1,000), **metals, NBFCs, energy/transmission-grid**; weak — **IT and real estate** (inter-linked; realty underperforming ~2-3 months).
- **SEBI MF rule changes**: equity MFs can take gold/silver exposure; both Value + Contra themes now allowed at one fund house; new **Balanced Hybrid** (40-60% equity:debt) and **Life-Cycle** funds; solution-oriented schemes (children's gift/retirement) to be removed.
- **NSE IPO**: ~**USD 2.5bn / Rs 22,000 cr**, Rothschild & Co appointed independent advisor; likely within ~2 months.
- **Corporate actions**: **Vishal Mega Mart block deal** ~Rs 7,915 cr (~14-15% equity, floor Rs 115, ~10% discount), stock **-6.5%**; **IRFC OFS failed** (poor response). **Tejas Networks +7%** today (after +17% prior day) near Rs 400.
- **Other**: Rupee itching to cross **91** (was 90.91); **NRI remittances down 16% YoY** — forex-reserve concern. **VIX 13.06** (4th down session), **PCR ~0.88** (weak).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (markets/fundamental + viewer Q&A): macro view above; cautious, "stay invested in select stocks", no fresh index positions below 25,500. Stock calls — see below.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): same consolidation, below 20-DMA/200-DMA; **Nifty support 25,400** (then 25,340/25,100/25,000); momentum weak (RSI ~43, MACD below zero). **Bank Nifty** relatively strong (dragonfly-doji, RSI ~60, MACD above zero) — resistance **61,700** (then ATM), support **60,600/60,500**. Suggested **short below 25,400** with strict stop at day-high. Gave entry levels for **SBI** (add in 25% tranches; pullback ~1,150, then 1,250) and **L&T** (~4,200 support, ~2% below current 4,288) on a viewer call.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

## Kutumba Rao's stock calls (viewer Q&A)
- **Buy/Add/Accumulate**: Heritage Foods (accumulate 320-350), Power Mech Projects (buy slowly), Polycab/NetWeb/Airtel (start in tranches).
- **Hold**: Vyash (ex-Sequent Scientific), KIMS Hospital, JK Enterprises, Saint DLM.
- **Avoid/Sell**: L&T Finance (sell on rallies), HBL Engineering (sell on rallies), HCC pack (exit on rallies), Malu Paper (avoid), Trident (weak).
- **LIC Housing Finance**: OK only for long-term/high-dividend (no multibagger).
- **Watch/dark-horse**: data-center names (Sterlite Tech as dark horse, KRN Heat Exchanger if easier to follow); Balaji Amines (flat), Renuka Sugars (dark-horse bet); Vishal Mega Mart (speculative bet, SL 110).
- (NHPC/IRFC: just enjoy dividends — rates far off. Mutual-fund picks given for a viewer: small-cap + flexi-cap schemes.)
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (Heritage Foods, Power Mech Projects, Polycab, NetWeb Technologies, Bharti Airtel), 3 sells (L&T Finance, HBL Engineering, HCC (Hindustan Construction))

kutumba.note: Correction is a good buying opportunity; accumulate 320-350
kutumba.detail: Said the recent correction is giving a good opportunity to buy and there is no need to panic. All dairy stocks benefit now that import/trade-deal fears (NZ, US, EU) have passed; efficient low-debt dairy companies will rise. One of the better bets to buy; at current levels it corrects and accumulates between 320 to 350.

kutumba.note: Corrected from top, now attractive; buy slowly
kutumba.detail: Having corrected from the top, the stock now looks attractive at current price, with good December results. He said definitely buy slowly.

kutumba.note: Good selection; start in tranches
kutumba.detail: Asked about Polycab, NetWeb and Airtel together. He said the stock selection is good, so start buying little by little in tranches (branches).

kutumba.note: Good selection; start in tranches
kutumba.detail: Part of the Polycab/NetWeb/Airtel question. He said the stock selection is good, so start buying little by little in tranches.

kutumba.note: Good selection; start in tranches
kutumba.detail: Part of the Polycab/NetWeb/Airtel question. He said the stock selection is good, so start buying little by little in tranches.

kutumba.note: Lot of churning post-merger; hold, no fresh buying
kutumba.detail: Said there is a lot of churning in the stock because Sequent Scientific has changed its name and became Vyash after mergers. He would hold; as of now there is no need to buy fresh.

kutumba.note: Hospital stocks on better footing; hold
kutumba.detail: Said hold. All hospital stocks are on a better footing; hospitals are one sector FIIs buy on and off, and large major hospitals across India are moving into institutional (FII/PE) hands. Definitely hold KIMS.

kutumba.note: Defense 'noise' not justified by results; hold, await Q result
kutumba.detail: Said hold what you have. A lot of noise was created around it being a defense company getting orders, but results did not justify it. It is a ~100-PE stock with negligible profits, so valuation is steep; only 'buy on noise'. Hold for now and watch the next quarter's result.

kutumba.note: Hype to reality; top line stabilizing; hold
kutumba.detail: Said the stock has come from hype to reality. The top line is stabilizing; the last result was slightly a disappointment and it has come into the 52-week low, but it is in a niche segment. He would hold on to Saint DLM as of now.

kutumba.note: Dull but steady long-term/high-dividend; no multibagger
kutumba.detail: Called it a dull counter but a steady-returns, long-term counter. Do not expect any multibagger returns. For extreme long-term holding and high dividend, LIC Housing is OK.

kutumba.note: Sell/exit on rallies; gold-lending tailwind fading
kutumba.detail: Said sell on rallies, given time. Their business grew in the last two quarters partly on gold-lending, which will now reduce; the business is concentrated in suburbs and tier-2 towns. The stock has already run up a lot, so booking profits or exiting on rallies is better even if not in profit.

kutumba.note: Lower-top/lower-bottom since failing 1000; sell on rallies
kutumba.detail: Said since it could not cross 1,000 after the third-quarter result, it remains in a lower-top, lower-bottom trend. The Q2 blockbuster result will not repeat in Q3 or Q4. As of now it is a stock to sell on any rallies.

kutumba.note: Speculative counter; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: Said exit on rallies, no doubt. The stock did not work against expectations; it gets on-and-off volumes because it is a speculative, trader-favorite counter, nothing more. A weak stock to exit on rallies.

kutumba.note: Loss-making; avoid; only large paper names
kutumba.detail: Said it is avoidable because it is a loss-making company. If at all one must be in the paper industry, be only in a large company such as TNPL, JK or ITC. Exit.

kutumba.note: Not a great stock; distribution visible
kutumba.detail: Said repeatedly that Trident is not a great stock. The textile space has been a bit active after the US deal, but more distribution is visible in the stock, which must be observed.

kutumba.note: Near 52-wk low, last result flat; watch
kutumba.detail: Said it is trading at lower levels near its 52-week low and the last result was flat. Unless there are changes such as amine prices rising, the stock will likely stay flat in the short term; otherwise watch.

kutumba.note: Only a dark-horse bet
kutumba.detail: Among Balaji Amines / Renuka Sugars / Trident, he said Renuka is only a dark-horse bet. That's it.

kutumba.note: Data-center theme; dark-horse bet
kutumba.detail: On a data-center basket (NetWeb, ETE Network, KRN Heat Exchanger, Orient Technology, Sterlite Technologies). He favors momentum / 52-week-high names in an uncertain market. He named Sterlite Tech as a dark-horse bet.

kutumba.note: Niche cooling/refrigeration; near 52-wk high
kutumba.detail: From the data-center basket, KRN Heat Exchanger makes cooling systems and caters to refrigeration in a niche segment; it is in hype and near its 52-week high. He suggested it if the investor can follow it easily; usually he picks momentum stocks near 52-week highs when the market is uncertain.

kutumba.note: Speculative bet post block deal; SL 110
kutumba.detail: After the ~Rs 8,000 cr block deal, he noted last time the stock ran from below 100 to 150 over six months. It is a high-PE stock but the placement was done successfully. As a speculative bet, buying with a stop loss at 110 could give returns; watch if the post-block-deal pattern repeats.

kutumba.note: Hold for dividend; target rates far off
kutumba.detail: For a viewer holding NHPC (bought ~100) and IRFC (~175), he said enjoy the dividend and keep holding; the target rates will take a lot of time to come.

kutumba.note: Hold for dividend; 175 far off
kutumba.detail: Said enjoy the dividend and keep holding; the rate of 175 rupees won't come soon, it has to come someday far off.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 26 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/xEuKASIcXII

## Global backdrop
- **US closed higher** overnight: **Dow +~300 pts**, **S&P 500 +0.8%**, **Nasdaq +1.26% (+288 pts)**. Renewed tech buying — **Salesforce +3.4%**, **Palantir +4.1%**, **Globant +~5.2%**. **Infosys & Wipro ADRs** also up.
- **NVIDIA** posted strong Q4: revenue **$68.13B** (est. $66.21B), **+20% QoQ**, **+73% YoY**; Q1 guidance **$78B ±2% (~15% QoQ growth)**. NVIDIA has only **~40,000 employees** vs a **$4.5-5T market cap**; sells each GPU for **~$38,000**.
- **Asian shares** up four straight sessions; India lagging.

## Anchor's big theme — Indian IT "missed the AI revolution"
- Long, emotional monologue: Indian IT majors (**TCS, Infosys, HCL Tech, Wipro, Tech Mahindra**) have a combined market cap of only **$241B** — less than 3-year-old **Anthropic's $370B valuation** (2,700 employees). TCS alone has **6 lakh employees**.
- Argues Indian IT "cheated the country" by exporting manpower and returning cash via **buybacks/dividends** instead of innovation/capex; warns engineering grads face **no jobs in 1.5-2 years** as **AI writes software**.
- The **Adanis/Ambanis** (data-centre/capex players) more likely to lead India's AI than legacy IT. Cites **South Korea**, whose market cap jumped **40-50% to ~$4T** (from $2.8T) by adopting chips/semis/AI, vs India's NSE at **~$4.8-5T** — "we are far, far behind."
- Calls for **academic R&D funding** over Nobel-prize bounties.

## Indian markets (Kutumba Rao — fundamental/markets view)
- **Monthly options expiry** day for Sensex & Bankex. Opening positive but **market weak below Nifty 25,500**; needs to hold above to turn constructive.
- Weak undercurrent: FIIs **and** DIIs bought yesterday yet **Nifty rose only ~50 pts**. Drags from **Reliance, SBI, ITC** and private banks (**Kotak, HDFC**); strength only in **metals** (Tata Steel, JSW).
- **Options:** highest call OI at **25,600**; strong put writing at **25,500/25,400**. **PCR weak at 0.88**; **VIX cooled to ~13.5** (watch for 12 = bull confidence). Below 25,500 for a couple of days → market could attempt **25,000**.
- Speculative longs in **SAIL** (+17% OI); shorts built in **Waaree Energy, Premier Energy, IRFC, Eternal**; flags **Saman Capital** for possible insider activity ("SEBI should probe").
- Big movers cited: **Concord Biotech (+17%)**, **Jai Balaji Steel (+14%)**, **Aditya Infotech (+7%)**, **Schaeffler (+4%)**. **Rupee ~90.91**.
- Stance: **stay invested, trade stock-specific**, stick to names with **Q4 earnings visibility**.

## Technical view (Ramakrishna)
- **Sensex below 200-DMA**; Nifty under a **falling trendline** (5 swing-high rejections since Feb). **Sell-on-rise** character; 20-DMA hard to cross.
- **Supports 25,000-25,100**; **resistances 25,800-900** (must clear for clean trend). Momentum indicators weak (RSI/MACD) except **Bank Nifty**.
- Bank Nifty: resistance **61,600**, support/put writing **60,000**.
- Reiterates a **~2-year consolidation** view (Sep 2024 → ~Sep 2026), analogous to 2021-2023, to let elevated **PEs** normalise as EPS catches up.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — fundamental/markets + viewer stock Q&A (named & present throughout).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst (levels, expiry trade).
- Anchor (Vasanth) drove the IT/AI editorial segment.

## Kutumba Rao's stock calls (viewer Q&A)
- **Hold:** Piramal (PPL) Pharma, Lemon Tree Hotels (till Q4 results), Shilpa Medicare, Vansal Pumps, ABFRL, Coforge, Orient Technologies.
- **Buy / Add:** SMS Pharma (on dips ~320-330, not at 360), Waaree Energies (long-term, with wait-and-watch on US export risk), Heritage Foods (at support), DCB Bank/L&T/SBI/Jio Finance/Dr Reddy's basket.
- **Avoid / Exit:** Happiest Minds (weak; exit on any rally, don't average).
- **Wait & watch / cautious:** Aavas Financiers, India Shelter Finance (steady but won't outperform).
- **Fund switch:** exit **Motilal Oswal Midcap Fund** if in profit (high IT weight — Persistent 10%, Coforge 10%, Paytm 7.7%; Kalyan Jewellers hurt earlier) → **HDFC Midcap / Nippon Midcap**.
- Note: **Bank of Baroda** and **Karur Vysya Bank** (add on pullbacks) were answered by **Ramakrishna (technical)**, not Kutumba Rao.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 8 buys (SMS Pharma, Waaree Energies, Heritage Foods, DCB Bank, Larsen & Toubro, State Bank of India, Jio Financial Services, Dr. Reddy's Laboratories)

kutumba.note: Hold; pharma defensive vs IT
kutumba.detail: PPL (Piramal) Pharma has come to almost its lowest point. He says hold here because pharma can act as a defensive counter to IT, where the impact is less than feared. Definitely hold as of now.

kutumba.note: Hold through Q4 results, then decide
kutumba.detail: Hold for this quarter's results. The Delhi/region hotels did well, so the quarter's result should be good. Decide whether to sell only post-results.

kutumba.note: Exit on any rally; do not average
kutumba.detail: Nothing positive; the company keeps making noise but hasn't delivered. Only the well-known promoter (Mr. Soota) supports its roughly Rs 5,000-6,000 crore valuation. Stock is near its 52-week low; exit on any rally and do not average.

kutumba.note: Hold; one of better oncology names
kutumba.detail: Hold as of now; it is one of the better companies in the oncology segment and recent news-driven activity is seen, so the loss should get covered. Negatives are a high PE and a weak last result (top line up, bottom line so-so). Reshuffle after Q4 if needed.

kutumba.note: Buy on dips around 320-330, not at current level
kutumba.detail: SMS (ESS ESS) shows extreme strength and is attempting 52-week highs. He repeatedly said buy whenever it came below 300. Do NOT buy at the current ~360 level; buy when it comes to around 320-330.

kutumba.note: Buy long-term (2-3 yr); watch US export fine print
kutumba.detail: They are the number-one producer of solar modules/panels with domestic demand, so he says buy for a 2-3 year horizon. Caution: a small hindrance to US exports; the company says it mostly assembles in India and has a US plant, limiting impact, but the US-government fine print must be watched. Averaging is acceptable.

kutumba.note: Hold; if Q4 good, exit on recovery to 450-500
kutumba.detail: Hyped at listing; a weak Q4 result pushed it to its 52-week low. Government-order revenues get added later, so if the fourth quarter is good the stock can recover to 450-500 levels. Hold here and exit on that recovery.

kutumba.note: Invest, no second thoughts
kutumba.detail: The stock is exactly at its support level now. He says definitely invest the planned Rs 50,000, no second thoughts.

kutumba.note: Part of well-chosen long-term basket
kutumba.detail: Part of a viewer's long-term basket (DCB Bank, L&T, SBI, Jio Finance, Dr Reddy's Labs) mixing an infra company, a bank and an NBFC. He says continue existing holdings and definitely invest fresh.

kutumba.note: Part of well-chosen long-term basket
kutumba.detail: Part of a viewer's long-term basket (DCB Bank, L&T, SBI, Jio Finance, Dr Reddy's Labs). He approves the infra+bank+NBFC mix; continue holdings and definitely invest fresh.

kutumba.note: Part of well-chosen long-term basket
kutumba.detail: Part of a viewer's long-term basket (DCB Bank, L&T, SBI, Jio Finance, Dr Reddy's Labs). He approves the selection; continue holdings and definitely invest fresh.

kutumba.note: Part of well-chosen long-term basket
kutumba.detail: Part of a viewer's long-term basket (DCB Bank, L&T, SBI, Jio Finance, Dr Reddy's Labs). He approves the infra+bank+NBFC mix; continue holdings and definitely invest fresh.

kutumba.note: Part of well-chosen long-term basket
kutumba.detail: Part of a viewer's long-term basket (DCB Bank, L&T, SBI, Jio Finance, Dr Reddy's Labs). He approves the selection; continue holdings and definitely invest fresh.

kutumba.note: Hold for the large-group angle
kutumba.detail: Directly the stock is weak. The only positive is the brand portfolio and that it belongs to a large industrial group, so at some point it will perform. Hold for the group angle.

kutumba.note: Don't panic-sell; watch AI strategy
kutumba.detail: No need to sell in panic. Normally considered a good mid-cap IT company; in the current commotion, watch their strategy before deciding. He is not advising a hurried exit.

kutumba.note: Don't sell; doing something in infra
kutumba.detail: He is doing something, at least playing a bit in infrastructure, so don't sell it at all. No need to exit in a hurry.

kutumba.note: Wait and watch; no rush to buy
kutumba.detail: It has corrected badly from its highs (more than 5 percent off, down to about half). At this level he would wait and watch; there is no need to buy in a hurry.

kutumba.note: Steady but won't deliver outperforming gains
kutumba.detail: Like other housing-finance names it gives steady returns, but for outperforming gains it definitely will not deliver. Should be understood as not a gainer for that purpose.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 25 February 2026 (TV5 News)
# https://youtu.be/Rcit4coK0Oc

## Global backdrop
- US markets bounced overnight: **S&P 500 +0.7%**, **Nasdaq +1.05%**, with a tech-sector bounce-back. Gift Nifty pointed to a positive open after yesterday's heavy fall.
- **Trump's State of the Union address** dominated the morning. He twice cited the stock market (boasting **53 all-time highs since his election**), defended tariffs ("taught countries that ripped us off a lesson"). The team read this as tariff policy continuing.
- New Trump announcement flagged as positive: a **"rate-payer protection pledge"** for AI data centers — big tech / data-center owners should set up their own power generation so retail/household power bills don't rise.
- **The AI-vs-software fear** is the central theme. Citrini's report (AI will cause huge disruption) drove the Indian IT index to a **30-month low** (Financial Express headline). **Anthropic/Claude** tools are seen as the disruptor — e.g., Anthropic's pledge to modernize **COBOL** (which still runs ~80% of in-person US credit-card and ~95% of US ATM transactions) sank **IBM -13%**.
- Counterpoint: an **HSBC report** rebutted Citrini — "software is already eating AI"; AI spreads only via software, so software won't go to zero.
- Currencies: rupee weakened to **~90.95** (RBI support kept it just below 91); Chinese yuan strengthening vs dollar. Gold steady/"resilient" (cited above ~$5,200/oz), warned against shorting given fragile geopolitics (possible Iran attack).
- **US imposed ~120% import duty on solar imports** — hit Indian solar exporters: **Waaree Energies, Premier Energies, Vikram Solar all ~10% down** at open.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday: heavy fall, **Nifty -288 pts (~-1.1%)**; **IT index -5%**, individual IT stocks down 6-8% (Persistent, Coforge crashed). Nifty IT logged its **worst monthly fall in ~23 years** (since dot-com 2000-02).
- Selling was **retail-driven panic**, not institutional: **FIIs net-sold only ~Rs 100 cr**, **DIIs bought ~Rs 3,000 cr**. TCS -44% and Infosys -46% from peaks, yet long-term holders mostly stayed invested.
- Breadth very weak: **~2,900 decliners vs 1,344 advancers**; stripping penny/no-volume names, fewer than ~300 active stocks actually rose.
- 18-month context: Nifty stuck around **25,400** since Sept 2024; Feb saw mild mid/small-cap outperformance (+2-3%) before the AI scare resumed.
- **Today's open: positive** — Nifty +120-143 pts, Sensex +400, **IT index +2.7%** on short-covering (huge short positions had built up). Power Grid +1.2% (Cabinet raised its subsidy investment from Rs 5,000 cr to Rs 7,500 cr). Broad gains across IT, metals, realty, FMCG, defence, energy.
- Corporate: **Tata Sons** board (esp. Noel Tata) weighing conditions for **Chandrasekaran's third term** as chairman — watch Tata group stocks. **IRFC offer-for-sale opens today at Rs 104** (5% discount).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets): no near-term recovery hope — expects a broad, pendulum-like trading range; "it may take some more time." Self-described contrarian buyer ("blood on the street") who bought after 2:30 yesterday. Key levels: **25,500** as possible base (call build-up there; calls also at 26,000), puts at 25,000; below 25,500 expects a retest of 25,000. PCR ~0.9; **0.7-0.9 a good buying zone** for position traders; VIX still >14 (a concern). Momentum strategy worked only for short-term/position traders. Answered all the viewer stock Q&A (see Kutumba Rao file).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): March series starts; budget gap-up still needs filling. Supports **25,327 (200-DSMA)** and **25,250-260 (200-DEMA)**; break of 25,320-325 → serious short, target 25,000-25,100; below 25,500 try short. RSI fell to 45, MACD bearish crossover. **Bank Nifty relatively strong** (above all MAs, RSI ~60, helped by Kotak; wick formation = buying from below) but consolidating around 61,000. VIX only -0.12% despite the fall — a small positive.
- **Vasanth** (anchor): raised the data-center/power theme, Trump's rate-payer pledge, and the solar import-duty hit.
- Mentioned but not present: **Kranti** (separate fundamental analyst, named by a caller as a source he follows).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (IRFC, VA Tech Wabag, Eris Lifesciences), 1 sell (South Indian Bank)

kutumba.note: Not a bad stock; brands; recovers when retailing improves
kutumba.detail: Hold all three (ABFRL, MSUMI, Adani Power). ABFRL is not a bad stock with a lot of brands; when retailing improves again it will automatically jump up. A Kumar Mangalam Birla group company on a weak wicket post-demerger, but recovery at some point along with economic growth could come.

kutumba.note: Hold; caller at breakeven
kutumba.detail: Among the three stocks the caller held (ABFRL, MSUMI, Adani Power), Kutumba Rao advised holding all three. The caller was at breakeven on Motherson Sumi Wiring.

kutumba.note: Hold; no need to be scared
kutumba.detail: Hold Adani Power; no need to be scared much. He added that power companies have good times due to data-center demand, and viewed Trump's rate-payer protection pledge for AI data centers as a positive for the space globally.

kutumba.note: Avoid; more downside likely
kutumba.detail: Avoid as of now. Even at current levels he can't rule out more downside. The stock already tested 250 and rebounded to 330-340, but failed to cross there and fell again.

kutumba.note: Buy in the offer-for-sale at the lowest point
kutumba.detail: Buy in the offer-for-sale opening today at Rs 104 (5% discount to market), as it's the lowest point. Value is around Rs 100, and at ~20 PE it becomes an attractive valuation. It is a simple, steady finance company with a moat, although it was earlier over-hyped (rose from Rs 22-25 to 150-160 in the railway-stock frenzy).

kutumba.note: Buy for long term only; value after correction
kutumba.detail: Buy for the long term only, not short term. After the recent correction value appears. It is especially in an exciting industry given post-climate-change water requirements and desalination demand.

kutumba.note: Buy for long term only; value after correction
kutumba.detail: Buy for the long term only, not short term. After the recent correction value appears in the stock.

kutumba.note: Avoid; exit on rallies, don't buy
kutumba.detail: Unnecessary stock; exit on rallies. It is a perennial 'turnaround' story that pops Rs 40-50 then gets hammered below par. As of now at the bottom he says avoid, and not to buy even on rallies.

kutumba.note: Weak stock; not worth even at this price
kutumba.detail: A weak stock he has repeatedly flagged. A COVID-era hype name that has made lower tops and lower bottoms since listing and hasn't risen; near 40 PE and near its 52-week low. Results came stable recently but it is definitely not worth even at this price.

kutumba.note: Hold long term; attractive valuation near 52-week low
kutumba.detail: Hold for the long term as of now. Near its 52-week low; setting aside the Shapoorji Pallonji-group overhang it is one of the larger infra players with a good order book at an attractive valuation. The majority of the Shapoorji stake is pledged; if the group gains liquidity (e.g., from a Tata Sons exit) the stock will improve.

kutumba.note: Avoidable; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: He has called it avoidable from the delisting/relisting onwards and still says avoidable now; exit on rallies. He also told a separate caller to hold Wipro rather than switch into Hexaware.

kutumba.note: Hold; caller at 28% profit
kutumba.detail: Hold HDFC Life (referred to as HFC Life). The caller was at 28% profit; he advised holding rather than booking, in contrast to South Indian Bank where he advised booking profits.

kutumba.note: Book profits; caller at 29% profit
kutumba.detail: Definitely book profits in South Indian Bank (caller at 29% profit). He contrasted this with HDFC Life, which he advised holding.

kutumba.note: Hold; can reinvent itself, large cash reserves
kutumba.detail: Hold Wipro instead of switching to Hexaware. Wipro has the capability of reinventing itself and has large cash reserves, which is its biggest advantage.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 24 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/XRLvgutyYhQ

## Global backdrop
- **Mixed global cues.** China reopened after a long holiday and Japan after weekend holidays — both **positive**; **Hang Seng red**; **US closed lower** overnight. **Gift Nifty** signalled a **98–100 point** gap-down.
- **AI disruption rout:** **IBM down 13%** in a single session — its worst since **2000** — blamed on **Anthropic**'s rapid tool releases. **EPAM, Globant, Accenture, Cognizant down 6–9%**; **Infosys ADR -5%**, **Wipro ADR -3%**. Market is "punishing the AI losers" (both SaaS and product cos).
- **Tariff noise:** Post-Supreme-Court ruling, **Trump** threatening harsher tariffs on countries backing out of deals; **EU froze** its US trade-deal talks (won't accept >15% over the struck deal); **FedEx is suing** the US govt to recover paid tariffs. Trump **State of the Union** expected tomorrow.
- **Geopolitics:** Iran tensions rising — record US arms mobilisation in the Middle East; a broader strike against Iran seen as "only a matter of time" (Thursday cited).
- **Safe havens / commodities:** **Gold ~$5,200**, **Silver ~$87**, **Crude ~$71** (steady), **Bitcoin weak ~$64,000**.

## Indian markets
- **Monthly F&O expiry day (NSE)** — flagged as crucial. Nifty closed **above 25,700** prior session; expected to open near **25,600**.
- **Month-to-date divergence:** **IT index -17%** vs **Bank Nifty up ~4.9–9%**. MTD IT damage: **Infosys -19%, Tech Mahindra -17%, HCL Tech -16%, TCS** also weak — an AI-driven **de-rating**.
- **IDFC First Bank:** **Rs 590 crore fraud** at a Haryana branch; ~**Rs 470 crore (80%) to be provisioned**, **~Rs 0.50 EPS hit** (one-time). Concern is weak **internal controls/audit**; stock fell **~15–16%** (briefly ~20%); trading near **1x price-to-book**. Caution: don't bottom-fish.
- **Sector stance:** Stay away from **IT** and problem stocks; favour **PSU banks** (credited Modi-govt banking reforms/mergers), **NBFCs, autos**, and **energy/grid** (data-centre-driven demand).
- **Opening readout:** Nifty **-114 pts**; declines lead (~**1:2** A/D). **IT index -2%**; Auto, Media, Realty, FMCG, Financials all red. **Bharti Airtel -1.2%** (Rs 20,000 cr plan); a digitech name **+2.5%** on a Rs 158 cr Reliance order. Gainers: **Blue Jet Healthcare ~+5%**, Aegis Logistics, Gujarat Gas, JK Tyres, Karur Vysya, Waaree Energies, UPL, ABB India.
- **Flows:** **FIIs sold**, DIIs bought modestly. **Rupee 90.87** (+7 paise). **India VIX ~14.1** (elevated → some fear).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): expects a **weakish but stabilising** market, **strong support 25,500–25,600**, no sharp fall; **stock-specific moves only**, no run-away bounce given tariff + war uncertainty. Notes **March-series rollover & 26,000 buying**, possible **rounding bottom at 25,500** (max puts there); **PCR ~1.06**. Speculative longs in **NTPC/IOC/Power Grid/CPSE**; shorts in **IDFC First, UPL** (restructuring poorly received), **Voltas, LTI Mindtree**. Delivery conviction: **Sun Pharma, Indian Hotels, Apollo Hospitals, Asian Paints, Container Corp**. Flagged volume in **D Development Engineers (+20%), Trident, Kitex, Suven, CCL (+5%)**. Verdict: "**buy-on-dips market**, not one to fear."
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): Market range-bound **14 sessions** in a **25,400–26,000** band, **downward channel**; **200-DMA ~25,331**. Needs a **decisive breakout**; trade cautiously with stop-loss; suggested **short below 25,550 if it holds 10 min**. **Bank Nifty strong**, near all-time high, clear breakout, big volume (partly IDFC long-short). OI: Nifty top call **26,000**, top put **25,700**; Bank Nifty top call **61,400**, support **61,000**. **VIX 14.17**.
- **Macro/AI theme:** Discussion of **Citroni Research "2028 Global Intelligence Crisis"** (viral substack) — mass AI adoption in 2026, govt revenue collapse as labour share of GDP (55–60%) falls, payments fee compression (Visa/Mastercard risk), GDP up but consumer demand down. Counter-essay by "Michael Blach" cited as homework. **Data centres** = "factories of the AI revolution"; beneficiaries include energy/grid, wiring (**KEI Industries, Polycab**, Anant Raj) — but priced-in and slow to flow through.

## Viewer stock Q&A (answered by Kutumba Rao)
- **Avanti Feeds** — wait/buy on dip near **Rs 1,175** (now ~1,310; ran from ~750 to 1,450–1,475); no hurry.
- **L&T Finance** — **book partial profits** (peaked ~320; gold-lending tailwind fading).
- **Sagility** — **Hold** (corrected from ~54–55 to 42–43; last result supports holding).
- **ETFs (Prakash, retiree):** general advice — **Nifty / Nifty-500 index ETFs via monthly SIP**; no specific call.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (L&T Finance)

kutumba.note: Wait for dip to ~1175; no hurry to buy at ~1310
kutumba.detail: The stock ran from around Rs 750 (after a dip to 650) up to Rs 1450-1475 and is now near Rs 1310. Kutumba Rao said to wait patiently rather than chase it; around Rs 1175 is a good consolidation point at which to buy. He framed it as a 'yes or no' - no need to buy in a hurry.

kutumba.note: Book partial profits; momentum revives only on a fresh high
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said L&T Finance performed extraordinarily and peaked around Rs 320, so book partial profits from here. Momentum returns only if it crosses a fresh high. He expects the gold-lending tailwind to fade as gold volatility peaks and margin/repayment pressures build in FY26-27, so the top line may moderate.

kutumba.note: Hold per last result; corrected from ~54-55 to 42-43
kutumba.detail: Asked by a caller at a 15% loss, Kutumba Rao said hold Sagility. The last result supports holding the stock; it weakened along with the broader market, having attempted ~54-55 recently before correcting to 42-43. The company is in a good sector/space.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 23 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/obawbJu9yZc

## Global backdrop
- **GIFT Nifty** indicated a **gap-up of ~150–180 points** Monday (it had shown 300+ over the weekend before cooling off). Friday's cash session had risen ~350 points.
- **US tariff uncertainty returned.** The **US Supreme Court** ruled Friday that Trump cannot use **IEEPA** (International Emergency Economic Powers Act) to impose his blanket tariffs — a ~10-month-delayed ruling.
- Trump is **routing around it**: invoking **Section 122** from **24 February**, raising the minimum tariff from **10% to 15% on all countries** (India, China, Russia, etc.). Section 122 is valid **only 150 days** (ordinance-like); beyond that he needs another section (**232 / 301 / Section 338 of the 1930 Tariff Act**, which could allow up to ~50%) or a Congress vote.
- **Effective tariff rate** fell from **16%** (pre-judgment) to **~13.7%** post the 15% move (~2.3% lower). Countries previously at 10% (Australia, UK, Singapore, UAE) and the UK's **0% deal rate** now move to **15%** — flattening the **level playing field** for India.
- **India–US interim trade pact meeting deferred** (today's headline). India had braced to pay **18%**; whether it becomes 15% or 18% is unresolved (final deal possibly April–May). Treasury Secretary **Scott Bessent** flagged further tools; ~**$175bn** in collected tariffs may face refund-related court action.
- **Gold ~$5,100; silver ~$87** (off ~90) — rising on renewed trade and geopolitical (Iran–US war) tension.

## Indian markets
- **Q3 results complete**: revenue growth **stabilized** but **profitability under pressure** — earnings visibility is the differentiator from here.
- **FII flows**: net buying ~**Rs 16,000 cr** in February, but a **second straight week of modest selling**; on the day FIIs sold ~**Rs 1,000 cr**, DIIs bought ~**Rs 2,700 cr**.
- Last week: Nifty/Sensex **+~0.2%**, mid-cap **+0.4%**, small-cap **−~0.5%**. IT sold aggressively by FIIs.
- **Rupee** depreciating toward **91/$** on crude + geopolitics; risk to deficit given India is a large crude importer.
- **Levels (Kutumba Rao)**: **25,800** key resistance, then **26,000** — needed for stability. Calls at 26,000; aggressive **call writing at 25,500/25,650**; puts building at 25,500/25,000. Below 25,500 → caution, possible sharp swing toward 25,000. **VIX rose to 14.36 (36-week high)** — moving into riskier territory. **PCR** near 1. Weekly + monthly **F&O settlement** this week.
- **Levels (Ramakrishna, technicals)**: series started at ~24,700, now ~+1,000 pts (~4%). **25,900** crossover needed for further upside; **25,400** low is crucial — break = weakness; resolution likely **by Wednesday**. Intraday R1 ~**25,690–25,696** (hold 10 min for long); support **25,413** (decisive break = short). **Bank Nifty** at all-time-high closes with better momentum than Nifty (weak Nifty RSI/MACD).
- **Bank stress**: **IDFC First Bank** (hit lower circuit) and **AU Small Finance Bank** dragging Bank Nifty on a **Haryana government-deposit fraud / forensic audit**; avoid these. Prefer strong PSU banks (SBI, Bank of Maharashtra, Union Bank, Bank of India).
- **Opening breadth positive**: NSE ~1,500 advances vs ~500 declines; Nifty +~90→170 pts, Sensex +~300. Gainers skewed to trade-deal/textile names (Trident, Welspun India, Honasa, Neuland, Zuven Pharma +5%, Morepen +8% on an order); losers included Netweb, Hyundai, Godfrey Phillips, Emcure.

## Sector / strategy view (Vasanth + Kutumba Rao)
- **Avoid export-oriented** names (textiles, aqua, gems & jewellery) for now — **except pharma**; favour **domestic-economy-facing** stocks.
- **Avoid IT** — not a tariff issue but an **AI-driven tectonic shift** to business models; wait for clarity.
- Buy on **earnings**: large caps at ATHs on real numbers (**Eicher Motors, Shriram Finance, L&T**); metals super-cycle (**Hindustan Copper**); repeatedly-flagged names with strong numbers — **GE Vernova, Force Motors, Kirloskar Industries, Netweb, AB Capital, L&T Finance, Hitachi Energy**.
- **Verdict (Kutumba Rao)**: still a **buy-on-dips** market for delivery / medium-to-long-term investors; **sell-on-rallies near 25,800 only for short-term position traders**.
- **Portfolio discipline (Vasanth, repeated)**: don't hand your whole portfolio to anyone — take advice but **decide and manage it yourself**. **Asset allocation** across equity/debt/gold-silver/real estate is mandatory; gold should be only **5% (max 10%)** of a portfolio.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/OI/levels view; viewer stock Q&A (his own calls: Stallion Fluoro, Anantraj, Hitech Pipes).
- **Kranthi** — fundamental analyst (answered Suzlon; advisor-relationship discussion).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst (Nifty/Bank Nifty levels, gold chart, Netweb/Olectra technicals).
- **Vasanth** — anchor/analyst (opening macro monologue, gold caller, mutual-fund and Laurus/Water Base/Ather guidance).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Anantraj)

kutumba.note: Operated counter; don't buy fresh at this price
kutumba.detail: He called it an operated counter with more noise than performance, hard to comment on. It disappointed badly versus its segment. If it is only a small rally, exit; no one should buy fresh at this price. After the rights issue is done, look at the price and quarterly performance before deciding.

kutumba.note: Buy as market consolidates, with a strict stop loss near 500
kutumba.detail: Its earlier rise was on data-center hype; it went above 700 then corrected, and now shows some stability. Last result commentary said projects are on track, bringing renewed buying interest. It dipped below 500 over three months and has risen to about 550; as the market consolidates one can buy, but keep a strict stop loss around 500.

kutumba.note: Avoid; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: Not a great stock; it moves in a 30-40 range on and off every 3-4 months, but has fallen continuously since the last quarter's result, going below 75 and bottoming near 87-90. Top line rose but bottom line did not, and raw-material/commodity costs are likely to rise. He would avoid it and exit on rallies.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 20 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/p32GxX8-mvI

## Global backdrop
- **Iran–US war fears** dominated. Trump threatened Iran has only **10–15 days** to strike a "meaningful deal or bad things happen"; US has its largest West Asia military deployment since the **2003 Iraq invasion** (two carriers, incl. Abraham Lincoln, deployed 7–8 months — strike possible "any time").
- US markets closed lower; renewed **software/tech selling** — **Globant -7.3%**, **EPAM -17%**, Accenture, Cognizant down; **Infosys ADR -3.3%**, **Wipro -2.6%**.
- **Brent crude ~$72**; **dollar index ~98** (strong) — both negative for India (weaker rupee + costlier crude imports = fiscal-deficit pressure). House view: even a real Iran strike likely caps crude at **$80–85**, not a catastrophe (Russia–Ukraine parallel); markets **over-reacted**.
- **AI Summit** running: **Mukesh Ambani/Reliance-Jio** to invest **Rs 10 lakh crore (~$110bn)** in AI over 7 years (new, not the earlier $200bn); Sam Altman (OpenAI), Anthropic CEO, Chandrasekaran (Tata Sons) spoke; PM round table with CEOs.

## Indian markets
- Friday open **flat-to-slightly negative**; Gift Nifty negative. Nifty opened **25,458** (-23, near flat); Sensex -43; **Bank Nifty +120 (outperforming)**. Later: Nifty flat, Sensex -62, Bank Nifty +140.
- Recap of **Thursday's plunge**: Nifty **-315 pts**, Bank Nifty **-800**, Sensex **~-1,200**, "like a waterfall." Modest institutional selling (**FII -Rs 880 cr, DII -Rs 596 cr**) — it was **retail panic/stop-loss square-offs**. Breadth brutal: **696 up vs 2,290 down** (~1:4). Nifty broke 20-DMA (25,539).
- **Levels:** Nifty support **25,450 / 25,100 / 25,000**; **200-DMA 25,301** (Sensex already closed below its 200-DMA — worrisome); resistance **25,885** (Thu high); swing high ~26,000 (11 Feb). Bearish engulfing candles on Nifty/Sensex/Bank Nifty. **VIX up to 13.46** (bull discomfort); **PCR collapsed 1.22 → ~0.7** in one day (buyer shortage); options 26,000 still piled, puts shifting to 24,800. Monthly settlement in **3 sessions**.
- **Stock-specific notes:** NCC opened -4–5% on NHAI ban but closed flat (**promoter buying**); Apollo Pipes also saw promoter buying; ABB India +4% (good order); CIE Automotive +3% (good result); Centum Electronics +8%; News & Soft -6–7% (after Thu spike). Other movers: PACE Digitek (Rs 89 cr RailTel order), Texmaco Rail/RVNL JV, Federal Bank (Rs 686 cr tax refund), Zydus Life (clean FDA inspection), GHV Infra (Rs 123 cr order), RVNL (Madras HC rejected appeal — negative), OMCs (IOC/BPCL/HPCL negative on crude).
- **AP investment news:** Waaree Group (Waaree Energy Storage Solutions) to build a **16 GWh integrated battery giga-factory** near Rambilli/Anakapalli — **Rs 8,175 cr, ~3,000 direct jobs**; both Telugu CMs (Revanth Reddy, Chandrababu Naidu) to speak at AI Summit.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer Q&A): markets weak/lower-side; multiple negatives (crude, dollar, fiscal) caused a mild panic "twister"; **wait-and-watch, aggressive buying unwarranted** until Middle East clarity; below-0.7 PCR is a good bet for position traders. Stock calls (his own): **Sezzle India** & **Heritage Foods** — accumulate long-term; **NTPC, PFC** — good dividend bets; **Canara Bank, PNB** — buy on dips for dividend yield; **Vedanta** — avoid (promoter-whim); **Kotak Mahindra Bank** — contrarian buy; **WPIL (Worthington Pumps)** — hold (slow, can reach 450–500); **Newjaisa/News & Soft** — don't chase, stop-loss if buying above 600.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical): turned to a **weak trend**; avoid trading today; only risky traders short with SL **25,485**; supports 200-DMA/25,100/25,000, resistance 25,885; gave a failed support-1 buy call Thursday.
- **Vasanth** (anchor/analyst): handled mail Q&A and part of caller Q&A — Sansera Engineering & Ingersoll Rand ("excellent" auto-ancillary/good co.), Coal India (dividend + 24–25% 3–5yr CAGR but weak 10yr), IDBI Bank (positional on disinvestment), partial-profit booking on BEL/DLF/HBL/NCC/Tata Power, Tasty Bite (hold/exit in rallies), Hari Om Pipes (hold, don't add), Kaveri Seeds (hold to 1,000+), Tanla (purely speculative), Wint Wealth corporate bonds (8–12%) for the FD-alternative caller.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 7 buys (Sezzle India, Heritage Foods, NTPC, PFC, Canara Bank, PNB, Kotak Mahindra Bank)

kutumba.note: Accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns.
kutumba.detail: Caller asked about entering at the current price point. Kutumba Rao said buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term; can give decent returns. By long term he means hold a minimum of more than one year (don't ask back in three or six months).

kutumba.note: Buy/accumulate from current levels for the long term (>1 year hold).
kutumba.detail: Same caller query. He said both stocks (Sezzle India and Heritage Foods) should be bought/accumulated from current levels for the long term, holding a minimum of more than one year; they can give decent returns.

kutumba.note: Good dividend-yield bet as an FD alternative; have at least 3-5 years.
kutumba.detail: As an alternative to a fixed deposit, he flagged PSU dividend-yield companies and said NTPC would be a good bet (check the dividend record date before buying). He cautioned to have at least three to five years, since PSU companies can post loss-years and an ill-timed exit forces a booked loss that eats the dividend.

kutumba.note: Good dividend-yield bet as an FD alternative, alongside NTPC.
kutumba.detail: In the same FD-alternative / dividend-yield discussion he said PFC too would be a good bet. Check the dividend record date and hold with a 3-5 year horizon.

kutumba.note: Large dividend payer; PSU banks slightly high now, so add on dips.
kutumba.detail: He said banks are slightly on the higher side as of now; if they fall a bit, large dividend payers like Canara Bank can be attempted on the dip for dividend yield above the FD level.

kutumba.note: Large dividend payer; add on a pullback for dividend yield.
kutumba.detail: Mentioned alongside Canara Bank as a large dividend payer. With PSU banks slightly high now, attempt on a fall for dividend yield above the FD level.

kutumba.note: Good dividend but runs at the promoter's whim; avoid for peace of mind.
kutumba.detail: He said Vedanta gives a good dividend now, but the company runs at the promoter's whim, so leave such risky bets aside; for peace of mind the PSU dividend stocks are a better opportunity.

kutumba.note: Contrarian buy; good management, fundamentals and nationwide franchise.
kutumba.detail: For a caller seeking a good private bank (already holding RBL and Federal), Kutumba Rao recommended Kotak contrarian-wise. Good management moving ahead with good fundamentals, a strong nationwide network and an excellent franchise.

kutumba.note: Good niche engineering company; slow and steady, can go back up to 450-500. Hold.
kutumba.detail: Caller holds 5,000 shares around 550. A good company with good market share on the engineering side, pumps for a niche segment. No immediate jump expected, but slow and steady it can go back up to 450-500 easily, so hold, no worry. When buying such large quantity, buy in tranches.

kutumba.note: Don't chase; sustains only with follow-up buying. Mandatory stop-loss above 600.
kutumba.detail: Caller bought around 600 in a hurry. Buying it repeatedly is unnecessary; it made a large move (crossed 600, up to ~620) but only sustains with follow-up buying, and fell sharply after results (bottomed ~475 over three months). If buying above 600, definitely keep a trading stop-loss; entering at higher levels is risky. The Thursday spike likely came on an unconfirmed social-media trigger.
