# Summary — Business Breakfast, 18 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/dTZiFrkTlu0

## Global backdrop
- **US markets** closed green after a **V-shaped recovery** from opening losses (S&P 500, Nasdaq).
- US **software stocks fell 2–2.5%** (EPAM, Globant, Cognizant, Accenture) on continuing AI concerns. **Infosys ADR +0.2%**, **Wipro ADR ~-2%**.
- **US–Iran nuclear talks** (second round) reached an apparent agreement, easing geopolitical tension → **gold -3%, silver -5.5%**; crude weakened.
- **Chinese markets shut all week** for New Year holidays; Taiwan/KOSPI positive.

## Indian markets
- Expected **flat open**; Gift Nifty ~**25,761** (+18 pts). Nifty actually opened roughly flat (+5–10 pts), Sensex +27, Bank Nifty +22–23.
- **Bank Nifty hit a fresh all-time-high close** (~2% recovery over two days); Nifty recovered ~200 pts from its bottom and filled a recent gap.
- **Trading zone 25,500–26,000**; key level **25,800** for safety. Calls heaviest at **26,000/26,200**, puts at **25,000–25,700**. **PCR ~1.12**, **VIX below 13**.
- Breadth positive: NSE **1,040 advances / 580 declines**; mid/small-caps outperforming.
- **Rupee ~90.72**/USD, may cross 91. **FII buying not yet significant** — no decisive directional move expected until it arrives.
- **It is a buy-on-dips, not sell-on-rallies market**; activity is becoming **stock-specific and news-driven**.

## AI Summit / IT theme (dominant story)
- **AI Impact Summit** in Delhi (day 2 of 5). **Ashwini Vaishnaw: $200B investment over next 2 years** (~$90B already underway, ~$90B on paper).
- **Infosys–Anthropic** agentic-AI partnership (Infosys rose up to 4%, closed ~+2%) and **TCS–AMD** 200 MW data-centre deal drove an IT rebound; IT led gains.
- New-AI-services seen as a **$300–400B opportunity by 2030**. Indian IT going the **tie-up route** (couldn't build enterprise AI in-house).
- **Vinod Khosla**: IT services/BPO "gone" within 5 years (by 2030); by 2050 nobody will need jobs as goods/services production becomes near-free. Strong reskilling warning for IT workforce.
- Beneficiaries flagged: data centres, **green/grid/power, cooling equipment, EPC, hardware, cloud** companies.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — fundamentals + viewer stock Q&A (see dedicated file). Worst over for IT short-term; consolidation with mild upward bias; small-caps with 2–3-year view; PSU defence/shipyard names attractive after correction.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals: bullish phase, RSI ~62 (Bank Nifty) / ~51.8 (Nifty), improving MACD. Above 25,770–800 → 26,000; support 25,610–25,650; Bank Nifty resistance ~61,800, support ~60,500. Gave **Bajaj Finance** entry: dip to **990** (or break above **1,032**).
- **Vasanth** — anchor; noted sector-rotation (yesterday's gainers = today's losers) and Quality Walls double down-circuit.

## Stock-specific notes
- **Movers (prev session):** Newgen Software +14%, Praj +10%, Force Motors ~+5% (20x in 4–5 yrs); Reliance Infra, Thermax, Acme Solar, HFCL strong.
- **Today's open:** Suven/Cohance Lifesciences +~5–6%, Netweb Tech +3% (world's smallest supercomputer launch), Dilip Buildcon +4–5% (contract win); IT pack saw renewed selling (Tech Mahindra, Persistent, Infosys); **Quality Walls -5% (down circuit again)**; **Ola Electric** near lifetime low on terrible results + CEO arrest-warrant news — "stay away."
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (PFC (Power Finance Corporation), Dixon Technologies, Quality Walls)

kutumba.note: Hold; don't average immediately
kutumba.detail: Result came flat but top line is holding above Rs 1,000 cr and the smart-meter business is continuing, so no need to fear. Don't average right now; wait for annual results and stabilization. Don't sell.

kutumba.note: Defence stock attractive after correction
kutumba.detail: Labelled as a defence stock; after correction to nearly Rs 2,400 (from ~3,700) it looks attractive. Results stable, order book good, Government of India focus increasing. Hold; don't average immediately.

kutumba.note: Hold despite weakness
kutumba.detail: Consistent weakness with a fall of more than 50% in recent times, but valuation is present at current levels. Hold; don't average immediately.

kutumba.note: Hold despite weakness
kutumba.detail: Consistent weakness recently, but valuation present at current levels. Hold; don't average immediately.

kutumba.note: Hold; becoming attractive at current levels
kutumba.detail: Results are all good. Like other PSU shipyards many bought the 2024 peak and got stuck; touching old highs will take time. Becoming attractive to buy around 2,400-2,500. Don't sell, hold.

kutumba.note: Exit on any small rally
kutumba.detail: Surprisingly weak result with flattish commentary derailed the turnaround thesis; fell from ~870-880 to ~650-660, near the 52-week low. Belongs to the good Dhanuka group so hold with patience for now, but exit on any small rally, closer to 210-220.

kutumba.note: Hold cautiously; watch margins/acquisition
kutumba.detail: Dec-quarter top line jumped but margins are disappointing; cash-rich and many await an acquisition that could take it back to 700-800 levels, but if cash exhausts without one there is downside risk. They paid Rs 12 cr interest (took a loan) which must be watched. Hold; watch one more quarter; SaaS model faces possible AI disruption.

kutumba.note: Accumulate on every decline
kutumba.detail: Buy without any doubt; accumulate on every decline. Post the REC merger this becomes one very big entity. Definitely a good buy at current levels.

kutumba.note: Avoid; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: IPO-euphoria encashment; a ~Rs 7,000-8,000 cr market cap is not justified for a fashion-jewellery company of this size. Already at its 52-week low and could halve from here. It is now a large-player game (Aditya Birla's Indriya). Exit on rallies.

kutumba.note: Hold; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: Good branding but flat and not growing, turnover stagnant for long; same risk as BlueStone applies. Watch whether the company restructures and kick-starts the business with new brands; otherwise it is a good takeover target. Exit on rallies.

kutumba.note: Speculative bet only
kutumba.detail: An extremely speculative counter, frequently in the ban list; swings to 175-180 then easily back to 135-140. Treat only as a speculative bet. At ~145-148 it can prepare a short-term rally and may attempt 160-180 again.

kutumba.note: Buy only for dividend; limited upside
kutumba.detail: Value is present but the stock is already near its peak (~194, ~14 PE) with limited appreciation ahead; may attempt 210-215. Dividend is only ~2.1% yield, so buy only for dividend. Could rotate out into better performers on a rally.

kutumba.note: Wait one more quarter for fresh buying
kutumba.detail: Corrected to ~3,300-3,400 after a brokerage report, recovered and now sustains at ~4,000; last quarter was flat. Would wait for another quarter of performance before fresh buying.

kutumba.note: Medium-term buy at current levels
kutumba.detail: Already corrected from 18,000 to ~10,000 and stabilizing at ~11,650. One of the largest third-party electronics manufacturers. Definitely a buy; for the medium term it looks good at current levels.

kutumba.note: Buy nearer 22-23, in March
kutumba.detail: HUL-demerged ice-cream entity, double down-circuit; market questions the ~Rs 6,000 cr market cap for an ice-cream company. Buyable closer to 22-23 levels, in March, with the summer season approaching.

kutumba.note: Hold; low-liquidity caution
kutumba.detail: A good company; hype has gone and it is now at a realistic valuation (~1,800-1,900 vs a prior 3,500). Results flat but competent, with an encouraging recent earnings-call transcript. Hold; watch carefully due to low volumes/liquidity which can cause sharp moves.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 17 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5SDRb7f3fDE

## Global backdrop
- **No global cues** — US markets shut for **Presidents' Day**; most Asian markets (China, Hong Kong, Korea) closed for **Lunar New Year**. India trading practically alone among major markets.
- **Gift Nifty** pointing to a **~90-point loss** after the prior session's **211-point relief rally**.
- **Trump/Iran** posture calmer; US-Iran **nuclear-deal talks** ongoing, no fresh statements.
- **India AI Summit** (begun in Bengaluru/Delhi) the dominant theme — **Anthropic CEO** calls India "a huge playground," targeting **doubling India revenue run-rate in 4 months**. **TCS-AMD** deal to design AI infrastructure (vs Nvidia). **Infosys** holding an **AI Investors' Day** today.
- **Defence**: French President in India (Mumbai); India-France defence deals expected, possibly a large deal led by **BEL**.

## Indian markets
- Today is a **Tuesday weekly options expiry**; index set to **open negative**; action expected to be **stock-specific**.
- Yesterday's recovery led by **boring/PSU names** — **Coal India, Power Grid, ONGC up 3-4%**, plus HDFC Bank, Adani Enterprises; **L&T** record breakout. Nifty closed near **25,700** after a ~230-point intraday recovery.
- **IT weak** — Q3 season over; Tech Mahindra, Wipro closed weak. Advice: stay away from IT until reinvention is clear.
- **Bank Nifty strong** — closed at all-time-high on a closing basis; every constituent up except ICICI Bank; PSU banks (Canara, Union, Bank of Baroda) jumped.
- **Capital-market stocks sold off** — **BSE -7.3%**, Angel One, Nuvama, on **RBI tightening collateral/MTF rules** (50% cash collateral).
- **Sector sentiment (Tijori)**: Defence & Aerospace most optimistic; positive interest in Auto, Textiles/Apparel, Engineering/Capital Goods, Solar. T&D super-cycle — **Rs 1.35 lakh crore** combined order book. Weak: tiles, sanitaryware, plywood/laminates, dairy, aviation, city gas.
- **Flows**: FIIs sold a modest **Rs 900 cr**; DIIs bought **~Rs 1,700 cr**. **PCR 1.11** (jumped sharply); **VIX ~13.3**. Rupee weak vs dollar — risk of slip toward **92** if it crosses 91.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets) — present and named throughout; delivered the morning macro/F&O read and answered most viewer stock Q&A.
- **Ramakrishna** (technical) — bullish-engulfing candle in Nifty & Bank Nifty; Nifty likely to consolidate (100-150 pt range), Bank Nifty directional; Nifty support **25,593 / 25,500**, call writing **25,750**, put writing **25,500**.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

## Kutumba Rao's notable views
- **Natco Pharma** flagged (mentioned by anchor): +7% on 2.25 cr volume after generic **semaglutide (GLP-1)** approval; KR notes the stock historically lacks price momentum despite good news.
- **"Baap" (Buy-At-Any-Price) stocks** thesis under strain — Asian Paints, HUL, ITC, Infosys, TCS; **HDFC Bank only ~4% annualised over 5 yrs**. New-economy names (Eternal, Swiggy, Lenskart, Nykaa, Paytm, PB Fintech, Delhivery, Meesho) drawing investor affection.
- **Auto ancillaries** running well (Jamna Auto, Bharat Seats, RACL Geartech, PPAP, Pix Transmission); **GMR Airport** rewarded post-results.
- **IT**: only contrarian, extreme-long-term buyers should add; companies must reinvent for AI; **Anthropic** could become a top-10 India IT player.

## Stock calls (viewer Q&A) — Kutumba Rao
- **Talbros Auto** — Hold | **Senco Gold** — Hold | **Infosys** — Buy only for extreme long-term/contrarian; average for an existing holder | **CDSL** — Sell (book the small loss vs Infosys) | **TVS Supply Chain** — Buy on dips (~107-108) | **Motilal Oswal ("Most")** — Buy, start with 25% | **NCC** — Buy/Accumulate (1yr+ view) | **Coal India** — Accumulate near 400-410 | **Netweb/Network Technologies** — Hold | **Persistent & Coforge** — Hold | **Sharath Industries** — Hold | **Tilaknagar Industries** — Wait & see (avoid for now) | **Divi's Labs** — Hold | **Suzlon** — Hold | **IOB** — Hold, exit on rally to 42-43 | **News & Soft** — Hold (fell with IT) | **HAL** — Hold.
- Answered by **Ramakrishna**, not Kutumba Rao: **Muthoot Finance** (buy small, add on rise), **Hindustan Copper** (fresh buy near 500-510).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (Infosys, TVS Supply Chain Solutions, Motilal Oswal Financial Services, NCC, Coal India), 1 sell (CDSL)

kutumba.note: One of the better auto-ancillary names, decent results
kutumba.detail: Kutumba Rao said hold Talbros Auto, calling it one of the better auto-ancillary companies with decent results. It is near its 52-week high (recently ~300, stable ~280) with signs of crossing the 52-week highs again, helped by auto ancillaries doing well lately.

kutumba.note: Eye-popping Dec result + precious-metals rally
kutumba.detail: He said hold Senco Gold. The December result was eye-popping: revenue ~Rs 3,000 crore (up 100% QoQ) and net profit from Rs 53 crore to Rs 267 crore. With 320-330 as a major base, the stock can attempt 400 again, helped by speculator fancy and the precious-metals rally.

kutumba.note: Only for extreme long-term/contrarian; average if holding
kutumba.detail: He said buy Infosys only if you have a contrarian, extreme long-term view, as it is a positional trade. These IT majors (TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Tech Mahindra) have huge cash reserves and will be compelled to reinvent for AI. For an existing holder needing cash he advised averaging Infosys rather than booking the loss.

kutumba.note: Sell to raise cash rather than book larger Infosys loss
kutumba.detail: For a caller needing money who held CDSL (~Rs 5,000 loss) and Infosys (~Rs 40,000 loss), Kutumba Rao said sell CDSL now, because booking the much larger Infosys loss is not right; he also suggested averaging Infosys. (Ramakrishna gave the opposite view, favouring selling Infosys.)

kutumba.note: Turnaround result, new high likely shortly
kutumba.detail: He said buy TVS Supply Chain on dips. After good turnaround results the stock already rose ~25-30% from around 95-96 and a new high looks likely very shortly. Since the recent rally was sharp, he would buy on a small correction toward 107-108 over the next 1-2 months.

kutumba.note: Start with only 25% of intended quantity
kutumba.detail: On 'Most' (Motilal Oswal), Kutumba Rao said buy with a medium-to-long-term view, but slowly — first buy only 25% of what you want. The recent quarter was flat; he advised watching the post-AI-summit narrative and any company announcements before adding more.

kutumba.note: Infra bottomed out; 1-year-plus view
kutumba.detail: He said buy/accumulate NCC at current levels with a one-year-plus view, as the infra space appears to have bottomed out well and such stocks could perform in coming days. He cautioned it is not a quarterly-payout stock and is currently a bit weak on financials, so do not expect quick returns.

kutumba.note: PSU safety-net, good dividend payout
kutumba.detail: He said accumulate Coal India anywhere close to 400 or 410, medium-to-long term. PSU stocks act as a safety net with sound performance; subsidiary IPOs and the announced government disinvestment pattern are positives, and there is a good dividend payout. It rose ~3% the prior session.

kutumba.note: Hardware/server maker, not an IT software firm
kutumba.detail: On Network Technologies/Netweb he stressed it is a hardware company supplying servers and high-end computing solutions (and some software) to IT firms, so it should not be valued like software companies. It took a hit in the recent tech selloff (had gone to ~4,500, back to ~3,600), but results are good and there is no need to panic — hold.

kutumba.note: Attractive fundamentally; caveat is AI adaptation
kutumba.detail: He said hold Persistent Systems at current levels, since clarity still needs to come on whether the IT companies reinvent for AI. Fundamentally the stock looks attractive, but the caveat is whether it adapts to AI.

kutumba.note: Attractive fundamentally; caveat is AI adaptation
kutumba.detail: He said hold Coforge at current levels alongside Persistent, pending clarity on AI reinvention. Fundamentally it looks attractive, with the caveat being whether it adapts to AI.

kutumba.note: Former dark horse; controlled/operated stock
kutumba.detail: He said if the holder wants to hold Sharath Industries, hold it. A former 'dark horse' (flagged at 40) on the aqua side that gave good returns earlier; recently dull with low volumes, around 170, close to its 52-week high. He cautioned it is more of a controlled/operated stock.

kutumba.note: Poor recent results, promoter pledging; wait and see
kutumba.detail: He advised waiting and watching rather than buying Tilaknagar Industries now. The recent results were poor (a loss despite a dramatic top-line jump and high capex), and promoters pledged maximum shares. He would wait to see whether a turnaround comes in the coming quarter before buying.

kutumba.note: Bought near 52-week low; large pharma name
kutumba.detail: He said hold Divi's Labs with nothing to fear. The caller bought near the 52-week low (~5,000; 52wk low 4,941) and the stock is already at ~6,100 (once went to 7,000). It is one of the better stocks to hold in an uncertain atmosphere, being one of the largest pharma market caps at Rs 1.6 lakh crore.

kutumba.note: Stagnant near 50 on institutional supply
kutumba.detail: He said hold Suzlon at current levels. Due to institutional supply the stock has stagnated around 50 and needs a breakout from there before it can move higher.

kutumba.note: Govt disinvestment/merger catalyst
kutumba.detail: He said hold IOB at current levels and exit on a rally. Government of India disinvestment (like the Bank of Maharashtra announcement) is expected, and it may be merged with another bank. When that announcement comes a small rally up to 42-43 could occur, which is the time to exit.

kutumba.note: Fell with IT; nothing wrong company-specific
kutumba.detail: On the caller's 50%-loss position, Kutumba Rao said it fell along with all IT stocks and there is nothing specifically wrong at the company. Recovery is likely only after IT sentiment improves (Ramakrishna added the chart is very weak with weak recent results).

kutumba.note: Monopoly PSU; correction is a buying opportunity
kutumba.detail: He said hold HAL, citing its monopoly status as a Government of India PSU counter. The current corrective mode is a buying opportunity, not a selling opportunity.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 13 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/Il5XOuIWc3Y

## Global backdrop
- **US markets closed negative** on AI panic: **Dow fell ~700–1,000 points** from highs, **Nasdaq down ~468 points (~2%)**. Trucking/transport stocks corrected with the AI panic.
- Trigger: **Anthropic** launched a new version/platform and reportedly raised **~$30bn funding at a ~$380bn valuation** — fanning fears of AI disruption to the software services sector.
- **Yen** had its best week vs the dollar in ~15 months after **Takaichi's** historic election win in Japan; fund flows are moving to Japan, drawing international funds away from India.
- **Asian cues negative**; **GIFT Nifty** indicating a gap-down (Nifty implied ~140 points lower).
- Watch items flagged: **Indo-US deal fine print**, and **Bangladesh elections** (preference for BNP over Jamaat-e-Islami).

## Indian markets (previous session)
- **Nifty** opened lower at 25,907 (also the day's high) and closed **-147 pts at 25,807**; daily chart formed a **bearish candle with upper shadow**.
- **Sensex -558 pts at 83,674**; **Bank Nifty** roughly flat (~-6 pts) at ~60,740, range-bound for a fourth straight session.
- **Nifty Mid & Small Caps -~0.5%** each.
- **IT was the drag**: index **-~5% on the day, -~6% on the week**, into a **10-month low**. Losers: **Infosys, TCS, Tech Mahindra, HCL Tech, Wipro (-4 to -6%)**.
- Gainers: **Bajaj Finance, Shriram Finance, Eicher Motors, ICICI Bank, Trent (+2–3%)** — NBFCs/select private banks cushioned the fall.
- **FII buying only Rs 108 cr; DII only Rs 277 cr**; rupee ~**90.61**. Advance/decline ~**1:3**. **India VIX +1.6% to 11.73**.
- **>130 stocks at 52-week highs** (Shriram Finance, Eicher, Voltas, Max Financial, Indus Towers, Jindal Steel, SBI, APL Apollo, L&T, ONGC, Bajaj Auto, JSW Steel, NTPC…); 52-week lows clustered in IT (Sonata, KPIT, OFSS, TCS, Poly Medicure, Mastek, Wipro, Happiest Minds…).

## Live open (during show)
- Markets opened **gap-down as expected**: **Nifty ~25,582–25,600, Bank Nifty ~60,582, Sensex ~83,019**. IT again the top drag (Infosys -7%, TCS -6%, HCL Tech -5%). Pockets of strength: life insurers (HDFC Life, SBI Life), Bharti Airtel; result-driven pops in Honasa (+4%), Tarq/DVG Transformer (+12%), Engineers India (+12%).

## Levels to watch
- **Nifty resistance 26,000** (strong hurdle / max call OI); **supports 25,700 then 25,500** (key — below 25,500 = further weakness). Put-call ratio fell to **~0.82** (fear creeping in).
- **Bank Nifty** clear consolidation around **60,800**; expected to cushion downside.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamentals/markets + viewer Q&A): markets slipped into a weak trend after failing 26,000; AI-panic in IT is the core driver and recovery is unlikely until the panic subsides and Indian IT visibly invests in the AI shift. Stay invested but exit weaker stocks; protect profits with trailing stops.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals): consolidation / gap-fill toward ~25,700; short-term weakness (fading momentum, RSI down). Lighten IT positions; buy-on-dips for a 10–15 day positional view at good supports.
- **Kranthi** (fundamentals): consolidation phase, FPIs not very negative; **buy-on-dip / sell-on-rally trader's market**. Stick to large-cap IT (TCS, Infosys) or transformative mid-cap IT (Persistent) on a contrarian long-term view; accumulate good-result mid/small caps; run profits or build only with strong cash flows.

## Viewer stock calls (attribution)
- **Kutumba Rao** answered: Kaynes/CG Power, Avanti Feeds, Marksons Pharma, Amara Raja, Heritage Foods, Bajaj Housing (+Satvik/HPL), Ola Electric, Reliance Infra, South Indian Bank, J&K Bank, Bell Rice/Usha Martin, Laurus Labs, Poly Medicure, REC, Vikran Engineering/Raymond, and the large-cap IT (TCS/Infosys/Wipro) exit-on-rally view.
- **Ramakrishna** answered: OFSS, SKM Egg Products, BEL, BSE, RVNL, NBCC, HAL/BDL, Chambal Fertilizers, Karur Vysya/IDBI Bank, Tata Technologies.
- **Kranthi** answered: DAM Capital, L&T/Adani Power/Vedanta/IDFC, large-cap IT (TCS/Infosys/Wipro hold), PhysicsWallah, Shriram Properties/Shriram Finance, PFC/NCC/Suzlon/Coal India, KPIT/TCS.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 2 buys (Avanti Feeds, Laurus Labs), 4 sells (Jammu & Kashmir Bank, TCS, Infosys, Wipro)

kutumba.note: No buy now; wait for stability after the first big IT fall
kutumba.detail: Said there is no need to buy Persistent (or Coforge) now. The first major fall was just yesterday and IT is down 3-15% YTD. Have patience and wait for stability; no hurry.

kutumba.note: No buy now; wait for stability after the first big IT fall
kutumba.detail: Grouped with Persistent. No need to buy at present; let some stability come first given the sharp IT-sector correction. No hurry to enter.

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; relief rallies not holding
kutumba.detail: Recently controversial with news flow; even after clarifications the relief rallies are not holding up. Advised exiting on rallies. (Caller was in a 20% loss.)

kutumba.note: Hold with a trailing stop
kutumba.detail: Advised holding CG Power with a trailing stop in place. (Caller was in a 20% profit.)

kutumba.note: Book ~50% profit; make the balance free of cost
kutumba.detail: Stock rose about 50% off the December low. Advised booking 50% profit here and at least making the remaining position free of cost (FOC). (Caller was in 150% profit.)

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; weak December result
kutumba.detail: December result was weak, below expectations, with a sharply lower bottom line. Advised exiting on rallies, even if it comes close up to 200.

kutumba.note: Hold; transformative capex stage, fundamentally strong
kutumba.detail: Company is in a transformative stage with heavy capex on new-generation products and lithium batteries; the Telangana unit is yet to be commissioned. Stock may stay weak near term with weak results, but fundamentally extremely strong, so hold; no need to sell at these levels.

kutumba.note: Hold; company doing well, dairy is seasonal
kutumba.detail: Quarterly result was slightly flat; stock stabilized around 430-440 and also dipped to 360-370. Company is doing extremely well and the product portfolio is diversifying. Dairy is seasonal and procurement-rate changes cause dips, but no need to fear. Hold.

kutumba.note: Hold with extremely long-term view
kutumba.detail: Corrected since listing, but the business is improving well. Advised holding with an extremely long-term view; no need to sell at current levels.

kutumba.note: Hold; recently listed, needs time
kutumba.detail: Recently listed and needs more time frame. As of now, hold.

kutumba.note: Hold; recently listed, needs time
kutumba.detail: Grouped with the recently listed names; needs more time frame to play out. Hold for now.

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; very weak counter at 52-week lows
kutumba.detail: Has been advised against for a while. A very weak counter trading at 52-week lows; not a stock to buy but one to exit on rallies until a turnaround in performance comes.

kutumba.note: Dark-horse holding/implementation company; news rallies fizzle
kutumba.detail: Should be seen as a holding/implementation company. Anil Ambani group on-and-off news flow brings brief rallies that fizzle out immediately. Not a counter to pay much attention to; called it a dark-horse counter.

kutumba.note: Hold; book some profit if you availed the rights
kutumba.detail: Gave good returns post ex-rights, running to ~45-46 after the last quarter result and now stabilizing around 40. No need to panic-sell for the long term. But if you bought ahead of the rights and availed them, book some profits now.

kutumba.note: Partial exit; best of its time likely over
kutumba.detail: Results are good, but he thinks the best of its time is over and it has reached a peak. Advised a partial exit at the 115-117 levels. Business may improve after peace/stability, but outperforming returns are not expected.

kutumba.note: Buy only on declines; if held, trailing stop and hold
kutumba.detail: Shows continuous height and action and has gained some fancy, but he treats it as a highly speculative bet (flat December result yet jumped from 160-165 to 190). Buy only on declines; if already bought, put a trailing stop and hold.

kutumba.note: No fresh buying; book some profits
kutumba.detail: At these levels, no fresh buying. Advised booking some profits since lower levels would have been available.

kutumba.note: Accumulate in the 920-950 support zone
kutumba.detail: Repeatedly dips below 1,000 (965; 930-940 in early Feb). Best entry as of now is the last three-to-six-month support zone; advised accumulating around 920 to 950.

kutumba.note: Hold; flagship med-equipment company
kutumba.detail: A flagship medical-equipment company (not small) that performs well. Corrected to near its 52-week low on subdued recent results, but not a price to sell. Maybe exit on rallies once it reaches the 1,580-1,600 levels. (Caller was in ~50% loss.)

kutumba.note: Hold; don't sell, P/E ~5 and high dividend
kutumba.detail: May stay weak until the PFC merger ratio gets clarity and Government of India approval comes. Don't sell, though, because it trades at a P/E of about 5 (literally peanuts) and is a high-dividend payer. Take any decision only after total restructuring clarity. (Caller in ~50% loss.)

kutumba.note: No buy now; enter ~75-76 after a further ~15% fall
kutumba.detail: At current levels, don't buy. Already at its 52-week low with no great result, but maybe some value as a manufacturing company. Advised entering close to around the 75-76 levels, i.e. after roughly another 15% correction.

kutumba.note: Exit large-cap IT on a technical rebound (from ~10% loss)
kutumba.detail: On the Giri mail about TCS/Infosys/Wipro held at ~10% loss: a mild technical rebound is likely from the sharp two-day correction (huge cash-reserve companies, now in panic; TCS below 2,600). When it comes closer, exit, as sector fancy may fade with global developments.

kutumba.note: Exit large-cap IT on a technical rebound (from ~10% loss)
kutumba.detail: Part of the same TCS/Infosys/Wipro answer. Mild rebound expected (Infosys came below 1,300); the ~10% loss has a possibility of being covered on a technical rally, so exit when it comes closer.

kutumba.note: Exit large-cap IT on a technical rebound (from ~10% loss)
kutumba.detail: Grouped in the same large-cap IT answer. Advised exiting on the technical rebound when the position comes closer to break-even, as the fancy in the IT sector may reduce.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 12 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/Nn-cJ9YsQIE

## Global backdrop
- **Mixed US cues.** Overnight selling in US tech: **Nasdaq -0.16%**, **Russell 2000 -0.42%**.
- **Strong jobs data** — **1.3 lakh jobs** created (well above estimates), unemployment **~4.2%**. **US inflation data due Friday**. Strong jobs raise doubts about the rate path under the new Fed chair.
- **Software sell-off** in US ADRs/peers: **Infosys ADR -5%**, **Wipro ADR -4.4%**, **Globant -7.5%**, **EPAM ~-7%**, **Accenture -4.3%**, **Cognizant -4.9%**.
- Anchor's thesis: **AI tools (Anthropic / Claude) are disrupting the IT-services business model** — pricing power will erode; Indian IT (pure body-shopping/outsourcing, "innovation zero," buyback-driven) is most exposed.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty failed to cross 26,020** yesterday; uptrend intact but momentum easing. Today opened weak (**Nifty -75/-80**, **Sensex -265**, **bank Nifty flat/outperforming**).
- **IT in a bear market** (**IT index -1.76%** yesterday); at open **Infosys -4%, Tech Mahindra -3%, Wipro -3%, TCS -2%** — "stay away from IT." **Titan -8%**.
- **Breadth weak at open:** NSE 873 adv / 726 dec; BSE 908 adv / 1,175 dec. **Mid-cap -0.5%, small-cap -0.67%**.
- **Flows:** FII **net buy 944 cr** (4th day; >2bn USD bought over last 10 sessions); **DII sold 125 cr**. **Rupee weak — fell 14+ paise to ~90.70**, close to 91 (a concern despite FII buying).
- **RBI move:** looks to **ban third-party sales incentives to bank staff** / curb mis-selling (insurance, ULIPs tacked onto loans, lockers, FDs); banks may have to refund mis-sold amounts. Negative for **private-bank fee income** if strictly enforced; bank stocks barely fell at open.
- **Market-cap shuffle:** **SBI overtook TCS** to become **4th-largest** (TCS now 5th). Leaders: **Reliance 19.99 lakh cr**, **HDFC Bank 14.3**, **Bharti Airtel 3rd**; **SBI ~60,000 cr behind Airtel** (11.5 vs 10.99 lakh cr).
- **Stock-specific:** **Network Technologies** promoters selling **3% stake / 515 cr block** at ~5% discount (selling pressure expected).

## Results season
- **Good numbers:** Lenskart (**Q3 net profit +70%, top line +38%, international sales 936 cr, SSSG 28%** — target up to 575), Divi's Labs, Yatra Online, Kirloskar Oil, Ircon, LG Equipment, Shringar House of Mangalsutra, SJVN (+4.5%), GE Power (+17%), Borosil Scientific (+20%), Hikal (+5.5%).
- **Disappointing:** **LG Electronics (profit -62% YoY, stock -8%)**, Patanjali Foods, AstraZeneca Pharma, **Amara Raja (-53%)**, **Jupiter Wagon (-35%)**, Himatsingka.
- **Results to watch today:** Hindustan Unilever, Hindalco, ONGC, Lupin, Bharat Forge, Coal India, Muthoot Finance, Indian Hotels, IRCTC, HIL, Natco Pharma.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamentals + viewer Q&A): "**buy-on-dips market, not sell-on-rallies**"; consolidation at higher levels is positive. Heavy IT/software caution. Extensive stock calls (see Kutumba Rao file). Verdict on Quess Corp/"Caption Services" debt: it's working-capital (borrowings 175 cr vs receivables 185 cr) — no concern.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals): Nifty narrow-range, VIX **11.55**, PCR **1.06**, RSI **57.88**, MACD positive. **Resistance 26,000 / 26,640** (Bollinger upper); **support 25,800-25,700** (gap-fill). Long bias; suggested intraday short attempt near 25,900 on bounce. Quarterly-dividend angle on Central Bank; Apollo Micro triangle breakout buy 250-260; MCX entry ~2,355.
- **Vasanth** (host/ideas) and a derivatives/options presenter (Sensex weekly expiry today): buy-on-dips, expect mild expiry volatility.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (JSW Energy, Narayana Hrudayalaya, BSE, CCL Products, SMS Pharma), 4 sells (TCS, Syngene International, Siemens Energy, Transformers & Rectifiers)

kutumba.note: Add and hold; one of the better energy plays
kutumba.detail: For a caller sitting on a 15-16% loss, he said you can both add and hold; no need to be afraid. Called it one of the better stars in the energy space with a strong acquisition portfolio that will grow well.

kutumba.note: Buy after results (13th)
kutumba.detail: Recently attempted 52-week highs and is modestly consolidating. Not a cheap stock at about 80 PE, but he expects a good result (tomorrow, the 13th) and advised buying after the result.

kutumba.note: Operator-driven, rigging more than performance
kutumba.detail: Said there is an operation/rigging by an operator; above 300 it has distributed heavily. He wouldn't be surprised if it fell to 200. The sector and order book are good, but as a stock be wary; only sustains if it trades and holds above 280 on volume.

kutumba.note: Positional buy up to the NSE IPO
kutumba.detail: Result hit it out of the park and the stock is at an all-time high. As a positional trade up to the NSE IPO, he said BSE will sustain and to buy it. A dip toward 3,000 (gap-fill) is a good buy point.

kutumba.note: Hold; order booking extremely good
kutumba.detail: Momentum is reappearing lately and the stock's order booking is extremely good, so hold. Flagged that the group's RCB (IPL team) bid could have some impact on the group.

kutumba.note: Hold; recovered to 700, strong above it
kutumba.detail: After the promoter stake sale the stock fell to about 570-600 and recovered back to 700, which shows its strength. He said it will be very strong once it clears 700 again; hold.

kutumba.note: Hold; transforming into a defense company
kutumba.detail: Good results; post-results it is consolidating strongly above 70. It is slowly transforming from an optical-fiber company into a defense company, and he wouldn't be surprised if it attempts 100 again. Hold.

kutumba.note: Shift to Reliance; software disruption risk
kutumba.detail: Suggested shifting from TCS to Reliance, which he sees as safer. Software's Nifty weight is falling and AI disruption hits Indian IT too; TCS is near a 52-week low and would need to cross 3,200-3,300 to recover, which doesn't look immediate. Be cautious in service-oriented software stocks across the globe.

kutumba.note: Buy only with a long-term view
kutumba.detail: Near a 52-week low and a sin stock the government keeps eyeing. Even as a diversified FMCG company, he said buy only if you have a long-term view; in a short-term position don't expect big returns.

kutumba.note: Exit on rallies; very weak
kutumba.detail: Near a 52-week low with weak December-quarter results; he advised against averaging the caller's 50% loss. Financial performance is not promising at all, so exit on rallies.

kutumba.note: Wait for today's result before buying
kutumba.detail: Has a result today, so he said it is better to buy after seeing the result rather than now.

kutumba.note: Hold with extended long-term view
kutumba.detail: Described it as a market proxy that tracks broad swings (e.g. Adani stocks). Now a consistent high-dividend payer, so hold, but with an extended long-term view.

kutumba.note: High debt is working capital, no concern
kutumba.detail: On a viewer's debt worry, he explained the debt is mostly working capital, normal for services firms. Borrowings of 175 crores against trade receivables of 185 crores, with neutral net cash flows; income growing and margins improving. Nothing to worry about, just invest and hold.

kutumba.note: Accumulate on a modest dip
kutumba.detail: Strong result; the stock hit 1,070, a 52-week high. He expects a modest correction toward 930-940 and said to accumulate at that level. Existing holders can hold.

kutumba.note: Stay in Canfin, don't shift to RBL/AU
kutumba.detail: Canfin is still strong with good post-result performance, so he would not suggest switching out of it (to RBL Bank or AU Small Finance Bank). Noted that AU, converting from small-finance to a regular bank and consolidating near 1,000, is momentum-wise currently the stronger name.

kutumba.note: Book some short-term profit (at least 50%)
kutumba.detail: Caller in 20% profit. For short-term players he advised booking some short-term profit, at least 50%; long-term players should keep a trading stop. Definitely book some profit here.

kutumba.note: Book some short-term profit
kutumba.detail: Caller in 20% profit; he gave it the same treatment as Siemens Energy, advising short-term players to book part of the profit and long-term players to keep a trading stop.

kutumba.note: Speculative only; prefer frontline realty
kutumba.detail: Said there are much better companies; treat it as speculative only or for those tracking inside info. Name changed to Valor Estate; came below 100 and mildly recovered on December results. Prefer frontline realty names like Brigade or Prestige.

kutumba.note: Hold but trim above 25,900
kutumba.detail: Held at 180; he suggested it yesterday too. As of now hold, but reduce the position when the market comes back above 25,900, because indications of crossing 26,000 are not appearing this series, which feels tight.

kutumba.note: Accumulate; strong consolidation
kutumba.detail: Said there is no loss at all and to accumulate; the stock has strong consolidation between 300 and 335. Definitely accumulate.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 11 February 2026 (TV5 News)
# https://youtu.be/5QjCZQedSWs

## Global backdrop
- **US closed mixed.** Dow stable near lifetime high above **50,000** (up ~50 pts); Nasdaq fell ~**130 pts** on continuing **AI anxiety**, which has now spread to financials/wealth-management. **Charles Schwab fell 8%** on fears AI threatens broking/wealth-management/insurance business models.
- **Asia positive** this morning — Taiwan, **Kospi** (one of the biggest gainers), and Japan (near lifetime high after a two-thirds election majority for the ruling party); Japanese market not fully open.
- **Geopolitics:** Iran–US talks in Oman called "productive," but **Trump threatening a second carrier/warplane** against Iran. Russia complained the US is pressuring countries not to buy its oil. Watch **crude prices** — main domestic transmission channel.
- **Trade/tariff news:** US–Bangladesh treaty (zero tariff on Bangladesh textiles if it buys US cotton) could hit **Indian yarn exports**. US signaled the **$500bn India deal is "only an intent"** and pulses tariffs dropped a bit — read as slightly positive. Track such news flows until a final draft sheet.

## Indian markets
- Nifty closed above **25,850–900** for a second straight gains-day; higher-highs/higher-lows, a **buy-on-dips** market. Key hurdle: **26,000** (a close above today would be bullish).
- **Mid/small-caps outperforming** large-caps: this month **MidCap +4%**, **SmallCap ~+3.5%**. Driver is **earnings**: 23 mid-size firms (Rs 10k–20k cr sales) posted Q3 **net sales +15%, operating profit +9.4%, net profit +7.8%**; small-caps **sales +13.4%, op profit +14%, net profit +16%**. **77% of Nifty 500** names in profit this month; breadth ~**2:1** (1856/1133).
- **IT lagging; all other sectors participating** — broad-based rally.
- **FII positioning bullish:** shorts cut from **2 lakh contracts (Feb 1) to 1.34 lakh**; now ~**22% net long** in index futures (10% at series start); aggressive short-covering, ~Rs 70 cr equity buy. **DIIs +Rs ~1,200 cr.** PCR steady at high **1.17**; **VIX below 12 (~11.67)**. **Rupee at 90.56** (watch hold above 90).
- **Options map:** calls piled at **26,000**, building at **26,500** (~40 lakh contracts); puts at **25,500/25,900**. Cross-and-hold 26,000 → 26,500 attempt; failure → modest profit-booking. Bollinger upper band ~**26,600**; downside reversion risk to **~25,475**.

## Results & stock-specific
- **Good numbers:** Apollo Hospitals, Eicher Motors (+4%), Grasim, Jubilant Foodworks, Torrent Power, TVS Supply Chain, HEG (+5%), Titan, Britannia (in-line), Gandhi Steel Tubes, Afcom Holdings, Gulshan Polyols, Techno Electric, India Glycols, Manoj Vaibhav Gems (YoY), All Cargo Terminals (+9%).
- **Movers/momentum:** Amber Enterprises, BSE (record high), Eternal (Nifty top performer), Poonawalla Fincorp (+23% in 7 sessions), RailTel (~+45%), LMW. Speculative longs in Swiggy, Torrent Power, Lupin, Amber, BSE, CDSL. Shorts in Policybazaar, RVNL, BDL, PI Industries. Conviction delivery: Kotak Bank, Motilal Oswal, Sun Pharma, Power Grid, APL Apollo. **F&O ban list:** SAIL, Sammaan Capital.
- **Events:** **BHEL OFS** — govt selling 5% at Rs 250 floor (8% discount; closed Rs 276) to raise **Rs 4,400 cr**; plus a **Rs 2,800 cr Coal India order** (OFS may pressure stock, opened ~5% down). **Tata Motors CV** won a **~70,000-vehicle Indonesian order** (35,000 Ultra T7 trucks + 35,000 Yodha pickups). Results due: **Divi's Labs, Amara Raja, M&M, Ashok Leyland, Max Financial.**
- **Disinvestment restarting:** Budget FY target **Rs 85,000 cr**; **IDBI** flagged as key near-term trigger (next ~15 days).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — Bullish/protect-profits stance; a confirmed close above 26,000 implies a **1,000-pt Nifty rally**. Handled most viewer stock Q&A (see Kutumba Rao file): Buy Suzlon, Add Canara Bank, Hold ONGC, Accumulate Maruti Suzuki/CG Power on dips, Buy Healthcare Global (long-term), Avoid Electronics Mart, NFO caution on SBI Quality Fund.
- **Kranthi / Vasanth** — Positive but cautious; wait for decisive 26,000 hold. Good mid/small-cap earnings recovery; large-cap action missing. Suggests running profits / part-booking after 20–30% gains.
- **Ramakrishna (technicals)** — Buy-on-dips; Nifty near Bollinger upper band (~26,600) where rallies fizzle; **500-pt range 25,475–26,400** key. Bank Nifty weak/inside-bar (profit-booking), but momentum (RSI 59.74, MACD positive) improving; volumes thin so OI less reliable. Support 25,800/25,900; resistance shift on a 26,000 cross.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 5 buys (Suzlon Energy, Canara Bank, Maruti Suzuki, CG Power, Healthcare Global Enterprises)

kutumba.price: 48-50 range; decisive cross of 54-55 for big move
kutumba.note: Buy with patience; good valuation, renewables demand strong
kutumba.detail: Definitely buy at a decent valuation; results came with top line sustained and ~Rs 300 cr profit even after removing a one-off. Renewable space has continuous demand, so no fear. Stock will consolidate in the 48-50 range and rise slowly given high float (promoter only ~12%, public+institutions ~88%); a decisive cross of 54-55 can bring a big move.

kutumba.note: Hold and add on declines; better-performing PSU bank
kutumba.detail: Definitely hold and add on declines. Average whenever it comes to Rs 140 and below as it is one of the better-performing PSU banks. It was recommended strongly cum-dividend earlier around 90.

kutumba.note: Hold; dividend stock near 52-week high
kutumba.detail: Definitely hold; the holder (bought at 294, now ~270) is not in a big loss since the stock is near its 52-week high. Avail the second interim dividend (record date 12th). The stock will likely attempt 310-320 once, where the position can be trimmed. It is more of a dividend-paying stock with long-run capital appreciation too.

kutumba.note: Accumulate slowly on dips toward 15,000
kutumba.detail: Post-results it went to 17,000-plus, corrected, and is now around 15,200. Accumulate slowly from here; strong support has developed at 14,600-700, so buy whenever it comes closer to 15,000. As the country's largest carmaker it sustains profitability and margins and improved quarter-on-quarter.

kutumba.note: Buy on dips into the 600-640 support band
kutumba.detail: Buy on declines. It bottomed around 540-550 and is rising back in steps. Strong support now is in a wide band of about 600 to 640; when it comes into that range, buy then.

kutumba.note: Long-term buy only; better to enter after ex-rights
kutumba.detail: If you have a long-term view, definitely buy, but no spectacular short-term gains. The stock corrected from ~800 to ~600 and the result was disappointing (slipped into a loss). With a proposed rights issue it will trade cum-rights, so it is better to think and take it after ex-rights.

kutumba.note: Avoid; weak bottom line, only a retailer
kutumba.detail: No positivity here. It is more of a retailer (not a manufacturer), so margins won't improve much, especially as e-commerce is taking sales even in fast-moving electrical goods. Profit came modestly (~Rs 29 cr) despite some top-line growth. If anything, exit even on a rally toward ~189.

kutumba.note: Avoid for now; lower-top lower-bottom pattern
kutumba.detail: For now avoid; it should be bought only on declines. The stock has formed lower-top, lower-bottom continuously for the last two quarters, so it won't jump easily. Maybe it reclaims 1,000 again; buy only if it crosses 1,000.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 10 February 2026 (TV5 News)
# https://youtu.be/_61BbiIeGkc

## Global backdrop
- **Positive global cues**: gains across Asia (Taiwan, Korea, Nikkei, Hang Seng). US closed strong — **Dow** near another record high, **S&P 500 +0.64%**, **Nasdaq +0.9%**, Russell 2000 also up.
- **Dollar Index weakened** as the Japanese PM's bumper-majority win drove the dollar–yen trade; gold and silver both traded positive. **Brent crude near $69** on renewed Iran tensions.
- Mega-cap tech led the US: **Oracle +9%**, **Microsoft +3%** — an AI-trade bounce-back after last week's losses ("on-and-off").

## Indian markets
- Nifty closed **+173 points** yesterday, now just **2% below its all-time high of 26,373** (set 5 January); has rallied **>1,000 points** off the lows.
- Driver: **US–India trade-deal joint statement**, plus a shift in **FII behaviour** — they bought **Rs 2,255 cr** (DIIs bought just Rs 4.5 cr). FIIs bought **$2 bn in nine sessions** — unusual after a year of selling.
- **Mid/small-cap revival**: the worst looks over. Small Cap index **+2.5%** yesterday vs Nifty <1%; **+8% over five sessions**. Retail portfolios recovering after weeks of weakness; **advance-decline 2,372:627 (~4:1)** breadth strongly positive.
- **Sector rotation**: EMS (Dixon, Amber, Kaynes) looking brighter again; Real Estate recovering; chemicals turning around (**Navin Fluorine** "extraordinary", CDMO revenue **+61% YoY** in Q3, EBITDA-margin guidance raised to 25–30%); shipping strong (**SCI +~20%** on results, govt's 40,000–50,000 cr package tailwind).
- **Negative**: **textile sector weak** — US struck a **0% tariff trade deal with Bangladesh** (reciprocal tariff down 19% to ~19% vs India's 18%), eroding India's competitiveness; Gokuldas, Indo Count, KPR, Vardhman, Welspun fell at open.
- **Jewellery rallied** on the trade deal — **Kalyan Jewellers +15%**, peers +7–15% — as **50% duty set to drop to 18%**.
- **Gravita India** announced a **Rs 565 cr acquisition** of Rashtriya Metal Industries (copper-recycling entry).
- Results: good — Genus Power, Aurobindo, Pfizer, BSE, Amber, Sansera, Linde, ISGEC, NRB Bearings, Nelcast, JNK India, Gulf Oil. Weak — Neuland Labs, PNC Infratech, Texmaco Rail, All Time Plastics, Happiest Minds, Ramco Cements. Bata +6%, Pfizer +5%, Navin Fluorine +5%, BSE +5% at open.

## Index levels (Kutumba Rao + Ramakrishna)
- **Nifty**: strong resistance **26,000–26,300** (call OI piled at 26,000 — now highest call rating); a stand above 26,000 allows fresh positions. Support / put OI at **25,500–25,800** (25,800 key); breach there = short-term trouble. Heading towards **27,000** if it clears resistance, but only stock-specific/selective. RSI 56.2, MACD uptrend.
- **Bank Nifty**: closed at **all-time high** (SBI-led, possibly its biggest rally in ~2 years); call interest at 60,000, support **59,800**; RSI ~60, MACD histogram positive.
- PCR jumped above 1.11 (profit booking likely above 1.2–1.3); **VIX** rose modestly above 12 but stable. Rupee stable near **90.66**.
- Strategy: **buy-on-dips**; book ~25% profit near 26,000 if positional-from-budget; long-term investors need not book. Fresh buying near **25,867/850** on a dip.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamentals + viewer stock Q&A (see companion file). Bullish: equities to beat gold/silver this year; restructure portfolios into companies with good results, exit poor-quality stocks ruthlessly.
- **Kranthi/Vasanth** — back from a pharma factory visit (mentioned unlisted **Foptech Ltd**, African pharma-manufacturing theme); fielded Visakha Industries (SIP-mode buy, turned around) and Cochin Shipyard (staggered accumulate ~1,500, 3-yr horizon). Framed the Trump-India trade saga as a "Vikram-Betal" story needing continual earnings recovery.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals; buy-on-dips, all indicators favourable, Bank Nifty at ATH can again pull Nifty up.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 7 buys (Nifty ETF, Laurus Labs, Nippon India Small Cap Fund, Kotak Flexi Cap Fund, HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund, Mirae Asset Large and Mid Cap Fund, IRB InvIT), 1 sell (PPL Pharma)

kutumba.note: Hold; near 1000 now, registrar business growing with new IPOs; can reach 1175-1200 by Mar-Apr.
kutumba.detail: For a viewer holding at 1089 (at a loss), he says hold and don't panic. KFin is near 1000 now (986) and will easily cross back up. As brokerage/registrar businesses grow and new IPOs keep coming, KFin will definitely improve. It can go up to 1175-1200 by next March-April.

kutumba.note: Hold but exit on bounce; narrow band 300-340, no immediate move to old 400+ highs.
kutumba.detail: The worst is likely over but the stock moves in a narrow band between 300 and 340. The viewer bought at 340; that price will come easily and when it does, he says exit. He does not expect the old highs of 400-plus to come immediately.

kutumba.note: Hold; stable depository business, no competition (only NSDL and CDSL).
kutumba.detail: For a viewer holding at 1493 (at a loss), he says hold. Whatever IPO rally happens, depository companies have a stable business with no competition - only NSDL and CDSL exist. Even without steady growth it is a steady business model; once costs are cut and profits shown better, there is scope for the stock to rise.

kutumba.note: Safest route if you can't bear individual-stock volatility (vs large-caps).
kutumba.detail: Asked about replacing individual large-cap stocks with a Nifty ETF, he says ETF is a safe play. If you are in individual stocks and cannot bear their ups, downs and volatility, a Nifty ETF is the safest route - no doubt about it.

kutumba.note: Hold, don't shift to Avantel yet; check result, may go 200+ after March then shift.
kutumba.detail: Holder of 200 free shares asked about switching to Avantel. He says check the result first - Lokesh was hit indirectly by American tariffs and some sanctions, seen in the stock. The best of times are over and it corrected to 200, but after March there are signs of it going to 200-plus; shift at that time, not now.

kutumba.note: Not cheap; high-PE stock already near 154-155; don't shift into it now.
kutumba.detail: On switching from Lokesh Machines into Avantel, he says Avantel is not a cheap stock even now. By PE-wise it is a high-PE stock and is already trading near 154-155. So don't shift right now from Lokesh into Avantel.

kutumba.note: Switch from PPL Pharma to Laurus Labs - no doubt.
kutumba.detail: The viewer is at about a 15% loss in PPL Pharma and asked about switching to Laurus. He says from PPL to Laurus you can shift, no doubt about it.

kutumba.note: Switch target from PPL Pharma - no doubt.
kutumba.detail: He endorses shifting from PPL Pharma into Laurus, saying there is no doubt about that switch.

kutumba.note: Hold; first post-name-change quarterly given optimistically; good long-term returns.
kutumba.detail: Hold Sequent Scientific. After the name change, the first conference was just the other day - this is the first-quarter result, given optimistically. The earnings-call transcript has to be seen, but in the long term it can give good returns.

kutumba.note: Continue SIP; performing nicely.
kutumba.detail: On Rekha's mutual-fund SIPs (Nippon India Small Cap, Kotak Flexi Cap, HDFC Large & Mid, Mirae Asset Large & Mid), he says they are fine - all performing nicely. Continue the SIPs as they give good returns in the long run.

kutumba.note: Continue SIP; performing nicely.
kutumba.detail: Part of Rekha's SIP set that he endorses continuing - all are performing nicely and give good long-run returns.

kutumba.stock: HDFC Large and Mid Cap Fund
kutumba.note: Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms ICICI Pru/Motilal Oswal Large&Mid but good long-run.
kutumba.detail: He says HDFC Large & Mid (and Mirae Asset) sometimes underperform compared to ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise in the long run they give good returns; continue the SIP.

kutumba.stock: Mirae Asset Large and Mid Cap Fund
kutumba.note: Continue SIP; sometimes underperforms peers but good long-run returns.
kutumba.detail: Part of Rekha's SIP set; sometimes underperforms ICICI Prudential or Motilal Oswal Large & Mid, but otherwise gives good long-run returns - continue.

kutumba.note: Buy; ~13% dividend, parent toll revenue up 6500->7500 cr YoY; five toll assets + a HAM asset.
kutumba.detail: Definitely buy IRB InvIT - no need to fear. The parent IRB Infra's toll revenues are strong and factorisation benefit accrues to this trust; toll revenue jumped year-on-year from 6,500 crore to 7,500 crore. It shows ~13% dividend, is near its 52-week high, and has five toll assets in Western and Southern India plus a HAM asset - looks stronger.

kutumba.note: Hold if you have the time horizon; undervalued, deploys cash into equities; else switch to MF.
kutumba.detail: Maithan Alloys is an undervalued company that deploys its cash into listed/unlisted equities rather than core business (it bought ~40,000-50,000 NSE shares at ~1,000, now doubled). If you have the time horizon, invest/hold; otherwise there are much better companies and a mutual fund is the better route.

kutumba.note: Hold and wait for the 12th board-meeting (Dec) result; turnaround would help, else may retest 52-wk low.
kutumba.detail: For a viewer at 352 now at a 50% loss, he says it disappointed since its hyped listing - even the September result was disappointing. There is a board meeting on the 12th for the December-quarter result; if a turnaround shows, the stock improves, otherwise it may retest the 52-week low. Having waited this long, it is better to hold and see the result.

kutumba.note: Hold, don't book profit; group flagship in capital markets, near 52-week high - keep a trailing stop and let it run.
kutumba.detail: For a viewer at average 120, he says hold and don't book profit. It is the group's flagship company in the capital markets and the stock is near its 52-week high. In stocks touching 52-week highs you should keep a trading stop, protect profits and let it run rather than selling just because it has risen.

kutumba.note: Hold long-term; good margins, US-India trade deal a positive; range-bound with short/medium-term global impacts.
kutumba.detail: For a viewer holding from IPO time, he says hold. The US-India trade deal is a positive and the stock shows traction, but it is range-bound. A company with good margins; global impacts weigh in the medium-to-short term, but it is a stock to hold on for the long term.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 9 February 2026 (TV5 News)
# https://youtu.be/jez7ljLi2Yk

## Global backdrop
- **Asian markets strong** this Monday: Japan rallied on PM **Takaichi's landmark two-thirds-majority win**; gains across Korea, Shanghai, Hang Seng. Japanese indices jumped **4–4.5%**.
- US Friday close: **Dow above 50,000** (new record high); **S&P 500** near the **7,000** mark (~70 points away).
- US IT-stock selling stopped — **EPAM, Nvidia, Accenture, Globant up 3–5%** Friday.
- Precious metals: **gold back to $5,000**, **silver near $80**. **Bitcoin rebounded 6.5% over the weekend to above $70,000** (after halving to ~$60,000 earlier).
- **Gift Nifty indicated +180–190 points**, pointing to a gap-up open.

## India–US trade deal (key event)
- Weekend **joint statement** received; net positive across sectors.
- **25% Russian-oil-linked tariffs removed**; **reciprocal tariffs cut from 25% to 18%** (some goods at **0%**).
- **Weighted-average US tariff on India to fall from 37.2% to 15.7%.** 18% reciprocal tariffs effective over the coming week.
- **0% on generic pharma / generic APIs and on electronics exports**; **agricultural products excluded** from the deal.
- Watch-points: US to closely monitor **India–Russia oil trade** (e.g. Reliance restarting imports after a 5-week pause); India's **$500bn purchase "intent"** (energy, aircraft) over 5 years is an intent, not a commitment.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty opened ~+120 pts; Sensex +450; Bank Nifty +400.** Breadth positive: **1,551 advances vs 297 declines**. Mid/small-caps higher; **IT index +1.2%**.
- Friday flows: **FIIs bought Rs 1,950 cr** (cash), **DIIs sold Rs 1,265 cr**.
- Structural shift: **DIIs overtook FIIs in Nifty 50 ownership** for the first time in years — **DII 24.8% vs FII 24.3%** (~0.5% gap).
- Stock of the morning: **SBI** (~1,124), driving Bank Nifty.

## Results highlights
- **SBI** — best-in-class: consolidated net profit **Rs 22,176 cr** vs ~**Rs 17,540 cr** est.; standalone **~Rs 21,128 cr** (20–25% beat). NIMs near **3%**, strong credit growth/asset quality. Market cap to cross **Rs 10 lakh cr** (2nd-largest bank after HDFC Bank). Trades at **1.75x**. **Jefferies target Rs 1,300.**
- **HBL Engineering** — **~90% YoY growth** in both top and bottom line (Q2 was an exceptional base; don't compare).
- **Caplin (Capstan)** — Q3 income **Rs 213 cr (+16.3%)**; 9M income **Rs 615 cr (+22%)**, net profit **+75%**. New "Home Services" subsidiary (vs Urban Company).
- **SMS Pharma** — slightly disappointing QoQ; YoY good, ~1% margin improvement; opened +.
- **Shipping Corp of India** — blockbuster: net profit **Rs 76 cr → Rs 405 cr YoY**; opened **+10.3%**.
- Other good numbers: Jeena Sikho, Atul Auto, Shilpa Medicare (**+8.3%**), Sutlej Energies, JK Tyres, Kalyan Jewellers.
- **Two IPOs open today**: **Fractal** (pure-play AI, no listed peers — check valuations) and **iFinance** (NBFC).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao (fundamentals)** — "Worst is over"; **buy on dips, not sell on rallies.** New highs certain; **Nifty could attempt 27,000 even in the Feb series** (resistance 26,000–26,300). Export visibility from the deal → FII revival. Advice: **exit weak "cats and dogs," stay in fundamentally sound stocks**; avoid software; hold consumption; banking can outperform; **stay invested.** (Stock calls in dedicated file.)
- **Ramakrishna (technicals)** — Nifty closed **+869 pts** last week. **Bullish view for positional traders; buy on dips**; intraday volatility/short opportunities persist. Bank Nifty set for **all-time-high closings** post gap-up. Levels: Nifty support **24,600**, resistance **25,750 / 26,000** then **26,300–26,400** zone; intraday long near **25,750–760**. VIX fell **1.87 to 11.94**; **RSI >52**; MACD/histogram improving; **PCR 0.96**. OI: highest call writing **25,600**, put writing **25,500**.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 7 buys (Groww, Rolex Rings, HBL Engineering, State Bank of India, SBFC Finance, KPI Green Energy, Tata Capital)

kutumba.note: Hold; one of top-3 power cos, scope to 250 once 182 crosses
kutumba.detail: Hold Adani Power, no need to hurry as it is one of the top-three power companies. The recent correction eliminated weaker players in the stock. Once it crosses the recent high of 182, there is scope to go up to 250 on the way. Definitely hold for the next one year.

kutumba.note: Buy on dip near 110; good results, broking recovery play
kutumba.detail: Groww announced good December-quarter numbers. As markets recover, brokerage companies' incomes rise and a gung-ho atmosphere returns. Buy it when it comes closer to 110; looks good for at least a positional trade if not a long-term hold.

kutumba.note: Buy near 110; auto-ancillary, stock split done, liquidity up
kutumba.detail: Rolex Rings is fine; it did a stock split and liquidity increased. Buy it only when it comes closer to 110. It is an auto-ancillary company, not comparable to broking names like Groww.

kutumba.note: Hold with strict stop-loss; exit on rally to 150-156
kutumba.detail: December bottom line stayed flat despite top-line growth because costs rose on management-contract hotels with pricing revision; should absorb it and do better in the coming holiday season. Already corrected ~30% from 170-180 highs. Hold at current levels with a strict stop-loss between 110 and 150, but in the short term exit on a rally to 150-156.

kutumba.note: Buy; good Q3 numbers, must re-win KAVACH orders
kutumba.detail: Numbers are good though not comparable to the exceptional Q2 base. Q3 numbers are good and year-on-year performance was good. Going forward it must win KAVACH orders again in competition with Kernex and Concord Controls to strengthen its order book. After listening to the con-call it feels buyable.

kutumba.note: Buy/accumulate, no need to wait for a fall; add on dips
kutumba.detail: Buy SBI 100%, no need to look back and forth. Recommended repeatedly for almost three years, including shifting out of Yes Bank. If bought at the high and it dips, average it. In the long term SBI will cross the 1,500 level and stabilize; it is a bank, no doubt. Accumulate and keep adding on dips.

kutumba.note: Avoid at current levels after 200% 6-month run
kutumba.detail: The stock rose 200% in just six months (about 80 to 280) on speculation and is at 52-week highs. There are doubts it can rise further from these levels unless the company delivers another successful blockbuster VFX project. As of now do not buy; consider only on a strong decline.

kutumba.note: Wait another quarter; weak Dec result, micro-cap
kutumba.detail: Recently listed with only one quarter of performance, and the December result was not good at all, leaving the stock near its 52-week low around 130. Fundamentals do not seem to justify the valuation. It is a micro-cap (about Rs 850 crore market cap). Wait for another quarter.

kutumba.note: Buy for short/short-medium term only, not long term
kutumba.detail: The result has come and is good, but the stock is not a fancy one. Keeping that in view, it can be bought for the short or short-medium term. Long-term bets in such companies do not work well.

kutumba.note: Hold; result slightly below est but strong execution pipeline
kutumba.detail: The result came slightly below expectations but there are many projects in execution, so better results will come in coming quarters. Definitely hold; a little (heavy) patience is needed.

kutumba.note: Hold/stay invested; worst over, govt infra spend tailwind
kutumba.detail: The worst is over and the group's debt situation is stabilizing. As infra companies, do not sell as of now even if weak, because Government of India infra/GSDP-growth spending will certainly be high in the coming year. It is near its 52-week low and on a weak wicket, but should do much better from current levels.

kutumba.note: Wait; buy near major support 380-390 on weekly weakness
kutumba.detail: At current levels (around 420-430) do not buy; wait. Recommended at 60-70 rupees three years back, it touched 500. Major support is around 380-390. Buy when that level comes during weekly weakness.

kutumba.note: Hold; weak-looking but high potential, not cheap
kutumba.detail: Hold; no need to fear. Performance may feel weak but potential is high, and such future-discounting stocks tend to move. It is not a cheap stock, around Rs 12,000 crore market cap. The December profit dip was a one-off, so no need to exit even if the stock is weak now.

kutumba.note: Average; earnings-call developments could take it past 500
kutumba.detail: Average here. There are a couple of developments in the company that were indicated on the earnings/conference call. If those play out, there are signs the stock will cross 500 again in the next two-to-three quarters.

kutumba.note: Buy/accumulate; medium-term target 425-430
kutumba.detail: Hold and buy. Earlier advised to accumulate even at 320-330, with a medium-term target of 425-430. Definitely a buy.

kutumba.note: Hold; metal cycle stabilizing, good recent result
kutumba.detail: The metal cycle is stabilizing again and the recent result was good. Hold.

kutumba.note: Hold; buy near 355, stabilizing 360-370
kutumba.detail: After the recent result it corrected and is stabilizing between 360-370. Definitely hold; buy too when it comes closer to 355.

kutumba.note: Avoid as investment; now a trading/F&O bet
kutumba.detail: It has become an institutional stock since going into F&O, with swift 5-10% weekly moves on and off. It has become more of a trading bet than an investment bet as of now.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 6 February 2026 (TV5 News)
# https://youtu.be/kqC5Bued2xw

## Global backdrop
- **Risk-off across the globe.** Korea's **Kospi fell more than 2%**; broad selling in equities, crypto and precious metals.
- **US tech sell-off, third successive day.** Selling concentrated in software/IT after **Anthropic's "Claude Cowork"** enterprise-automation toolkit — **EPAM down ~4%**, Globant, Gartner, Accenture hit. **OpenAI** also launching an "AI co-workers" agents platform. Nasdaq slipped **below its 200-MA**.
- **Amazon down ~10% after-hours** — earnings were okay but the **$200bn capex forecast** spooked investors questioning AI/infra return-on-investment at Meta, Alphabet, Amazon.
- **Crypto crushed: Bitcoin halved from its all-time high**, hitting ~**$60,000** intraday (now ~$64,000); post-Trump crypto gains wiped out.
- **Precious metals off highs: silver down ~21%** (near $70), gold near **$4,700**. **Dollar index back up to ~97.99** from 96 — de-dollarization theme appears to be stalling.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty set for a gap-down open.** Key support **25,450** (held pre-Budget); range pivot around **25,850**; resistance at last Tuesday's high and **26,000**. Break of **25,500** opens **25,100** for shorts; longs only above **25,740**.
- **RBI MPC outcome (~10am) seen as a non-event** — no rate change expected. Over the past year rates were cut **1.25%**; RBI infused **Rs 50,000 cr** via OMO yesterday; rate-cut cycle may be temporarily paused (no inflation concerns, strong growth). Watch commentary on the rupee, liquidity, possible OMO/CRR moves. **GSDP/CPI base-year change** (to 22-23 / 24) flagged as a coming distortion.
- **Breadth negative at open.** NSE mid/small: **678 advances vs 1023 declines**; BSE **920 vs 1219**. Nifty -35, Sensex -90, Bank Nifty -23 at open.
- **Sector action:** Metals selling (Hindustan Copper -3%, Nalco -2.3-3%), Auto/IT/Pharma indices down; only **Realty** positive.
- **Derivatives (per Kranthi):** Aggressive **26,000 call writing**; heavy **25,000 put build-up**; PCR dropped from 1.1 to **0.81**; **VIX ~12** (fear gauge cooling); the **25,500 monthly put** flagged as a hedge appreciated on the gap-down. FIIs sold **>Rs 2,000 cr**, DIIs bought only **~Rs 1,100 cr**; rupee at **90.34**.

## Results scorecard (this season)
- **Strong:** Hitachi Energy (net profit **+90% YoY**, EBITDA Rs 180cr→**370cr**, in **upper circuit**), Nykaa (surprise beat, **+4%**), Sai Life Sciences (Hyderabad, excellent), Bharti Airtel (good numbers, muted reaction), ABB Power (**+10%**), Standard Glass (~23% glass growth, ~37% consolidated YoY).
- **Weak/disappointing:** Kaynes Technologies (cut guidance, **-4.5%**, near 52-wk low), **Hexaware (-10%)**, **NCC** ("disaster," guidance scrapped), Sequent Scientific (Rs ~27cr other-income loss QoQ).
- **Momentum pockets:** Textiles (Loyal Textile, Indian Terrain — 20% circuits), Force Motors (+5%), fast-food (Devyani International, Sapphire Foods +8%), GNG Electronics (+8.5%).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — macro/disruption view + viewer stock Q&A. Headline call: **IT/software faces structural disruption** from agentic AI; growth rates "very questionable"; only firms that reinvent survive. Market will be **stock-/sector-specific** for the next six months, no runaway rally. Stock calls below (see Kutumba Rao file).
- **Kranthi** — derivatives, speculation flows, asset allocation. Avoid speculating in Bitcoin now; accumulate silver/gold only **slowly with a 5-10 yr view**; **equity is the long-term outperformer** — stress on asset allocation. Co-handled Kaynes and Natco.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals. Markets consolidating after a large up-candle; **RBI a non-event**, direction driven by fund flows. Nifty support **25,560/25,550**, short if broken, long only above **25,740**; Tuesday gap may fill toward ~25,2100/25,100 zone. Bank Nifty slightly better placed (falling-trendline support; 61,700 call / 60,900 put highest writing). **Stay cautious.**
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Hitachi Energy)

kutumba.note: Hold despite disappointing result; GoI EV-bus orders supportive
kutumba.detail: The result disappointed and the company keeps failing to complete its expansion, which is a big question. He hasn't been in touch recently and will update next week, but says EV-bus orders from the Government of India are being released proactively. So hold for now.

kutumba.note: Even good Dec results couldn't lift the stock; exit on rallies
kutumba.detail: The stock was at 520 in Sep-Oct and has halved; even after good December top-line and bottom-line results it couldn't rise, so 'we are missing out on something.' Avoid; if already held, exit on rallies around 310-320 and shift to a better-managed energy/transformer company like Hitachi Energy.

kutumba.note: Result okay-not-great; back in buying range
kutumba.detail: Yesterday's result wasn't great but is okay. The renewable space gets on-and-off interest and the stock has come below 50, into the buying range. Hold.

kutumba.note: Record results (+90% net profit); buy on every dip
kutumba.detail: Net profit rose 90% YoY with highest-ever revenue, EBITDA (180cr to 370cr) and PAT/PBT. He had recommended buying it on every dip; the stock opened strongly and is in upper circuit. He also flags it as the preferred switch destination from Transformers & Rectifiers and Kaynes.

kutumba.note: Range-bound 120-140 absent game-changing news
kutumba.detail: Range-bound 120-140 unless game-changing news comes. Glass showed ~23% growth and consolidated Q3 ~37% YoY, but PAT only rose ~26-27%. The bio-process-systems entry will take more time to outperform. 140 was the issue price; no need to sell or add - hold.

kutumba.note: Run the 20% profit; good growth-path company
kutumba.detail: GMR Airports is a company on a good growth path and the holder is at 20% profit, so run the profit. He recommends holding it rather than switching.

kutumba.note: Strongly opposes; BSNL-dependent, obsolete inventory written off
kutumba.detail: He completely opposes Tejas Networks, even with fresh money. About 80% of revenue depends on BSNL where the outlook is uncertain, and last quarter they wrote off a huge amount of obsolete inventory as telecom technology keeps changing. Buy a quality telecom name like Bharti Airtel instead.

kutumba.note: One of the 3-yr outperformers; exit only on rallies if needed
kutumba.detail: Over the last three years it has surprisingly been one of the outperforming stocks and only came down a bit in mild corrections. A decent stock to hold; if one wants to exit, do so on rallies, but at this time hold. A good Bharti Group company.

kutumba.note: Smaller banks to merge; value plus good dividend
kutumba.detail: Hold at current levels. All smaller banks will eventually get merged, an indication the Finance Minister has given, so there is value. A good dividend will also come, so keep it.

kutumba.note: Strange laggard; Africa-focus payoff and acquisitions yet to show
kutumba.detail: A strange situation - a good company with no visible fault, yet the stock won't rise. He sees it as an early mover on the Africa pharma focus, but the results of recent acquisitions still have to show in the balance sheet and P&L cash flows. Hold at the current price range for now.

episodes.title: Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
episodes.summary_md:
# Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 February 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/v2OVhLrFPUs

## Headline
- **Budget Day (1 Feb)** disappointed markets. The single sore point: a hike in **STT** for an estimated **Rs 15,000 crore** of extra revenue, which the panel says "destroyed" market sentiment.
- **Nifty** fell ~700 points intraday, touched **24,570**, recovered to close ~**24,825** (about a 500-point loss).
- Otherwise the budget was rated "decent" / growth-oriented; the complaint is purely about it ignoring capital markets.

## The budget — what was good
- **Capex raised ~10% to Rs 12.1 lakh crore**; fiscal deficit kept under control.
- **Defence capex +18%** to **Rs 7.87 lakh crore**; **railway capex +10%**; **power capex +18%**.
- **Data centres: 21-year tax holiday** — seen as a magnet for large FDI (few players: Amazon, OpenAI, Meta-type firms); benefits equipment makers (**ABB, Hitachi**).
- **Semiconductor mission**, basic customs duty cuts on many goods, **Rs 10,000 crore for bio-pharma / research**, employment-creation push for youth.
- Economic Times' **Swaminathan Aiyar**: a "critics' choice rather than crowd-pleaser" budget. FM framed it around **Mission 2047**.

## The budget — what was bad (STT)
- STT hike called counter-productive — invokes the **Laffer Curve** ("higher taxes, lower revenue").
- F&O active traders already fell from **~45 lakh to ~20–22 lakh** after SEBI's curbs; **95%** of speculators lose anyway.
- STT collections: FY25 rose ~63%; FY26 target **Rs 78,000 crore** (Kutumba Rao expects only **~Rs 62,000–63,000 crore**); FY27 target cut to **Rs 73,700 crore** — first-ever STT target cut, yet he doubts even 75–80% will be met.
- "Killing the machine that funds the economy" — IPO market already weak (60–70% of recent issues underwater); hurts the govt's own **Rs 80,000 crore disinvestment** target.

## Indian markets — internals
- **VIX spiked from ~10–10.5 to 15** (highest since June 2025) and stayed elevated (~15.09) even post-budget.
- **PCR (Nifty put-call)** collapsed to **0.59** — lowest since December 2024.
- **Bank Nifty:** every single constituent closed negative (rare) — Bank of Baroda worst, Kotak least bad. No commentary on banks in the budget.
- **IT outperformed**: Wipro, TCS, Infosys closed marginally positive.
- **PSU stocks hit 5–6%**: BEL, ONGC, SBI, Coal India, NTPC; metals (Hindalco) down.
- **Trading range now ~24,300–25,300**; needs to hold above 25,200–300 to re-attempt 26,000. Risk of 24,000 if 24,400 support breaks.
- **FII/DII selling was light (~Rs 588 crore net)**; today (2 Feb) seen as the real tell on how big players read the budget.

## Today's session (2 Feb open)
- Negative open: **Nifty ~-50, Sensex ~-50, Bank Nifty -200+**; later all three turned mildly positive (small recovery).
- Breadth weak. **Defence and data-centre stocks** led: Anant Raj +5%, Data Patterns +4%, Hindustan Copper +4%; BEL, Cochin Shipyard, GRSE, Mazagon Dock, Netweb, IIFL Finance, Chennai Petro buying.
- Losers: Reliance Infra, Aster DM, Intellect Design, Craftsman Automation, Sun TV, Dr Lal PathLabs, Neuland Labs.

## Earnings (Q3) flagged as good
- **RR Kabel** (excellent), **Delhivery** (EBITDA Rs 209 crore), **Studds Accessories** (YoY better, QoQ flat), **TD Power Systems** (one of the best), energy companies broadly strong.
- Results to watch today: Hyundai, Bajaj Housing Finance, Ather Energy, Tata Chemicals, Thermax, Sundaram Finance, Indus Towers, PG Electroplast.

## Sector ideas from the panel
- **Infra/railway (accumulate on dips):** RVNL, IRCON, Ashoka Buildcon, IRFC.
- **Data-centre ecosystem:** Orient Technology, Anant Raj, Cummins, Techno Electric.
- **Defence (40–50% off peaks):** Cochin Shipyard, Garden Reach, HAL.
- **Semiconductor:** CG Power.
- **Medical tourism (large hospitals only):** Apollo, Max Healthcare, Narayana, KIMS, Yatharth.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamentals lead; budget critique, STT analysis, viewer stock Q&A, an intraday options call.
- **Kranthi/Vasanth** — budget read (uptrend reversed at page 16 on borrowing + STT); defence/semiconductor/data-centre stock ideas; gold-silver view.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals (Bollinger/RSI/MACD oversold, RSI ~30); resistance 25,000 / 25,200 (200-DMA), support 24,400 then 24,000; advised avoiding trading today; an options call.

## Trading stance
- **Cautious** for the next 2–3 sessions; clarity by Friday. Avoid aggressive trading until global cues (US trade deal) stabilise; use stop-losses. Market will move "extremely stock-specific / sector-specific."
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 3 buys (Vedanta, Reliance Industries, Windlas Biotech)

kutumba.note: Accumulate near 640-650; demerger likely within 6 months
kutumba.detail: He said the Vedanta demerger issue looks set to be completed within the next six months, so one can buy. The correction in non-ferrous metals will not be prolonged because renewable-driven demand will revive non-ferrous metals; Vedanta is one of the larger players. Whenever it closes around Rs 640-650 the stock should be accumulated.

kutumba.note: Hold long term; strong research pipeline, no need to panic
kutumba.detail: A viewer holds Natco Pharma at Rs 950 and asked whether to hold or book profit. Kutumba Rao said hold for the long term with no need to panic, because Natco also has a strong research pipeline.

kutumba.note: Add slowly on every dip in this range; a no-brainer stock
kutumba.detail: Asked about buying for a 1-5+ year horizon given value unlocking, he said one can add slowly here with no trouble. In this price range, keep adding slowly whenever a dip comes. He called it a no-brainer stock.

kutumba.note: Buy at attractive valuation; stock-picker's paradise now
kutumba.detail: Asked by a viewer (Saira Sriram), he said to definitely lock it down and buy, as the stock now has an attractive valuation. He added that with the budget over and only the Indo-US deal ahead, it is now a stock-picker's paradise - favour companies that benefit indirectly from the budget or have completed expansion capex.

kutumba.stock: ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 Fund / Bandhan Infrastructure Fund
kutumba.note: Switch out of broader-market and infra themes into diversified funds
kutumba.detail: A viewer (Nagarajan) was at a loss in ICICI Prudential Nifty 200 (broader-market) and Bandhan Infrastructure (sectoral). Kutumba Rao said negative sentiment is heavier in the broader market versus Nifty 50, and infra is unlikely to see any big move even post-budget. He advised exiting these as far as possible and moving into diversified flexi-cap or multi-cap funds, and warned against chasing gold/silver - asset allocation must be followed strictly.
