# Summary — Business Breakfast, 30 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/QMf3eBG7zFU

## Headline
Friday before a **big-day Budget** (Budget session this week; markets work this Sunday and Monday, expiry Tuesday). Weak open expected — **GIFT Nifty ~150 points down** — driven by pre-Budget nervousness and a wild overnight commodities blow-up.

## Commodities — overnight "dhamaal"
- **Silver** spiked to **$121/oz** on COMEX, crashed to **$106**, now ~**$109-110**. In rupee terms it crossed **Rs 4,20,000/kg**, fell to **Rs 3,55,000** (down ~Rs 65,000 in hours), closed ~Rs 3,98,000-3,99,000.
- **Gold** crossed **$5,602/oz**, fell to $5,440, now ~$5,170-5,180 (**-4%**). MCX close **Rs 1,83,962**; likely to open ~Rs 1,77,000 (was near Rs 1,93,000 prior day).
- **Copper** +10% overnight to **$14,500**; a **commodity super-cycle** (copper, aluminium, zinc) is in play. FOMO chasing flagged as a risk.
- Note: **Tether (stablecoin)** reportedly bought **~140 tonnes of gold** over 14-15 months — a factor behind the gold rally.

## Global backdrop
- Indications **US may attack Iran** adding to nervousness.
- **Bank of America CEO**: "India is a land of opportunity."
- **Rupee** is the **weakest world currency** — near all-time low while the dollar index is near a 4-year low. USD/INR touched **92**, closed ~**91.96-91.99**.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty**: 20-DMA at **25,693** is key resistance; support **25,200-300** / 200-EMA **~25,160**. Opened down ~165 pts near **25,250**; intraday low ~25,224. RSI 43.07, improving.
- **Bank Nifty**: very strong, healthy candle (+~350 pts), near **all-time high**; resistance ~**60,500**, support ~**59,100**; RSI ~57; highest call writing 62,000, put writing 59,500.
- **VIX** cooled ~1.15% to **13.37**; PCR ~1.06, rose slightly.
- Breadth negative at open: **443 advances vs 1,255 declines**; only 4 Nifty stocks green (Grasim, Titan, Power Grid +1).
- **FPIs** sold another **Rs 400 crore** (DIIs keep buying); attitude unlikely to shift before a **US trade deal**.
- **Settlement** day — new series behaviour watched.

## Results scorecard
- **Good/Excellent**: Tata Motors CV, **Vedanta** (to split into 5 cos, list by ~May), Nippon Life, **MTAR** (Hyderabad; ~100% PAT jump, 52-wk high), GE Shipping (+6%), Orient Cement, TD Power (strong order book + guidance), **Sirma SGS** (PAT ~110%, +5.2%), Dabur, ITC, **Sharda Cropchem** (blockbuster QoQ & YoY), **Vintage Coffee** (record revenue Rs 150 cr +71% YoY, EBITDA Rs 28 cr, PAT Rs 19 cr +53%), Paytm (loss→profit turnaround).
- **Disappointing**: **Blue Star, Voltas, Dixon (-3%, weak)**, REC (flat).
- **Sector moves at open**: Metals down (JSW Steel -2%, Hindustan Zinc -5.3% on silver), Swiggy -6.3%, Eternal -2.5%; FMCG firm. **Aurobindo Pharma +4%** on news. **MosChip** firm on a 28nm system-on-chip (ISRO satellite app) presented to IT Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw — result tomorrow expected ordinary.

## Budget expectations (Kranthi)
- Market's key watch: **capital-gains tweaks** and **STT reduction** (since LTCG now 10-12%, STT cut would be a "big booster"). Tax burden + ease-of-entry/exit are the FPI overhang vs alternatives like South Korea.
- Budget's prominence is fading post-GST-Council but still matters; disinvestment/policy announcements awaited. Big positive announcements could even push the market to new highs intraday.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — markets/fundamentals + viewer stock Q&A. View: volatile day, **buy-on-dips not sell-on-rallies**; "Make India Indispensable"; likes Economic Survey (trade surplus ~$23-24bn flipped to ~$6-7bn deficit; needs export diversification; private investment lagging). Liked PSU stocks pre-Budget (now 17-month-high share).
- **Kranthi/Vasanth** — Budget/capital-gains focus.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals (Nifty/Bank Nifty levels, OI), highlighted **Bank of Maharashtra** as a PSU bank to watch (FY25 total assets +20.2%, advances +17.9%, deposits +13.4%, net NPA 0.2%).
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Indo Farm Equipment)

kutumba.note: Hold from IPO; sees upside to Rs 3,500-3,600
kutumba.detail: Advised a viewer holding since the IPO to hold. MTAR touched a 52-week high of 2,844 and trades around 2,805-2,810 after an excellent consolidated result (~100% jump). He feels it can go up to Rs 3,500-3,600 thanks to its Bloom-Energy-type product and data-centre supplies, and called it the best of the Chennai recommendations.

kutumba.note: Budget-related micro-cap; slowly accumulate, average after results
kutumba.detail: A viewer bought at Rs 267 on Muhurat day; it has fallen to Rs 170. He called it a Budget-related micro-cap to slowly accumulate over the long term. Advised waiting for this quarter's results and management guidance; if numbers come better, then average.

kutumba.note: Hold; IPO froth cooled, watch NPA recovery
kutumba.detail: A viewer is at a ~29% loss. He called it a decent company whose speculative IPO-time activity has cooled off. A recent rise in NPAs is a concern and the recovery needs watching, so he advised holding for now.

kutumba.note: Hold; demerger of medical-devices business could add momentum
kutumba.detail: A viewer is at a ~42% loss. Given the big correction in mid- and small-cap stocks, he advised holding. Their medical-devices business is heading for a listing/demerger, and after that news there is a chance of some momentum in the stock.

kutumba.note: Decide only after the upcoming result
kutumba.detail: A viewer asked whether to hold or sell. He noted the company did a large equity expansion (~Rs 200 crore) that is hard to service, so EPS only becomes market-digestible with a lot of effort. They disappointed at the last result; he advised taking a decision only after seeing the upcoming result.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 29 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/2fuGeEo9U_g

## Global backdrop
- **US markets closed roughly flat.** Tech-major earnings landed after hours: **Microsoft** numbers were in line but the stock reacted negatively; **Meta** and **IBM** traded positively post-results.
- **Gold and silver are the big story.** Gold crossed **$5,600** (a record; vs ~$1,000 in 2008 around the GFC). Silver near **$118–120**. In India ~10g gold seen near **Rs 65,000** yesterday, likely to open **Rs 70,000–75,000**; MCX silver likely to open **above Rs 4 lakh/kg**.
- **Gold's ascent is accelerating:** $3,500→$4,000 took 118 days, $4,000→$4,500 took 56 days, $4,500→$5,000 took 14 days, $5,000→$5,500 just 2 days. Driver is **central-bank accumulation** since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine (and seizure of ~300t of reserves), not retail.
- **Multi-asset fund gold/silver exposure** flagged: Nippon ~**10.05%** gold; DSP **10.38%**; Aditya Birla Sun Life **9.68%**; Kotak only **5.38%** gold but ~**13–14%** silver (highest combined); ICICI Pru almost no silver (0.35%), gold 7.16%.
- Powell held rates (status quo expected); his commentary stressed Fed independence and warned successors to "stay out of elected politics" after the **Lisa Cook** removal case (Supreme Court negated Trump's order). Read as **dollar-weakness signal** — dollar at 4–5 year lows vs most currencies (though strong vs INR).
- Markets seen as hostage to **"Trump tantrums"** (Greenland "TACO trade", possible US government shutdown).

## Indian markets
- **Nifty +~300 pts over two sessions** (~167 pts yesterday) — partly short-covering, partly pre-Budget positioning. First close above previous day's high in ~25–27 days after 15 weak sessions.
- **Breadth strong:** ~400 of Nifty 500 closed positive yesterday; broad-based with mid/small-caps and beaten-down names recovering; delivery volumes up.
- **Flows turning:** FIIs swung to ~**+Rs 500 cr** (selling paused); DIIs bought ~**Rs 3,300 cr**.
- **Key levels (Ramakrishna):** support **25,000–25,160** (200-day EMA), strong put support **25,300**; resistance small at **25,500**, then **25,440** to extend rally. RSI 40.45 improving, VIX cooled to **13.53** (-6.43%), PCR 0.92. Bank Nifty 59,500 call / 59,000 put highest writing; possible move to **60,000**. Today: monthly Sensex options expiry → slight volatility expected. **Rupee opened near record ~91.99/USD**.
- **Budget (Sun 1 Feb) is the central swing factor.** Bull case (market/economy-friendly Budget + US trade deal) → new highs; failure to sustain 25,400–25,600 → toward **24,500/24,000**. Economic Survey due today.
- **Sectors leading:** energy, metals, defence. **Defence Index +8%** over two sessions; BEL was 2nd most-traded Nifty 500 stock, with breakout; HAL/BDL strong.
- **Earnings reactions:** strong from **L&T** (record Q3 order book **Rs 1.35 lakh cr**), GE Vernova (record), Gland Pharma (+8–8.5%), Hindustan Copper (+7%), TVS Motors, Garden Reach, Manorama Industries, Acutaas Chemicals, SJS Enterprises, Cigniti, SBI Cards, Samhi Hotels, Borosil Renewables. Weak: Cochin Shipyard (weak execution), NSDL, Maruti Suzuki (-3%), Somany (flat).
- **Trade/macro view:** EU–India FTA praised as a win-win; US deal possibly within 1–2 weeks; FTAs progressing serially (Mauritius 2021 → UK → EU).

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — back from a month-long US trip; macro/Budget framing, central-bank gold thesis, and viewer stock Q&A. Budget wish-list: large infra capex, PLI/DLI for high-growth sectors (AI data centres, clean energy, defence, railways), and crucially **capital-gains tax stability** as the "biggest trigger". Sees Budget as decisive given tariff/geopolitical risk.
- **Kranthi (Vasanth)** — defence-stock thesis (40–50% off highs, attractive long-term valuations, buy on dips/accumulate over 3–5 yrs); answered cement/finance viewer calls.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals, levels, OI, VIX; "buy-on-dips mode" view.
- **Host: Vasanth.**

## Notable trade idea
- Kutumba Rao flagged a **speculative "lottery-ticket" Nifty 26,000 CE (3-Feb expiry, post-Budget)** at ~Rs 22–23, no stop-loss — high risk, premium can go to zero on a disappointing Budget. (Index option, not a stock call.)
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 6 calls (Somany Ceramics, CG Power, BPCL, Nippon Gold BeES, Laurus Labs, HG Infra)

kutumba.note: Flat results; near 52-week low, hold for post-Budget infra push
kutumba.detail: Somany's results were flat - top line and bottom line both flat with no big improvement, so no need to average now. Maybe hold for the Budget; if there is an infra push, building/construction/infra companies are likely to rise post-Budget. It is closer to the 52-week low, so it's not the time to sell.

kutumba.note: Good Q3, semiconductor play; hold, upside on level crossing
kutumba.detail: December/third-quarter result was good - top line rose, bottom line sustained, and capex is completing. It is one of the first blocks in the semiconductor space. Once the stock crosses back over the 620-630 levels, it may go all the way to 750-770. Definitely hold.

kutumba.note: No fresh buying near 52-wk high; trim after interim dividend
kutumba.detail: PSU refiners benefited from Russian oil and sustained refining margins, but US pressure may slowly erode that. Last two quarters' results were good and the stock is close to its 52-week high, but he would not suggest fresh buying. With the 100% interim dividend already announced/approved, he suggests reducing the position a bit after the dividend.

kutumba.note: Don't take a gold loan to invest; use savings only
kutumba.detail: A viewer wanted to pledge his gold for a Rs 16 lakh loan to invest in Gold BeES. Kutumba Rao said no - never invest with borrowed money, use only savings. If gold corrects (possible within six months to a year) the leveraged position is risky; understand that risk before acting.

kutumba.note: Good results, holding above 1000; book if bought just before results
kutumba.detail: Results were good. The stock is holding above 1,000 and corrected after touching its 52-week high of 1,141. Those who bought just before the results (after the last quarter) can book profit, only that - there's nothing to be afraid of. The CDMO segment disappointed a little quarter-on-quarter.

kutumba.note: Hold all infra ahead of Budget; Dec results awaited
kutumba.detail: He said to compulsorily hold all infra stocks ahead of the Budget, as it is a sector the government must address. Fundamentally a good counter, no doubt. September results were soft due to monsoon (lower execution); December results are still awaited but likely better with no monsoon. If asked, hold.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 28 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/EKOrgGfZoCA

## Global backdrop
- **Gift Nifty** pointed to a mildly positive open (~**25,436**, +10 pts). Asia broadly higher; **South Korea** firm despite a **25% US tariff** order.
- **S&P 500** touched a fresh lifetime high (**+0.41%**); **Nasdaq +~0.9%**, led by tech.
- **Dollar index** very weak, down near **96** overnight. Trump dismissive ("if the dollar falls, what's the loss to me… the dollar is doing great").
- Dollar weakness drove **precious metals**: **Gold** holding above **$5,200**; **Silver** ~**$1,154**, up ~**8%** overnight. Gold/silver ETFs "running at lightning speed."
- **Rupee** recovered slightly to **91.92** (prev close ~91.7271) but flagged as among the weakest of the world's major currencies — negative for crude/gold imports, foreign travel, overseas education.
- Retail **gold sales reportedly down 70%** at ~**Rs 1.6 lakh/tola**.

## Indian markets
- **Budget on Sunday, 1 February** (Nirmala Sitharaman) is the next big event; budget sessions begin today. Hope/expectations crowded onto the budget now that the **India–EU FTA** is concluded.
- **Pre-budget weakness historic:** Sensex **-3.9%** over the last month — weakest pre-budget run since **2016**. **Small-cap index -~7%** over the month.
- Yesterday's **expiry session very volatile**; Nifty closed **+~120 pts** (~25,175) on **short covering**. **Bank Nifty +732 pts** to ~**59,200** — clear January-series winner.
- **PSU re-rating milestone:** **SBI overtook ICICI Bank** to become the **2nd-largest bank by market cap** (after HDFC Bank). Ranking: HDFC, SBI, ICICI, **Axis** (now 4th, above Kotak), Kotak. Axis up **4%** on better-than-Kotak numbers.
- **Mid/small-cap pain:** despite good results, no buying. F&O losers over the month: **Syngene -24.8%, Godrej Properties -24.3%, Kalyan Jewellers -24%, ITC -20%, HPCL/Dixon -15%**; plus Swiggy, NBCC, CG Power, Tube Investments, Premier Energies. Sentiment-driven, not fundamentals.
- **Bondada Engineering** (Hyderabad solar EPC) posted strong Q3 — **net profit +119% YoY to Rs 54 cr**, **revenue +89% to Rs 712 cr**, **EBITDA +121% (~Rs 85 cr)**, margins **10.2%→11.9%** — yet stock near 52-week low (~320 vs 530 high) and **fell ~4%** on the day.
- **FIIs net sellers ~Rs 1,952 cr**; **DIIs rescued the market** (~Rs 7,760–9,000 cr buying). Bull rally needs FIIs to stop selling.
- **Options data:** highest call OI **25,500**, put **25,000** → expected **500-pt range (25,000–25,500)** into the budget; **26,000** the upside target if budget pleases. **India VIX** spiked to **16.06** intraday, settled ~**14.45**. Next expiry **3 February** (post-budget).
- **OFS:** **Vedanta** offering ~**1.6% of Hindustan Zinc** (~**Rs 4,600 cr**) at a **6% discount**; HZ -2–3%, Vedanta +2%.
- **Results today:** L&T, Maruti Suzuki, Cochin Shipyard, GRSE, TVS Motors, Lodha, Phoenix, Piramal Pharma, SBI Cards. **Asian Paints -6%** on a miss; **IGI +9%**, **Mahindra Logistics**, **Bikaji/Gopal snacks** strong.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** — anchored the macro open (dollar, metals, rupee, budget setup, PSU-bank re-rating, mid/small-cap damage, Bondada numbers) and read viewer mails/calls. Gave the asset-allocation framing on the gold-vs-Jio SIP question.
- **Kranthi / Vasanth** — fielded most viewer stock Q&A (Hold/Add/Profit-book verdicts). Note: small-cap funds (ICICI Pru) reopening to inflows suggests valuations bottoming; long-term buyers with patience can start nibbling.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals: keep a **long-side view** but expect **consolidation near the 200-DMA**. Nifty **support 24,950–24,920 / 25,200–25,175**, **resistance 25,450–500**; 25,275 = 200-day support. Bank Nifty **support 58,500**, **resistance 60,000**, crossed 50-day EMA. RSI/MACD/PCR (0.98→1.02) improving; breadth still weak (1,300 adv / 1,600 dec). "Slight budget rally" started; trail stops ≥150 pts.
takeaways: Kutumba Rao: 1 buy (Gold ETF (Tata Gold ETF / Nippon Gold BeES / ICICI Pru Gold))

kutumba.stock: Gold ETF (Tata Gold ETF / Nippon Gold BeES / ICICI Pru Gold)
kutumba.note: Continue gold SIP/ETF; weak dollar and central-bank buying support gold
kutumba.detail: On a viewer's gold-vs-Jio-Finance SIP question, Kutumba Rao framed them as different asset classes and advised allocating across both. He endorsed continuing or adding gold via ETF (Tata Gold, Nippon Gold BeES or ICICI Pru Gold), noting these gave roughly 95-100% returns over the last year. He argued a weakening dollar index and central banks shifting reserves from treasuries into gold support gold over the long term.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 27 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/MmiGR9gvOn0

## Global backdrop
- **Gift Nifty** pointed to a ~**+100 point** gap-up; actual open was softer (~**+60** in Nifty, **+135** Sensex).
- **Trump raised South Korea tariffs from 15% to 25%**; Korea market still up ~**1.5%**. Hang Seng strong, Shanghai weak; rest of Asia positive.
- **Dollar index below 97.** **Gold ~$5,075/oz** (hit **$5,100** intraday); **silver ~$110–111** (touched **$117**). MCX gold/silver futures to open strong.
- **Crude ~$65.76**, up on renewed US–Iran tension.
- **US Fed FOMC this week** — no rate change expected; possible clarity on the next Fed Chair (two contenders).

## Two weekend catalysts
- **India–EU Free Trade Agreement** all but finalized ("mother of all trade deals" — von der Leyen); signing in ~6 months, effective likely **1 Jan 2027**. **~17% of India's exports** go to Europe (autos, textiles). Positives for **Sona BLW, Gokuldas, KPR Mills, Bharat Forge**.
- **US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent** signaled the **25% penal tariff** on India (for Russian-oil purchases) may be **removed** — alongside the standard 25% additional tariff.

## Indian markets
- **Rupee at 91.94/USD**, near all-time low (92 breached).
- Nifty has **lost 850 points in January.** Key levels: must reclaim **25,348** (23 Jan high); support **24,919**; pivotal floor **25,000** — breakdown = "bear-market zone."
- **India VIX 14.19** (up ~6.5% last week, ~+50% off the ~9 all-time low); jumped ~10% to **15.6** intraday. **PCR 0.77 / RSI ~29** — oversold but no short-covering bounce yet.
- **Market breadth weak**: Friday Nifty 500 had **73 advances vs 426 declines** (~1:4–4.5). (Opened positive Monday: ~**1,142 adv / 650 decl**.)
- **FIIs net index short ~Rs 38,000 cr**; shorts trimmed from **91% (19 Jan) to 85% (23 Jan)**, yet index still fell ~500 pts — **selling now from retail/HNIs/local crowd**, not MFs. FIIs sold ~**Rs 4,000 cr** Thu–Fri; **~Rs 40,000 cr YTD**.
- **Budget in 4 days = the key trigger.** A budget rally could add **500–700 points**; markets (incl. coming Sunday session) open. Disappointment risks **24,000**; a good budget could aim **26,000**.
- **Two monthly options expiries** this week (today + day-after) → high volatility; trade with **consolidation-to-positive bias**, careful on trading positions.
- **Bank Nifty** broke its 50-DMA, closed below lower Bollinger Band (~700-pt loss on Thursday); now aligning with Nifty rather than outperforming. Nifty highest call writing **25,300**, put **24,700**.

## Results scorecard
- **Good/strong:** MCX (superb), Nuvama, Laurus Labs (blockbuster generics; CDMO soft QoQ), Granules India (EBITDA +33% YoY), Godrej Consumer, JSW Energy, DCB Bank, Axis Bank, Urban Company Q3.
- **Weak:** Kotak Bank and IndusInd Bank (weak, both **−4.5%** at open, though above estimates), Avantel (51.7 cr sales but only **2.7 cr** net profit — repeated execution miss).
- **Movers at open:** Hindustan Zinc **+3%** (silver record high), Adani Enterprises **+2.5%**, Adani Ports +2.3–3%, Gokuldas/UltraTech up; weak: Eternal, Cipla, Maruti, Wipro, M&M, Kotak, IndusInd.

## Analysts featured
- **Kranthi / Vasanth (stock ideas, viewer Q&A)** — bias that Nifty holds above **25,000**; deep-dives on **MCX** (commodity super-cycle, possible **Rs 1,000 cr** PAT) and **Capstone/Caption Services** (entering Urban-Company-style home services; bonus 1:2; multibagger thesis). Verdicts on viewer stocks below.
- **Ramakrishna (technicals)** — markets weak/oversold; budget rally possible (~500–700 pts); don't trade the first hour; Nifty resistance **25,137**, support **24,950–24,980**; silver trailing-stop guidance.

## Notable individual calls
- **MCX** — buy/strong (super-cycle, turnaround to ~Rs 1,000 cr PAT).
- **Capstone/Caption** — multibagger; home-services entry; re-enter on 5–10% dips around bonus record date; target ~Rs 2,000 cr m-cap.
- **Newgen Software** — slow accumulate for 1-yr+ horizon. **KRN Heat Exchangers** — trading bet only, buy post-results near Rs 820.
- **Ola Electric** — avoid/exit on rallies (weak core, weak promoter). **Facor Alloys** — avoid.
- **Silver** — hold with trailing stop ~Rs 3,15,000 (trading ~3,54,000); near-term ~Rs 4 lakh possible. **Moschip** — only with 2–3 yr view, after results.
- **Lodha Developers** — hold/accumulate (long-term), exit on rally only if a trading stop triggered.
- **Eternal vs Swiggy** — hold Eternal (index inclusion → fund buying), don't shift.
- **Titagarh** — slow accumulate, 2–3 yr horizon; book some near Rs 1,250 bounce.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 23 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/4LN3maIArUs

## Global backdrop
- **US closed positive**: Dow **+~300 pts**, Nasdaq **+211 pts (+0.9%)**. **Russell 2000** (small/micro-cap) at a fresh **lifetime high** — strong over recent days. Tuesday's losses fully recovered Wed/Thu.
- Stock moves: **Intel -13%** (disappointing guidance), **Tesla +4.2%**, **Meta ~+5.7-7%**.
- **Precious metals surge**: **Gold lifetime high $4,969/oz** (near $5,000); **Silver ~$99** (just ~$1 from $100). Indian gold/silver **ETFs fell hard** (silver ETFs -8/-9/-10% intraday) — seen as good entry points.
- **Davos/Greenland**: Trump said no aggressive military action on Greenland, but secured everything else (security beef-up, US missiles/forces, mining rights) — a **unilateral** push to counter China in critical minerals (parallel drawn to Venezuela/crude). "Board of Peace" (Gaza) now backed by ~**19 countries**; **India non-committal** (wants trade deal first).
- **Gold targets**: Goldman Sachs **$5,400 by end-2026**; Jefferies' **Christopher Wood $6,600** in coming months; some say **$7,000**. Central banks still accumulating; supply limited; silver/copper industrial demand huge (China doing R&D to cut silver use in solar panels). View: gold & silver **continue to appreciate**, but no panic on dips and no runaway expectations.

## Indian markets
- **Long weekend ahead**: Sun + Mon (Republic Day) → 3-day holiday. **Tuesday = monthly Nifty/NSE expiry**. **Budget on Feb 1 (Sunday)** — only **five trading sessions** to Budget, with **zero noise / zero expectations** (rare). Positive surprises could move markets, but limited expected (no elections pending).
- **Yesterday's recovery** after a very tough week: **Nifty +0.53%**, **Midcap +1.34%**, **Smallcap +0.76%**, **Bank Nifty +0.68%** (despite weak HDFC Bank & ICICI Bank). Strong midcap buying in result-driven counters: **Waaree Energies (~+10%)**, Minda Corp, Schneider Electric, Credit Access Grameen, Welspun Living, Granules, Trident.
- **Rupee stabilised ~91.63/$** (fear of 92 eased). **FII selling -2,550 cr**, **DII buying +4,223 cr** → net institutional **buyers**. **PCR improved to 0.87** (from 0.78). Nifty formed a **higher-high/higher-low**. Markets still **oversold**.
- **Davos India angle**: EU president (echoing Trump's "mother of all deals") said she's "making a deal" with India — positive for dormant/selling FIIs. **Blackstone CEO** (ET front page) talking up bigger **direct investments / listed acquisitions** in India = stable long-only money. India's long-term outlook intact.
- **ICICI Prudential Smallcap Scheme** reopens for subscriptions after an **18-month gap** → supports value buys in smallcaps (selective, not broad-based).

## Results & corporate news
- **Yesterday's results** — good: **Zensar** (YoY good, QoQ flat), **Ixigo**, **Home First Finance**, **Mphasis**, **Blue Stone Jewellery** (excellent, recent listing), **Premier Energies** (decent), **Alufluoride** (Hyderabad micro-cap; 9M EPS ~26, FY could top 30 — "one stock to watch"). **Coforge** mixed (one-time labour-code expense of **148 cr** capped net profit at **297 cr**). Weak: **Suryoday SFB**, **Bandhan Bank** (profit shrank). **IndiGo Q3 net -78% to Rs 549 cr** (Dec operational meltdown not fully recovered, labour code, rupee).
- **IIFL Finance -13%** yesterday: good result/gold-loan book, but **income-tax special audit** ordered.
- **IPO market selective**: **Shadowfax** (logistics/courier, Rs 1,900 cr) subscribed just **2.7x**. **PhonePe ~$1.5 bn IPO** coming — Walmart (~71%) to sell ~9%; Tiger Global & Microsoft exiting; promoters earlier sold ~$430 mn stake to General Atlantic. **Balaji Wafers**: General Atlantic took **7% for Rs 2,500 cr**; PE interest in packaged foods hot (cf. Haldiram's — 21% for Rs 20,000 cr → ~Rs 1-lakh-cr valuation).
- **Today's board meetings**: **Laurus Labs** (result; high expectations), **Capstan Services** (bonus-issue ratio to watch; results ~early Feb). **Granules** — good price action, results expected good.

## Technical levels (Ramakrishna)
- Nifty closed **above 200-day EMA/SMA** (relief). **Resistance 25,575–25,650**; **support 24,900** (recent swing); regaining **25,500 decisively** is the key positive trigger (also flagged by Kranti). RSI **33.82** (weak), MACD bearish crossover, histogram below zero — **caution / consolidation**.
- OI: Nifty **25,400 call** & **25,300 put** highest writing (slightly positive). **Bank Nifty** +400 pts but **sharp doji** (indecision); OI **60,000 call / 59,000 put** highest writing. **VIX -3.12% to 13.35** (cooling — positive).
- **Trade plan**: lock in profits today (long weekend, India-only holiday → gap risk); trade cautiously, don't extrapolate a one-day rally. Intraday short idea: below **25,200–210** with 50-pt SL, 70–80 pt target.

## Analysts featured
- **Vasanth** (anchor), **Kranti** (fundamental), **Ramakrishna** (technical).
- **Kutumba Rao did NOT appear** in this episode; all viewer stock calls were answered by **Kranti** and **Ramakrishna**.

## Stock calls (viewer Q&A — Kranti / Ramakrishna)
- **Titagarh Wagons / Jupiter Wagons / RVNL** — **Hold** (3-5 yr view); on track, railway budget may add positivity; not Kavach-linked (RVNL somewhat). Jupiter has corp-governance issues; Titagarh clean balance sheet (5-6 yr 10-bagger now consolidating 700-800).
- **Revathi Equipment** — **Avoid**; no fundamental story, controlled small-equity stock (fell 2,000 → 650).
- **Capstan Services** — **Wait**; don't add more before the bonus-ratio announcement; don't over-concentrate one stock.
- **eMudhra** — **Avoid/Hold, don't average now**; weak post-result, range bottom; only average if it crosses 2,500 on good news.
- **Cyient (DLM)** — **Hold**, no need to buy immediately (long consolidation).
- **Alpex Solar / Precision Wires** — **Hold** if held; both fundamentally fine; Precision Wires watch copper-cost pass-through; decide after results.
- **Praj Industries** — **Book profits on rallies**; cyclical, alternative-fuel narrative slowing (US anti-net-zero); follow India (ethanol push) not US.
- **Canara Robeco AMC** — **Avoid/switch**; niche AMC underperforming post-listing, low AUM; prefer ICICI AMC; technically in sell mode; **Canara Bank** has more momentum.
- **SBI** — **Hold**.
- **Tejas Networks** — **Hold** (don't book 50% loss now / don't switch); only shift to **Laurus Labs** if it dips below ~1,000 on blockbuster results — Laurus will outperform Tejas.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 21 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/K3YCTIyzunI

## Global backdrop
- **Risk-off / global sell-off.** US closed lower overnight: **S&P 500 -2.1%**, **Nasdaq -2.39%**. European markets also sold off. CBOE **VIX at its highest since November 2025**.
- **Japanese bond bloodbath.** A **snap election** is due next month in Japan; both ruling and opposition parties promised **fiscal expansion + tax cuts**. 30Y/40Y JGB yields jumped **25–30 bps**; the Finance Minister's plea to calm markets didn't help.
- **Spillover to US Treasuries.** US 10Y yield rose **0.07% to 4.29%**. A **Danish pension fund** said it will **exit US Treasuries by month-end**; the EU is preparing retaliatory tariffs — "country by country" hardening against the US.
- **Trump as catalyst.** Took the **Greenland** issue aggressively, warned Denmark; speaks at **Davos / WEF at 6:30pm IST today** — markets watching. The disruption of the "world order" is the framing.
- **Safe havens bid.** **Gold ~$4,800**, **silver ~$94–95**. The presenter flagged these as the main portfolio support right now.
- **US SCOTUS tariff (IEEPA) ruling postponed to February 20** (~1 month). Read as: court unlikely to rule strongly against the tariffs.

## Indian markets
- **Brutal sell-off yesterday (20 Jan).** Nifty closed **~25,232, down ~350 pts** (intraday low ~25,170–180), after testing the **200-DMA ~25,100**. NSE advance/decline **385 up vs 2,627 down (~1:7)** — panic/cascading selling.
- **Mid/small caps in a bear market:** **Microcap 250 -20%**, **Smallcap index -16%** from peak; many stocks down 20–50%, some 60–70%.
- **Drivers cited:** weekly options expiry, **Trump uncertainty**, **MTF (margin) selling / margin calls**, weak earnings, weak rupee. **FII selling was modest (~Rs 3,000 cr)**, more than offset by DII buying.
- **Result-day hammering even on good prints:** **Havells -7%** (decent result, negative reaction) dragged **Polycab -4.63%**; LTIMindtree also hit. AU Bank and United Spirits printed well; Persistent mixed.
- **Today's results due:** Eternal, **Dr Reddy's** (expected weak), HPCL, KEI, Bank of India, Oracle Financial, Supreme Industries, PNB Housing, Waaree Energies, Dalmia Bharat (mostly mid-caps; only Eternal & Dr Reddy's are Nifty names).
- **Technicals (Ramakrishna):** Nifty/Sensex/midcap at ~3-month lows, smallcap near 8-month low. **11 straight down sessions, ~4% over 10 sessions — called a "healthy correction."** Nifty **RSI ~29**, **PCR 0.72** (oversold), **MACD below zero**. Supports **25,000 / 200-DMA ~25,100–25,160**. **VIX +7.63 to 12.73** (eyeing 13.5–14 by budget). **Bank Nifty -487 pts**, RSI 48.82, lost its outperformance. Bias: **consolidation-to-negative** near term; expect a 200-DMA test then a possible bounce.
- **Market open (21 Jan):** Nifty ~ -32/-40 pts, Sensex -190/-200, Bank Nifty -126; breadth negative (NSE 477 up vs 1,231 down). Pharma index positive; HPL Power +3.5%.
- **Stock-specific notes:** **Caption (Caption Services)** — Hyderabad small-cap, board meeting **23rd** to consider a **bonus issue** (ratio to be watched; previously gave a 2:1 i.e. 1 bonus per 2 shares); news now discounted.

## Analysts featured
- **Vasanth** (anchor/host) — set the macro framing: risk-off, capital protection / "return OF capital," buy only relative-strength stocks meeting valuation + earnings-visibility + management-commentary + relative-strength filters; otherwise stay a "silent spectator." Stressed behavioral finance (oversold RSI, fear at bottoms, full-invested investors with no dry powder).
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst) — answered most viewer stock Q&A. View: not yet a "brutal" sell-off; **25,000 holds**; recalled that Trump's first term (2017) defied "global recession" calls with one of the best US rallies in 70 years, but this time only the **Magnificent Seven** held the US up. Sees a **"global reset"** (Europe/Canada diverging from the US) making equities vulnerable to geopolitics; mid/small caps are **high beta** — be prepared for 30–40% falls. Advised SIPs OK (including **gold/silver ETFs** via rupee-cost averaging), lumpsum buyers cautious, diversify across asset classes (equity/gold-silver/debt/REITs-InvITs).
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) — provided the levels/indicators above; "cautious, churn weak stocks into strong," strict stop-loss for longs, short on breaks of the previous swing.

## Note on Kutumba Rao
**Kutumba Rao was NOT on this episode** (not named or present). All viewer stock Q&A was handled by **Kranthi** (fundamentals) and **Ramakrishna** (technicals), with **Vasanth** anchoring. Their calls are captured in the companion `kutumba_rao.md` prose note for the human reader but are **excluded from buys.json**.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 20 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/xTzBdaLjnHM

## Global backdrop
- **US markets closed** Monday for the **Martin Luther King** holiday; **Asian markets mixed**, **GIFT Nifty** slightly positive.
- **European markets fell heavily** (DAX, CAC) on the **Greenland threat** and talk of **retaliatory EU tariffs worth $108 billion** if the US imposes a **10% tariff on EU goods from February 1**.
- **Trump** grandstanding at **Davos** (set up a "USA House", speech due tomorrow); threatening tariffs over not getting the Nobel Peace Prize. A **US Supreme Court ruling on Trump tariffs** could come any day.
- **Gold** trading above **$4670** (futures **Rs 1,48,100**); **Silver** near **$94.55**, crossed **Rs 3 lakh** in India (~Rs 3,28,000 MCX futures). Both at record highs. **Bitcoin** fell to **92,000**.
- **IMF** reiterated a conservative India growth target of **~7.5%** (5–8% range) — a potential positive for long-only FPIs.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty** very weak: closed **25,585** yesterday, dipped below 25,500 intraday (low **25,494**), forming **lower highs / lower lows**. **RSI ~37** (oversold), **PCR 0.7**.
- **25,500** is the key medium/short-term support; held over the last 4–5 sessions. A decisive break risks a fall to the **200-DMA at ~25,100** and a "dire" scenario. Resistance: **25,900**; nearer-term **25,638–640** (Ramakrishna). Swing support **25,450–25,479**; break → 200–300 point drop.
- **Bank Nifty** relatively stronger (higher low; opened +86 pts) — the only place with some safety for longs.
- **FII selling non-stop** — **Rs 3,263 cr** sold yesterday (20th consecutive negative day); **DIIs bought Rs 4,234 cr**. FPIs have sold **>Rs 2 lakh cr** this FY; FII stake in Indian equities at a historic low of **~17%**. **DII/SIP inflows of Rs 45–50,000 cr/month** are the key cushion.
- **Heavyweights dragging**: **Reliance −10%** this month (Q3 growth below expectations; needs to hold 200-DMA), **HDFC Bank −6.4%**, **Bharti Airtel −4.5%**, ICICI Bank weak.
- **Earnings season weak**: Business Standard headline — **worst earnings growth in 17 quarters**. Banks' revenue and profits down; **CASA ratio** falling (HDFC Bank ~40% → ~30%) as savers chase higher FD/other rates. No big positive surprises.
- **Mid/small-caps already in a bear market** — portfolios down **50–60%**, only ~40–50 resilient names. Today is **expiry/settlement day** → some volatility expected; consolidation likely to continue.
- **Bear-market caution**: both analysts warn that if the **Budget** or **Q3 earnings** disappoint, large-caps could follow into a bear market. Historical analogs cited: **1992–2000** and **2009–2013** long bear phases. Signs present but no confirmation yet.
- **Advice**: **protect capital**, avoid **revenge buying / averaging down**, but **do NOT stop mutual-fund SIPs** — long-term SIP discipline (e.g. Rs 10,000/month for 25 yrs ≈ Rs 1.33 cr at 12–15% avg).

## IPOs, results & corporate action
- **Bharat Coking Coal** listed yesterday +77% (147x subscribed); up another ~5% today. Sustainability questioned (commodity play).
- **Shadowfax Technologies** IPO (logistics) opens today — fundamentals look OK.
- **Yesterday's results**: good — Hindustan Zinc, Tata Capital, Havells India (beat), L&T Mindtree (mixed-positive), Atlanta Electricals (excellent), Tips Music, Auto Corp of Goa (decent); weak — Oberoi Realty, Sai Silks (−5%).
- **Today's results due**: SRF, United Spirits, AU Small Finance Bank, Persistent Systems.
- **Block deals** in **AB Lifestyle** and **ABFRL** (ABFRL opened −8%). Board meetings (fund-raising): Bazaar Style Retail, Restaurant Brands Asia. Capstone (~Rs 334–335) board-meeting buzz.
- **Opening**: Nifty −25/30 pts, Sensex −69/70, breadth negative (713 adv / 962 dec). **Metal index +0.5%** (Hindalco, JSW Steel, Tata Steel), Auto firm (Eicher, M&M), **silver ETFs +3–4%**, PSU banks (SBI, Canara) showing interest. Realty, IT, Oil & Gas, Pharma, Energy weak.

## Analysts featured
- **Host (Prasad)** — macro overview, bear-market caution, SIP discipline.
- **Kranthi** (with **Vasanth** co-hosting) — stock ideas and viewer Q&A; recurring theme: "look at this quarter's results before entering" given the downtrend.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals (levels, OI, moving averages) and several viewer calls.
- **Note: Kutumba Rao did NOT appear in this episode.** All viewer stock Q&A was answered by Kranthi and Ramakrishna.

## Viewer stock calls (by Kranthi / Ramakrishna)
- **Laurus Labs** — fundamentally strong but near 52-wk high (~1063); buy on dips below 1000 / wait for results (Kranthi).
- **Hindustan Copper** — full momentum, vertical move; trade with strict stop-loss, or invest on dips (3 entry points) (Kranthi).
- **Stanley Lifestyle** — thematic (premiumization); add slowly with patience after results (Kranthi).
- **Aatham Investments** — stretched (4x book); momentum/speculative play only (Kranthi).
- **Ganesha Ecosphere, Premier Energies, Kemp Bond Chemicals** — weak/consolidating; wait for results / avoid (Kranthi).
- **Tejas Networks** — very negative result; give up hopes this year, BSNL-dependent (host).
- **Vimta Labs** — nutraceutical doing well; Hold (Kranthi).
- **Suzlon** — renewable in correction; exit into any surprise rally, else hold long-term (Kranthi).
- **Godrej Properties** — technically weak, broke supports; hold to results (5 Feb), exit if weak; no fresh adds (Ramakrishna).
- **Central Bank of India** — slow mover, consistent dividends; Hold (host).
- **Amara Raja Batteries** — strong results, on track; Hold 1–2 yrs (Kranthi).
- **D Development Engineers, JK Enterprises (Jay Kay), TD Power** — enter after results; JK can be slowly added for long-term risk appetite (Kranthi).
- **Reliance Industries** — weak, 200-DMA broken; wait, enter only above ~1500 decisively (Ramakrishna).
- **Fortis Healthcare** — healthcare in uptrend, tariff-immune; add slowly medium/long term (Kranthi).
- **Standard Glass** — hold only if result good, else exit.
- **HBL Engineering** — good order book/earnings visibility; hold long-term (host).
- **Mutual funds (Sujata)** — switch JM Flexi Cap → HDFC Flexi Cap and SBI Small Cap → Nippon/Bandhan Small Cap (both underperforming); consider Nifty 50 Equal Weight index funds (DSP/HDFC).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 19 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/C1HB0QjWGLU

## Global backdrop
- **Negative global cues.** **Dow futures -350 pts**, **S&P -58**, **Nasdaq -280**. Most Asian indices in the red except **Shanghai** and Korea's **Kospi**.
- **Trump tariff threat.** From **Feb 1**, US to slap an **additional 10% tariff** on eight European nations (Sweden, Norway, Germany, UK, Denmark, France, Netherlands, Finland), rising **+15% to 25% from Jun 1** — leverage over **Denmark's refusal to sell Greenland**.
- **Davos / WEF** this week — Trump attending with a large delegation; markets nervous about his speech.
- Trump invited **Modi** to join a Gaza "Board of Peace" — **$1bn** permanent-membership fee; host skeptical of the value.
- **US markets closed today** (Martin Luther King Jr. Day), so US futures are the read-through.

## Indian markets
- **GIFT Nifty** points to a weak open, **~180 pts lower**.
- **Weekend results** (≈1/3 of Nifty weight): **RIL** Q3 fine but below estimates (oil & gas disappointed; O2C, quick commerce, Jio Platforms good; **Jio Platforms IPO signalled**); **HDFC Bank** in line; **ICICI Bank** good but hit by **one-time provisions**.
- **Sandeep Bakshi re-appointed ICICI MD & CEO** — flagged as India's best banker.
- **Levels:** support **25,473** (Jun-12 low) / **25,450**; resistance **25,900**. Range-bound expected; break of 25,450 = alert.
- **Options OI:** PCR **0.76** (down from 0.81); Nifty **25,900 call** / **25,500 put** highest writing → ~400-pt range; Bank Nifty **60,200 call / 60,000 put**. Tuesday settlement keeps these active.
- **Momentum weak on Nifty** — RSI <40, MACD below signal, histogram below zero. **VIX rising** into the Budget (≈2 weeks away). **Sammaan Capital** in F&O ban.
- **Bank Nifty outperforming** — +500 pts Friday, broke out of a 4-5 day consolidation, near upper Bollinger band.
- **Flows:** FIIs **sold Rs 4,346 cr** Friday; DIIs **bought Rs 3,935 cr**. FIIs clearly in selling mode.
- **Last week:** MSCI Asia ex-Japan +2% vs Nifty +0.07% (clear underperformance). **PSU Banks +5%, Metals +5%**; **Pharma -2%, Realty -2%**.
- **Results scoreboard:** Good — **Netweb, Himadri Speciality, UCO Bank, Punjab & Sind Bank, Bajaj Healthcare, JB Chemicals, SMC, Mahindra**. Weak — **RBL Bank, Yes Bank, Wipro**.
- **Listing:** Coal India subsidiary **Bharat Coking Coal** lists today (**147x subscribed**).

## Opening-trade moves
- Indices opened in losses. **Bank Nifty -0.37%, Midcap -0.36%**; Pharma, Oil & Gas, Realty, IT, Capital Market, Auto down. FMCG, Capital Goods, Metals up.
- **Gainers:** **Netweb +9-12%** (super growth numbers — hit stock of the day), CG Power +7%, **Poonawalla Fincorp +5%**, Tech Mahindra +2.3-3%, Jindal Saw, InterGlobe, ABB Power, Tata Tech, Polycab, Hindustan Zinc, JSW Infra, Solar Industries.
- **Losers:** **Wipro -9%** (weak Q4 guidance — "accident of the day"), **RBL Bank -7%**, **ICICI Bank -2.5%**, **Reliance -2%+**, Yes Bank; plus Tata Motors PV, Sun Pharma (**$10bn acquisition news**), Eternal, Reliance Infra, IDBI, Sobha, HEG, Brigade, Jubilant Life, TBO Tek, City Union Bank, Yatharth Hospital.

## IPO spotlight — Digilogic Systems (BSE SME)
- Hyderabad **defence/aerospace TMS** (test-measurement-simulation) firm; **18-yr history**, client base DRDO/BDL/HAL/BEL; **95% defence**.
- **Price band Rs 98–104**, opens **20 Jan**, closes **22 Jan**, lists **28 Jan**. Issue ~**Rs 80 cr** (fresh **Rs 69.67 cr** + OFS **Rs 11.33 cr**); fresh proceeds → expansion (~Rs 51 cr), debt repayment (Rs 8 cr). Min app 1,200 shares.
- **Financials:** FY25 revenue **Rs 72 cr**, PAT **Rs 8 cr**, EPS **3.89**; H1 FY26 income **Rs 18.3 cr**, PAT **Rs 1.6 cr**, EPS **0.72**. Host flagged issue as **expensive on forward P/E** (trailing ~27-28x reasonable, but FY26 EPS doubts).
- Promoters: new **Pahadi Shareef** EMS/manufacturing facility (65-70k sq ft, commissioned by **2028**, also open to commercial electronics); FY25 jump driven by **production orders** (higher reuse → better margins); says trend continues as prototyping programmes move to production; calls it a **long-term game** (listing gains not promised).

## Analysts featured
- **Ramakrishna (technicals):** range-bound, buy-on-dips in the lower band; try a long above **25,620** if it holds ~5 min. Bank Nifty outperforming.
- **Kranthi / Vasanth (stock ideas / defence):** quizzed Digilogic promoters; handled viewer stock Q&A (Advait Energy, Triveni, RIR Power, PTC Industries — all "wait for results / add slowly").
- **Note:** Kutumba Rao did **not** appear in this episode; viewer stock calls were taken by Kranthi/Vasanth and Ramakrishna.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 14 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/hte-Yn0rc-E

A Sankranti-day episode: festive mood off-air, but no "Sankranti glow" in the market. Global cues mixed, FII selling persists, and traders are flagged to stay cautious into a holiday-shortened week with two big pending events (US Supreme Court tariff ruling, earnings). A large festival/lifestyle segment from Nani's Restaurant fills the middle of the show. Note: **Kutumba Rao did not feature/speak** this episode — the viewer stock Q&A was handled by **Vasanth Kranthi** and the anchor **Vasanth**, with technicals from **Ramakrishna**.

## Global backdrop
- **US markets** closed lower last night; global cues **mixed**.
- **Geopolitics** centred on **Iran** — Trump signalling support to anti-government protesters ("help is on the way"), seen as a regime-change push (compared to Venezuela; Greenland flagged next).
- **Commodities super-cycle** in focus: **crude ~$65** (expected to stabilise at the higher $60–65 band as supply, incl. Venezuela, keeps pace); **gold** touched ATH **$4,625** then eased; **silver ~$89.99**, eyeing **$100 this quarter** (~Rs 3 lakh/kg); US reportedly weighing **refined-copper tariffs**; **aluminium** at a 3-year high.
- **US Supreme Court tariff decision** possibly tonight (may be postponed again). If it strikes down Trump's tariffs, the **25% reciprocal/penal tariffs** (US revenue **$100–150bn**) could need refunding — a market-moving risk. India reacts **Friday** (Thursday is a market holiday).
- **India–US:** Jaishankar–Rubio phone call covering defence, energy, trade and critical-minerals supply chains; **trade-deal negotiations continue** with no blueprint yet.

## Indian markets
- **FIIs sold Rs 1,500 cr** yesterday; **DIIs bought Rs 1,182 cr**. FPIs "not in a hurry" until a trade-deal blueprint emerges.
- **Nifty** forming higher highs / higher lows; yesterday closed **25,648** (right on the **20-week MA ~25,644**). Key supports: **25,500** (Monday's bottom) and **25,473**; **PCR ~0.79–0.86** (nearing oversold); **India VIX ~11.2** (eased ~1.5%).
- **Options OI:** highest put OI shifted down from 25,500 to **25,000** (fear-driven, near 200-DMA); highest call OI at **26,000** — needs a clear break above 26,000 for a smooth up-move.
- **Expiry today** (pulled forward due to tomorrow's holiday); **Bank Nifty outperforming** (closed **+128 pts**, above 20-EMA; long-week formation) while **Nifty weak** (-58 pts yesterday, weak open today).
- **Breadth weak (ET data):** **70%** of Nifty 500 stocks below their 50-DMA; **60%** below 200-DMA. Ex-darlings beaten down — **Dixon Tech** (no buy interest), **Cohance Life Sciences** (-50% from 200-DMA), Transformers & Rectifiers, Tejas Networks, Kaynes, Whirlpool, Valor Estate, Blue Jet, Elecon, Reliance Power, NCC. Stocks **above** 200-DMA are mostly **commodities**: **Tata Motors (+87%)**, **Hindustan Copper (+83%)**, NALCO, Ashok Leyland, Shriram Finance, Muthoot Finance, Vedanta, IIFL, Hindustan Zinc.
- **Results today:** **Infosys**, **HDFC AMC**, **HDB Financial Services**, **Union Bank**. Yesterday: **Tata Elxsi** strong numbers (stock +10% pre-results). **Polycab** ~**Rs 1,200 cr block deal** pending.
- **IPO:** **Bharat Coking Coal** (Coal India subsidiary, ~Rs 1,071 cr) oversubscribed **147x** (Rs 1.2 lakh cr in bids); attractive **Rs 21–23 price band**. Subscriptions: employee 5x, shareholders 87x, NII 258x, QIB 31x, retail 49x. Likely lists **Monday**.
- **Opening:** Nifty ~25,700; Bank Nifty -52, Sensex -100; advance/decline turned negative (733/961 NSE); mid-cap & IT indices down. **Gainers:** MMTC (+5%+, silver play), HEG, small PSU/private banks (IOB, Bank of Maharashtra, Central Bank +2–3%), Puravankara (+8% on business update), plus metals/commodities. **Losers:** Kalyan Jewellers, Hexaware, Sundaram Finance, Alkyl Amines, Tata Elxsi (profit-taking), Jindal Stainless, Eicher Motors, Federal Bank, L&T Finance, Escorts.

## Analysts featured

### Vasanth Kranthi (stock ideas / F&O setup; joined from Mumbai)
- **No immediate trend reversal**; sentiment negative ("Trump eclipse"). Market in a **consolidation in a positive direction** while **25,500 holds** (~80% of Nifty stocks still above key MAs). A **trend reversal** could come from **large-cap earnings surprises** — watch **Banking & Financials, Reliance, L&T**.
- **F&O long buildups:** **Oil India** (crude up / PSU positivity), **Dalmia Bharat**, **Inox Wind**, **Premier Energies** (results optimism), **Page Industries**, **Tata Elxsi**.
- **Short buildups:** **HCL Tech**, Dabur, Asian Paints, Solar Industries, Alkem Labs, and notably **Bajaj Holdings** (watch carefully).
- **Defensive delivery-volume rise:** United Spirits, APL Apollo, Colgate-Palmolive, SBI, Motilal Oswal, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Marico.
- View: 3–4 month overhang, but India tends to positively surprise when consensus is bearish; **accumulate dips in a staggered way** with cash flow. On the IPO: take listing gains; for serious long-term investing, **accumulate after 1–2 quarters of results**.

### Ramakrishna (technicals)
- Yesterday's settlement-day volatility: gap-up fizzled within ~30 min, then sharp late recovery (-58 pts). **Bank Nifty stronger** (+128, above 20-EMA). **Bottom ~25,500** (Monday) supports near term.
- **Momentum still negative:** Nifty **RSI ~41**, MACD below reference line; Bank Nifty **RSI ~53** but MACD still below line. **Short-term MAs (20/50-DMA) ~200–250 pts away** — stay cautious until crossed; need a clean move above 26,000 for smooth upside.
- **Avoid positional trading** next few days (holidays + event risk). Intraday yesterday's strategy failed (~20-pt SL hit after 3 wins; ~70–75% probability, always use stops).
- **Trade idea today:** if Nifty dips to **~25,640–25,650**, attempt an **intraday long with ~40-pt stop, target ~40–50 pts**, close same day.

## Viewer Q&A (answered by Kranthi / Vasanth — not Kutumba Rao)
- **Shivaparvathi (Kaikalur), Rs 10k SIP:** Jio BlackRock Flexi Cap, Bandhan Small Cap, HDFC Nifty 50 Index / Nifty 50 Equal Weight all OK (Equal Weight returns ≈ Nifty). Defence & Banking are cyclical/thematic — only if ready for cycles; **prefer Banking over Defence**.
- **Ravinder Reddy:** **Hold** South Indian Bank (buy @36, good resilience), TCS (positive CEO Krithivasan commentary, "worst behind"), Cummins India (add on dips). South Indian Bank the weaker of the three but good momentum.
- **Swati (Nellore), Rs 2L Capital Infra InvIT:** invest **for ~9% dividend yield** only (near 52-wk low); capital appreciation uncertain — diversify across 2–3 InvITs / REITs (e.g. add Power Grid InvIT for ~6%+).
- **Venkata Sudhir, Rs 15L MFs:** ICICI Pharma Health & Diagnostic, ICICI Flexi Cap, Nippon India Banking, Nippon India Small Cap, Invesco Large & Mid Cap — good selection; **don't lumpsum at once**, spread / deploy on big falls, continue SIPs; watch the two sectoral funds carefully.
- **Lakshmanachari (Nizamabad):** Diwali holdings — resend specific stock names to assess averaging/adding.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 12 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5U9ZSIhFT6o

## Global backdrop
- **US markets strong** into 2026 — **S&P 500** closed near its life-high Friday; **Nasdaq +0.8%**. The 2025 pattern (US strong, India weak) is continuing unchanged.
- **Gold ~4,600** and **silver ~$83/oz** at record highs; commodities/metals being treated as **safe havens** amid global turmoil. Base metals (gold, silver, copper, zinc) being bought; even ferrous metals may follow.
- Emerging markets (Korea, etc.) trading in profit; **Japan closed** for a holiday.
- **Geopolitical uncertainty** flagged as the dominant driver, attributed largely to the **USA**: Jerome **Powell** says DOJ is pressuring him with an enquiry/summons (alleged retaliation for not cutting rates); **Venezuela** crisis and reports (Economic Times) of more such attacks to come; **Greenland/Denmark** takeover talk straining NATO; **Iran** unrest with protester deaths. Trump's re-election called a "historic blunder."

## Indian markets
- **Nifty lost ~700 points over the week** (from Monday's high); another ~200-point fall Friday — five straight negative closings. Markets are **underperforming** global peers.
- **FPIs continuous sellers** — sold **Rs 3,769 cr** Friday; **DIIs bought Rs 5,600 cr**. FPI **short positions** rose from **1.28 lakh** to **1.86 lakh contracts** over the week (Nifty lot-size change a partial factor); Friday they unwound 1,000 longs and added 17,000 shorts. Only ~0.5% net long — a near-record short, **oversold** setup (**PCR 0.62**, was 0.66). A bounce is likely; an Oct-14-2025 analog saw Nifty rally ~900-1,000 points off similar extremes.
- **Pre-budget rally?** Under 15 sessions to the budget. A possible **FPI tax sweetener** (LTCG exemption) is a positive "feeler"; rupee depreciation and richer Indian valuations keep FPIs looking elsewhere.
- **Breadth very weak** — Friday A/D 577:2,384 (~1:5); **Midcap −2.6%**, **Smallcap −3.5%** (portfolios likely −5-6%). Nifty 500 breadth 87 advances vs 412 declines.
- **Levels:** 50-DMA broken; testing **100-DMA**; **200-DMA at 25,250**. Key support **25,500** must hold — break risks further weakness and a possible bear-market shift if earnings + budget disappoint. Currently a **"bear hug,"** not yet a bear market.
- **Earnings season** kicks off: **TCS** and **HCL Tech** Q3 today (watch AI commentary, IT spends); **Reliance** Friday; **ICICI Bank** and **HDFC Bank** Saturday — ~30-40% of Nifty weight reports this week.
- **Results so far:** **Tejas Networks** — terrible Q3 (**Rs 134 cr operating loss**, **Rs 197 cr net loss**; income YoY −88%); **DMart** weak initial updates but decent numbers (slight indirect drag on Reliance sentiment).
- **BSE in focus:** SEBI chief said no further derivatives fixes needed; **NSE IPO NOC** expected this month, IPO possibly next month.
- **Technicals (Ramakrishna):** Fibonacci retracements crossing 100% toward 160%; immediate Nifty support **25,560 / 25,500**; **RSI 38.55**, MACD negative crossover, at lower Bollinger band. **India VIX** rose 3% to **10.93** (off historic lows ~9.1-9.2), could rise to **13.5** by budget. Bank Nifty closed below 20-DMA and 50-DMA; supports far (~**58,750 / 58,000**, ~1,200 pts below), resistance **59,500 call / 25,800 Nifty call**. Intraday short idea: short below **25,590** (60-70 pt target, ~50 pt stop) if it holds 5 minutes.
- **Stance:** Capital protection; keep cash; no panic selling and no exuberant buying. Book partial profits where they exist; deploy any surplus slowly into good-earnings companies; keep mutual-fund SIPs running. Metal stocks (zinc, copper, Vedanta, Hindustan Copper, Hindalco, Hindustan Zinc) the lone relative bright spot.

## Analysts featured
- **Kranthi / Vasanth** — handled the macro discussion and most viewer stock Q&A (Tejas, Yes Bank context, Central Bank, Greaves/HG Infra/Suzlon, Edelweiss, Excel, Trent/Kaynes/Nuvama/BlackBuck). Default verdict in this weak tape: **hold, don't add, wait for results/budget**.
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals (Nifty/Bank Nifty levels, VIX, intraday short setup; gave a technical Yes Bank read of ~27 over 6-12 months).
- **Kutumba Rao — NOT present this episode.** The host noted he **returns on the 18th**, and deferred the Tejas Networks caller's add/hold decision to him. No Kutumba Rao stock calls were made.

## Notable single stocks / news
- **Lemon Tree Hotels** +3% on board approval for restructuring.
- **IREDA** +4% (positive results); **IFCI** ~+6%.
- Metals/financials gaining: Hindalco, HPCL, HDFC Life, SBI Life, Asian Paints, Shriram Finance, M&M, Manappuram, Waaree/Premier Energies, Muthoot, Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc, IEX.
- Losers: Tejas Networks −4% (~390), BEL, Max Healthcare, Eternal, Apollo Hospitals, InterGlobe Aviation, ICICI Bank, BHEL, Signature Global, Kirloskar Oil, TBO Tek, AFFLE, DCM Shriram, Century Ply, SBI Card, ABB Power.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 9 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/8gzVd1qGj0I

## Global backdrop
- **Weak global cues**; GIFT Nifty pointing to a soft open after Thursday's sell-off.
- **Trump's "Graham Bill"** threatens tariffs of **up to 500%** on China, Japan, India — described as a "throwaway"/black-swan number with no analytical basis (vs. India's own max import tax of ~150–200% on luxury cars). Markets cracked from **11:00** when the news hit the US session.
- **US Supreme Court** to rule today (decision possibly ~8pm) on whether Trump's tariff policy is legal. An adverse ruling could give markets a **temporary relief**, but Trump would re-impose via other routes; if tariffs are struck down, the US may have to **refund ~$600bn** already collected (Treasury pressure), though Trump could try to overrule.
- Other Trump flashpoints: **Venezuela** occupation (extracting ~**300bn barrels** of oil reserves for US majors like Chevron; US Senate blocked further strikes 50/47), threats on **Iran** and **Greenland**.
- **Brent crude ~$63** — a negative; comfort zone $60–65, worry only above **$65**.
- Read as **US arm-twisting India** (e.g. over Russian-oil purchases) to pressure equities.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty −265, Sensex −780** Thursday; Nifty ~1% down, closed below short MAs for a **4th straight day**, below the 20-DMA, now set to test the 50-DMA. **100% Fibonacci retrace** of the Oct→Jan ~1700-pt rise already done.
- **Make-or-break** session for Nifty. Fibonacci supports flagged: **25,755 (33%)**, **24,470 (50%)**, **24,250 (61.8%)**. OI/technical supports: **25,700 / 25,500**; resistance **26,000** (big round number, highest call writing) and 26,100. **Bank Nifty** outperforming (lost only ½%); supports **59,000–59,200**, must reclaim **60,200** to improve.
- **Breadth extremely weak**: 435 advances vs 2536 declines; Nifty-500 ~1 advance per 10 declines. **VIX** rose 7% to ~**10.6**. **PCR ~0.66** (oversold — bounce possible any time). RSI 44.11 / bearish MACD on Nifty.
- **Flows:** DIIs **+Rs 3,700 cr**; FIIs **−Rs 3,367 cr cash**, but **~Rs 12,000 cr** including F&O (index futures −5,285 cr, stock futures −3,270 cr) — historically heavy net shorts, no near-term short-covering trigger without a trade deal. FPIs have exited **~Rs 2,50,000 cr**.
- **Sector pain:** export-oriented names hit (Reliance, metals, textiles like **Gokuldas Exports**, **Page Industries**, **Avanti Feeds**); IT weak on pre-results nerves (TCS kicks off earnings Monday). **Power capital goods** hammered 4–10% (CG Power, Transformers & Rectifiers, Hitachi Energy, BHEL) on news the govt will let **Chinese player TBEA** into **PGCIL** tenders; execution failures a recurring theme. **BHEL** won a **Rs 5,400 cr** order.
- **Today:** **Bharat Coking Coal IPO** opens (Rs 21–23/share); **IEX** market-coupling hearing. At open: Nifty +20, Sensex +80, Bank Nifty −50; **Vodafone Idea +5%** (reliefs), Indus Towers up; oil marketers, Reliance slightly negative on firm crude.

## Stance / what to do
- **Protect capital**; do NOT average down or buy fresh just because prices fell. Take profits on any sudden spurt; keep MTF "free of cost."
- **Stay invested** in strong/domestic-focused names; **reshuffle on results** — exit weak stocks, hold those with earnings visibility. For quality names, **accumulate in a staggered manner** (nobody knows the bottom).
- **Traders:** mostly **avoid today** (Friday whipsaw risk); only sharp, both-sides players should trade. Be **very choosy on IPOs**.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao — NOT present** in this episode (not named anywhere); therefore no Kutumba Rao calls are recorded.
- **Kranthi** — markets + fundamental viewer Q&A (a different analyst from Kutumba Rao). Host = **Vasanth** (recovering from illness).
- **Ramakrishna** — technicals (levels, momentum, OI).

## Stock & fund calls (viewer Q&A) — by Kranthi & Ramakrishna (NOT Kutumba Rao)
- **NRB Bearings** — start buying from here (auto-ancillary tailwind; results may be good); use small dips. — *Kranthi*
- **Rolex Rings** — wait for this quarter's results; good entry near the ~100 previous-high low. — *Kranthi*
- **Capson (Capson Services)** — perennial buyer, **add on every dip** (28% sales / 25% profit 10-yr CAGR, 53% 3-yr stock CAGR; ~Rs 600 cr micro-cap); disclosed personal/friends' shareholding bias. — *Kranthi*
- **Angel One** — **avoid** (volumes down ~50%, broking under pressure; stock 2800→2400). — *Kranthi*
- **Shriram Finance** — fast-growing NBFC, FIIs entering aggressively; **buy on pullback ~940**, not immediately (Ramakrishna). — *Kranthi + Ramakrishna*
- **Tata Technologies** — **add slowly in dips** after results / management commentary; institutional interest returning. — *Kranthi*
- **Tejas Networks** — **hold** (domestic-focused/telecom buying interest). — *Kranthi*
- **Trent** — long-term hold only (PE cooling 120→87; retail under pressure); add staggered in dips; avoid short-term. — *Kranthi*
- **Rain Industries** — wait; high debt/restructuring overhang; technically buy only above 154 (Ramakrishna). — *Kranthi + Ramakrishna*
- **Senco Gold** (and jewellers Thangamayil, Kalyan) — **hold**, strong sales updates on high gold prices. — *Kranthi*
- **Laurus Labs** — technical entry **1040–1050** on the pullback before results (Ramakrishna).
- **SMS Pharma** — technically strong (higher highs/lows, near all-time high); buy on dips (Ramakrishna); fundamentally wait for results.
- **MF picks (74-yr viewer, Rs 90L equity already):** Parag Parikh **or** HDFC Flexi Cap; Nippon India Mid Cap; Bandhan Small Cap; WhiteOak Multi Asset Fund (50% debt+cash / 23% equity / 11% gold). — *Kranthi*

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 8 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/2T6wZUkB9yI

## Global backdrop
- **US markets mixed.** After a strong prior-session rally, **Dow fell ~500 pts** at last close; **S&P** pulled back modestly off record lows. **Asian indices** trading lower/mixed in early trade. **GIFT Nifty −10 to −13 pts**, pointing to a **negative open** for India.
- Sentiment dragged by **geopolitical risk** and **renewed tariff-related worries**; FIIs continuing to sell.
- **Gold and silver** saw fresh **profit-booking** overnight (after recent run-ups in bullion).

## Indian markets (prior session)
- **Third straight down session.** Nifty closed **−37 pts at ~26,140** after facing strong resistance twice at **26,180–26,190** and recovering from day's lows ~26,069; formed another **doji** but held above the 20-DMA. **Sensex −102 at 84,961** (recovered ~350 pts off lows ~84,617, tested 85,075). **Bank Nifty −128 at ~59,991**, slipping just below 60,000 — small bearish candle with long lower shadow (minor weakness) but above all key MAs.
- **FIIs net sold Rs 107 cr** equities; total market volumes **subdued**. **India VIX** dropped below 10 (−0.67% to **9.95**), still above its 20-DMA.
- **Sector moves:** Consumer durables, IT, pharma up **0.5–1%+**; auto, oil & gas, realty, telecom down ~½%. **BSE Midcap +0.5%, Smallcap +0.12%.**
- **Gainers:** Titan, Tech Mahindra, HCL Tech, Wipro, Jio Financial (+~1.5% to ~4%). **Losers:** Maruti Suzuki, Max Healthcare, TVS, Power Grid (−1.5% to −4%).
- **Breadth:** ~140 stocks at **52-week highs** (Torrent Pharma, Titan, JB Chemicals, NMDC, ABB India, BHEL, Nalco, Laurus Labs, KEI, Eicher, Granules, Vedanta, Nestle, SBI Life, etc.); **100+ at 52-week lows** (Vedant Fashions, Premier Energies, Mahanagar Gas, Whirlpool, Dixon, Kaynes).

## Stance / what to do
- **As long as Nifty holds 26,000** decisively, no need to panic; a **break of 26,000** risks panic selling. Range-bound between highest call OI (**26,500**) and highest put OI (**25,500**).
- **Stay invested; buy any dip; book some profit on sudden spurts** — managing cash flow is the key skill in this range-bound, "boring" ~45-session phase. Indian **macros remain strong** (PMO/Invest India data; JP Morgan "India Investment Focus" report reaffirming strong macros) → positive for FPI/FDI sentiment.
- **Bottom-fishing** seen in long-subdued names (Tata Elxsi, Elecon, Saregama, Sonata, KPIT, Tata Technologies); volume build-up in HDFC Bank, Suzlon, ICICI Bank, Hindustan Copper; IEX lifted by a court order.
- **Technicals:** clear direction likely only **after the Budget**; expect stock-/sector-specific moves on **Q3 results**. **Sensex expiry today** → minor volatility; likely a small-range consolidation day (mostly no clear trade unless Nifty hits 26,000 or crosses 26,200). **Bank Nifty stronger** (~400 pts from all-time high) and likely to support Nifty.

## Analysts featured
- **Kranthi** — markets/fundamental analyst, answered viewer Q&A (fundamentals).
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst (levels, momentum, entry triggers).
- **Note:** **Kutumba Rao did NOT appear** in this episode; his sidecar `buys.json` is intentionally empty. The viewer stock calls (KSE/KCI, CG Power, Protean, BHEL, City Union Bank, GNA Axles, Ola Electric, Morepen, VPRPL, Amara Raja, Kaynes, Dixon, Tejas, Inox India, HDFC Bank, Paras Defence, Suzlon, MRF, Page Industries, etc.) were given by Kranthi and Ramakrishna — see the kutumba_rao note for the full reference list.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 6 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/fYkcW8G3-sU

## Global backdrop
- **US markets closed strong overnight**: **S&P 500 +0.64%**, **Nasdaq +0.69%**, **Dow Jones +600 pts (+1.23%)** led by old-economy/oil names (**Chevron +5.5%**).
- **Venezuela**: US arrested and removed the president overnight; despite denials of "occupation," Trump signalled "the US will run Venezuela" — eyeing the **world's largest oil reserves** and saying crude prices will fall from here.
- **Commodities super-cycle**: **Brent ~62 dollars**; **gold back near 4,600 dollars**; **silver +7% overnight**; **copper at a record high, first time above 13,000 dollars/tonne**.
- **Copper deep-dive (JP Morgan)**: refined-copper **deficit of ~3,30,000 tonnes for 2026**; **LME copper stocks at multi-year/record lows**; supply glut from mine shutdowns/restructuring (**Cobre Panama, El Teniente, Grasberg**) plus surging worldwide energy demand. House view: copper prices "could double from here" and need not surprise.
- **Geopolitics flagged as the key short-term risk** — overnight tariff-hike noise from **Trump** and pivot of attention from Venezuela toward **Greenland**.

## Indian markets
- **Nifty couldn't hold the ~26,300 all-time-high zone; closed -0.33%** the prior session on **IT weakness (IT index -1.43%/~1.5%)**; HDFC Bank among losers.
- Despite the negative close, Nifty printed **higher highs / higher lows** (highs 26,340→26,373; lows 26,118→26,210) — read as a possible trend reversal.
- **FII selling very small at just 36 crores**; **DIIs bought 1,764 crores**; **put-call ratio back to ~1.0** (from 1.46) — markets **not overbought**.
- Theme: **buy on declines, sell on rallies**; favour **large-caps** as a heavy **2026 IPO pipeline** (PhonePe ~13,500 cr, Zepto ~12,000 cr, OYO ~6,650 cr, SBI Funds Mgmt ~1.4bn dollars/~12-13k cr, plus Fractal AI, Shiprocket, Shadowfax, boAt, Curefoods) will **drain secondary-market liquidity**, hitting mid/small-caps hardest.
- **Today**: Nifty weekly expiry (6 Jan); **results season kicks off** (GM Breweries first, as usual); IT results lead and are the swing factor.
- Positive Q3 business updates: **L&T Finance, Kotak Mahindra Bank, Axis Bank, Trent**; **Dabur mixed**; **Utkarsh SFB weak**.
- **Banks/financials in focus** — M&A buzz (IndusInd interest, RBL foreign buyers, Shriram Finance/Japanese investor); budget likely to bring PSU-bank reforms. Keep **PSU banks, select private banks and NBFCs** on radar.
- **Open session**: opened weak/negative (GIFT Nifty had pointed up — de-coupled). Breadth positive (~**1070:640**). Metals, PSU banks, IT, pharma, auto, capital-markets opened green; Trent **down ~7.5%** after Q3 update; Hind Copper, Brainbees, Motherson Sumi up; Bank Nifty turned green (~+100 pts) and outperformed.

## Technical levels (Ramakrishna)
- **Nifty**: healthy profit-booking at ATH; minor pullback possible 1-2 sessions. Supports ~**25,900-26,000**; resistance the big round number **26,500**. RSI eased to **58.79**, MACD positive crossover held. OI: call writing at **26,300** (today's resistance), highest put writing **25,700**.
- **Bank Nifty** stronger — above Bollinger upper band and above the falling trend-line; support ~**59,400-59,500**, near resistance ~**66,500-66,600**; RSI **65.05**, MACD/histogram improving. Highest call writing 66,500, highest put writing 60,200 (ITM → better performance expected).
- Intraday idea: short the **26,300 call (6-Jan expiry)** at 24-25 with stop ~45, target near zero.

## Analysts featured
- **Kranti** (fundamentals/stock ideas) — handled most viewer stock Q&A; cautioned that metals are **cyclical** (cited SAIL buyers from 4 yrs ago still under water) and not to chase "metal-name" stocks blindly. Overall stance: **stay invested, cash is king, book spikes, buy dips**.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — levels above; technical buy/entry calls on specific names.
- **Vasanth** — anchor/host (hoarse-voiced this episode).
- **Kutumba Rao — NOT present in this episode** (no fundamental/market segment or stock calls from him).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 5 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/S-NzhXd6sVE

> Note: Today's show was hosted from **Vizag** by **Vasanth** (anchor/analyst), with **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) and, after the break, **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst) handling viewer Q&A. **Kutumba Rao was not present in this episode**, so there are no Kutumba Rao stock calls (buys.json is empty).

## Global backdrop
- **Venezuela** dominated the open: the US attacked, removed the country's president (and his wife) to America. Vasanth frames it as a move for control of resources, not "narco-terrorism."
- Venezuela holds the **world's largest crude reserves (~300 billion barrels)**; Saudi second at **267 billion**, Iran **209 billion**. It also holds **161 metric tonnes of gold (~$22.55 billion)**, the largest in Latin America.
- Venezuelan output has collapsed from **3 million bpd to under 1 million bpd**; **China** is the near-sole buyer, so the direct crude-price impact is small.
- Net read for India: **positive** — "drill, baby, drill" in Venezuela points to more supply, easing oil-price risk and reducing the need for Russian oil.

## Indian markets
- Friday (Jan 2) closed at **all-time highs** on Sensex, Nifty and Bank Nifty (first ATH close since October) — a strong green candle; 2026 starts on a positive note.
- **Nifty support** ~**26,100** (20-DMA) and **26,180** (Jan 2 low); upside target **26,500**, with resistance near **26,406** and a higher zone around **25,600–700** (sic, as stated) / **26,300–26,406**.
- Technicals (Ramakrishna): close at upper Bollinger band; 10- & 20-DMA trending up; **RSI 62.39** (Bank Nifty **67.55**) — not yet overbought; **PCR 1.46**; **VIX ~9.45** (+2.86%) — downside risk near-negligible. **Bank Nifty OI:** highest call writing **60,300**, highest put writing **60,000**; **Nifty** highest put writing **26,300**. Overall **buy-on-dips**.
- Kranthi's caveat: indication is currently **"sell on rallies"**; this week is crucial as **FIIs return full-fledged** from holidays — watch whether the ATH (26,300) holds or drifts back to 26,000.
- **Intraday open:** GIFT Nifty had signalled +70–80 but markets opened flat-to-negative (Nifty ~26,320, Sensex −50/−140, **Bank Nifty positive ~+100**). Breadth positive (**1,431 advances vs 862 declines**). IT weak ahead of results (Infosys, HCL Tech, Wipro, Tech Mahindra, Trent); oil explorers (ONGC, Reliance) and PSU banks firm.

## Sectors and earnings season
- **Q3 earnings season** begins this week (GM Brewers tomorrow; Elecon Engineering, Transformers & Rectifiers Thu/Fri; IT later), then the **Budget** at month-end.
- **Banks/Financials** flagged as the key positive — strong Q3 business updates: **AU SFB** loan book +24% YoY / +6.5% QoQ, deposits +22%; **Bajaj Finance** AUM +22%, loan growth +15%; **M&M Finance** +7%; **Bank of Baroda** +14%. Also good: RBL, PNB, HDFC Bank, Ujjivan SFB, CSB, Punjab & Sind, South Indian Bank.
- Other Q3 updates: **Sobha** (sales **Rs 2,115 cr**) and **Marico** positive; **Avenue Supermarts/DMart** weak; **Vedanta** record aluminium/alumina/zinc output → metals expected to post stellar numbers.
- Preferred sectors for 2026: **Banks/Financials/NBFCs, autos, IT, chemicals, metals/precious metals** (book some profit on metals at higher levels). Strong **large-cap bias** over mid/small-caps.

## Analysts featured
- **Vasanth** — anchor/analyst (macro, sectors, Nifty levels). Hosting from Vizag.
- **Ramakrishna** — technical analyst (levels, OI, RSI/MACD/VIX, technical Q&A).
- **Kranthi** — fundamental analyst handling viewer stock Q&A (NOT Kutumba Rao).
- **Kutumba Rao** — **not present** today.

## Viewer Q&A (Kranthi / Ramakrishna — NOT Kutumba Rao)
- **RBL Bank / Bandhan Bank:** both weak private banks — **hold**, wait one quarter (RBL: wait till Budget if intending to sell).
- **Paytm:** **hold**; rating/brokerage upgrades; can rally 10–15% next 1–2 quarters.
- **Orient Cement:** **hold** (merging into Ambuja, 9–12 months); tracks Ambuja, which may break a falling trendline and recover over 6–12 months.
- **Kaynes Technologies:** **add slowly on dips** (CG Power & Kaynes named by minister for advanced chip launch within a year).
- **Dixon:** start adding bit by bit (not aggressively).
- **Tata Investment Corp (~703):** value largely unlocked — **hold/no fresh buying**; long consolidation likely.
- **Senco Gold → PN Gadgil:** decide after Q3 results; gold-jewellery stocks consolidating; PN Gadgil expanding (promoter holding 83%); Senco looks better — leans PN Gadgil. HDFC Bank → AMC: only if risk appetite (HDFC AMC subsidiary, but duplication); else stick to HDFC.
- **NTPC Green, Havells:** **hold** long term. **Force Motors:** **hold** with trailing stop-loss.
- **Orient Technologies:** book some profit on spikes (post-bonus); watch 500 range.
- **CAMS:** buy with long-term (1yr+) view; market-infra play (demat accounts seen doubling by 2028).
- **Tata Chemicals, Vinati Organics:** **hold** long term (chemicals flat ~18 months; only SRF up).
- **BSE:** **hold** (strong futures volumes, product innovation); preferred over **Groww** (BSE = better choice between the two).
- **Ramco Cement, Ambuja Cement:** **hold** — cement to be in limelight post-Budget on infra capex.
- **Kotis Healthcare:** enter 50% now, rest after it crosses 1,000.
- **SJVN:** add a bit (good volume, supports 70–72) at least till Budget. **CG Power:** **accumulate slowly** (Cobra Post allegations dismissed as baseless; stock stable).
- **Clean Science:** **hold** (decent company; promoters cut stake 75%→50%; recent IPOs/mid-cap IT consolidating).
- **Dwarikesh Sugar:** **avoid averaging** now (sugar weak, 52-wk lows); average only near 45–50.
- **International MF exposure (Gopi):** Franklin India US Opportunities (active FoF), Kotak US passive FoF, Edelweiss US Technology Fund; PPFAS already ~50% US equities.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 2 January 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/sgMUNtw0RzQ

## Global backdrop
- **GIFT Nifty** pointed to a positive open, indicating roughly a **30-35 point** gain.
- Most Asian markets were shut for the holiday; those open (**Hang Seng, Taiwan, Kospi**) traded positive. Japan and China still on holiday — meaningful global cues expected only from Monday.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday (Jan 1) India was the only major market trading; thin, holiday-mood volumes. **Nifty** closed up **~17 pts** in a narrow ~80-84 pt range (high 26,113/26,197). **Bank Nifty +30 pts**; Sensex slightly negative. **Nifty Next 50** and **Midcap 100** outperformed.
- **ITC** was the biggest drag — about **95 Nifty points** alone — on a coming "sin tax." A **National Calamity Contingent Duty (NCCD)** + central excise hike on tobacco takes effect **Feb 1** (GST route not yet ready). **Godfrey Phillips -17%, ITC -9%**; NTC, VST also fell. Cigarette prices could rise up to **15%**, with some volume hit. ITC saw **Rs 6,000 cr** delivery-based selling.
- **Flows:** FIIs net sellers again at **Rs 3,268 cr**; DIIs bought only **Rs 1,525 cr**.
- **Macro:** December **GST revenue Rs 1,76,857 cr**, YoY growth just **1.3%** (vs Rs 1,70,276 cr last Nov) — muted, weak festive sales.
- **Corporate actions / news:**
  - **QSR consolidation:** **Sapphire Foods** to merge into **Devyani International** — swap **1.77:1** (better than the 1:2 feared), so Devyani **+7%**, Sapphire **-6%** intraday. Consolidates KFC/Pizza Hut brands.
  - **Indian Bank** Q update strong (total business **+13.2%**, advances **+14.2%**); **Punjab & Sind Bank** weak.
  - Government awarded a fresh **Rs 20,000 cr** highway order (Solapur–Nashik, Maharashtra).
  - **MCX** ex-split today (trading ~2,227 post-split).
  - Big 2026 IPO pipeline cited: **BOAT, NSE, OYO, PhonePe, Reliance Jio, SBI Mutual Fund, Zepto** and more.
- **Q1 outlook:** Earnings season, Budget, and a possible trade deal make the quarter eventful; results begin in ~5-6 sessions.
- **Open day:** Nifty opened **+27-28 pts**, Sensex **+110**, Bank Nifty **+135**; autos led on strong monthly sales; **ITC down a further ~4%**.

## Analysts featured
- **Kranthi** (fundamental analyst) — handled most viewer stock Q&A. View: market in **index management**, holding **26,000**; **stay invested**, treat declines as buying opportunities; **avoid building ITC positions** in the uncertainty (falling knife). *(Kutumba Rao was NOT on this episode.)*
- **Ramakrishna** (technical analyst) — Nifty resistance **26,300-350** / intraday **26,204**; supports **26,050 / 26,000 / 26,098**. RSI 56.6, positive MACD breakout, histogram above zero. Bank Nifty stronger (RSI 61.95), resistance ~62,300, support 59,400. **VIX at record-low ~9.18** flagged as a risk for sharp pre-results profit-booking. Fourth attempt at the all-time high; range-bound/consolidation likely until ~Tuesday.
- **Vasanth** — anchor.

## Viewer stock Q&A (by Kranthi / Ramakrishna — NOT Kutumba Rao)
- **Force Motors** (Kranthi): underdog, +100% in 1yr; add only with high risk appetite on deep cuts; wait for quarter results.
- **Coforge** (Kranthi): wait — acquisition uncertainty not yet cleared.
- **Ramaa Steel Tubes & SBC Exports** (Kranthi): fundamentally very poor; exit on rallies, don't average.
- **Jyothy Labs** (Ramakrishna): don't average; technically weak (~277), weak chemicals; wait for next results.
- **GRM Overseas / SRM / SKM Egg** (Kranthi): all speculative; avoid for momentum.
- **PNC Infra, Exicom** (Kranthi): avoid/don't average (fundamental issues). **Central Bank** (Kranthi): decent, accumulate / enter after this quarter's results.
- **GMR Airports** (Kranthi): buy at current rate for long term.
- **NCC, Bajaj Housing Finance** (Kranthi): hold (whole EPC sector in a slump; possible post-Budget rally); don't average BHFin unless blockbuster results.
- **Finolex Pipes, Apollo Pipes** (Ramakrishna): avoid/don't buy below ~200; pipes already ran a year.
